<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-triage, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24486748716, workflow_id: errors-agents-triage, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24486748716 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-triage -->
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error `[UriError]: cannot call joinPath on URI without path` is thrown in `debugConfigurationManager.ts` when the debug `Launch` class's `uri` getter calls `resources.joinPath(this.workspace.uri, '/.vscode/launch.json')` on a workspace folder whose URI (`unknown:`) has no path component. This occurs in the Agents (sessions) product when an active session has no repository metadata — the `AgentSessionAdapter._buildWorkspace()` method uses `URI.parse('unknown:')` as a fallback, producing a pathless URI that gets added as a workspace folder. On any workspace folder change event, the debug configuration manager creates `Launch` objects from all folders and accesses their `uri` getter, which throws. This is the **same root cause** as #308827 and its related bucket family (8+ error buckets, 760+ hits).

### Culprit Commit

No single culprit commit was identified for this specific bucket. The error first appeared on 1.115.0-insider commit `6c8fcf18` (April 6) and has been present across all subsequent 1.115.0-insider builds. The root cause code (`URI.parse('unknown:')` fallback in `_buildWorkspace()`) pre-dates the 1.115.0 release branch, suggesting this is a latent issue that was either introduced with the sessions workspace infrastructure or became triggerable when sessions without repository metadata started becoming active more frequently.

The broader error class was already identified in #308827, which covers the same root cause through different call paths (including `workspaceExtensionsConfig`, `promptFilesLocator`, `snippetsService`, etc.). This issue (#310370) is specifically the `debugConfigurationManager` call path variant.

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant WCS as WorkspaceContextService
    participant CM as ConfigurationManager
    participant Launch as Launch
    participant URI as URI.joinPath

    WCS->>CM: onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders / onDidChangeWorkbenchState
    CM->>CM: initLaunches() — creates Launch per folder
    CM->>CM: selectConfiguration(undefined)
    CM->>Launch: this.selectedLaunch.uri (line 433)
    Launch->>URI: joinPath(workspace.uri, '/.vscode/launch.json')
    Note over URI: workspace.uri = URI.parse('unknown:')<br/>— no path component
    Note over URI: 💥 [UriError]: cannot call<br/>joinPath on URI without path
```

**Root cause origin:**
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Session as AgentSession (no repo metadata)
    participant Builder as _buildWorkspace()
    participant FolderMgmt as WorkspaceFolderManagement
    participant WCS as WorkspaceContextService

    Session->>Builder: _extractRepositoryFromMetadata() → [undefined, ...]
    Note over Builder: ⚠️ Root cause: repoUri is undefined<br/>→ falls back to URI.parse('unknown:')
    Builder->>FolderMgmt: workspace = { repositories: [{ uri: unknown: }] }
    FolderMgmt->>FolderMgmt: getActiveSessionFolderData() → { uri: unknown: }
    Note over FolderMgmt: No path validation on repository URI
    FolderMgmt->>WCS: addFolders / updateFolders
    Note over WCS: Folder with pathless URI is now<br/>in the workspace
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` | **Root cause** — `_buildWorkspace()` (line 978) uses `URI.parse('unknown:')` as fallback when session has no repository metadata, producing a pathless URI |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/workspace/browser/workspaceFolderManagement.ts` | **Data flow** — `getActiveSessionFolderData()` (line 82) passes through the `unknown:` URI as a workspace folder without validating it has a path |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/debug/browser/debugConfigurationManager.ts` | **Crash site** — `Launch.uri` getter (line 646) calls `joinPath(this.workspace.uri, ...)` and `selectConfiguration` (line 433) accesses the `uri` getter |
| `src/vs/sessions/services/workspace/browser/workspaceContextService.ts` | **Event source** — `_doUpdateFolders` (line 163) fires `onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders` that triggers the debug configuration manager |
| `src/vs/base/common/uri.ts` | **Throw site** — `URI.joinPath()` (line 362) throws when URI has no path |

### Repro Steps

1. Open the VS Code Agents (sessions) application
2. Have an active session whose metadata does not include repository information (no `repositoryPath`, `workingDirectoryPath`, or cloud repo metadata) — the `_extractRepositoryFromMetadata()` method returns `[undefined, ...]`
3. The session's workspace gets a folder with `URI.parse('unknown:')` as the URI
4. Any workspace folder change event triggers `ConfigurationManager.registerListeners()` → `initLaunches()` → `selectConfiguration(undefined)`
5. `selectConfiguration` accesses `this.selectedLaunch.uri` (line 433), which triggers the `Launch.uri` getter that calls `resources.joinPath(this.workspace.uri, '/.vscode/launch.json')`
6. `URI.joinPath` throws because `unknown:` has no path component

The error is deterministic once a session without repo metadata becomes active and the debug configuration manager initializes.

### Fix Proposal

This is the same root cause as #308827. The fix should be applied at the source of the pathless URI rather than in each consumer:

**Primary fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` → `_buildWorkspace()`

**Approach**: When `repoUri` is `undefined`, either:
1. Return `undefined` from `_buildWorkspace()` instead of constructing a workspace with a pathless URI. The method signature already allows `ISessionWorkspace | undefined`.
2. Or change the fallback URI to include a root path: `URI.from({ scheme: 'unknown', path: '/' })` — this ensures `joinPath` can operate without throwing.

**Secondary defense**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/workspace/browser/workspaceFolderManagement.ts` → `getActiveSessionFolderData()` (line 82) — add a guard to reject URIs without a path (`if (!repository.path) return undefined`), preventing any pathless URI from becoming a workspace folder regardless of how it was produced.

**Note**: A fix in `debugConfigurationManager.ts` alone (e.g., try/catch around the `uri` getter) would only address this specific call path; the same error would persist through the 7+ other code paths identified in #308827.

### Recommended Owner

`@sandy081` — Already assigned to #308827 which tracks the same root cause. Primary maintainer of `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` and the sessions workspace infrastructure where the fix should be applied.

| Product | Agents |

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*

Comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The sessions `ConfigurationService` throws `"Unknown folder"` when a workspace folder is removed before its configuration has been loaded into the internal `folderConfigurations` map. This is a race condition inherent to the sessions configuration service design — the `onWorkspaceFoldersChanged` handler iterates `e.removed` folders and calls `compareAndDeleteFolderConfiguration()`, which throws if the folder config was never loaded. The error affects the Agents product across all platforms (Linux, Windows, Mac) and has been present since version 1.114.0-insider (March 27, 2026), with increasing hit counts as adoption grows — currently **~600+ hits / 268 users** on the latest 1.116 insider builds.

### Culprit Commit

No single culprit commit — this is an inherent design bug in the sessions `ConfigurationService` introduced when the service was originally created. PR #299302 by `@sandy081` ("fix reading configuration from workspace folders", merged March 5, 2026) is the closest related PR. The error has been present across versions 1.114 through 1.117, hitting consistently on every insider build.

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Caller as Workspace Consumer
    participant WCS as SessionsWorkspaceContextService
    participant CS as Sessions ConfigurationService
    participant Cfg as Configuration (platform)

    Caller->>WCS: addFolders([A]) / updateFolders(...)
    WCS->>CS: onWillChangeWorkspaceFolders (added: [A])
    CS->>CS: loadFolderConfigurations([A]) (async)
    Note over CS: ⏳ Awaiting folder config load...
    WCS->>WCS: workspace.update(newWorkspace)
    WCS->>CS: onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders (removed: [])
    Note over CS: ✅ No removals, OK

    Caller->>WCS: removeFolders([A])
    WCS->>CS: onWillChangeWorkspaceFolders (added: [])
    Note over CS: Nothing to load
    WCS->>WCS: workspace.update(newWorkspace)
    WCS->>CS: onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders (removed: [A])
    CS->>Cfg: compareAndDeleteFolderConfiguration(A)
    Note over Cfg: ⚠️ folderConfigurations.get(A)<br/>returns undefined if config<br/>never loaded (race/init timing)
    Note over Cfg: 💥 throw new Error('Unknown folder')
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/platform/configuration/common/configurationModels.ts` | 💥 Crash site — `compareAndDeleteFolderConfiguration()` throws at line 946 |
| `src/vs/workbench/services/configuration/common/configurationModels.ts` | Data flow — override delegates to super at line 140 |
| `src/vs/sessions/services/configuration/browser/configurationService.ts` | ⚠️ Root cause — `onWorkspaceFoldersChanged()` at line 262 iterates `e.removed` unsafely |
| `src/vs/sessions/services/workspace/browser/workspaceContextService.ts` | Trigger — `_doUpdateFolders()` fires events at line 163 |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Insiders with the Agents product (sessions enabled)
2. Create or enter a multi-folder workspace in a session
3. Rapidly add and remove workspace folders (or trigger a workspace update that removes a folder before its configuration has been fully loaded)
4. The error fires when a removed folder's configuration was never loaded into the `folderConfigurations` map — this can happen if:
   - `initialize()` hasn't completed when a folder removal event arrives
   - `loadFolderConfigurations()` fails or is still in-flight when a removal occurs
   - The `_configuration` object is replaced by `initialize()` (line 102-114) while folder changes are in progress

This is non-deterministic and depends on timing between async config loading and folder removal events.

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/services/configuration/browser/configurationService.ts` → `onWorkspaceFoldersChanged()`

**Approach**: Follow the pattern used by the workbench `ConfigurationService.onFoldersChanged()` (line 944-962 of `src/vs/workbench/services/configuration/browser/configurationService.ts`). Instead of iterating `e.removed` and calling `compareAndDeleteFolderConfiguration()` directly, iterate over `this.cachedFolderConfigs.keys()` and only delete folders that are no longer in the workspace. This ensures only folders whose configs were actually loaded are deleted:

```
// Current (unsafe) pattern in sessions ConfigurationService:
for (const folder of e.removed) {
    const change = this._configuration.compareAndDeleteFolderConfiguration(folder.uri);  // throws!
}

// Suggested fix (safe pattern from workbench ConfigurationService):
for (const key of this.cachedFolderConfigs.keys()) {
    if (!this.workspace.folders.some(f => f.uri.toString() === key.toString())) {
        this.cachedFolderConfigs.deleteAndDispose(key);
        changes.push(this._configuration.compareAndDeleteFolderConfiguration(key));
    }
}
````

This guarantees `compareAndDeleteFolderConfiguration` is only called for folders that have entries in the `folderConfigurations` map, eliminating the race condition.

### Recommended Owner

`@sandy081` — Original author of the sessions `ConfigurationService` and configuration infrastructure (PR #299302), and primary contributor to sessions workspace/configuration services.

| Last Seen | Fri Apr 10 2026 21:24:39 GMT+0000 (Coordinated Universal Time) |
| Platforms | Mac, Windows, Linux |
| Product | Agents |

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*

Comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

`getSessionTasks` in `sessionsConfigurationService.ts` calls `_ensureFileWatch(folder)` with a URI that has no path component. This happens when a session's repository has no metadata, causing `_buildWorkspace` in `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` to fall back to `URI.parse('unknown:')` — a URI with scheme `unknown` and an empty path. The `joinPath` call inside `_ensureFileWatch` then throws because it cannot join paths on a URI without a path. The error affects 101+ users across all platforms.

### Culprit Commit

No single regression commit was identified. This is a **latent interaction bug** between two code paths:

1. `_buildWorkspace()` in `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` (line 978) uses `URI.parse('unknown:')` as a fallback when a session has no repository metadata — creating a URI with no path.
2. `getSessionTasks()` in `sessionsConfigurationService.ts` (line 148–153) checks `if (folder)` but a pathless URI object is truthy, so it passes through to `_ensureFileWatch` which calls `joinPath` and crashes.

The `_ensureFileWatch` and `getSessionTasks` code was introduced by `@benibenj` in PRs #297027 / #297371 (Feb 2026). The `URI.parse('unknown:')` fallback was introduced later. The error first appeared in telemetry on April 7 with commit `d09c516f` (1 hit) and grew to 147 hits / 101 users by `41dd792b`.

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Provider as copilotChatSessionsProvider
    participant Model as agentSessionsModel
    participant RunScript as runScriptAction
    participant Config as sessionsConfigurationService
    participant URI as URI.joinPath

    Provider->>Model: _refreshSessionCache → fire onDidChangeSessions
    Model->>RunScript: observable recompute (_computeFn)
    RunScript->>Config: getSessionTasks(activeSession)
    Note over Config: _getSessionRepo(session)<br/>→ repo.uri = URI.parse('unknown:')
    Config->>Config: folder = repo.workingDirectory ?? repo.uri
    Note over Config: ⚠️ Root cause:<br/>folder is truthy but has no path<br/>(if (folder) passes)
    Config->>Config: _ensureFileWatch(folder)
    Config->>URI: joinPath(folder, '.vscode', 'tasks.json')
    Note over URI: 💥 Error thrown:<br/>[UriError]: cannot call joinPath<br/>on URI without path
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/sessionsConfigurationService.ts` | **Crash site** — `_ensureFileWatch` (L378) calls `joinPath` on pathless URI |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` | **Root cause producer** — `_buildWorkspace` (L978) creates `URI.parse('unknown:')` fallback |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/runScriptAction.ts` | Data flow — observable chain triggers `getSessionTasks` |
| `src/vs/base/common/uri.ts` | Guard — `joinPath` correctly validates URI has a path |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product)
2. Create or open a session that has **no repository metadata** (e.g., a session where `_extractRepositoryFromMetadata` returns all `undefined` values — this can happen with sessions that haven't fully resolved or have incomplete metadata)
3. The observable chain from `runScriptAction.ts` triggers `getSessionTasks` on the active session
4. `_buildWorkspace` returns a workspace with `uri: URI.parse('unknown:')` (no path)
5. `getSessionTasks` passes this URI to `_ensureFileWatch`, which crashes on `joinPath`

This is non-deterministic in the sense that it depends on session metadata availability. It triggers most often when sessions are being resolved or refreshed with incomplete data.

### Fix Proposal

**Two complementary fixes are recommended:**

**Fix 1 (primary)** — `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/sessionsConfigurationService.ts` → `getSessionTasks()`
**Approach**: Add a path check before calling `_ensureFileWatch`. Change the guard from `if (folder)` to `if (folder?.path)` to ensure the URI actually has a path before attempting to join paths on it. This mirrors the defensive pattern already used in `_getTasksJsonUri` (line 360) which also calls `joinPath` but only after confirming `folder` is defined.

**Fix 2 (defensive)** — `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` → `_buildWorkspace()`
**Approach**: When `repoUri` is undefined, either return `undefined` from `_buildWorkspace` instead of creating a workspace with a fake `unknown:` URI, or give the fallback URI a path (e.g., `URI.parse('unknown:/')`) so downstream consumers don't crash. The current `URI.parse('unknown:')` creates a URI that is technically invalid for any file-system-like operations.

### Recommended Owner

`@benibenj` — author of `sessionsConfigurationService.ts` (`getSessionTasks`, `_ensureFileWatch`) where the primary fix should go (PRs #297027, #297371, #302478)

| Product | Agents |

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*

Comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The `WorkspaceSettingsReader` in `workspacePluginSettingsService.ts` calls `joinPath(f.uri, configFolder)` on every workspace folder URI whenever workspace folders change, but does not guard against folders whose URIs have no path component. When a session's workspace contains a repository or worktree URI without a path (e.g., a URI with only a scheme and authority), `URI.joinPath` throws `[UriError]: cannot call joinPath on URI without path`. This affects the Agents product across all platforms, with **3,470 total hits / 1,443 users** spanning versions 1.114 through 1.116.

### Culprit Commit

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Commit | [`342a781`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/300585/commits/342a78145fe05163a37faf8f17a19ac78f702e86) |
| Author | `@connor4312` |
| PR | #300585 |
| Message | plugins: initial support for workspace plugins |
| Why | Introduced `WorkspaceSettingsReader` which unconditionally calls `joinPath` on all workspace folder URIs without validating that the URI has a path. The error has been present since this file was first added (merged March 11, milestone 1.112.0). |

### Code Flow

````mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant WFM as WorkspaceFolderManagement
    participant WCS as SessionsWorkspaceContextService
    participant OBS as observableFromEvent
    participant WSR as WorkspaceSettingsReader
    participant URI as URI.joinPath

    WFM->>WCS: addFolders([{uri: pathlessUri}])
    WCS->>WCS: _doUpdateFolders()
    Note over WCS: Creates WorkspaceFolder<br/>with URI lacking path
    WCS->>OBS: fire(onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders)
    OBS->>WSR: getValue() callback
    WSR->>URI: joinPath(f.uri, ".claude")
    Note over URI: ⚠️ Root cause:<br/>f.uri.path is empty string
    URI->>URI: 💥 throw Error("[UriError]:<br/>cannot call joinPath on<br/>URI without path")
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/plugins/workspacePluginSettingsService.ts` | **Root cause** — line 190: unguarded `joinPath` in observable callback |
| `src/vs/sessions/services/workspace/browser/workspaceContextService.ts` | **Trigger** — line 163: fires `onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders` without validating folder URIs have paths |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/workspace/browser/workspaceFolderManagement.ts` | **Data producer** — provides workspace folder URIs from session data |
| `src/vs/base/common/uri.ts` | **Crash site** — line 362: `joinPath` throws when `uri.path` is falsy |

### Repro Steps

1. Open the VS Code Agents product (vscode.dev with agents enabled)
2. Start or switch to a session whose workspace includes a repository URI without a path component (e.g., a URI with only scheme + authority, or a session that hasn't fully resolved its workspace folder path)
3. The `WorkspaceFolderManagement` contribution adds this folder to the workspace context
4. The `WorkspaceSettingsReader` observable reacts and attempts to `joinPath` on the pathless URI
5. The error is thrown as an unhandled error from the observable callback

> **Note**: The exact workspace configuration that produces a pathless URI depends on the session provider and its data. The error is cross-platform (Windows, Linux, Mac) and affects ~1,400 unique users, suggesting it occurs under relatively common workspace configurations.

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/plugins/workspacePluginSettingsService.ts` → `WorkspaceSettingsReader` constructor (line 190)

**Approach**: Filter out workspace folders whose URIs have no path before calling `joinPath`. Change the `observableFromEvent` callback from:
```
folders.map(f => joinPath(f.uri, configFolder))
```
to:
```
folders.filter(f => f.uri.path).map(f => joinPath(f.uri, configFolder))
````

Alternatively (defense in depth), also add a guard in `SessionsWorkspaceContextService._doUpdateFolders` to skip or normalize workspace folders whose URIs lack a path, preventing the invalid state from propagating to all downstream observers.

### Recommended Owner

`@connor4312` — author of `workspacePluginSettingsService.ts` (PR #300585) and the workspace plugin system. This is a missing guard in the code they introduced.

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-triage, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24486748716, workflow_id: errors-agents-triage, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24486748716 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-triage -->
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error `[UriError]: cannot call joinPath on URI without path` is thrown when `workspaceRecommendations.ts` handles a file change event and iterates workspace folders, calling `joinPath(folder.uri, '.vscode/extensions')` on a folder whose URI (`unknown:`) has no path component. This is the **same root cause** as the family of errors tracked in #308827 — workspace folder URIs in the Agents product lack a `path` component because `AgentSessionAdapter._buildWorkspace()` uses `URI.parse('unknown:')` as a fallback when a session has no repository metadata.

### Culprit Commit

No single culprit commit was identified. The error bucket history shows this error has been present since at least **1.114.0-insider** (first seen 2026-03-27) across all insider versions through 1.116.0. This is a systemic issue in the Agents workspace model, not a single-commit regression. The `URI.parse('unknown:')` fallback at `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts:978` has existed since the sessions workspace infrastructure was introduced.

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant IPC as File Watcher (IPC)
    participant FS as FileService
    participant WR as WorkspaceRecommendations
    participant ExtUri as extUri.joinPath
    participant URI as URI.joinPath

    IPC->>FS: onDidFilesChange
    FS->>WR: onDidFilesChange(e)
    WR->>WR: getWorkspace().folders.some(folder => ...)
    Note over WR: Iterates ALL workspace folders<br/>including folder with URI 'unknown:'
    WR->>ExtUri: joinPath(folder.uri, '.vscode/extensions')
    ExtUri->>URI: URI.joinPath(URI.parse('unknown:'), ...)
    Note over URI: 💥 [UriError]: cannot call<br/>joinPath on URI without path
    Note over WR: ⚠️ Root cause: _buildWorkspace()<br/>uses URI.parse('unknown:') fallback<br/>when session has no repo metadata
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` | **Root cause** — `_buildWorkspace()` (line 978) uses `URI.parse('unknown:')` (no path) as fallback when session has no repository metadata |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/workspace/browser/workspaceFolderManagement.ts` | **Data flow** — `getActiveSessionFolderData()` passes the `unknown:` URI through as a workspace folder URI without validating it has a path |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/extensions/browser/workspaceRecommendations.ts` | **Crash site** — line 61-62: `onDidFilesChange` handler calls `joinPath(folder.uri, '.vscode/extensions')` on all workspace folders |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/extensions/browser/workspaceRecommendations.ts` | **Crash site (2nd bucket `f6bd80ca`)** — line 80: `fetchWorkspaceExtensions()` calls `joinPath(workspaceFolder.uri, '.vscode/extensions')` |
| `src/vs/base/common/uri.ts` | **Throw site** — `URI.joinPath()` line 362 throws when URI has no path |

### Repro Steps

1. Open the VS Code Agents (sessions) application
2. Have an active session whose metadata does not include repository information (no `repositoryPath`, `workingDirectoryPath`, or cloud repo metadata)
3. The session's workspace folder is created with a `unknown:` URI (no path component)
4. Any file change event fires (e.g., from MCP tool registration, schema association changes, or actual filesystem activity)
5. `WorkspaceRecommendations.onDidFilesChange` iterates all workspace folders and calls `joinPath(folder.uri, '.vscode/extensions')`, which throws on the pathless URI

The error is deterministic once a session without repo metadata becomes active — it triggers on every file change event. Telemetry shows **16,600 hits from 104 users** on version 1.115.0 alone (commit `41dd792b`).

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` → `_buildWorkspace()`

**Approach**: This is the same root cause as #308827 and should be addressed together. The fix should either:

1. **Preferred**: Change the fallback URI to include a root path: `URI.from({ scheme: 'unknown', path: '/' })` — this ensures `joinPath` can operate without throwing, fixing all 8+ downstream call paths at once.
2. **Alternative**: Return `undefined` from `_buildWorkspace()` when `repoUri` is undefined, preventing a workspace folder from being created at all for sessions without repository metadata. The method signature already allows `undefined`.
3. **Defense in depth**: Add a guard in `workspaceFolderManagement.ts` → `getActiveSessionFolderData()` to reject URIs without a path (`if (!repository.path) return undefined`).

### Recommended Owner

`@sandy081` — Already assigned to the parent issue #308827 with the same root cause. Primary maintainer of the sessions workspace management layer and `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts`.

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*

Comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error `[UriError]: cannot call joinPath on URI without path` occurs in the Agents product when a session has no repository metadata. The `_buildWorkspace()` method in `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` creates a fallback URI `URI.parse('unknown:')` which has **no path component**. When workspace folders change, `textFileService.ts` calls `joinPath(folder.uri, '.vscode')` on every folder URI, and this path-less URI causes the throw. This is a persistent issue across versions 1.114–1.116 affecting ~1,600+ users cumulatively.

### Culprit Commit

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Commit | [`f62d4324`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/f62d4324c5cbb0a5188f117f5a5c2344be2ae790) (via PR #309777) |
| Author | `@aeschli` |
| PR | #309777 |
| Message | Empty sessions view: cannot call joinPath |
| Why | This PR changed `URI.parse('unknown://')` to `URI.parse('unknown:')` and improved the error message, but **both URIs have empty paths** — the fix was incomplete. The original `unknown://` fallback was introduced earlier when `_buildWorkspace` was created. The PR author noted: "Maybe you want to revisit `unknown:///`" — suggesting the proper fix was not applied. |

> **Note**: The error existed *before* PR #309777 as well (since at least March 27, 2026). The original `URI.parse('unknown://')` also has no path. PR #309777 was an incomplete fix attempt.

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant WFM as WorkspaceFolderManagement
    participant WCS as SessionsWorkspaceContextService
    participant TFS as TextFileService
    participant URI as URI.joinPath

    WFM->>WFM: updateWorkspaceFoldersForSession()
    Note over WFM: Session has no repo metadata<br/>→ getActiveSessionFolderData()<br/>returns uri: URI.parse('unknown:')
    WFM->>WCS: addFolders([{uri: unknown:}])
    WCS->>WCS: _doUpdateFolders()
    WCS->>TFS: fire onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders
    TFS->>TFS: getDefaultEncodingOverrides()
    loop Each workspace folder
        TFS->>URI: joinPath(folder.uri, '.vscode')
        Note over URI: ⚠️ folder.uri.path is ''<br/>(empty = falsy)
        URI-->>TFS: 💥 throw [UriError]:<br/>cannot call joinPath on<br/>URI without path
    end
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` | **Root cause** — `_buildWorkspace()` line 978 creates `URI.parse('unknown:')` with no path |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/workspace/browser/workspaceFolderManagement.ts` | Data flow — passes the path-less URI as a workspace folder |
| `src/vs/workbench/services/textfile/browser/textFileService.ts` | **Crash site** — `getDefaultEncodingOverrides()` line 813 calls `joinPath(folder.uri, '.vscode')` |
| `src/vs/base/common/uri.ts` | Error thrown at `URI.joinPath()` line 362 when `uri.path` is falsy |

### Related Issues

- **#308827** — Same root cause (`unknown:` URI with no path), different crash site (`workspaceExtensionsConfig.ts` / `mcpToolRegistration`). Already assigned to `@sandy081`.

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Agents (sessions desktop)
2. Create or switch to a session that has **no repository metadata** (e.g., an empty/new session without a configured repository)
3. The workspace folder management adds a folder with URI `unknown:` (no path)
4. Any code listening to `onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders` that calls `joinPath` on the folder URI will throw

The error is triggered automatically when workspace folders update — no explicit user action beyond having a session without repo metadata is required.

### Fix Proposal

**Primary fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` → `_buildWorkspace()`

**Option A (preferred)**: When `repoUri` is `undefined`, do not create a workspace at all — return `undefined` from `_buildWorkspace()` instead of using a meaningless `unknown:` fallback. The downstream code in `workspaceFolderManagement.ts` already handles `undefined` workspace data (lines 45-49).

**Option B (minimal)**: Change `URI.parse('unknown:')` to `URI.parse('unknown:///')` which includes a root path `/`. This prevents the `joinPath` error but leaves a non-resolvable URI as a workspace folder, which may cause other issues (as `@aeschli` noted in PR #309777).

**Option C (defensive)**: Add a guard in `textFileService.ts` → `getDefaultEncodingOverrides()` to skip folders whose URIs have no path: `if (folder.uri.path) { ... }`. This protects against *any* path-less URI but only addresses this one call site — other listeners (like #308827's `workspaceExtensionsConfig.ts`) would still crash.

### Recommended Owner

`@sandy081` — Already assigned to the sibling issue #308827 (same root cause, different crash site) and is the primary author of the sessions workspace management code (PR #298395, multiple `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` changes).

## Details
| Property | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Bucket ID | [`7bee8418-38a2-e42b-2af1-d0d5067493ad`](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/7bee8418-38a2-e42b-2af1-d0d5067493ad?product=Agents&commit=41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b) |
| Version | 1.115.0-insider |
| Commit | [`41dd792b`](https://insiders.vscode.dev/github/microsoft/vscode/tree/41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b) |
| Last Seen | Tue Apr 14 2026 18:35:42 GMT+0000 (Coordinated Universal Time) |
| Platforms | Mac, Windows |
| Product | Agents |

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*

Comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

`BannerPart.show()` calls `clearNode(this.element)` but `this.element` is only assigned during `createContentArea()`, which runs when the workbench layout initializes the part. If `WorkspaceTrustManagementService` fires `onDidChangeTrust` before the BannerPart's DOM element is created (a startup timing race), `this.element` is `undefined` and `clearNode()` throws `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'firstChild')`. This is a pre-existing race condition that affects the **Agents** (sessions) product especially, likely due to its different startup sequence.

### Culprit Commit

No single culprit commit was identified. The bucket history shows this error spanning from version 1.113 through 1.117 with increasing hit counts (from 1 hit/version to 76 hits on commit `47add6df` in 1.116). This is a pre-existing race condition between BannerPart layout initialization and workspace trust event firing, not a regression from a specific commit. The growing hit count likely reflects increasing usage of the Agents product.

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant WS as WorkspaceTrustMgmt<br/>(workspaceTrust.ts)
    participant Handler as WorkspaceTrustUXHandler<br/>(workspace.contribution.ts)
    participant Banner as BannerPart<br/>(bannerPart.ts)
    participant DOM as clearNode<br/>(dom.ts)

    Note over Banner: createContentArea() NOT yet called<br/>⚠️ this.element = undefined
    WS->>WS: updateWorkspaceTrust()
    WS->>Handler: onDidChangeTrust(trusted=false)
    Handler->>Handler: updateWorkbenchIndicators(false)
    Handler->>Handler: getBannerItem(true) → item
    Handler->>Banner: show(bannerItem)
    Banner->>DOM: clearNode(this.element)
    Note over DOM: 💥 TypeError:<br/>Cannot read properties of<br/>undefined (reading 'firstChild')<br/>(this.element is undefined)
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/browser/parts/banner/bannerPart.ts` | **Crash site** — `show()` (line 188) calls `clearNode(this.element)` without checking if element exists |
| `src/vs/workbench/browser/part.ts` | **Root cause** — `element!: HTMLElement` (line 181) uses definite assignment assertion but element is actually `undefined` until `create()` is called |
| `src/vs/base/browser/dom.ts` | **Throw site** — `clearNode(node)` (line 219) accesses `node.firstChild` on `undefined` |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/workspace/browser/workspace.contribution.ts` | **Trigger** — `updateWorkbenchIndicators()` (line 403) calls `bannerService.show()` during trust change |
| `src/vs/workbench/services/workspaces/common/workspaceTrust.ts` | **Event source** — `updateWorkspaceTrust()` (line 348) fires `onDidChangeTrust` |

### Repro Steps

1. Use the **Agents** (sessions) product variant of VS Code Insiders
2. Open a workspace that is **not trusted** (or where trust state changes on startup)
3. The workspace trust service resolves trust state and fires `onDidChangeTrust` before the workbench layout has fully initialized the BannerPart DOM element
4. This is a timing-dependent race condition — it's more likely to occur when workspace trust resolution is fast (e.g., cached trust state) and UI layout is slower
5. The error is non-deterministic; it depends on startup timing

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/browser/parts/banner/bannerPart.ts` → `show()`, `close()`, `focus()`

**Approach**: Add a guard clause in `show()` to check if `this.element` is initialized before operating on it. If the element doesn't exist yet, the method should either:
1. **Defer the banner**: Store the pending banner item and render it when `createContentArea()` is called (preferred — ensures the banner is shown once layout is ready), or
2. **Early return**: Simply bail out with a no-op if `this.element` is undefined (simpler but may drop the banner)

Option 1 (deferred rendering) is recommended. The `createContentArea()` method should check for a pending item:
```
// In show(): if (!this.element) { this.pendingItem = item; return; }
// In createContentArea(): if (this.pendingItem) { this.show(this.pendingItem); }
````

The same guard should be applied to `close()` (line 99) and `focus()` (line 156), which also access `this.element` without null checks.

### Recommended Owner

`@lszomoru` — Area owner for Workspace Trust and BannerPart per working-areas.md

> Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24486158115/agentic_workflow) · ● 4.3M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

---

Title: [Error] Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'element')

Description:
## Error Message
````
Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'element')
````

## Stack Trace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;at DR.element ([./src/vs/base/browser/ui/list/listView.ts:845:27](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b/src/vs/base/browser/ui/list/listView.ts#L845))

| Commit | [`41dd792b`](https://insiders.vscode.dev/github/microsoft/vscode/tree/41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b) |
| Last Seen | Thu Apr 09 2026 20:14:47 GMT+0000 (Coordinated Universal Time) |
| Platforms | Windows, Mac, Linux |
| Product | Agents |

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*

Comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'element')` occurs in `listView.ts:845` when `TraitSpliceable.splice` (listWidget.ts:240) tries to look up an element at a stale/out-of-bounds trait index during a full list replace in the AI Customization list widget. The crash is triggered when `commitDisplayEntries()` replaces all list items via `splice(0, list.length, newEntries)` and a focus/selection/anchor trait holds an index that exceeds the current view items length. This affects the Agents product across all platforms.

### Culprit Commit

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Commit | [`ec038bfa`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/ec038bfa43a98172fcaf92bc8868ce8ae5fd3517) |
| Author | `@joshspicer` |
| PR | #307745 |
| Message | fix: customization provider API rendering bugs and sessions window isolation |
| Why | This PR added multiple new refresh triggers (`onDidChangeInstructions`, `availableHarnesses` in autorun) and changed the group layout for instructions. The increased refresh frequency creates more opportunities for trait indices to become stale relative to the view's items array during async load–splice cycles. The error first appeared in commit `6c8fcf18` (first build after this PR merged). |

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Trigger as refresh() trigger<br/>(autorun / event)
    participant Load as loadItems()
    participant Filter as filterItems()
    participant Commit as commitDisplayEntries()
    participant ListSplice as List.splice()
    participant Trait as TraitSpliceable
    participant View as ListView

    Trigger->>Load: refresh() → loadItems()
    Load->>Filter: filterItems()
    Filter->>Commit: groupMatchedItems() → commitDisplayEntries()
    Commit->>ListSplice: list.splice(0, list.length, displayEntries)
    ListSplice->>Trait: CombinedSpliceable iterates traits first
    Note over Trait: trait.get() returns stale index i<br/>where i >= view.items.length
    Trait->>View: view.element(i)
    Note over View: 💥 this.items[i] is undefined<br/>Cannot read 'element'
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/base/browser/ui/list/listView.ts:845` | Crash site — `this.items[index].element` where `items[index]` is undefined |
| `src/vs/base/browser/ui/list/listWidget.ts:240` | `TraitSpliceable.splice` — reads trait indices and accesses `view.element(i)` without bounds checking |
| `src/vs/base/browser/ui/list/listWidget.ts:1621` | `List.splice` — orchestrates via `CombinedSpliceable` (traits first, then view) |
| `src/vs/base/browser/ui/list/splice.ts:17` | `CombinedSpliceable` — iterates spliceables in order |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/aiCustomizationListWidget.ts` | `commitDisplayEntries()` — calls `list.splice(0, list.length, displayEntries)` to replace all items |

### Repro Steps

1. Open the Agents app (VS Code Insiders 1.115.x)
2. Navigate to the AI Customizations editor (Instructions section)
3. Focus or select an item in the list
4. Trigger rapid list refreshes — e.g., by switching workspaces, enabling/disabling extensions that register customization providers, or having file system changes fire `onDidChangeInstructions`
5. The list refresh cycle (`loadItems` → `filterItems` → `commitDisplayEntries`) replaces all items, and the `TraitSpliceable` reads a stale focus/selection index that exceeds the new items count

The error is non-deterministic and depends on timing between user interaction (which sets trait indices) and async refresh triggers.

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/base/browser/ui/list/listWidget.ts` → `TraitSpliceable.splice()`

**Approach**: Add a bounds check before accessing `this.view.element(i)` in the `TraitSpliceable.splice` method. When `i >= this.view.length`, skip that trait index (treat it as if the element no longer exists, so it won't be preserved across the splice). This is a defensive guard in the base list infrastructure that prevents the crash regardless of how trait indices become stale:

```
// In TraitSpliceable.splice, line 240:
const pastElementsWithTrait = this.trait.get()
    .filter(i => i >= 0 && i < this.view.length)  // ← guard
    .map(i => this.identityProvider!.getId(this.view.element(i)).toString());
````

Alternatively, the `aiCustomizationListWidget.ts` could clear the list's focus/selection before calling `commitDisplayEntries()` to prevent stale indices from being read during the splice.

### Recommended Owner

`@joshspicer` — author of the culprit commit (PR #307745) and primary contributor to the AI Customization list widget

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*

Comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

`renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded()` accesses `this.input.currentModeKind` (line 1006 of `chatWidget.ts`) without a null guard. The `input` getter returns `this.inputPartDisposable.value!` — a non-null assertion that fails at runtime when the `ChatInputPart` hasn't been created yet (widget not rendered) or has been disposed. This is triggered through the async `showModel()` → `updateWidgetLockState()` → `lockToCodingAgent()`/`unlockFromCodingAgent()` call chain, where the widget lifecycle can change during awaited session loading. 205 hits across 135 users spanning versions 1.113–1.117 indicate a persistent async lifecycle race.

### Culprit Commit

No specific culprit commit was identified. The issue body has no commit range analysis, and the error telemetry shows hits across 5+ insider versions since v1.113 (2026-03-31), indicating a pre-existing race condition in the widget lifecycle rather than a single regression commit. The `lockToCodingAgent` / `unlockFromCodingAgent` methods have always called `renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded()` without guarding for an uninitialized input part.

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant VP as ChatViewPane
    participant SM as showModel()
    participant CS as ChatService
    participant CW as ChatWidget
    participant RW as renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded()

    VP->>SM: showModel(token, modelRef)
    SM->>CS: await acquireOrLoadSession()
    Note over CS: ⚠️ Async gap: widget<br/>may be disposed or<br/>not yet rendered
    CS-->>SM: session reference
    SM->>VP: updateWidgetLockState(sessionType)
    VP->>CW: lockToCodingAgent(name, displayName, agentId)
    CW->>RW: renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded()
    RW->>CW: this.input.currentModeKind
    Note over CW: 💥 this.input is undefined<br/>(inputPartDisposable.value is null)
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatWidget.ts` | Crash site (line 1006) and root cause — `renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded()` accesses `this.input` without null guard; `lockToCodingAgent()` (line 2161) and `unlockFromCodingAgent()` (line 2181) call it unsafely |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widgetHosts/viewPane/chatViewPane.ts` | Trigger — `showModel()` (line 745) awaits async session loading then calls `updateWidgetLockState()` which invokes lock/unlock on the widget |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product) with a chat session type that resolves to a coding agent (e.g., a contributed session)
2. Rapidly open/close the chat view pane, or trigger a session commit event while the pane is being disposed
3. The async `showModel()` starts loading a session, the widget gets disposed during the await, and `lockToCodingAgent` runs on the stale widget
4. The error is non-deterministic — it depends on the timing of widget disposal vs. async session resolution

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatWidget.ts` → `renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded()`

**Approach**: Add an early return guard at the top of `renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded()` to check whether the input part has been initialized:

```typescript
if (!this.inputPartDisposable.value) {
    return;
}
```

This is consistent with the existing defensive pattern already used elsewhere in the widget — for example, `unlockFromCodingAgent()` uses `this.inputEditor?.updateOptions(...)` (line 2187) with optional chaining. The same safety should apply to `renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded()`.

Additionally, `lockToCodingAgent()` and `unlockFromCodingAgent()` could check `this._isDisposed` before proceeding, since these methods may be called from an async context after the widget has been disposed.

### Recommended Owner

`@roblourens` — already assigned; active maintainer of the chat widget and sessions infrastructure (most recent commits to both affected files)

> Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24485184189/agentic_workflow) · ● 9.7M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

<!-- generated by issue reporter -->

Comments:
We have a temporary workaround on our end by updating the launch.json file to use `"console": "integratedTerminal"`

Here is what Opus thinks caused it, unsure if the recent VSCode update did actually change how those processes are ran on windows or if it was spitting out garbage: 

> Why the default stopped working: By default (without the console setting), VS Code launches dlv as an invisible background child process and communicates with it over a pipe/socket. Recent VS Code updates changed how these internal child processes are spawned on Windows — specifically how stdio handles and process groups are managed. This broke the timing of the DAP (Debug Adapter Protocol) handshake: VS Code starts dlv, then tries to connect to its DAP port, but the internal process spawning is now slightly slower, so the connection attempt happens before dlv is ready to accept it, and it times out.

For more context, this also affected 3 of my coworkers **DIRECTLY** after they updated to todays release
- Related Issue: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/310218
The error message you're getting is coming from the Go extension, those strings aren't in vscode.

We also have not made any changes in this area afaik.
This issue is caused by an extension, please file it with the repository (or contact) the extension has linked in its overview in VS Code or the [marketplace](https://aka.ms/vscodemarketplace) for VS Code. See also our [issue reporting guidelines](https://aka.ms/vscodeissuereporting). If you don't know which extension is causing the problem, you can run `Help: Start extension bisect` from the command palette (F1) to help identify the problem extension.

Happy Coding!

Comments:
## Regression Details

- **Product**: Agents
- **Regression type**: New error
- **Last release without this error**: [1.116.0-insider (ae130017)](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/60483725-f071-1372-ccc2-51712e734964?product=Agents&commit=ae130017f8afe532557dbb8539a6ef3bdaec6389)
- **First release with this error**: [1.117.0-insider (98b4d3e2)](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/60483725-f071-1372-ccc2-51712e734964?product=Agents&commit=98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62)
- **Commit range**: [ae130017...98b4d3e2](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/ae130017f8afe532557dbb8539a6ef3bdaec6389...98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62)

### Deminified Stack

```
at (anonymous) (copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts:1168:0) → createNewSession
at (anonymous) (sessionsManagementService.ts:261:27) → createNewSession
at (anonymous) (newChatViewPane.ts:250:0) → _createNewSession
at (anonymous) (newChatViewPane.ts:710:0) → _onWorkspaceSelected
at (anonymous) (newChatViewPane.ts:153:0)
at (anonymous) (event.ts:1355:12)
at (anonymous) (event.ts:1386:8)
at (anonymous) (sessionTypePicker.ts:126:0) → onSelect
at (anonymous) (actionList.ts:1161:0) → onListSelection
```

### Analysis

The error is thrown in `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts:createNewSession` (line 1168) when a user selects a GitHub repository workspace but the session type is not a Copilot Cloud session. The flow is: user selects a workspace via `sessionTypePicker.ts` → `newChatViewPane._onWorkspaceSelected` → `_createNewSession` → `sessionsManagementService.createNewSession` → `copilotChatSessionsProvider.createNewSession` which throws the validation error.

This is a new error in 1.117.0-insider (18 users, 36 hits) that did not exist in any prior insider build. The validation error is not being caught/handled and propagates as an unhandled error.

> Generated by [errors-agents-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24461922297/agentic_workflow) · ● 5.7M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-agents-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24461922297, workflow_id: errors-agents-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24461922297 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

When a user has a GitHub repository workspace selected and attempts to switch session types via the session type picker, the picker incorrectly determines which session types are supported. It passes `session.resource` (the session's own URI, e.g. `agentsessions-cloud://...`) to `getSessionTypes()` instead of the workspace's repository URI. Since the session resource URI never has the `GITHUB_REMOTE_FILE_SCHEME`, the method always returns `[CopilotCLISessionType]` as "supported" — the opposite of what's correct for GitHub repos. This allows the user to select CLI, which throws the unhandled error. 36 hits, 18 users affected.

### Culprit Commit

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Commit | [`852f0ef7`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/852f0ef7ce04426b5750737c00f57ac62dda8d71) |
| Author | `@sandy081` |
| PR | #309563 |
| Message | fix #309128 and add docs to the sessions provider |
| Why | This PR refactored `getSessionTypes()` from accepting a `sessionId` (and looking up the session instance to check its type) to accepting a `workspaceUri` (and checking the URI scheme). However, the call site in `SessionTypePicker.refresh()` was changed to pass `session.resource` (the session URI) instead of the workspace repository URI. Since the session URI scheme is never `GITHUB_REMOTE_FILE_SCHEME`, the supported types are always computed incorrectly for GitHub workspaces. |

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User
    participant Picker as SessionTypePicker
    participant NewChat as NewChatWidget
    participant MgmtSvc as SessionsManagementService
    participant Provider as CopilotChatSessionsProvider

    User->>Picker: Selects session type (e.g. CLI)
    Note over Picker: ⚠️ Root cause:<br/>refresh() called getSessionTypes(session.resource)<br/>instead of getSessionTypes(workspaceUri).<br/>CLI shown as enabled for GitHub repos.
    Picker->>NewChat: onDidSelectSessionType(CopilotCLISessionType.id)
    NewChat->>NewChat: _onWorkspaceSelected(githubWorkspace, 'copilot-cli')
    NewChat->>MgmtSvc: createNewSession(providerId, githubRepoUri, 'copilot-cli')
    MgmtSvc->>Provider: createNewSession(githubRepoUri, 'copilot-cli')
    Note over Provider: 💥 Error thrown:<br/>"Only Copilot Cloud sessions can<br/>be created for GitHub repositories"
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/sessionTypePicker.ts` | **Root cause** — `refresh()` passes `session.resource` instead of workspace URI to `getSessionTypes()` |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` | **Crash site** — `createNewSession()` throws when session type mismatches workspace |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/newChatViewPane.ts` | **Data flow** — `_onWorkspaceSelected()` passes unchecked session type to management service |
| `src/vs/sessions/services/sessions/browser/sessionsManagementService.ts` | **Data flow** — `createNewSession()` forwards session type to provider |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product, version 1.117.0)
2. Open or connect to a **GitHub repository** workspace (a repo accessed via the `GITHUB_REMOTE_FILE_SCHEME`, e.g. `vscode.dev` on a GitHub repo)
3. In the new chat view, the session type picker will incorrectly show "CLI" as the enabled/selected type
4. Click the session type picker and select "CLI" (or it may already be defaulted to CLI)
5. The unhandled error "Only Copilot Cloud sessions can be created for GitHub repositories" is thrown

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/sessionTypePicker.ts` → `refresh()` function (line ~41)

**Approach**: Change `provider?.getSessionTypes(session.resource)` to pass the workspace's repository URI instead. The session's workspace is available via `session.workspace` (an `IObservable<ISessionWorkspace | undefined>`). Read the workspace, get the first repository's URI, and pass that to `getSessionTypes()`. For example:

```
const workspace = session.workspace.get();
const repoUri = workspace?.repositories[0]?.uri ?? session.resource;
this._supportedSessionTypes = provider?.getSessionTypes(repoUri) ?? [];
````

This ensures `getSessionTypes()` receives a URI with the correct scheme (`GITHUB_REMOTE_FILE_SCHEME` for GitHub repos), so it correctly returns `[CopilotCloudSessionType]` and disables CLI in the picker.

### Recommended Owner

`@sandy081` — Author of the culprit commit (PR #309563) and primary contributor to the sessions management system. MEMBER of the VS Code team.

> Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24466296252/agentic_workflow) · ● 10M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

Title: [security] v1.116 lowers security by breaking GitHub Copilot AI per-workspace opt-in

Description:
Users may have some workspaces used with GitHub Copilot, but also have other sensitive workspaces for which under no circumstances must any local information be sent to an LLM. **VSCode v.116 has broken the ability to opt-in to GitHub Copilot AI, and instead forced sensitive workspaces to _opt-out_ of GitHub Copilot AI.** This is not workable and against security best practices. **Now VS Code exposes a system to inadvertently leaking information in arbitrary directories.**

**Expected:** Before v1.116 I could disable GitHub Copilot globally, and only enable it on a per-workspace basis. I would do this by disabling the plugin globally, and then selecting "enable (workspace)" only in each workspace for which I wanted to use GitHub Copilot. This is referred to as "opt-in". This is security best practices: I should not be required to hunt down all my sensitive workspaces and opt them out. Instead, I should be allowed to only opt-in workspaces that I want to use with AI.

**Actual:** In v1.116 [GitHub Copilot is built-in](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_116#_github-copilot-is-now-builtin). There is now only a single `chat.disableAIFeatures` setting which allows the user to turn off AI either globally, or on a per-workspace basis. The plugin no longer has an option to enable it on a per-workspace basis, because the plugin is now built in. Although it appears I can _disable_ AI, for one particular workspace, this is an "opt-out" paradigm which is extremely insecure.

### Discussion

Imagine if a user has 10 sensitive directories (e.g. bank statements, love letters, the formulas for secret sauces). Some of them may not even have VS Code workspace yet, as VS Code automatically creates workspaces when a directory is opened. It is unacceptable, intrusive, and unreasonable that the user must go into each of these 10 sensitive directories, create VS Code workspaces for them, and then opt them out. What if the user forgets a directory? Plus the user has to open VS Code in that directory opt them out!

### Implications: Abandoning VS Code

The lack of an opt-in mechanism is simply too great of a security risk. I cannot expose all my system to the possibility of inadvertently sending information to LLMS from sensitive directories. If VS Code is removing its AI opt-in feature, I will be removing VS Code as my default editor.

**VS Code is not secure unless it brings back an opt-in feature for AI.** I will not expose my entire system to an information-leaking security risk.

Comments:
To add insult to injury, if you _disable_ AI for a particular workspace using `"chat.disableAIFeatures": true`, if the workspace is restricted (the secure mode) then every time you try to open that workspace, VS Code **nags you  trust the folder** (i.e. to make the workspace _less_ secure) in order to disable AI!

> ### Do you trust the authors of the files in this folder?
> A feature you are trying to use may be a security risk if you do not trust the source of the files or folders you currently have open.
>
> If you don't trust the authors of these files, we do not recommend continuing as the files may be malicious. …

<img width="981" height="548" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a1524d00-9c76-4340-a525-c1a378f3be81" />

Apparently it sees a feature related to AI and warns of a security risk, _even though the AI "feature" is the option to turn off AI_!

**This is a secondary issue.** However it illustrates two things:

* The default use of AI is a security issue, as the warning basically admits.
* The disconnect that VS Code thinks you must _lower_ security of a workspace in order to _disable AI_ to make it more secure shows how forcing an opt-out paradigm is against security best practices and was ill-thought-out to begin with.

I reiterate: **VS Code must have an AI opt-in mechanism**, not an opt-out mechanism.
@garretwilson the intent was not to break this scenario but preserve the ability to allow to globally disable the extension and then enable it on a per-workspace.

However it turns out that the action appears disabled, which is clearly a bug:

<img width="677" height="326" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0671d259-0332-49c2-9a22-5a61098386b7" />

You can still configure `chat.disableAIFeatures` as a workspace setting and that will work, but its not the ideal solution.

Thanks for the report, we will fix this asap.
Verification Steps:

- Open Extensions view in VS Code on Folder 1
- Type `@builtin chat` 
- Right click on Copilot Chat extension and run **Disable AI Features** action
- Make sure AI features are disabled
- Close and Open VS Code on Folder 2
- Make sure AI features are disabled
- Go to Extensions View and type `@builtin chat` 
- Right click on Copilot Chat extension and run **Enable AI Features (Workspace)** action
- Make sure AI features are enabled in this Folder 2
- Open VS Code on Folder 1 and make sure AI features are still disabled in Folder 1 and also on any other folders

Title: Allow sub-agents to be invoked with a different model

Description:
Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.43.0
VS Code Version: Version: 1.115.0 (Universal)
OS Version: macOS 26.4.1
Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent mode launching a subagent
Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude Sonnet 4.6 (probably is affected for all MS CoPilot models)
Logs: na

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Ask to do something with a sub agent. Tell the orchestrator to use a specific model from Microstoft CoPilot (no third party involved here)
2. Verify that the sub agent did not run with that model, it has only run with the same model launching the sub agent



___________ AI writeup and suggestion ____________
AI WriteUp for better understanding:

## Summary

When the agent invokes a sub-agent via `runSubagent`, there is no way to specify which model that sub-agent should use. The sub-agent always inherits the model selected in the VS Code UI by the user. This prevents the agent from delegating tasks to a more suitable model (e.g. a cheaper/faster model for simple lookups, or a more capable model for complex reasoning steps).

## Current Behaviour

Sub-agents are spawned using the same model that was selected by the user for the top-level conversation. There is no `model` parameter on the `runSubagent` tool. Prompting the agent to "use Claude Opus for this sub-agent" has no effect — the model is fixed at the session level.

## Expected Behaviour

The `runSubagent` tool should accept an optional `model` parameter:

```json
{
  "prompt": "Analyse this codebase for security vulnerabilities...",
  "agentName": "Explore",
  "model": "claude-opus-4-5"
}
```

When omitted, the sub-agent inherits the session model (preserving current behaviour). When specified, the sub-agent runs with the requested model.

## Constraints / Scope

- This should **only be supported for official GitHub Copilot models** (i.e. models available in the Copilot model picker). Third-party or custom model endpoints should not be injectable through this path.
- The available model identifiers should be validated against the user's current Copilot subscription/model access, and the call should fail gracefully (with a clear error) if the requested model is not available.
- This is an opt-in capability — existing agent definitions that don't specify a model are unaffected.

## Use Cases

- Use a fast/cheap model (e.g. GPT-4o mini) for high-volume read-only exploration sub-agents.
- Use a high-capability model (e.g. Claude Opus) for complex reasoning or security analysis sub-agents, independent of what the user has selected for the main conversation.
- Agent definitions (`.agent.md`) could declare a preferred model for their specific task domain.

## Impact

Without this capability, the agent cannot optimise cost/quality trade-offs across sub-tasks, and power users cannot build multi-model agentic workflows within VS Code Copilot.

Comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.116.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

Happy Coding!
A PR for this was just merged :)
Great to know ! Looking forward testing it. Thanks vs-code team (:

Title: `run_in_terminal` tool response should include the exit code

Description:
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.43.0
- VS Code Version: Version: 1.115.0 (Universal)
- OS Version: macOS 26.4.1
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent mode
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): All models
- Logs: na

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Let CoPilot Chat Execute a Terminal command that does not have stderr or stdout - for example just a "touch test.file"
2. Review the output window. There will be shown in the chat window, that no output occured. Even though those basic commands and anything else should always output an exit code which the AI interprets also.

####
___________ AI writeup and suggestion ____________
AI WriteUp for better understanding:

## Summary

When the agent uses the `run_in_terminal` tool, the tool response only contains stdout/stderr. The exit code of the executed command is not included. This means the agent cannot detect silent failures within the same turn without resorting to the workaround of appending `; echo $?` to every command.

## Current Behaviour

A command like `chmod 444 /tmp/test.txt` that succeeds silently returns an empty tool response. A failing command that produces no stderr (exit code ≠ 0, no output) is indistinguishable from a successful one within the same reasoning turn.

The exit code *is* surfaced in the next turn's injected context block (`Exit Code: <n>`), but only for the *last* command and only after the current turn has already completed — too late for the agent to react within the same step.

## Expected Behaviour

The `run_in_terminal` tool response should include the exit code alongside stdout/stderr, for example:

```json
{
  "stdout": "",
  "stderr": "",
  "exitCode": 0
}
```

This allows the agent to branch on failure immediately, retry the command, or surface a clear error to the user — all within the same turn.

## Workaround

Appending `; echo "Exit: $?"` to every command provides the exit code in stdout, but is fragile:
- It pollutes stdout and can be confused with actual command output.
- It requires every command invocation to be authored defensively.
- It doesn't work cleanly for multi-command pipelines.

## Impact

Without inline exit codes, the agent may silently proceed after a failed command, leading to confusing downstream errors that are difficult to trace back to the root cause. Reliable exit code access is a basic requirement for robust agent command execution.
 


Comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.116.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

Happy Coding!
We do this already

<img width="1125" height="685" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1db9fe9a-6b06-4239-ab3b-85dab472cf5f" />

Thanks for clarification ! Gonna verify again on my side to see if it is resolved for what I was looking at.

Title: Extension host proxy support does not cover http2.connect() paths; patched tls.connect still allows direct target DNS/connect instead of proxy tunnel

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->

# VS Code Extension Host Proxy Patching Does Not Cover Raw HTTP/2 (`http2.connect()`) Traffic

## Summary

VS Code's extension host proxy patching appears not to transparently cover Node.js `http2.connect()` traffic. On proxy-required networks, HTTP/1.1, `fetch`, and `undici` paths work through the configured proxy, but raw HTTP/2 clients can still fall back to direct DNS and direct target socket connection behavior. This causes failures such as `getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND <host>` when external name resolution is only possible through the proxy.

**Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?:** N/A

## System Information

- VS Code Version: 1.117.0-insider (user setup), Commit `98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62`
- OS Version:
  - Host: Windows 11 Enterprise 10.0.26100
  - Remote: WSL2 Ubuntu 25.04 (`Linux 6.6.114.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2`)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure VS Code / extension host to use an HTTP proxy on a network where direct DNS resolution for external hosts fails and only the proxy can reach external destinations.
2. Run an extension in the Node extension host that uses Node's `http2.connect()` directly (for example, a gRPC or HTTP/2 client that does not use `fetch`, `undici`, `http.request`, or `https.request`).
3. Attempt to connect to an external HTTPS endpoint over HTTP/2.
4. Observe that the connection path does not resolve and tunnel through the configured proxy. Instead, it falls back to direct target DNS/socket behavior and fails with errors such as `getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND <host>` on proxy-mandatory networks.

## Expected behavior

All outbound extension host traffic should honor the configured proxy consistently, including HTTP/2 clients using `http2.connect()`. If proxy support is enabled, the extension host should establish a proxy tunnel before TLS/HTTP2 session setup, rather than attempting direct DNS/connect to the target host.

## Actual behavior

`http.request`, `https.request`, `fetch`, and `undici`-based traffic are proxy-aware, but `http2.connect()`-based traffic is not transparently proxied. In practice, `tls.connect` is patched for certificate handling, but if it is invoked without a preconnected socket, the path still allows direct target DNS/socket connection behavior. On networks where external DNS only works through the proxy, this results in persistent `ENOTFOUND` / direct-connect failures.

## Why I believe this belongs in VS Code

VS Code explicitly patches extension host networking through `src/vs/workbench/api/node/proxyResolver.ts`, including `http`, `https`, `net`, `tls`, and `undici` via `@vscode/proxy-agent`.

## Relevant code

- <https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62/src/vs/workbench/api/node/proxyResolver.ts#L366-L370>

- <https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62/src/vs/workbench/api/node/proxyResolver.ts#L388-L417>

This suggests the extension host owns the proxy-patching model, but there is a gap for raw HTTP/2 connection establishment.

## Likely root cause

There appears to be no transparent proxy-aware handling for `http2.connect()` itself. If `http2.connect()` eventually reaches patched `tls.connect()` without a pre-established proxy tunnel socket, the "no socket" branch still allows creation of a new direct socket connection to the target host instead of first resolving and connecting via proxy.

## Impact

Any extension relying on raw Node HTTP/2 / gRPC transports may fail in enterprise environments where:

- proxy usage is mandatory
- direct outbound 443 is blocked
- external DNS resolution is unavailable except through the proxy

## Possible fixes

1. Make the `tls.connect` patch proxy-aware even when called without a preconnected socket, so it can resolve the proxy and establish a CONNECT tunnel first.
2. Alternatively, provide/document a supported proxy-aware path for extension host `http2.connect()` usage.
3. Consider adding test coverage for extension-host HTTP/2 connectivity behind a mandatory proxy.

## Notes

- Confirmed in current VS Code Insiders WSL2 remote session (`.vscode-server-insiders`, commit `98b4d3e24443f6566e694021a376e1760c4a7f62`) with `--use-host-proxy` on extension host.
- Current WSL2 extension host environment includes `HTTP_PROXY` / `HTTPS_PROXY` / `NO_PROXY`, but HTTP/2 paths can still fail via direct target DNS/connect behavior.
- Observed in a VS Code-derived environment and consistent with behavior in the shared extension-host proxy patching model.


Comments:
# HTTP/2 Proxy Support

## Proposed Fix

This issue can be resolved with coordinated changes across two repositories:

1. **`@vscode/proxy-agent`** - add a new `createHttp2Patch` function (diff below)
2. **`microsoft/vscode`** - wire `createHttp2Patch` into the extension host's module loading system (diff below)

### Approach

The fix follows the same pattern already established by `createHttpPatch`, `createNetPatch`, and `createTlsPatch`. A new `createHttp2Patch(params, originals, resolveProxyURL)` function wraps `http2.connect()` to:

- Resolve the proxy URL for the target authority
- Establish an `HTTP CONNECT` tunnel through the proxy before the HTTP/2 session begins
- Layer TLS over the tunnel for `https://` targets (since providing `createConnection` bypasses http2's internal `tls.connect()`)
- Handle proxy authentication (proactive lookup, `HTTP 407` challenge-response retry via `lookupProxyAuthorization`, and URL-embedded basic-auth fallback)
- Load additional CA certificates via the patched `tls.connect` (`options.socket` branch in `patchTlsConnect`)
- Fall back to direct connection when proxy support is `"off"` or no proxy is resolved (including localhost targets)
- Warn and fall back to direct for SOCKS proxies (not supported for HTTP/2 tunneling)

On the `microsoft/vscode` side, the extension host's module loader is updated to intercept `require('http2')` and return the patched module - exactly as it already does for `http`, `https`, `net`, and `tls`.

<details><summary>Concept diagram</summary>
<p>

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Extension/Client calls http2.connect] --> B[createHttp2Patch wrapper]
    B --> C{Proxy support active?}

    C -- No --> D[Direct/original connection path]
    C -- Yes --> E[Resolve proxy URL]
    E --> F{Proxy type}
    F -- SOCKS --> G[Warn and fall back to direct]
    F -- HTTP/HTTPS proxy --> H[Open HTTP CONNECT tunnel]

    H --> I[Authenticate to proxy<br/>proactive auth + 407 retry + URL creds fallback]
    I --> J{Target scheme}
    J -- https --> K[Layer TLS over tunnel]
    J -- http --> L[Use raw socket]

    K --> M[Use patched tls.connect<br/>and cert handling]
    L --> N[Start HTTP/2 session]
    M --> N

    B --> O[VS Code module loader intercepts require http2]
    O --> P[Extensions receive patched http2 module]
```

</p>
</details> 

### Testing

Tested inside the VS Code devcontainer on a proxy-mandatory network (`http://192.168.127.254:3128`) where direct DNS resolution for external hosts fails:

| Test                                                                     | Result |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------ |
| `createHttp2Patch` exports and returns `{ connect }`                     | PASS   |
| Patched `connect` differs from original `http2.connect`                  | PASS   |
| Localhost connections bypass the proxy                                   | PASS   |
| `http2.connect('https://www.google.com:443')` tunnels through proxy      | PASS   |
| Direct fallback when `resolveProxyURL` returns `undefined`               | PASS   |
| Proxy support `"off"` uses original `connect`                            | PASS   |
| Extension host `require('http2')` returns patched module (live Code OSS) | PASS   |
| Live HTTP/2 session from extension host through proxy (live Code OSS)    | PASS   |
