```diff
+
+function patchHttp2Connect(
+ params: ProxyAgentParams,
+ originalConnect: typeof http2.connect,
+ resolveProxyURL: (url: string) => Promise<string | undefined>
+): typeof http2.connect {
+ function connect(
+  authority: string | URL,
+  options?: http2.SecureClientSessionOptions | ((session: http2.ClientHttp2Session, socket: net.Socket | tls.TLSSocket) => void),
+  listener?: (session: http2.ClientHttp2Session, socket: net.Socket | tls.TLSSocket) => void
+ ): http2.ClientHttp2Session {
+  if (typeof options === 'function') {
+   listener = options;
+   options = undefined;
+  }
+
+  if (params.getLogLevel() === LogLevel.Trace) {
+   params.log.trace('ProxyResolver#http2.connect', toLogString([authority, options]));
+  }
+
+  const proxySupport = params.getProxySupport();
+  const hasCallerCreateConnection = options?.createConnection !== undefined;
+  const doResolveProxy = proxySupport === 'override' || proxySupport === 'fallback' || (proxySupport === 'on' && !hasCallerCreateConnection);
+
+  if (!doResolveProxy) {
+   return originalConnect(authority, options || {}, listener);
+  }
+
+  // http2.connect() accepts authority as "host:port" or a full URL.
+  // Normalise to a URL string for resolveProxyURL (which expects one).
+  const authorityStr = typeof authority === 'string' ? authority : authority.toString();
+  const targetUrl = authorityStr.includes('://') ? authorityStr : `https://${authorityStr}`;
+  let parsed: URL;
+  try {
+   parsed = new URL(targetUrl);
+  } catch {
+   params.log.error('ProxyResolver#http2.connect', `Invalid authority: ${authorityStr}`);
+   return originalConnect(authority, options || {}, listener);
+  }
+
+  const targetHost = parsed.hostname;
+  if (!targetHost) {
+   params.log.error('ProxyResolver#http2.connect', `Missing hostname in authority: ${authorityStr}`);
+   return originalConnect(authority, options || {}, listener);
+  }
+  const targetPort = parsed.port
+   ? parseInt(parsed.port, 10)
+   : parsed.protocol === 'https:'
+    ? defaultHttpsPort
+    : defaultHttpPort;
+
+  const patchedOptions: http2.SecureClientSessionOptions = {
+   ...options,
+   // createConnection must return synchronously, but tunnel
+   // establishment is async.  We return a Duplex "bridge" that
+   // buffers outbound writes until the real socket is ready and
+   // forwards inbound data via push().
+   //
+   // http2 internally wraps sockets without a native _handle in
+   // JSStreamSocket, which reads/writes through the JS stream
+   // interface.  A bare net.Socket can't be used here because its
+   // read/write sides are tied to a network fd - without one,
+   // JSStreamSocket's writes vanish and no 'data' events ever fire.
+   // A custom Duplex avoids this by explicitly bridging http2's
+   // JSStreamSocket to the real tunnel socket.
+   createConnection: (_authority: URL, _options: http2.SessionOptions): stream.Duplex => {
+    return createHttp2Bridge(params, targetHost, targetPort, targetUrl, resolveProxyURL, options as http2.SecureClientSessionOptions | undefined);
+   },
+  };
+
+  return originalConnect(authority, patchedOptions, listener);
+ }
+
+ return connect as typeof http2.connect;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Create a Duplex bridge for http2's createConnection.
+ *
+ * http2 wraps sockets without a native _handle in JSStreamSocket, which
+ * reads via 'data' events and writes via stream.write().  This bridge
+ * connects JSStreamSocket's JS-level I/O to the real tunnel (or direct)
+ * socket, buffering outbound writes until the async connection is ready.
+ */
+function createHttp2Bridge(
+ params: ProxyAgentParams,
+ targetHost: string,
+ targetPort: number,
+ targetUrl: string,
+ resolveProxyURL: (url: string) => Promise<string | undefined>,
+ sessionOptions?: http2.SecureClientSessionOptions
+): stream.Duplex {
+ let remoteSock: net.Socket | null = null;
+ const pendingWrites: Array<{
+  chunk: Buffer;
+  encoding: BufferEncoding;
+  callback: (err?: Error | null) => void;
+ }> = [];
+
+ const bridge = new stream.Duplex({
+  read() {
+   // Data is pushed externally via bridge.push() when remoteSock
+   // delivers data.  Resuming the source here ensures backpressure
+   // is relieved once http2 is ready for more.
+   remoteSock?.resume();
+  },
+  write(chunk, encoding, callback) {
+   if (remoteSock) {
+    remoteSock.write(chunk, encoding, callback);
+   } else {
+    pendingWrites.push({ chunk, encoding, callback });
+   }
+  },
+  final(callback) {
+   if (remoteSock) {
+    remoteSock.end(callback);
+   } else {
+    callback();
+   }
+  },
+  destroy(err, callback) {
+   for (const pending of pendingWrites) {
+    pending.callback(err || new Error('Stream destroyed'));
+   }
+   pendingWrites.length = 0;
+   remoteSock?.destroy(err || undefined);
+   callback(err);
+  },
+ });
+
+ resolveHttp2Socket(params, targetHost, targetPort, targetUrl, resolveProxyURL, sessionOptions)
+  .then(sock => {
+   // If the bridge is destroyed before the socket is resolved, destroy the socket to avoid a leak
+   if (bridge.destroyed) {
+    sock.destroy();
+    return;
+   }
+   remoteSock = sock;
+
+   for (const pending of pendingWrites) {
+    sock.write(pending.chunk, pending.encoding, pending.callback);
+   }
+   pendingWrites.length = 0;
+
+   sock.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => {
+    if (!bridge.push(chunk)) {
+     sock.pause();
+    }
+   });
+   sock.once('end', () => bridge.push(null));
+   sock.once('error', (err) => bridge.destroy(err));
+   sock.once('close', () => {
+    if (!bridge.destroyed) { bridge.destroy(); }
+   });
+   bridge.once('close', () => sock.destroy());
+  })
+  .catch(err => {
+   params.log.error('ProxyResolver#http2.connect',
+    `Failed to establish connection to ${targetHost}:${targetPort}: ${toErrorMessage(err)}`);
+   bridge.destroy(err instanceof Error ? err : new Error(String(err)));
+  });
+
+ return bridge;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Resolve the proxy and return a connected socket ready for http2.
+ *
+ * When createConnection is provided, http2 skips its internal tls.connect()
+ * entirely - it assumes the returned socket is already transport-ready.
+ * For https:// targets we must therefore layer TLS ourselves over the raw
+ * tunnel (or direct) socket.  The patched tls.connect (createTlsPatch)
+ * handles additional CA certificate injection via the options.socket branch,
+ * so we don't need to call getOrLoadAdditionalCertificates explicitly here.
+ */
+async function resolveHttp2Socket(
+ params: ProxyAgentParams,
+ targetHost: string,
+ targetPort: number,
+ targetUrl: string,
+ resolveProxyURL: (url: string) => Promise<string | undefined>,
+ sessionOptions?: http2.SecureClientSessionOptions
+): Promise<net.Socket | tls.TLSSocket> {
+
+ const rawSock = await resolveHttp2RawSocket(
+  params, targetHost, targetPort, targetUrl, resolveProxyURL
+ );
+
+ const isSecure = targetUrl.startsWith('https:');
+ if (!isSecure) {
+  return rawSock;
+ }
+
+ // Layer TLS over the raw socket so http2 speaks h2 over TLS.
+ // Because tls is the patched version (createTlsPatch), the
+ // options.socket branch injects additional CA certificates
+ // into the secureContext automatically.
+ return new Promise<tls.TLSSocket>((resolve, reject) => {
+  const tlsSock = tls.connect({
+   ...sessionOptions,
+   socket: rawSock,
+   servername: sessionOptions?.servername ?? targetHost,
+   ALPNProtocols: ['h2'],
+  });
+  tlsSock.once('secureConnect', () => {
+   tlsSock.removeListener('error', reject);
+   resolve(tlsSock);
+  });
+  tlsSock.once('error', (err) => {
+   tlsSock.destroy();
+   reject(err);
+  });
+ });
+}
+
+/**
+ * Return the raw (non-TLS) socket - either a direct TCP connection or
+ * an HTTP CONNECT tunnel through the proxy.
+ */
+async function resolveHttp2RawSocket(
+ params: ProxyAgentParams,
+ targetHost: string,
+ targetPort: number,
+ targetUrl: string,
+ resolveProxyURL: (url: string) => Promise<string | undefined>
+): Promise<net.Socket> {
+
+ const proxyURL = await resolveProxyURL(targetUrl);
+
+ if (!proxyURL) {
+  params.log.trace('ProxyResolver#http2.connect', `direct connection to ${targetHost}:${targetPort}`);
+  return connectDirectly(targetHost, targetPort);
+ }
+
+ params.log.trace('ProxyResolver#http2.connect', `tunnel to ${targetHost}:${targetPort} via ${proxyURL}`);
+
+ let proxyParsed: URL;
+ try {
+  proxyParsed = new URL(proxyURL);
+ } catch {
+  throw new Error(`ProxyResolver#http2.connect: invalid proxy URL: ${proxyURL}`);
+ }
+
+ // SOCKS proxies require protocol-level negotiation that the HTTP CONNECT
+ // tunnel code below doesn't implement.  Fall back to a direct connection
+ // rather than failing hard - the proxy resolver already handles SOCKS for
+ // HTTP/1.x via the dedicated SocksProxyAgent.
+ if (proxyParsed.protocol.startsWith('socks')) {
+  params.log.warn('ProxyResolver#http2.connect',
+   `SOCKS proxy not supported for HTTP/2 tunnel, falling back to direct connection to ${targetHost}:${targetPort}`);
+  return connectDirectly(targetHost, targetPort);
+ }
+
+ const caCertificates = await getOrLoadAdditionalCertificates(params);
+
+ const proxyHost = proxyParsed.hostname;
+ if (!proxyHost) {
+  throw new Error(`ProxyResolver#http2.connect: missing hostname in proxy URL: ${proxyURL}`);
+ }
+ const isHttpsProxy = proxyParsed.protocol === 'https:';
+ const proxyPort = proxyParsed.port ? parseInt(proxyParsed.port, 10) : (isHttpsProxy ? defaultHttpsPort : defaultHttpPort);
+ // Extract basic-auth credentials embedded in the proxy URL (e.g.
+ // http://user:pass@proxy:8080) as a fallback when no auth provider
+ // is configured or the provider returns nothing.
+ const proxyBasicAuth = proxyParsed.username
+  ? Buffer.from(`${decodeURIComponent(proxyParsed.username)}:${decodeURIComponent(proxyParsed.password)}`).toString('base64')
+  : undefined;
+
+ return establishHttp2ConnectTunnel(
+  params, proxyHost, proxyPort, isHttpsProxy, caCertificates,
+  proxyURL, proxyBasicAuth, targetHost, targetPort
+ );
+}
+
+function connectDirectly(host: string, port: number): Promise<net.Socket> {
+ return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
+  const sock = net.createConnection({ host, port });
+  sock.once('connect', () => {
+   sock.removeListener('error', reject);
+   resolve(sock);
+  });
+  sock.once('error', reject);
+ });
+}
+
+/**
+ * Open a TCP/TLS connection to the proxy and send an HTTP CONNECT request.
+ * Returns the live socket and the proxy's response on success; destroys the
+ * socket and throws on any network/protocol error.
+ */
+async function attemptHttp2Connect(
+ proxyHost: string,
+ proxyPort: number,
+ isHttpsProxy: boolean,
+ caCertificates: string[],
+ targetHost: string,
+ targetPort: number,
+ timeoutMs: number,
+ authHeader: string,
+): Promise<{ sock: net.Socket; response: Http2TunnelResponse }> {
+ const sock = await http2ConnectToProxy(
+  proxyHost, proxyPort, isHttpsProxy, caCertificates, targetHost, targetPort, timeoutMs
+ );
+ try {
+  const response = await sendHttp2ConnectRequest(sock, targetHost, targetPort, authHeader, timeoutMs);
+  return { sock, response };
+ } catch (err) {
+  sock.destroy();
+  throw err;
+ }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Establishes an HTTP CONNECT tunnel through the proxy, including
+ * proactive auth lookup, 407 challenge-response retry, and URL credential
+ * fallback.  Mirrors the auth retry pattern used in HttpsProxyAgent2.
+ */
+async function establishHttp2ConnectTunnel(
+ params: ProxyAgentParams,
+ proxyHost: string,
+ proxyPort: number,
+ isHttpsProxy: boolean,
+ caCertificates: string[],
+ proxyURL: string,
+ proxyBasicAuth: string | undefined,
+ targetHost: string,
+ targetPort: number
+): Promise<net.Socket> {
+ const state: Record<string, any> = {};
+ const timeoutMs = params.getProxyConnectTimeout?.() ?? defaultProxyConnectTimeoutMs;
+
+ // Proactive auth: try lookupProxyAuthorization first (e.g., cached Kerberos ticket)
+ let authHeader = '';
+ if (params.lookupProxyAuthorization) {
+  try {
+   const auth = await params.lookupProxyAuthorization(proxyURL, undefined, state);
+   if (auth) {
+    authHeader = `Proxy-Authorization: ${auth}\r\n`;
+   }
+  } catch (err) {
+   params.log.error('ProxyResolver#http2.connect', `proactive auth lookup failed: ${toErrorMessage(err)}`);
+  }
+ }
+ if (!authHeader && proxyBasicAuth) {
+  authHeader = `Proxy-Authorization: Basic ${proxyBasicAuth}\r\n`;
+ }
+
+ const { sock, response } = await attemptHttp2Connect(
+  proxyHost, proxyPort, isHttpsProxy, caCertificates, targetHost, targetPort, timeoutMs, authHeader
+ );
+
+ if (response.statusCode === 200) {
+  return sock;
+ }
+
+ // 407 challenge-response: the proxy rejected the first attempt but told
+ // us which auth scheme it wants via Proxy-Authenticate.  Ask the auth
+ // provider for credentials and retry on a fresh connection (the proxy
+ // may have closed the first one after the 407).
+ if (response.statusCode === 407 && params.lookupProxyAuthorization) {
+  const proxyAuthenticate = response.headers['proxy-authenticate'];
+  if (proxyAuthenticate) {
+   let auth: string | undefined;
+   try {
+    auth = await params.lookupProxyAuthorization(proxyURL, proxyAuthenticate, state);
+   } catch (err) {
+    sock.destroy();
+    params.log.error('ProxyResolver#http2.connect', `auth retry failed: ${toErrorMessage(err)}`);
+    throw new Error(`ProxyResolver#http2.connect: proxy CONNECT rejected (407): ${response.statusLine}`);
+   }
+
+   sock.destroy();
+   if (auth) {
+    const retry = await attemptHttp2Connect(
+     proxyHost, proxyPort, isHttpsProxy, caCertificates,
+     targetHost, targetPort, timeoutMs,
+     `Proxy-Authorization: ${auth}\r\n`,
+    );
+    if (retry.response.statusCode === 200) {
+     return retry.sock;
+    }
+    retry.sock.destroy();
+    throw new Error(
+     `ProxyResolver#http2.connect: proxy CONNECT rejected after auth: ${retry.response.statusLine}`
+    );
+   }
+  }
+ }
+
+ sock.destroy();
+ throw new Error(`ProxyResolver#http2.connect: proxy CONNECT rejected: ${response.statusLine}`);
+}
+
+function http2ConnectToProxy(
+ proxyHost: string,
+ proxyPort: number,
+ isHttpsProxy: boolean,
+ caCertificates: string[],
+ targetHost: string,
+ targetPort: number,
+ timeoutMs: number
+): Promise<net.Socket> {
+ return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
+  const sock = isHttpsProxy
+   ? tls.connect({
+    host: proxyHost,
+    port: proxyPort,
+    ...(caCertificates.length ? { ca: [...tls.rootCertificates, ...caCertificates] } : undefined),
+   })
+   : net.createConnection({ host: proxyHost, port: proxyPort });
+
+  // Timeout, connect, and error race to settle this promise.
+  // settle() prevents double-rejection (e.g. timer fires, destroy()
+  // triggers an error event) and clears the timer.
+  let settled = false;
+  function settle(): boolean {
+   if (settled) { return false; }
+   settled = true;
+   clearTimeout(timer);
+   return true;
+  }
+
+  const timer = setTimeout(() => {
+   if (!settle()) { return; }
+   sock.destroy();
+   reject(new Error(
+    `ProxyResolver#http2.connect: proxy TCP connect timeout ` +
+    `(proxy=${proxyHost}:${proxyPort}, target=${targetHost}:${targetPort})`
+   ));
+  }, timeoutMs);
+
+  // For HTTPS proxies, wait for 'secureConnect' (TLS handshake done)
+  // rather than 'connect' (TCP only).  Resolving on 'connect' would let
+  // sendHttp2ConnectRequest write before TLS is ready, and the timeout
+  // would not cover TLS negotiation time.
+  const readyEvent = isHttpsProxy ? 'secureConnect' : 'connect';
+  sock.once(readyEvent, () => {
+   if (!settle()) { return; }
+   resolve(sock);
+  });
+
+  sock.once('error', (err: Error) => {
+   if (!settle()) { return; }
+   reject(new Error(
+    `ProxyResolver#http2.connect: proxy TCP connect failed ` +
+    `(proxy=${proxyHost}:${proxyPort}, target=${targetHost}:${targetPort}): ${toErrorMessage(err)}`
+   ));
+  });
+ });
+}
+
+interface Http2TunnelResponse {
+ statusCode: number;
+ statusLine: string;
+ headers: Partial<Record<string, string | string[]>>;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Parse a raw HTTP/1.x response head (status-line + headers) from a buffer.
+ *
+ * Node.js doesn't expose a standalone HTTP header parser - its internal llhttp
+ * parser is coupled to sockets/streams. Because we speak raw HTTP/1.1 over a
+ * plain net.Socket for the CONNECT handshake (so we can keep the socket and
+ * splice it into the HTTP/2 tunnel afterwards), we must parse the response
+ * ourselves. This is the standard approach used by other proxy libraries.
+ */
+function parseRawHttpResponse(raw: string): Http2TunnelResponse {
+ const headerEnd = raw.indexOf('\r\n\r\n');
+ const head = headerEnd !== -1 ? raw.substring(0, headerEnd) : raw;
+ const lines = head.split('\r\n');
+
+ const statusLine = lines[0];
+ const statusMatch = /^HTTP\/1\.[01] (\d+)/.exec(statusLine);
+ if (!statusMatch) {
+  throw new Error(`Malformed HTTP response status line: ${statusLine}`);
+ }
+ const statusCode = parseInt(statusMatch[1], 10);
+
+ // Duplicate header names are coalesced into arrays to match Node.js's
+ // IncomingMessage.headers behaviour for multi-value headers like
+ // Set-Cookie or Proxy-Authenticate.
+ const headers: Record<string, string | string[]> = {};
+ for (let i = 1; i < lines.length; i++) {
+  const colonIndex = lines[i].indexOf(':');
+  if (colonIndex !== -1) {
+   const key = lines[i].substring(0, colonIndex).trim().toLowerCase();
+   const value = lines[i].substring(colonIndex + 1).trim();
+   const existing = headers[key];
+   if (existing === undefined) {
+    headers[key] = value;
+   } else if (Array.isArray(existing)) {
+    existing.push(value);
+   } else {
+    headers[key] = [existing, value];
+   }
+  }
+ }
+
+ return { statusCode, statusLine, headers };
+}
+
+function sendHttp2ConnectRequest(
+ proxySock: net.Socket,
+ targetHost: string,
+ targetPort: number,
+ authHeader: string,
+ timeoutMs: number
+): Promise<Http2TunnelResponse> {
+ const authority = net.isIPv6(targetHost) ? `[${targetHost}]` : targetHost;
+
+ return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
+  let buf = '';
+  let settled = false;
+
+  // Timeout, data, error, and write-error are four racing event sources
+  // that compete to settle this promise. The `settle()` guard ensures
+  // exactly one wins; subsequent callbacks become no-ops. The winner
+  // detaches the remaining listeners and clears the timer so nothing
+  // leaks on the long-lived proxy socket.
+  function settle(): boolean {
+   if (settled) { return false; }
+   settled = true;
+   clearTimeout(timer);
+   proxySock.removeListener('data', onData);
+   proxySock.removeListener('error', onError);
+   return true;
+  }
+
+  const timer = setTimeout(() => {
+   if (!settle()) { return; }
+   reject(new Error(
+    `ProxyResolver#http2.connect: proxy CONNECT timeout (${targetHost}:${targetPort})`
+   ));
+  }, timeoutMs);
+
+  function onData(chunk: Buffer) {
+   if (settled) { return; }
+   buf += chunk.toString('latin1');
+   if (!buf.includes('\r\n\r\n')) { return; }
+   if (!settle()) { return; }
+
+   try {
+    resolve(parseRawHttpResponse(buf));
+   } catch (e: any) {
+    reject(new Error(
+     `ProxyResolver#http2.connect: ${e.message} (${targetHost}:${targetPort})`
+    ));
+   }
+  }
+
+  function onError(err: Error) {
+   if (!settle()) { return; }
+   reject(new Error(
+    `ProxyResolver#http2.connect: proxy socket error during CONNECT ` +
+    `(${targetHost}:${targetPort}): ${toErrorMessage(err)}`
+   ));
+  }
+
+  proxySock.on('data', onData);
+  proxySock.once('error', onError);
+
+  proxySock.write(
+   `CONNECT ${authority}:${targetPort} HTTP/1.1\r\n` +
+   `Host: ${authority}:${targetPort}\r\n` +
+   authHeader +
+   `Connection: keep-alive\r\n\r\n`,
+   (writeErr?: Error | null) => {
+    if (!writeErr || !settle()) { return; }
+    reject(new Error(
+     `ProxyResolver#http2.connect: failed to write CONNECT request: ${toErrorMessage(writeErr)}`
+    ));
+   }
+  );
+ });
+}
+

  export function createFetchPatch(params: ProxyAgentParams, originalFetch: typeof globalThis.fetch, resolveProxyURL: (url: string) => Promise<string | undefined>) {
  return async function patchedFetch(input: string | URL | Request, init?: RequestInit) {
```

</details>

---

### Design notes

**Why `resolveHttp2Socket` layers TLS explicitly and forwards caller options**

When `createConnection` is provided, http2 [skips its internal `tls.connect()` entirely](https://github.com/nodejs/node/blob/5f02bdb7de10592e2a0f39a9f1c2e1113b4d8300/lib/internal/http2/core.js#L3618) - it assumes the returned socket is already transport-ready.

For `https://` targets, `resolveHttp2Socket` therefore wraps the raw tunnel socket in `tls.connect({ ...sessionOptions, socket: rawSock, servername, ALPNProtocols: ['h2'] })` and waits for `'secureConnect'` before returning. The caller's original `options` (passed to `http2.connect()`) are threaded through as `sessionOptions` and spread as the base of the `tls.connect` call, so TLS-relevant properties like `rejectUnauthorized`, `ca`, `cert`, `key`, `passphrase`, and `checkServerIdentity` are forwarded to the target connection. 

`socket` and `ALPNProtocols` are placed after the spread to always take precedence, while `servername` honours a caller override but falls back to `targetHost`. When `ca` is present, the patched `patchTlsConnect` early-returns (skipping V2 cert injection), which respects the caller's explicit trust configuration - consistent with how `createHttpPatch` handles it. Because `tls` is the patched version (via `createTlsPatch`), the `options.socket` branch in `patchTlsConnect` fires automatically when no `ca` is set, injecting additional CA certificates into the `secureContext`. 

The resulting `tls.TLSSocket` has a native `_handle` with `isStreamBase === true`, so http2 uses it directly - the most efficient path.

**Why a `stream.Duplex` bridge instead of a bare `net.Socket`**

`createConnection` must return synchronously, but tunnel establishment is async. http2 wraps any socket without a native `_handle` in [`JSStreamSocket`](https://github.com/nodejs/node/blob/main/lib/internal/js_stream_socket.js) ([`core.js` lines 1297-1299](https://github.com/nodejs/node/blob/main/lib/internal/http2/core.js#L1297-L1299)), which reads via `stream.on('data', ...)` and writes via `stream.write()`. 

A bare `new net.Socket()` with no network fd cannot participate - its `write()` buffers data with no consumer, and no `'data'` events fire because there is no network read side. A custom `Duplex` whose `_write()` forwards to the real socket and whose readable side is fed by `push()` correctly bridges JSStreamSocket to the tunnel. 

Note that `createConnection` returns the bridge Duplex, not the `tls.TLSSocket` - http2 never sees the TLS socket directly. `JSStreamSocket` wrapping is therefore always used as a permanent intermediary between http2's framing layer and the real transport socket, for both the proxy and direct paths. 

The bridge correctly shuttles decrypted plaintext from `tls.TLSSocket`'s `'data'` events into `bridge.push()`, and forwards http2's writes via `remoteSock.write()` through the TLS layer.

<details><summary>Diagram</summary>
<p>

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client calls http2.connect] --> B{Proxy patch applies?}
    B -- No --> C[Use original http2.connect]
    B -- Yes --> D[Inject createConnection bridge]

    D --> E[Return bridge immediately]
    E --> F[Resolve real socket in background]

    F --> G{Proxy resolved?}
    G -- No --> H[Direct TCP socket]
    G -- Yes --> I[HTTP CONNECT tunnel]

    H --> J{HTTPS target?}
    I --> J
    J -- No --> K[Use raw socket]
    J -- Yes --> L[Layer TLS on socket]

    L --> M[Forward caller TLS options]
    M --> N[Force ALPN h2 and choose servername]
    K --> O[Attach socket to bridge]
    N --> O

    O --> P[Bridge becomes active]
    P --> Q[Drain queued writes]
    Q --> R[Enter steady-state bidirectional flow]
```

</p>
</details> 

**Why `'secureConnect'` instead of `'connect'` for HTTPS proxies**

`tls.connect()` emits `'connect'` when TCP connects but `'secureConnect'` only after the TLS handshake completes ([Node.js TLS docs](https://nodejs.org/api/tls.html#event-secureconnect)). http2 itself uses the same pattern at [line 1336-1337](https://github.com/nodejs/node/blob/main/lib/internal/http2/core.js).

**Why `'latin1'` instead of `'ascii'` for CONNECT response parsing**

`'ascii'` strips the high bit from bytes > 127, corrupting non-ASCII content such as UTF-8 realm strings in `Proxy-Authenticate` headers. `'latin1'` is a lossless 8-bit encoding that preserves all byte values.

---

### Smoke test script

A standalone script that can be run inside an extension host (or directly with Node.js + the patched module) to verify the tunneling works:

<details>

<summary>See test script</summary>

```typescript
import * as http2 from "http2"; // returns patched module in extension host

const TARGET = "https://www.google.com";

const session = http2.connect(TARGET);
session.on("connect", () => {
  console.log("[http2] session connected");
  const req = session.request({ ":path": "/" });
  req.on("response", (headers) => {
    console.log("[http2] :status =", headers[":status"]);
  });
  req.setEncoding("utf8");
  let body = "";
  req.on("data", (chunk) => {
    body += chunk;
  });
  req.on("end", () => {
    console.log("[http2] body length:", body.length);
    session.close();
  });
  req.end();
});
session.on("error", (err) => {
  console.error("[http2] session error:", err.message);
});
```

Expected output on a proxy-mandatory network:

```text
[http2] session connected
[http2] :status = 200
[http2] body length: <non-zero>
```

Without this patch, the same script fails with:

```text
[http2] session error: getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND www.google.com
```

</details>

Title: VSCode asked me to upgrade while I'm already Pro+ (might be cross-device issue)

Description:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.43.0

Title: Getting "Retried with Autopilot" messages with autopilot disabled

Description:
On a flaky internet connection, I got a "Retried with Autopilot" message in a chat session with auto approvals enabled, but autopilot disabled. I think the plugin retried on a network timeout, witch might be a normal and expected behavior, but at a minimum **the action label is confusing** (and/or wrong).
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.43.0
- VS Code Version:
  - Version: 1.115.0

  - ElectronBuildId: 13703022
  - Chromium: 142.0.7444.265

Title: Global rate limit timer behaves incorrectly in VS Code

Description:

Type: <b>Bug</b>

Bug: global rate limit timer behaves incorrectly in VS Code

What happens:
The Claude Hikaru 4.5 in VS Code shows “You’ve hit your global rate limit,” but after pressing Try Again the reset time is inconsistent and can increase instead of steadily counting down. Changing the model shows the same message and issues.

Examples seen in chronological order:

* 2 seconds
* 1 minute
* 43 seconds

Additional detail:
I also waited about 1 real minute, but the timer in VS Code only decreased by around 10 seconds instead of counting down normally.

Expected:
A stable countdown that matches real elapsed time, or a clearer explanation that the wait is based on a rolling/account-wide limit rather than a true timer.

Please investigate whether this is:

* expected account-level throttling
* multiple-session quota behavior

Environment:



VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z)
OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200

Title: Cli version mismatch

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.116.0
- OS Version: Windows NT x64 10.26220

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Update the VSCode to the latest version
2. Download the VSCode CLI from https://code.visualstudio.com/download
3. The latest VSCode version is 1.116.0 and the CLI version is 1.115.0
4. Since we use `./code command-shell --cli-data-dir /home/azureuser/vscode-agent/cli --on-port=8884 --on-host=127.0.0.1` command to create VSCode server in the remote. When the version is mismatch, this error will show, and the only way to workaround is downgrade the version of VSC.

<img width="577" height="216" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/828b24cb-3d62-4d7c-a374-9b330d5f81f3" />


Please consider synchronize the publishing process of VSCode and the VSCode CLI so that their versions align more closely, as the VSCode version has been updated frequently recently.

Comments:
`command-shell` does not do compatibility assurances and is designed for internal use only. If you use it it's up to you to make sure versions match. Consider `code serve-web` or `code tunnel` instead.

Title: "Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused. Please upgrade your account or revert to Copilot Free."

Description:

I have an active paid GitHub Copilot Pro subscription on my account, and the billing page confirms: “GitHub Copilot Pro is active for your account”. 

<img width="1057" height="343" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/96bfbdfb-0121-4808-95ac-67b45d82cae0" />

However, in VS Code Copilot Chat fails to load any models and returns HTTP 403 with this message:

“Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused. Please upgrade your account or revert to Copilot Free.”

This appears to be a backend entitlement/provisioning mismatch, because:

* authentication works
* Copilot token is successfully issued
* model fetch fails with 403
* the error incorrectly treats my paid account as a paused trial

Please check and refresh/reset the Copilot entitlement state for my account.

Relevant log excerpt:

* Logged in successfully
* Got Copilot token
* Failed to fetch models: 403
* “Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused...”

What can I do?

My problem does not appear to be related to premium request counters or the consumptive billing model. It seems that my previous free trial may have been blocked, while my account is nevertheless shown as “Copilot Pro”.

* github.com/copilot returns “Access denied”
* VS Code cannot load **ANY** models
* VS Code logs return 403

This suggests an account entitlement/provisioning mismatch: the billing page shows an active paid Copilot Pro subscription (as in screenshot), but the Copilot service appears to still treat my account as a paused trial.

Could you please escalate this as an account entitlement/provisioning issue and refresh or reset the Copilot entitlement on my account?

Title: Integrated browser doesn't work with GitHub Copilot

Description:
Type: <b>Bug</b>

Title: Problema con GitHub Copilot!

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->

Title: Support sandbox modes for chat agents

Description:
## Summary

Create explicit sandbox modes for chat agents.

## Discussion points

- Sandboxing should have modes similar to Claude Code, including a YOLO-style mode.
- YOLO mode should effectively be represented by auto-approval enabled together with sandboxing enabled.
- There should also be a regular confirmation mode, similar to the current non-sandboxed behavior, where commands still require confirmation as appropriate.

## Expected outcome

Define and expose sandbox modes that make the relationship between sandboxing and command confirmation behavior clear to users.

Comments:

> Generated by [errors-agents-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24426425462/agentic_workflow) · ● 4.6M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-agents-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24426425462, workflow_id: errors-agents-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24426425462 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error `potential listener LEAK detected, popular` is triggered when the `onDidColorThemeChange` event emitter on the theme service accumulates too many listeners (exceeding the leak detection threshold). Each `XtermTerminal` instance created via `createDetachedTerminal` registers a listener on this global service. In the Agents product, each terminal tool invocation creates a `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror` (or `DetachedTerminalCommandMirror`), each instantiating a full `XtermTerminal`. When an agent session runs many terminal commands, the cumulative listener count exceeds the threshold.

### Culprit Commit

No single culprit commit was identified. This is a design-level issue: the `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror` constructor eagerly creates a full `XtermTerminal` (with all its global service listeners) for each terminal command output snapshot. The error first appeared in telemetry on April 10, 2026, likely due to growing Agents product usage pushing the listener count over the threshold. The error persists across all commits from `3d70aab9` (1.115.0) through `1dfb3b9d` (1.116.0).

Related issue: #308254 (same root cause, different stack frame — listener leak from detached terminal links contribution).

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Chat as ChatTerminalToolProgressPart
    participant Section as ChatTerminalToolOutputSection
    participant Snapshot as DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror
    participant Service as TerminalService
    participant Xterm as XtermTerminal

    Chat->>Section: _renderSnapshotOutput(snapshot)
    Section->>Snapshot: new DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror()
    Note over Snapshot: Constructor eagerly creates<br/>a full XtermTerminal
    Snapshot->>Service: createDetachedTerminal()
    Service->>Xterm: new XtermTerminal()
    Note over Xterm: Registers listeners on:<br/>• onDidColorThemeChange<br/>• onDidChangeConfiguration<br/>• onDidChangeLogLevel
    Note over Xterm: ⚠️ Root cause: Each instance<br/>adds listeners to global singletons
    Xterm-->>Service: xterm instance
    Note over Chat: After N tool invocations...
    Note over Xterm: 💥 ListenerLeakError thrown:<br/>"potential listener LEAK<br/>detected, popular"
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/base/common/event.ts:1062` | Crash site — `Stacktrace.create()` in leak detection |
| `src/vs/base/common/event.ts:1262` | Emitter subscription check triggers leak detection |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/xterm/xtermTerminal.ts:289` | Root cause — registers `onDidColorThemeChange` listener per instance |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/chatTerminalCommandMirror.ts:611` | Data producer — `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror` constructor eagerly creates XtermTerminal |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/chatTerminalCommandMirror.ts:413` | Data producer — `DetachedTerminalCommandMirror._getOrCreateTerminal()` also creates detached terminals |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/terminalService.ts:1098` | `createDetachedTerminal` entry point |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatContentParts/toolInvocationParts/chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts:1396` | Creates `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror` per tool result |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product / sessions window)
2. Start an agent session that runs many terminal commands (e.g., a coding agent running build/test/lint commands)
3. As the agent runs 50+ terminal commands, each creates a `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror` to display the output
4. Each mirror creates a full `XtermTerminal` that registers listeners on global services
5. When the listener count on `onDidColorThemeChange` exceeds the threshold, the leak error is thrown
6. The error is more likely to trigger with longer agent sessions that produce more terminal outputs

### Fix Proposal

Two complementary approaches:

**1. Lazy terminal creation in `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror`** (primary fix)
**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/chatTerminalCommandMirror.ts` → `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror` constructor
**Approach**: Move the `createDetachedTerminal` call out of the constructor and into the `attach()` or `render()` method. The terminal should only be created when the snapshot is actually being displayed, not when the mirror object is constructed. This would also allow the terminal to be disposed when scrolled out of view in virtualized lists.

**2. Lightweight rendering for snapshot output** (longer-term)
**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/chatTerminalCommandMirror.ts` → `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror`
**Approach**: For read-only snapshot terminals that display static output, consider using a lightweight rendering approach (e.g., a canvas or pre-rendered HTML) instead of a full `XtermTerminal` instance with all its service listeners. Snapshots don't need live theme change handling, configuration watching, or log level monitoring.

**3. Increase listener threshold for detached terminals** (quick mitigation)
**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/xterm/xtermTerminal.ts` constructor
**Approach**: When creating XtermTerminal for detached/snapshot use, consider not registering on global service events, or unsubscribing when not attached to the DOM.

### Recommended Owner

`@meganrogge` — area owner for terminal chat integration, authored the `DetachedTerminalSnapshotMirror`/`DetachedTerminalCommandMirror` classes and the `createDetachedTerminal` infrastructure (PRs #285985, #283906, #280442)

## Regression Details

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24426425462, workflow_id: errors-agents-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24426425462 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error `ListError [AgentSessionsView] Invalid index 0` is thrown when `AgentSessionsControl.reveal()` calls `getRelativeTop()` on the sessions list tree while the tree model and list widget are out of sync. The tree model reports a node exists at index 0 but the list widget has 0 rendered items. This is a race condition triggered during orientation transitions (switching to side-by-side view) in the Chat View Pane.

### Culprit Commit

No narrowed commit range was provided by the regression scan. Based on telemetry bucket history, the error first appeared at [`3d70aab9`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/3d70aab9a4ed407832e43584ac7a661be08e2d74) (1.115.0-insider, Apr 3) with 1 hit, then increased across 1.116.0-insider builds (up to 12 hits, 8 users). The `reveal()` method with the `getRelativeTop` pattern was originally introduced in PR #290233 (merged Jan 25), but the error only started appearing later — likely exposed by subsequent changes to session list layout, grouping, or visibility logic.

> The root cause is a missing guard in the `reveal()` method itself, rather than a specific commit introducing a regression.

### Code Flow

````mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Layout as ChatViewPane.layoutSessionsControl
    participant Control as AgentSessionsControl
    participant AsyncTree as AsyncDataTree
    participant AbstractTree as AbstractTree
    participant ListView as ListWidget

    Layout->>Control: update() [via Throttler]
    Control->>AsyncTree: updateChildren()
    Note over AsyncTree: Tree model updated,<br/>nodes map populated
    AsyncTree-->>Control: Promise resolves
    Layout->>Control: reveal(sessionResource)
    Control->>Control: hasNode(session) → true ✅
    Control->>AsyncTree: getRelativeTop(session)
    AsyncTree->>AbstractTree: getRelativeTop(dataNode)
    AbstractTree->>AbstractTree: model.getListIndex() → 0
    AbstractTree->>ListView: getRelativeTop(index=0)
    Note over ListView: 💥 this.length === 0<br/>0 >= 0 → throws ListError
````

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentSessionsControl.ts` | **Root cause** — `reveal()` method lacks guard before `getRelativeTop()` call (line 836) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widgetHosts/viewPane/chatViewPane.ts` | **Trigger** — orientation switch to side-by-side calls `reveal()` after async update (line 987–992) |
| `src/vs/base/browser/ui/list/listWidget.ts` | **Crash site** — `getRelativeTop()` throws `ListError` when index ≥ length (line 2003–2004) |
| `src/vs/base/browser/ui/tree/abstractTree.ts` | **Data flow** — `getListIndex()` returns valid index from tree model, passes to list widget (line 3103–3110) |
| `src/vs/base/browser/ui/tree/asyncDataTree.ts` | **Data flow** — `hasNode()` checks `nodes` Map (different structure than list widget length) |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product) with the Chat View Panel visible
2. Have at least one agent session in the sessions list
3. Resize the Chat View pane width rapidly across the `SESSIONS_SIDEBAR_VIEW_MIN_WIDTH` threshold — this forces repeated orientation switches between stacked ↔ side-by-side
4. The race window exists between `update()` completing (tree model updated) and the list widget fully rendering its items
5. Alternatively, this can trigger during initial layout when the chat view pane is first opened in a width that selects side-by-side orientation

> This is a race condition and is non-deterministic. Rapid resizing or slow data source responses increase the likelihood.

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentSessionsControl.ts` → `reveal()`

**Approach**: Wrap the `getRelativeTop()` call in a try-catch to gracefully handle the case where the tree model and list widget are out of sync. If `getRelativeTop()` throws, fall through to `reveal(session, 0.5)` unconditionally (which is the desired behavior when the element isn't visible anyway):

```typescript
reveal(sessionResource: URI): boolean {
    // ... existing checks ...

    try {
        if (this.sessionsList.getRelativeTop(session) === null) {
            this.sessionsList.reveal(session, 0.5);
        }
    } catch {
        // Tree model and list widget can be temporarily out of sync
        // during orientation transitions — fall through to focus/select
    }

    this.sessionsList.setFocus([session]);
    this.sessionsList.setSelection([session]);
    return true;
}
```

Alternatively, a more targeted fix would check the list widget's length before calling `getRelativeTop`, but the try-catch is more resilient against other edge cases in the tree widget lifecycle.

### Recommended Owner

`@bpasero` — primary author of the chat sessions integration code in `chatViewPane.ts` and the agent sessions layout logic (PRs #289325, #305555, #284396). The `reveal()` call site in the orientation switch code falls within his ownership area.

Title: FR: Update integrated browser

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
The integrated browser seems to be Chromium 142.0.7444.265, which was released about 6 months ago. 

It would be fantastic if it could be updated to at least v147, the current stable version. 

Even better would be if we could somehow install beta, dev or canary. Obviously it is fine if that's not practical/possible (probably needs to build vscode from source...)

This would allow us to test our apps with the latest browser functionality - currently I have to open a separate browser for that.

Comments:
I see now that it depends on what version of electron VS Code uses. Currently 1.117.0-insider uses electron 39.8.7. The latest stable electron is v41.2, which uses chromium 146.0.7680.179

useful links:
* https://github.com/ewanharris/vscode-versions
* https://releases.electronjs.org/release?channel=stable


Hi @nickchomey, you are correct that it depends on the version of Electron. While we stay up-to-date with Electron's security patches, the major version can lag behind for various reasons.

I'll close this issue as there's nothing additional that can be done for the integrated browser, but you can follow https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/292445 to track the progress of upgrading VS Code to Electron v41.
Thanks! I looked for such an issue but couldnt find it. i'll follow there

---

how do i fix this ]

same error
Looks like they have blocked all Copilot Pro trials recently, causing this "Language model unavailable" issue for some users.
https://github.blog/changelog/2026-04-10-pausing-new-github-copilot-pro-trials/

But it still didn't work, when I canceled the trial and tried to go back to the free plan...

Title: Allow selection of code changed by Copilot

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

When using Copilot in Agent mode, I often want to keep the change that was made, but there is some part of the replaced code that I want to use for a manual improvement to Copilot's edit, that I want to ask Copilot about, or that I want to do something else with. E.g.:

<img width="1681" height="176" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3ee2308e-ee6d-4660-84da-3001112e3e6e" />

If is frustrating that I cannot copy the red text. Because I can't, I often find myself copying the green instead, clicking Undo, pasting, and then copying the original.

Please allow selection and copying of the replaced text.

Comments:
I'd like it too but this isn't really practical for us to do, since this isn't a "real" diff editor. The easiest way is to either go through git/scm or to use these buttons to open the changes in a diff editor

<img width="422" height="124" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d174ab3a-adb2-4c8e-b8b8-2addae5e5701" />

---

Title: Agents: Review pass on configurations that should be hidden or modified

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
We've already done an initial pass on commands: we should consider another pass on config (commands, settings, any UI elements or entry points) that doesn't make sense (at all, or at least not as-is) in Agents.

Example: Various debug commands.

Comments:
## Commands

### 1. Debug Commands in Command Palette
While the Run/Debug top-level menu is hidden, all debug commands (Start Debugging, Step Over, Toggle Breakpoint, Add Configuration, Open Debug Console, etc.) still appear in the Command Palette.

The breakpoint editor contributions add gutter functionality that might be useful when viewing code, but stepping/starting debug sessions doesn't seem as relevant.

**Proposed action**: Hide debug commands from the Command Palette. 

### 2. Problems commands

These currently no-op, and given the output-oriented nature of the app, they don't feel as relevant.

<img width="1062" height="186" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/da1f871e-08eb-4034-ac99-abba84eef934" />

**Proposed action**: Hide problems commands from the Command Palette.

## UI

### 1. Top-level menus

I'm testing on Windows, so considering https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/309894, I don't have the top-level menus. I'll explore through the lens of assuming the top-level menus will be available in Windows/Linux too at some point (so consider which of the current VS Code top-level menu items do or don't make sense in Agents). Also worth noting that some of these may have already been removed (I can't verify since the top-level menus aren't available at all on Windows).

**Proposed action:** Remove the following items from each menu area:
- `File`
     - `Close Folder` (folders aren't the sole focus / particularly "opened" in Agents) 
- `Edit`
     - `Find in Files` and `Replace in Files` (again since folders aren't the sole focus - which files would it be searching?)
- `Selection`
     - Should we generally remove these, as the navigation concepts are different in Agents vs VS Code? 
- `View`
     - `Problems`
     - `Output`
     - `Debug Console`
     - `Chat` (should we remove this, since chat is basically always visible in Agents?)
- `Go`
     - Should we generally remove these, as the navigation concepts are different in Agents vs VS Code? 
- `Run`
     - Already removed entirely (already available through separate button on top right)
- `Terminal`
     - Remove entirely (already available through separate button on top right)
- `Help`
     - Should we remove the entire first section (`Welcome` through `Ask vscode`), given they're welcome experiences specific to VS Code?
     
## Settings

### 1. Near term

Given the continued evolution of features and settings in Agents, I think we could take a couple passes.

**Proposed action:**
- Rename `vscode` to `Agents`
     - <img width="1977" height="1206" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/418f584c-e5d8-4f43-bae6-f1ca69c894c8" />

### 2. Mid to long term

**Proposed action:**
- Curate an updated list of `Commonly Used` settings
     - Since this is one of the more common user-facing entry points
- Perform an even deeper analysis of settings to modify or remove

## Notifications

**Proposed action:**
Update user-facing strings that hardcode `VS Code`.
- Account / auth entries, including about invalid GH token and then being prompted to install using VS Code account menu
- Entries below

### MCP

- [`mcpListWidget.ts#L170`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/mcpListWidget.ts#L170) — `"This server is managed by VS Code and forwarded to all compatible agent sessions."`
- [`mcpListWidget.ts#L813`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/mcpListWidget.ts#L813) — `"MCP servers contributed by installed VS Code extensions."`
- [`mcpListWidget.ts#L840`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/mcpListWidget.ts#L840) — `"MCP servers built into VS Code. These are available automatically."`

### Prompt Validation

These are reachable but only when a user opens a prompt file in the editor and that file has an unsupported YAML frontmatter attribute. It's a fairly specific scenario — editing customization files with invalid attributes. They're low-frequency compared to the MCP ones.

- [`promptValidator.ts#L264`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/promptSyntax/languageProviders/promptValidator.ts#L264) — `"Attribute '{0}' is ignored when running locally in VS Code."`
- [`promptValidator.ts#L266`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/promptSyntax/languageProviders/promptValidator.ts#L266) — `"Attribute '{0}' is not supported in VS Code agent files."`
- [`promptValidator.ts#L272`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/promptSyntax/languageProviders/promptValidator.ts#L272) — `"Attribute '{0}' is not supported in rules files by VS Code agents."`
- [`promptValidator.ts#L278`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/promptSyntax/languageProviders/promptValidator.ts#L278) — `"Attribute '{0}' is not supported by VS Code agents."`

### Notifications / Misc

- [`terminalView.ts#L211`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/terminalView.ts#L211) — `"Be sure to restart VS Code if this is a newly installed font."`

Title: Language model unavailable in GitHub Copilot Chat (models not loading)

Description:

Type: <b>Bug</b>

1. Open Visual Studio Code
2. Install GitHub Copilot Chat extension
3. Sign in to GitHub account
4. Open Copilot Chat panel
5. Try to start a new chat session

Actual Result:
The chat shows "Language model unavailable" and does not respond.

Expected Result:
Copilot Chat should load normally and respond to prompts.

- GitHub account is successfully logged in
- Copilot subscription is active (premium requests visible)
- VS Code version: 1.115.0
- Copilot Chat version: 0.43.0

Error logs show:
"Failed to fetch models"
"Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused"
"Language models loaded: false"

The issue seems related to Copilot not loading language models despite having an active subscription. The logs indicate a 403 error and mention that Copilot Pro trials are paused. This prevents the chat feature from working.

Description:

## AI Translation:

### Title:
Lost credits due to faulty AI

### Body:
Type: <b>Bug</b>

Hello,

I lost several hours and numerous credits because of a poor analysis by the Claude Sonnet 4.6 agent, which failed to identify a trivial Laravel model binding issue. I am requesting a refund for the credits consumed during this session.

Comments:
## Stable Release Anomaly Details

- **Anomaly type**: `new`
- **Previous stable release**: [1.114.0](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/3c9a3a4c-6e52-bc43-b2a8-5145e896176a?product=VSCode&commit=e7fb5e96c0730b9deb70b33781f98e2f35975036)
- **Current stable release**: [1.115.0](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/3c9a3a4c-6e52-bc43-b2a8-5145e896176a?product=VSCode&commit=41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b)
- **Last release without this error**: [1.114.0](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/3c9a3a4c-6e52-bc43-b2a8-5145e896176a?product=VSCode&commit=e7fb5e96c0730b9deb70b33781f98e2f35975036)
- **First release with this error**: [1.115.0-insider](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/3c9a3a4c-6e52-bc43-b2a8-5145e896176a?product=VSCode&commit=dcd017c96b0f0d515131b0478847f8cec911570e)
- **Commit range**: [compare link](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/e7fb5e96c0730b9deb70b33781f98e2f35975036...dcd017c96b0f0d515131b0478847f8cec911570e)

**Impact**: 19.0K users, 74.8K hits on 1.115.0 stable across Linux, Mac, Windows.

**Deminified call path**: `event.ts` → `chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts:371` → `instantiationService.ts` → `chatToolInvocationPart.ts:196` → `chatToolInvocationPart.ts:120` → `chatToolInvocationPart.ts:98` (render) → `autorunImpl.ts` → `transaction.ts` → `observableValue.ts` → `chatToolInvocation.ts:241` → `languageModelToolsService.ts:582` → `mainThreadLanguageModelTools.ts:63`

The listener leak originates in the chat tool invocation UI rendering path. When `chatTerminalToolProgressPart` is constructed, it creates an event listener chain through the observable/transaction system that exceeds the threshold during tool invocation state transitions.

> Generated by [errors-stable-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24411677600/agentic_workflow) · ● 10.4M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-stable-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-stable-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24411677600, workflow_id: errors-stable-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24411677600 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-stable-scan -->
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

Each `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` instance (one per terminal tool invocation) subscribes to global service emitters like `keybindingService.onDidUpdateKeybindings()` in its constructor. In agentic chat sessions with many concurrent terminal tool invocations, the accumulated listener count on these emitters exceeds the leak detection threshold (line 371 at commit `41dd792b`), throwing the "potential listener LEAK detected, popular" error. A secondary race condition in `_initializeTerminalActions()` — called both directly and via `whenConnected.then()` — compounds the issue by registering duplicate `onDidRegisterTerminalInstanceWithToolSession` listeners.

### Culprit Commit

This is a **pre-existing latent bug** — the problematic code was identical in both 1.114.0 (`e7fb5e96`) and 1.115.0 (`41dd792b`). The error became detectable in 1.115.0 due to increased agentic feature adoption, which pushed the number of concurrent `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` instances above the leak detection threshold. No single commit in the regression window introduced the bug.

**Fix PRs** (both merged to `main`, milestone **1.116.0** — not in 1.115.0 stable):

| PR | Author | What it fixes |
|----|--------|---------------|
| [#308000](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308000) | `@meganrogge` | Removes the per-instance `onDidUpdateKeybindings` subscription (the source of this stack trace) and refactors the action bar to a scoped `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` |
| [#308819](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308819) | `@Copilot` | Fixes the `_initializeTerminalActions()` race condition (double-call → single guarded call) |

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant ToolSvc as languageModelToolsService
    participant Invoc as chatToolInvocation
    participant InvPart as chatToolInvocationPart
    participant TermPart as chatTerminalToolProgressPart
    participant KBSvc as keybindingService (global)

    ToolSvc->>Invoc: transitionFromStreaming()
    Invoc->>Invoc: _state.set({Executing})
    Note over Invoc: Observable triggers autorun
    Invoc->>InvPart: autorun → render()
    InvPart->>InvPart: partStore.clear() (dispose old)
    InvPart->>TermPart: createToolInvocationSubPart() → new ChatTerminalToolProgressPart
    Note over TermPart: Constructor subscribes to<br/>keybindingService.onDidUpdateKeybindings
    TermPart->>KBSvc: _register(onDidUpdateKeybindings)
    Note over KBSvc: ⚠️ With 50+ tool invocations,<br/>listener count > threshold
    KBSvc-->>KBSvc: LeakageMonitor.check()
    Note over KBSvc: 💥 "potential listener LEAK<br/>detected, popular"
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatContentParts/toolInvocationParts/chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts` | **Root cause + crash site** — constructor subscribes to global emitter per instance (line 371) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatContentParts/toolInvocationParts/chatToolInvocationPart.ts` | Rendering loop — creates `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` via `createToolInvocationSubPart()` |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatProgressTypes/chatToolInvocation.ts` | State transitions trigger re-renders via observable |
| `src/vs/base/common/event.ts` | Leak detection infrastructure — `LeakageMonitor` throws when threshold exceeded |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code 1.115.0 stable
2. Start an agentic chat session (e.g., Copilot agent mode)
3. Let the agent execute many terminal tool invocations (30+) in a single session
4. Each tool invocation creates a `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` that subscribes to `keybindingService.onDidUpdateKeybindings()`
5. When the total listener count on the keybinding service emitter exceeds the threshold (~50+), the leak error is thrown

The error is non-deterministic in the sense that it depends on the number of concurrent terminal tool invocations. Sessions with fewer invocations won't hit the threshold.

### Fix Proposal

**Already fixed** in PRs [#308000](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308000) and [#308819](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308819), both targeting **1.116.0**:

1. **Fix location**: `chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts` → constructor  
   **Approach (PR #308000)**: Remove the per-instance `onDidUpdateKeybindings` subscription entirely. Replace the manual `ActionBar` with a scoped `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` that manages keybinding tooltips internally, eliminating the need for per-instance keybinding change listeners.

2. **Fix location**: `chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts` → constructor / `_initializeTerminalActions()`  
   **Approach (PR #308819)**: Guard the double `_initializeTerminalActions()` call with a `didInitializeTerminalActions` flag to prevent the async race condition that registers duplicate `onDidRegisterTerminalInstanceWithToolSession` listeners.

**For 1.115.0 stable**: Consider cherry-picking PR #308000 and #308819 if a patch release is planned.

### Recommended Owner

`@meganrogge` — terminal chat area owner, authored the primary fix PR (#308000) and reviewed/merged the secondary fix (#308819)

**Deminified call path**: `event.ts` → `menuService.ts:437` → `event.ts` → `codeCellRunToolbar.ts:56` → `instantiationService.ts` → `cellRenderer.ts:346` → `listWidget.ts` → `rowCache.ts` → `listView.ts`

The listener leak originates in notebook cell rendering. When a `codeCellRunToolbar` is created (via `cellRenderer.ts:346`), it triggers a menu service event listener chain that exceeds the threshold. This is in the notebook editor rendering pipeline.

> Generated by [errors-stable-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24411677600/agentic_workflow) · ● 10.4M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-stable-scan%22&type=issues)

## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error "potential listener LEAK detected, popular" fires on the global `PersistedMenuHideState.onDidChange` emitter when its listener count exceeds the 175-listener threshold. Notebook cell rendering is the primary contributor — each cell template creates 4 menus (2 from `RunToolbar`, 2 from `CellTitleToolbarPart`), each subscribing to this singleton emitter via `menuService.ts:437` (`startLazyListener`). This error bucket appeared as "new" in 1.115.0 because PR #303543 changed the `ListenerLeakError` message format to include a "dominated"/"popular" classification, creating new telemetry buckets. The underlying listener accumulation pre-dates 1.115.0 but was previously spread across many per-emitter buckets with unique messages. **Impact: 22.2K users, 38.6K hits.**

### Culprit Commit

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Commit | [`18b02b61`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/18b02b61aed43f511ddec124e2d400d1067194a6) |
| Author | `@jrieken` |
| PR | #303543 |
| Message | Classify listener leak errors as dominated or popular |
| Why | Changed `ListenerLeakError` message from a generic format to include `dominated`/`popular` classification. This split all leak errors into 2 telemetry buckets instead of N unique ones, making the pre-existing notebook menu listener accumulation surface as a new high-hit-count bucket. |

> **Note**: This commit did not introduce the listener accumulation itself — it changed how listener leak errors are reported in telemetry. The underlying issue (too many menus subscribing to a single global emitter) pre-dates this change.

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant NB as NotebookCellList
    participant CR as CellRenderer.renderTemplate
    participant RT as RunToolbar (×2 menus)
    participant TT as CellTitleToolbarPart (×2 menus)
    participant MS as MenuImpl (startLazyListener)
    participant HS as PersistedMenuHideState.onDidChange

    NB->>CR: splice → alloc → renderTemplate
    CR->>RT: createInstance(RunToolbar)
    Note over RT: Creates primaryMenu + secondaryMenu<br/>Subscribes to both .onDidChange
    RT->>MS: menu.onDidChange(updateActions)
    MS->>HS: hiddenStates.onDidChange(...)
    Note over HS: ⚠️ Listener count grows<br/>per template (4 menus each)

    CR->>TT: createInstance(CellTitleToolbarPart)
    TT->>MS: titleMenu.onDidChange / deleteMenu.onDidChange
    MS->>HS: hiddenStates.onDidChange(...)
    Note over HS: 💥 Exceeds 175 threshold<br/>"potential listener LEAK detected, popular"
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/base/common/event.ts` | Leak detection site — `LeakageMonitor.check()` fires the error; `ListenerLeakError` message format changed by culprit commit |
| `src/vs/platform/actions/common/menuService.ts` | `PersistedMenuHideState._onDidChange` — the singleton emitter that accumulates listeners; `startLazyListener` (line 437) subscribes to it per menu |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/view/cellParts/codeCellRunToolbar.ts` | Creates 2 menus per cell template (lines 46–47), subscribes to both `onDidChange` (lines 55–56) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/view/cellParts/cellToolbars.ts` | `CellTitleToolbarPart` creates 2 more menus per cell template (lines 154–155) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/view/renderers/cellRenderer.ts` | Instantiates `RunToolbar` + `CellTitleToolbarPart` per cell template (lines 319–347) |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code 1.115.0 (any platform)
2. Open a notebook file with ≥ 30 code cells (enough to create many cell templates in the list view)
3. Scroll through the notebook to force template allocation for visible cells
4. The `PersistedMenuHideState.onDidChange` emitter accumulates 4 listeners per cell template (from the 4 menus) plus listeners from all other active menus in the workbench
5. Once the total exceeds 175, the `ListenerLeakError` fires with kind "popular" (since many different call stacks contribute, no single one >30%)

> This is **non-deterministic** in the sense that the exact trigger point depends on how many other menus are active in the workbench (toolbars, context menus, status bar items, etc.). More complex workbench layouts lower the notebook cell count needed to trigger it.

### Fix Proposal

Two complementary approaches:

**Option A — Increase threshold on the global emitter** (quick fix):
**Fix location**: `src/vs/platform/actions/common/menuService.ts` → `PersistedMenuHideState` constructor
**Approach**: Set a custom `leakWarningThreshold` on the `_onDidChange` emitter since it is architecturally a shared singleton that legitimately receives subscriptions from every active menu. Example: `new Emitter<void>({ leakWarningThreshold: 500 })`.

**Option B — Reduce per-template menu count** (proper fix):
**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/view/cellParts/codeCellRunToolbar.ts` and `cellToolbars.ts`
**Approach**: Share a single menu instance across cell templates for each `MenuId` instead of creating a new menu per template. The menu actions are the same for all cells of the same type — only the toolbar context changes per cell. This would reduce the per-template listener contribution from 4 to 0 on the global `hiddenStates` emitter.

### Recommended Owner

`@jrieken` — author of the culprit commit (PR #303543) and owner of the event/menu infrastructure. The `PersistedMenuHideState.onDidChange` emitter needs a higher threshold since it's a legitimate shared singleton. The notebook team (`@amunger`) may also want to consider Option B to reduce per-template menu allocations.

---

Title: [Unhandled Error] potential listener LEAK detected, popular — runInTerminalTool / Event.toPromise

Description:
## Error Message
````
potential listener LEAK detected, popular
````

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-stable-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24411677600, workflow_id: errors-stable-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24411677600 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-stable-scan -->
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The **"potential listener LEAK detected, popular"** error fires when a terminal instance's `onDisposed` emitter accumulates ≥175 listeners. The primary accumulation path is in `TerminalChatService.registerTerminalInstanceWithToolSession()`, which is called with a **new UUID** (`terminalToolSessionId`) for every tool invocation — even when reusing the same cached foreground terminal. Each call adds an `onDisposed` listener keyed by the unique session ID, so old entries for the same terminal are never cleaned up. A secondary path is `_registerCompletionNotification()` in `runInTerminalTool.ts`, which similarly keys listeners by a unique `termId` per invocation. In agentic sessions that run many commands on the same terminal, these listeners accumulate past the global leak threshold (175).

### Culprit Commit

There is no single culprit commit — the listener accumulation pattern in `registerTerminalInstanceWithToolSession` predates the regression window (it exists in both 1.114.0 and 1.115.0). However, the regression was made observable by the increased terminal tool usage patterns introduced in this window:

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| PR | [#307199](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/307199) |
| Author | `@meganrogge` |
| Message | Add completion notifications and async prompt monitoring for background terminals |
| Why | Introduced `_registerCompletionNotification()` which adds per-`termId` listeners (including `onDisposed`) to terminal instances. Combined with the existing per-invocation leak in `registerTerminalInstanceWithToolSession`, this accelerated listener accumulation past the 175 threshold. |

Related PRs in the regression window:
- **[#306832](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/306832)** (Apr 1): Fixed `this._register()` → `Event.once()` for singleton-scoped listeners — a necessary fix but doesn't address the per-invocation accumulation
- **[#308069](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308069)** (Apr 6): Attempted to fix listener leak using `DisposableMap` but kept `termId`-based key, leaving the core issue unresolved

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Agent as Chat Agent
    participant RIT as RunInTerminalTool.invoke()
    participant Init as _initTerminal()
    participant TCS as TerminalChatService
    participant Term as Terminal.onDisposed

    Agent->>RIT: invoke(command)
    RIT->>RIT: termId = generateUuid()
    RIT->>Init: _initTerminal(session, termId, toolSessionId)
    Init->>Init: Cached terminal found → reuse
    Init->>TCS: registerTerminalInstanceWithToolSession(newUUID, sameTerminal)
    Note over TCS: ⚠️ Root cause: new UUID key<br/>→ old listener NOT cleaned up
    TCS->>Term: instance.onDisposed(() => cleanup)
    Note over Term: Listener count: N+1

    Agent->>RIT: invoke(next command) [repeated]
    RIT->>Init: Same terminal reused
    Init->>TCS: registerTerminalInstanceWithToolSession(anotherUUID, sameTerminal)
    TCS->>Term: instance.onDisposed(() => cleanup)
    Note over Term: Listener count: N+2 ... N+175

    Agent->>RIT: invoke(command #175+)
    RIT->>RIT: RichExecuteStrategy.execute()
    RIT->>Term: Event.toPromise(onDisposed)
    Note over Term: 💥 LeakageMonitor.check()<br/>"potential listener LEAK detected, popular"
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chat/browser/terminalChatService.ts` | **Root cause** — `registerTerminalInstanceWithToolSession()` adds per-invocation `onDisposed` listener keyed by unique `terminalToolSessionId`; old entries for same terminal not cleaned up |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/runInTerminalTool.ts` | **Secondary accumulation** — `_registerCompletionNotification()` adds per-`termId` listeners; also the invocation site that triggers cached terminal reuse |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/executeStrategy/richExecuteStrategy.ts` | **Crash site** — `Event.toPromise(this._instance.onDisposed, store)` at line 68 triggers the leak check when adding the 175th+ listener |
| `src/vs/base/common/event.ts` | **Detection** — `LeakageMonitor.check()` fires when listener count ≥ 175 (global threshold) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/terminalInstance.ts` | **Emitter** — `_onDisposed` emitter that accumulates the leaked listeners |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code with Copilot agent mode enabled
2. Start a chat session in agent mode
3. Give the agent a task that requires running many terminal commands sequentially (e.g., "Run these 50 test files one at a time and report results")
4. The agent reuses the same cached foreground terminal for each command
5. Each invocation calls `registerTerminalInstanceWithToolSession` with a new UUID, adding an `onDisposed` listener
6. After ~160-175 commands on the same terminal, the leak threshold is exceeded
7. The next `Event.toPromise(instance.onDisposed)` in `richExecuteStrategy.execute()` triggers the error

The error is more likely to trigger in long-running agentic sessions with many sequential terminal commands. Background terminal transitions (timeout, continue-in-background) accelerate the leak through `_registerCompletionNotification`.

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location 1**: `terminalChatService.ts` → `registerTerminalInstanceWithToolSession()`
**Approach**: Before adding a new registration, check if the terminal instance already has an existing `terminalToolSessionId` mapping (via `_toolSessionIdByTerminalInstance.get(instance)`). If so, clean up the old entry in `_terminalInstanceListenersByToolSessionId` by calling `deleteAndDispose(oldToolSessionId)` before registering the new one. This ensures that re-registering the same terminal instance properly disposes the old `onDisposed` listener.

**Fix location 2**: `runInTerminalTool.ts` → `_registerCompletionNotification()`  
**Approach**: Change `_backgroundNotifications` key from `termId` (unique per invocation) to `terminalInstance.instanceId` (unique per terminal). This way, re-registering completion notifications for the same terminal properly disposes the old listeners via `deleteAndDispose`. The `termId` can still be stored inside the `DisposableStore` for use in the notification callbacks.

### Recommended Owner

`@meganrogge` — Primary author of the terminal tool infrastructure (`runInTerminalTool.ts`, `terminalChatService.ts`) and all related PRs in the regression window (#306832, #307199, #308069, #308587).

### Analysis

This error occurs in `extensionsWorkbenchService.ts` at `openSearch()` when attempting to read the `search` property of an undefined object. The error appeared in the first 1.116.0 insider build and affects 27 users across Windows and Mac.

The deminified stack shows only a single frame (`extensionsWorkbenchService.ts:openSearch`), suggesting the error is thrown at the entry point of the search operation — likely `this.searchValue` or a similar property is undefined when `openSearch` is called.

**Key source files**: `extensionsWorkbenchService.ts`
