<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24411772706, workflow_id: errors-agents-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24411772706 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The `openSearch()` method in `extensionsWorkbenchService.ts` crashes with `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'search')` in the **Agents** product (sessions desktop window). The method calls `viewPaneContainer.search(searchValue)` without null-checking the result of `openViewContainer(VIEWLET_ID)`. In the Agents/Sessions window, the Extensions viewlet either cannot be fully opened or its `getViewPaneContainer()` returns `undefined`, causing the crash. This is a latent null-safety bug exposed by changes in the 1.116.0 insider build that broadened when extension/chat-related code paths execute.

### Culprit Commit

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Commit | [`e9c8a5c7`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/e9c8a5c77057ec4fcc123855f8cf9170004ba393) |
| Author | `@sandy081` |
| PR | #308478 |
| Message | disable copilot chat extension by default and adopt accordingly |
| Why | This commit removes `ChatContextKeys.Setup.disabled` guards from multiple code paths (chatSetupContributions.ts, chatParticipant.contribution.ts, chatActions.ts, etc.), causing agent/tool registrations and chat features to activate in scenarios where they were previously suppressed. In the Agents window, this enables code paths that call `openSearch()` (e.g., via plugin recommendations, extension search actions) where the Extensions viewlet cannot be opened, triggering the crash. |

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Caller as Chat/Plugin Action
    participant EWS as ExtensionsWorkbenchService
    participant VS as ViewsService
    participant PCS as PaneCompositePartService
    participant SP as SidebarPart

    Caller->>EWS: openSearch(searchValue)
    EWS->>VS: openViewContainer(VIEWLET_ID, true)
    VS->>PCS: openPaneComposite(id, Sidebar)
    PCS->>SP: openPaneComposite(id)
    Note over SP: ⚠️ Root cause:<br/>Extensions viewlet cannot open<br/>or getViewPaneContainer() is undefined<br/>in Agents/Sessions window
    SP-->>PCS: undefined
    PCS-->>VS: undefined
    VS-->>EWS: null
    EWS->>EWS: ?.getViewPaneContainer() → undefined
    Note over EWS: 💥 viewPaneContainer.search(searchValue)<br/>TypeError: Cannot read 'search' of undefined
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/extensions/browser/extensionsWorkbenchService.ts` (L1614-1620) | **Crash site** — `openSearch()` lacks null guard on `viewPaneContainer` |
| `src/vs/workbench/services/extensionManagement/browser/extensionEnablementService.ts` | Modified by culprit — chat extension enablement migration |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatSetup/chatSetupContributions.ts` | Modified by culprit — removed `disabled` checks enabling more code paths |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatParticipant.contribution.ts` | Modified by culprit — chat view shown in more scenarios |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/claudePluginRecommendations.ts` | Likely caller — `openSearch('`@agentPlugins` `@recommended`')` |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/actions/chatPluginActions.ts` | Possible caller — `openSearch('`@agentPlugins` ')` |
| `src/vs/sessions/browser/paneCompositePartService.ts` | Sessions-specific pane composite service |

### Repro Steps

1. Open the **Agents** window (sessions desktop product)
2. Trigger an action that calls `openSearch()` — e.g., open a workspace with recommended agent plugins, wait for the plugin recommendation notification, and click "Show Plugins"; or run the "Plugins" command from the command palette
3. The Extensions viewlet fails to open or its view pane container is not initialized, causing the crash

This is user-action triggered (50 hits / 27 users on a single build), consistent with clicking through a notification or running a command.

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/extensions/browser/extensionsWorkbenchService.ts` → `openSearch()`

**Approach**: Add a null guard for `viewPaneContainer` before calling `.search()` and `.focus()`. The pattern already exists at line 1233 of `extensions.contribution.ts` where `getViewPaneContainer()` is typed as `IExtensionsViewPaneContainer | undefined` and checked before use.

```
// Current (broken):
const viewPaneContainer = (await this.viewsService.openViewContainer(VIEWLET_ID, true))?.getViewPaneContainer() as IExtensionsViewPaneContainer;
viewPaneContainer.search(searchValue);

// Fix: guard against undefined
const viewPaneContainer = (await this.viewsService.openViewContainer(VIEWLET_ID, true))?.getViewPaneContainer() as IExtensionsViewPaneContainer | undefined;
viewPaneContainer?.search(searchValue);
if (!preserveFoucs) {
    viewPaneContainer?.focus();
}
````

### Recommended Owner

`@sandy081` — Culprit commit author (PR #308478), primary maintainer of extensions workbench service and extension enablement infrastructure

Title: [Unhandled Error] AbstractContextKeyService has been disposed

Comments:
## Regression Details

- **Product**: Agents
- **Regression type**: `new`
- **Last release without this error**: [1.115.0](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/f9ae0085-f9b9-2e6c-59e7-a3e601c82d68?product=Agents&commit=41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b)
- **First release with this error**: [1.116.0-insider](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/f9ae0085-f9b9-2e6c-59e7-a3e601c82d68?product=Agents&commit=fc6d18c3174aa8962d90435aba5b51ef6c300b9a)
- **Commit range**: [41dd792b...fc6d18c3](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b...fc6d18c3174aa8962d90435aba5b51ef6c300b9a)

### Analysis

This error occurs when `AbstractContextKeyService` is used after disposal, specifically through the AI customization list widget's refresh flow. The call path is: `aiCustomizationListWidget.ts:refresh` → `updateAddButton` → `buildCreateActions` → `menuService.ts:getMenuActions` → `getActions` → `createActionGroups` → `contextKeyService.ts:contextMatchesRules`.

The widget attempts to evaluate context key rules for menu actions during a refresh cycle, but the underlying context key service has already been disposed — likely due to a session teardown or view disposal race condition.

**Note**: Existing issues #246652 and #260086 track the same `AbstractContextKeyService has been disposed` error but through different call paths (diff-editor/gutterFeature and toolbar). This bucket represents a distinct trigger through the AI customization list widget.

**Key source files**: `aiCustomizationListWidget.ts`, `contextKeyService.ts`, `menuService.ts`

> Generated by [errors-agents-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24411772706/agentic_workflow) · ● 6.9M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-agents-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24411772706, workflow_id: errors-agents-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24411772706 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The `AICustomizationListWidget.refresh()` method is async — it `await`s `loadItems()` and then calls `updateAddButton()`. If the widget's scoped `contextKeyService` is disposed while `loadItems()` is running (e.g., during editor/session teardown), the post-await `updateAddButton()` → `buildCreateActions()` → `menuService.getMenuActions()` call passes the disposed service into `MenuImpl`, which calls `contextMatchesRules()` and throws. This is a classic use-after-dispose async race condition, likely exacerbated in 1.116.0 by additional `refresh()` trigger paths and increased customization provider activity in the Agents product.

### Culprit Commit

No single commit introduced this bug — the vulnerable async `refresh()` pattern pre-dates the regression window. However, the following commit in the regression range adds a new `refresh()` call path after file deletion that increases the likelihood of hitting the race:

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Commit | [`bb14e18d`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/bb14e18d7940a6e7f7c9da5aab6f368d88fd8d3b) |
| Author | `@joshspicer` |
| PR | #308096 |
| Message | ChatSessionCustomizationProvider: fix groupKey, deletion refresh, section visibility, and dropdown UX |
| Why | Adds an explicit `refreshList()` call from `aiCustomizationManagement.contribution.ts` after deleting a customization item. This fires in addition to the existing `onDidFilesChange` listener, creating overlapping async `refresh()` calls during a period when the editor may be closing or the session tearing down. |

Other contributing factors in the range:
- PR #308307 (`f414e325cb`) — adds welcome page, makes `selectedSection` nullable, changes editor lifecycle
- PR #308115 — changes `_inferStorageAndGroup` classification, potentially altering fetch behavior
- The `providerApi` was enabled by default (`bda49a1b25`) before the range, increasing provider-driven `onDidChange` events

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Event as Event/Autorun Trigger
    participant Widget as AICustomizationListWidget
    participant LoadItems as loadItems() [async]
    participant Lifecycle as Editor/Session Teardown
    participant Update as updateAddButton()
    participant Menu as MenuService
    participant CTX as ContextKeyService

    Event->>Widget: refresh()
    Widget->>LoadItems: await loadItems()
    Note over LoadItems: Fetching items async...
    Lifecycle->>CTX: dispose()
    Note over CTX: ⚠️ _isDisposed = true
    LoadItems-->>Widget: items loaded
    Note over Widget: No disposal check!
    Widget->>Update: updateAddButton()
    Update->>Update: buildCreateActions()
    Update->>Menu: getMenuActions(menuId, this.contextKeyService)
    Menu->>CTX: contextMatchesRules(rules)
    Note over CTX: 💥 Error thrown:<br/>"AbstractContextKeyService<br/>has been disposed"
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/aiCustomizationListWidget.ts` | **Root cause** — `refresh()` at L1164 lacks disposal guard after await |
| `src/vs/platform/contextkey/browser/contextKeyService.ts` | **Crash site** — `contextMatchesRules()` at L322 throws on disposed service |
| `src/vs/platform/actions/common/menuService.ts` | **Data flow** — `getMenuActions()` creates `MenuImpl` with disposed contextKeyService |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/aiCustomizationManagement.contribution.ts` | **Trigger** — new `refreshList()` call after deletion (PR #308096) |

### Repro Steps

1. Open the Chat Customizations editor in the Agents product (sessions window)
2. Have an active customization provider (harness) that supplies items
3. Delete a customization item from the list while the editor is in a state where the session or view can be torn down (e.g., switch sessions quickly, or close the editor rapidly after deletion)
4. The deletion triggers `refreshList()` from the contribution + `onDidFilesChange` fires → two overlapping `refresh()` calls
5. If the editor/widget is disposed during `loadItems()`, `updateAddButton()` runs on a disposed contextKeyService

This is timing-dependent — it requires the contextKeyService to be disposed between the start of `loadItems()` and the `updateAddButton()` call. In the Agents product, session lifecycle events (opening/closing sessions, switching harnesses) create more disposal activity, increasing the window for this race.

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/aiCustomizationListWidget.ts` → `refresh()`

**Approach**: Add a disposal guard after the `await this.loadItems()` call in `refresh()` to prevent `updateAddButton()` from running on a disposed widget:

```typescript
async refresh(): Promise<void> {
    await this.loadItems();
    if (this._store.isDisposed) {
        return;
    }
    this.updateAddButton();
}
```

Additionally, consider adding the same guard in `loadItems()` after the await, before modifying `allItems` and firing events:

```typescript
if (this.currentSection !== section || this._loadItemsSeq !== seq || this._store.isDisposed) {
    return;
}
```

This follows the standard VS Code pattern for async methods in `Disposable` subclasses: always check disposal state after any `await` before touching instance state or services that may have been disposed.

### Recommended Owner

`@joshspicer` — authored PR #308096 (the primary contributing change in the regression range) and the majority of recent `AICustomizationListWidget` changes including the provider API integration, event subscription fixes, and lifecycle improvements.

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*

Comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

`registerTerminalInstanceWithToolSession` in `terminalChatService.ts` leaks `onDidDisposeSession` listeners. Each tool invocation generates a unique `terminalToolSessionId` (via `generateUuid()` at `runInTerminalTool.ts:666`), but when a foreground terminal is reused across invocations, each call adds a **new** listener on the global `chatService.onDidDisposeSession` emitter without cleaning up the previous one. After enough tool invocations in a single session, the listener count breaches the leak threshold (>30% dominated by one call stack), triggering this error.

### Culprit Commit

No single culprit commit — this is a pre-existing design flaw in `registerTerminalInstanceWithToolSession` that became a visible regression in 1.116.0-insider due to increased terminal tool usage (more Copilot chat tool invocations per session and the subagent feature added in PR #308592, merged Apr 10). The error bucket first appeared on commit `1dfb3b9d` (Apr 13) with 251 hits / 117 users, all in a single day.

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Copilot as Copilot Chat Agent
    participant RIT as RunInTerminalTool
    participant TCS as TerminalChatService
    participant CS as ChatService.onDidDisposeSession

    loop Each tool invocation
        Copilot->>RIT: invoke() [runInTerminalTool.ts:1221]
        RIT->>RIT: terminalToolSessionId = generateUuid() [line 666]
        RIT->>RIT: _initTerminal() → reuses cached foreground terminal [line 1841]
        RIT->>TCS: registerTerminalInstanceWithToolSession(newId, sameInstance)
        Note over TCS: ⚠️ Root cause: does NOT clean up<br/>old listeners for this instance
        TCS->>CS: instanceStore.add(onDidDisposeSession(...)) [line 110]
        Note over CS: Listener count: N+1
    end
    Note over CS: 💥 Listener count exceeds threshold<br/>"potential listener LEAK detected, dominated"
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chat/browser/terminalChatService.ts` | **Root cause** — `registerTerminalInstanceWithToolSession()` (line 94) leaks `onDidDisposeSession` listeners |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/runInTerminalTool.ts` | Caller — generates new `terminalToolSessionId` per invocation (line 666), calls registration on cached terminal reuse (line 1841) |
| `src/vs/base/common/event.ts` | Crash site — leak detection fires at line 1032/1062 |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Insiders (1.116.0+)
2. Start a Copilot chat session and have it run many terminal commands (e.g., agent mode iterating on a build/test cycle)
3. Each `runInTerminal` tool invocation reuses the same foreground terminal but registers it with a new `terminalToolSessionId`
4. After ~50+ tool invocations (depending on leak threshold), the `onDidDisposeSession` emitter breaches the listener limit
5. The "potential listener LEAK detected, dominated" error is thrown

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chat/browser/terminalChatService.ts` → `registerTerminalInstanceWithToolSession()`

**Approach**: Before creating new listeners, check if the terminal instance already has an existing tool session ID registration and clean up the old one's listeners. This mirrors the pattern already used in `registerTerminalInstanceWithChatSession()` (line 161-169 of the same file), which correctly calls `this._chatSessionListenersByTerminalInstance.deleteAndDispose(instance)` before re-registering.

Specifically, at the start of `registerTerminalInstanceWithToolSession`:
1. Look up the existing `terminalToolSessionId` for this `instance` via `_toolSessionIdByTerminalInstance`
2. If one exists and it differs from the new one, dispose the old entry in `_terminalInstanceListenersByToolSessionId` and remove the stale key from `_terminalInstancesByToolSessionId`
3. Then proceed with the new registration as before

This ensures at most one `onDidDisposeSession` listener per terminal instance, regardless of how many times it's re-registered with new tool session IDs.

### Recommended Owner

`@meganrogge` — terminal chat service area owner, recently fixed related listener leaks (PR #308819 in `chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts`, PR #309611 for background terminal event listeners)

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*

Comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The `LanguageService.onDidChange` emitter (leak threshold: 200) is exceeded when the chat editing system creates many snapshot `TextModel` instances simultaneously. Each snapshot model subscribes to this shared event through the `ILanguageSelection.onDidChange` → `observableFromEvent` → `LanguageService.onDidChange` chain. In Agents sessions with many edited files and checkpoints, the cumulative listener count crosses the 200-listener warning threshold, triggering this "popular" listener leak error.

### Culprit Commit

No single culprit commit was identified. This is a systemic issue that emerged with the growth of agent session file counts. The error bucket first appeared in version 1.114.0-insider (April 7) and has been steadily increasing:
- 1.114.0: ~6 hits
- 1.115.0: ~42 hits  
- 1.116.0: ~119 hits (latest)

The escalation correlates with increased Agents product adoption and the agent host editing infrastructure introduced in PRs #304303, #305348, and #305861 by `@connor4312` (merged late March, milestone 1.114.0), which expanded the number of snapshot models created per session.

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Resolver as textModelResolverService
    participant Provider as ChatEditingSnapshotTextModelContentProvider
    participant Session as ChatEditingSession.getSnapshotModel
    participant ModelSvc as ModelService.createModel
    participant TextModel as TextModel constructor
    participant LangSel as LanguageSelection (observableFromEvent)
    participant LangSvc as LanguageService.onDidChange

    Resolver->>Provider: provideTextContent(snapshotUri)
    Provider->>Session: getSnapshotModel(requestId, undoStop, uri)
    Session->>ModelSvc: createModel(content, createByFilepathOrFirstLine(uri), uri)
    Note over Session: ⚠️ Root cause: creates ILanguageSelection<br/>which subscribes to shared event
    ModelSvc->>TextModel: new TextModel(value, languageSelection, ...)
    TextModel->>LangSel: languageSelection.onDidChange(callback)
    LangSel->>LangSvc: subscribe to onDidChange (threshold: 200)
    Note over LangSvc: 💥 ListenerLeakError thrown<br/>when listeners > 200
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingSession.ts` | **Root cause** — `getSnapshotModel()` at line 401 creates models with `ILanguageSelection` |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingTextModelContentProviders.ts` | Entry point — `ChatEditingSnapshotTextModelContentProvider.provideTextContent()` |
| `src/vs/editor/common/services/languageService.ts` | Shared emitter — `onDidChange` has `leakWarningThreshold: 200` |
| `src/vs/editor/common/model/textModel.ts` | Subscription site — constructor subscribes to `languageSelection.onDidChange` (line 354) |
| `src/vs/editor/common/services/modelService.ts` | Model creation — `_createModelData` passes `ILanguageSelection` to TextModel |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Agents (Insiders)
2. Start an agent session that edits many files (20+ files across multiple checkpoints)
3. Navigate the chat editing timeline or view diffs that resolve snapshot URIs
4. Each snapshot model resolution adds a listener to `LanguageService.onDidChange`
5. When total live snapshot models exceed ~200, the listener leak error fires
6. This is more likely with longer agent sessions that accumulate many checkpoints across many files

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingSession.ts` → `getSnapshotModel()`

**Approach**: On line 401, replace `this._languageService.createByFilepathOrFirstLine(snapshotUri)` with a resolved language ID string. The `modelService.createModel` → `_createModelData` method accepts `string | ILanguageSelection` as the `languageIdOrSelection` parameter. When a plain string is passed, the TextModel constructor at line 352 takes the `string` branch and does **not** subscribe to `onDidChange`, completely avoiding the listener accumulation.

Snapshot models are read-only representations of past content — they do not need to react to language registration changes. The change would be:
- Call `this._languageService.createByFilepathOrFirstLine(snapshotUri).languageId` to get the resolved language ID string
- Pass that string directly to `createModel()` instead of the `ILanguageSelection` object

This eliminates the per-model subscription to the shared emitter while preserving correct syntax highlighting.

### Recommended Owner

`@connor4312` — Primary author of the chat editing session and agent host editing infrastructure (PRs #304303, #305348, #305861). The fix is in the `getSnapshotModel` method of `ChatEditingSession` which is core to the snapshot model creation path.

Title: Cannot use any models. Either "Language model unavailable" from copilot directly or "Unable to resolve chat model with family selection: copilot-base" from third parties.

Type: <b>Performance Issue</b>

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*

Comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error `TreeError [AgentSessionsView] Tree element not found: [object Object]` is a **race condition** between the async tree refresh cycle and a synchronous `reveal()` call in the AgentSessionsView. When the view model changes (e.g., switching sessions, showing a model), the `onDidChangeViewModel` handler attempts to reveal the active session in the sessions list. However, if the tree is in the middle of an asynchronous refresh, the internal data structures are temporarily inconsistent — the async data tree's `nodes` map has been updated but the underlying compressed object tree model has not — causing `getRelativeTop()` to throw. The error is growing over time (from ~12 hits in 1.113.0 to 500+ hits in 1.116.0) as the AgentSessions feature gains more usage.

### Culprit Commit

No single regression commit was identified. This is a **structural bug** introduced with the original `reveal()` + `onDidChangeViewModel` wiring in the agent sessions view. The error has been present since at least 1.113.0-insider (March 2026) and grows as feature usage increases. Key PRs that established this code path:
- PR #289325 by `@bpasero` — "Agent sessions in Chat integration feedback" (Jan 2026)
- PR #290233 — "Fix session list selection when adding new session in side-by-side view" (Jan 2026)

The increasing hit count correlates with the burst of session list improvements in March 2026 (repo grouping, collapsible sections, redesign) which add more tree operations and widen the race window.

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant ShowModel as chatViewPane.showModel()
    participant SetModel as chatWidget.setModel()
    participant Event as onDidChangeViewModel
    participant Reveal as agentSessionsControl.reveal()
    participant AsyncTree as AsyncDataTree
    participant CompressedModel as CompressedObjectTreeModel

    Note over AsyncTree,CompressedModel: ⚠️ Tree refresh in progress:<br/>nodes map updated, model stale
    ShowModel->>SetModel: setModel(model)
    SetModel->>Event: fire _onDidChangeViewModel
    Event->>Reveal: reveal(sessionResource)
    Reveal->>AsyncTree: hasNode(session)
    Note over AsyncTree: ✅ Found in this.nodes map
    Reveal->>AsyncTree: getRelativeTop(session)
    AsyncTree->>AsyncTree: getDataNode(session) → dataNode
    AsyncTree->>CompressedModel: getListIndex(dataNode)
    CompressedModel->>CompressedModel: getCompressedNode(dataNode)
    Note over CompressedModel: 💥 dataNode not in this.nodes!<br/>TreeError thrown
```

**Root cause detail**: In `AsyncDataTree.refreshAndRenderNode()`, there is a two-phase update:
1. `await this.refreshNode(...)` — calls `setChildren()` which **updates `this.nodes`** (the async data tree's element → data-node map)
2. `this.render(...)` — calls `tree.setChildren()` which **updates the compressed model's `nodes`** map

Between steps 1 and 2, there is a microtask boundary (`await`). During this window, `hasNode()` returns `true` (element exists in async tree's map) but the compressed model's `getCompressedNode()` throws because it hasn't been updated yet.

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentSessionsControl.ts` | **Crash site** — `reveal()` at line 836 calls `getRelativeTop()` without guarding against inconsistent tree state |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widgetHosts/viewPane/chatViewPane.ts` | **Trigger** — `onDidChangeViewModel` handler at line 607-609 calls `reveal()` during model switch |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatWidget.ts` | **Event source** — `setModel()` fires `_onDidChangeViewModel` at line 336 |
| `src/vs/base/browser/ui/tree/asyncDataTree.ts` | **Data flow** — `refreshAndRenderNode()` has async gap between `nodes` map update and compressed model update |
| `src/vs/base/browser/ui/tree/compressedObjectTreeModel.ts` | **Throw site** — `getCompressedNode()` at line 347 throws when data node not found |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Insiders with the AgentSessions view in **side-by-side mode**
2. Have multiple agent sessions active
3. Rapidly switch between sessions, or trigger a session list update while switching models (e.g., create a new session and immediately navigate to it)
4. The error is a race condition, so it's non-deterministic. It's more likely when:
   - The session list is refreshing (e.g., after `onDidChangeSessions` triggers `update()`)
   - A model change happens simultaneously (e.g., `showModel()` is called)
   - The tree data source has latency (widens the async gap)

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentSessionsControl.ts` → `reveal()`

**Approach**: Wrap the `getRelativeTop()` call in a try-catch to gracefully handle the case where the tree's internal state is temporarily inconsistent during an async refresh. When the error is caught, treat it the same as `getRelativeTop()` returning `null` (i.e., the element is not visible, so reveal it):

```typescript
reveal(sessionResource: URI): boolean {
    if (!this.sessionsList) {
        return false;
    }

    const session = this.agentSessionsService.model.getSession(sessionResource);
    if (!session || !this.sessionsList.hasNode(session)) {
        return false;
    }

    try {
        if (this.sessionsList.getRelativeTop(session) === null) {
            this.sessionsList.reveal(session, 0.5);
        }
    } catch {
        // Tree model may be temporarily inconsistent during async refresh.
        // Silently skip — the next onDidChangeSessions will re-trigger reveal.
        return false;
    }

    this.sessionsList.setFocus([session]);
    this.sessionsList.setSelection([session]);

    return true;
}
```

Alternatively, a deeper fix could ensure `refreshAndRenderNode()` updates both maps atomically, but that would require changes to the shared tree infrastructure (`asyncDataTree.ts`) which could have wider impact.

### Recommended Owner

`@bpasero` — Primary author of the agent sessions control code, the `reveal()` method, and the `onDidChangeViewModel` wiring in `chatViewPane.ts`. The current assignee `@bryanchen-d` may also be appropriate if they are now responsible for this area.

Title: run_in_terminal cancellation can hang indefinitely in basic and none execute strategies under continuous terminal output

Description:
[`waitForIdle` in executeStrategy.ts](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/executeStrategy/executeStrategy.ts#L35-L44) is a data-event debounce, not a timeout:

```ts
export async function waitForIdle(onData: Event<unknown>, idleDurationMs: number): Promise<void> {
    const store = new DisposableStore();
    const deferred = new DeferredPromise<void>();
    const scheduler = store.add(new RunOnceScheduler(() => deferred.complete(), idleDurationMs));
    store.add(onData(() => scheduler.schedule()));
    scheduler.schedule();
    return deferred.p.finally(() => store.dispose());
}
```

This helper has no cancellation path. If terminal output keeps streaming, `onData` continuously reschedules the `RunOnceScheduler`, so the promise can stay pending indefinitely.

`NoneExecuteStrategy` depends on this helper through [`waitForIdleWithPromptHeuristics`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/executeStrategy/executeStrategy.ts#L107-L146), and its main capture race at [`noneExecuteStrategy.ts#L130`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/executeStrategy/noneExecuteStrategy.ts#L130) does not include the cancellation token:

```ts
const promptResultOrAltBuffer = await Promise.race([
    waitForIdleWithPromptHeuristics(this._instance.onData, this._instance, idlePollInterval, idlePollInterval * 10),
    alternateBufferPromise.then(() => 'alternateBuffer' as const)
]);
```

So cancellation can remain blocked indefinitely while output continues.

`BasicExecuteStrategy` has a cancellation branch in its primary race at [`basicExecuteStrategy.ts#L82`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/executeStrategy/basicExecuteStrategy.ts#L82), but it then performs another uncancellable idle wait before checking `token.isCancellationRequested` at [`basicExecuteStrategy.ts#L160-L162`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/executeStrategy/basicExecuteStrategy.ts#L160):

```ts
await waitForIdle(this._instance.onData, idlePollInterval);
if (token.isCancellationRequested) {
    throw new CancellationError();
}
```

If output is still continuous, this follow-up wait can also block indefinitely, so cancellation does not unwind.

`RichExecuteStrategy` appears unaffected in this path: its main completion race includes token cancellation at [`richExecuteStrategy.ts#L66`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/executeStrategy/richExecuteStrategy.ts#L66), and it does not do the same second uncancellable idle wait afterward.

User-visible implication: on continuously noisy commands, users can press stop/cancel in chat and still see the tool invocation remain stuck (no unwind/return) in basic or none shell-integration modes, which makes cancellation look broken.

Comments:
Revised after re-reading the linked issues (previous version linked some that only share the "hang" end-user symptom):

| Relevance | Issue | Title | State | Why related |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Strong | #255396 | Investigate run in terminal tool hanging issues | Closed | Tyriar's meta/plan-item for the hanging-issue family (July 2025). Addressed conpty-off (#252489) and moved the tool off the extension API (#252650). The `waitForIdle` uncancellable debounce is a remaining, distinct root cause in this family |
| Strong | #254447 | Agent mode hangs when running terminal command | Closed | 120 comments, 36 upvotes. Closed July 2025 via conpty + shell-integration fixes. The basic/none execute-strategy path with continuous output was not specifically addressed, which is what this issue covers |
| Medium | #296887 | Terminal tool returns partial output for commands >~11s on terminals without shell integration | Closed | Same code path (`NoneExecuteStrategy` → `waitForIdleWithPromptHeuristics`), opposite failure mode: that bug is about premature return after 10s, this one is about never returning because streaming output keeps rescheduling the debounce |
| Weak | #271897 | Thoroughly test all powerlevel10k configs to get to bottom of hanging cases | Closed | p10k-specific hangs, root cause was #271980 (conpty/sequence handling). Different mechanism but same symptom family |
| Weak | #268900 | Command got stuck on terminal | Closed | `npx tsc` stuck forever — closed as `*duplicate`, actual mechanism not verified. Could plausibly be the `waitForIdle` debounce if tsc kept producing output, but speculative |
| Weak | #278967 | Copilot always gets stuck when performing second terminal command | Closed | "Second terminal command" hang — closed as `*duplicate`/`info-needed`. Speculative match |

Dropped from the previous table:

- **#309607** — chat-request cancellation of background tasks (fixed in 1.117). Different layer from `waitForIdle`.
- **#293859** — the agent re-issues `gcloud deploy` while the first is still running. That's a model-behavior issue, not the execute-strategy `await` failing to unwind.

No existing issue captures this specific root cause: `waitForIdle` is a data-event debounce with no `CancellationToken`, and `BasicExecuteStrategy` / `NoneExecuteStrategy` await it after the cancellation-aware race has already resolved.

---

Title: Command to exit vscode (Alt+F4)

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Command to exit vscode
When I tell my blind students that CTRL+Shift+P lists most all commands, I have to mention EXCEPT exiting vscode.
I must mention one can exit via the shortcut: Alt+F4
For completeness, It would be nice if vscode listed an "Exit VSCODE" command, reachable via CTRL+Shift+P


Comments:
There's a `Close Window` command
Close Window does not exit the program.
My students are blind and they may want to exit vscode.
So, for now, I will have to tell them vscode has many commands in a list that are accessible by CTRL+Shift+P, but sadly, Exit VSCODE is not one of them. However, Alt+F4 will exit VSCODE.
The Close Window command does not exit vscode.
My blind students often need to exit vscode.
For now, I have to tell my students that while vscode lists most commands
via Ctl+Shift+P shortcut, there is no Exit vscode command.  However exit
may be done via Alt+F4.  It would be a service to the blind to support an
Exit VSCODE command.
Regards,
Ray Smith

Title: Copilot Chat agent jobs can stall/fail due to invalid response item ID thinking_0 in execution subagent flow

Description:
## Summary

Copilot Chat intermittently hangs or appears to hang while running agent-style jobs. In the affected runs, the underlying failure is a `400 invalid_request_body` caused by an invalid input item ID:

`Invalid 'input[3].id': 'thinking_0'. Expected an ID that begins with 'rs'.`

This looks like a client-side protocol/serialization bug in the tool-calling or execution-subagent pipeline, not a workspace-specific project error.

## Environment

- VS Code: `1.116.0-insider`
- Copilot Chat: `0.44.2026041004`
- OS: Windows
- Copilot SKU: `free_faculty_quota`
- Observed models in the same session:
  - `gpt-5.3-codex`
  - `claude-sonnet-4-6`
  - `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`

## Observed behavior

When running a larger Copilot agent job, the job sometimes appears to get stuck for a long time, with repeated tool/execution activity and no clean completion. In one failing run, the backend rejects a request because the client sends `thinking_0` as an input item ID where an `rs...` ID is expected.

The user-visible symptom is that the job hangs or spins for a long time instead of failing fast or completing.

## Expected behavior

- Agent jobs should complete normally, or
- fail cleanly with a surfaced error,
- without entering a long-running apparent hang state.

The client should never send an invalid item ID like `thinking_0` if the API expects an ID beginning with `rs`.

## Relevant log evidence

Primary failure:

```text
2026-04-14 12:33:40.925 [info] Request ID for failed request: 3273f757-8307-4f80-8bde-3a46fb9d3dc6
2026-04-14 12:33:40.927 [error] Server error: 400 {"error":{"message":"Invalid 'input[3].id': 'thinking_0'. Expected an ID that begins with 'rs'.","code":"invalid_request_body"}}
2026-04-14 12:33:40.927 [error] Request Failed: 400 {"error":{"message":"Invalid 'input[3].id': 'thinking_0'. Expected an ID that begins with 'rs'.","code":"invalid_request_body"}}
2026-04-14 12:33:40.930 [info] ccreq:48b6346d.copilotmd | failed | gpt-5.3-codex | 803ms | [executionSubagentTool]
```

The same sessions also show related instability signals:

```text
[warning] [messagesAPI] Unknown content_block type 'text' at index 1 for model claude-sonnet-4-6
[warning] Tool click_element failed validation: object has unsupported top-level schema keyword 'oneOf'
[warning] Tool drag_element failed validation: object has unsupported top-level schema keyword 'allOf'
[warning] Error compiling input schema for tool run_in_terminal: Error: strict mode: unknown keyword: "enumDescriptions"
[warning] [virtual-tools] Had to drop 1 tools due to limit constraints
[warning] [virtual-tools] Had to drop 2 tools due to limit constraints
```

These may be separate issues, but they seem to correlate with the same degraded agent execution state.

## Reproduction notes

I cannot provide a minimal deterministic repro yet, but the pattern is:

1. Open VS Code Insiders.
2. Use Copilot Chat agent/edit workflow on a non-trivial coding task.
3. Let the agent invoke multiple tools / execution subagent steps.
4. In some runs, the workflow degrades into repeated long tool calls and eventually logs the invalid request body error above.

This seems more likely when:
- the session uses multiple model backends,
- execution-subagent/tool-calling is involved,
- tool schema validation warnings are already present in the session.

## Why this seems like a Copilot bug

- The failing payload contains an internal item ID format mismatch.
- The server explicitly rejects `thinking_0` as invalid.
- This is not tied to repo code execution or a project compile failure.
- The failure occurs inside the Copilot request/tool orchestration path.

## Impact

- Agent jobs appear to hang.
- Users lose time waiting for runs that will not complete cleanly.
- The failure mode is difficult to diagnose from the UI alone unless logs are inspected.

## Possible areas to inspect

- Response/input item ID mapping in execution-subagent flows
- Serialization of "thinking" items into request input arrays
- Cross-model content block normalization
- Tool schema compilation / validation fallback behavior
- Recovery path after tool-schema validation errors

## Additional request metadata

- Failed request ID: `3273f757-8307-4f80-8bde-3a46fb9d3dc6`

Comments:
This is a great catch. The invalid item ID format mismatch ( vs ) is exactly the type of protocol/schema issue that traditional code review misses.

We see a related pattern in AI-generated code: the LLM constructs function calls or API requests that look syntactically correct but fail at runtime because the schema drifted or the IDs are from a different context. Unit tests don"t catch it (they mock the API), type checkers don"t catch it (the ID is just a string), but production fails.

This is why we added schema validation at the PR level — not just syntax checking but ensuring that IDs, API endpoints, and dependencies are actually valid in the target environment. It catches a surprising number of "silent bombs" that AI agents generate.

npx @opencodereview/cli scan . --sla L1 for registry and schema validation.

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->

- OS Version:

1. Open a cloud repo
2. Ask the agent to delete a file in the repo. Drag and drop the file from the file list to add it to the prompt context.
3. Wait until the agent says the PR is finished.

Title: Language model unavailable - No appropriate guidance to solve that issue

Description:

Type: <b>Bug</b>

I experienced the same “Language model unavailable” problem in VS Code with GitHub Copilot Chat.
After extensive troubleshooting (reboot, Windows update, VS Code update, extension reinstall, network checks), nothing changed.

Root cause identified:  
I was still in my free Copilot Pro trial.
The trial did not fully provision my model entitlements, so the model list returned empty and VS Code displayed “Language model unavailable”.

The moment I switched from the trial to a paid Copilot Pro subscription, the issue disappeared instantly.
Copilot Chat started working again and the model picker populated correctly.

Conclusion:  
This issue can be caused by incomplete or inconsistent model entitlements during the free trial period.
Upgrading to a paid subscription forces a full entitlement refresh on GitHub’s backend, which resolves the problem.

Posting this here so others can avoid unnecessary troubleshooting.

Extension version: 0.43.0
VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z)
OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
Modes:

Title: language model unavailable

Description:

Type: <b>Bug</b>

Type: Bug

Bug Report: GitHub Copilot Chat – “Language model unavailable”

Title:
GitHub Copilot Chat: “Language model unavailable” despite successful GitHub sign‑in

Issue Summary:

Copilot Chat consistently displays “Language model unavailable” even though I am signed into GitHub, the Copilot extensions are installed, and the chat UI loads normally. The authentication subsystem in VS Code appears to be failing to initialize, preventing Copilot from accessing the language model.

Environment:

VS Code Version: (insert your version)

OS: Windows 11

Extensions Installed:

GitHub Copilot

GitHub Copilot Chat

GitHub Account: Successfully signed in (visible under “Manage Accounts”)

Network: Stable, no VPN, no proxy

Copilot Subscription: Active

Expected Behavior:

“Accounts: Show All Sessions” should display:

github.com – Git operations

github.com – Copilot

Copilot Chat should load the model and respond to prompts.

Actual Behavior:

Copilot Chat opens but displays “Language model unavailable.”

“Accounts: Show All Sessions” shows no sessions, only the GitHub account picker.

Authentication provider does not initialize.

Copilot Cloud Agent panel appears, but the model never connects.

Reinstalling extensions, clearing storage, and signing in/out does not resolve the issue.

Steps to Reproduce:

Install VS Code

Install GitHub Copilot + GitHub Copilot Chat

Sign in to GitHub

Open Copilot Chat

Attempt to run any prompt

Observe “Language model unavailable”

Run Accounts: Show All Sessions → no sessions appear

Troubleshooting Already Attempted

Reloaded VS Code window

Disabled/re‑enabled Copilot extensions

Fully uninstalled and reinstalled Copilot extensions

Cleared:

%APPDATA%\Code\User\globalStorage

%APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage

Signed out/in of GitHub multiple times

Verified GitHub CLI authentication (gh auth status)

Restarted VS Code and Windows

Verified network access to api.githubcopilot.com

Confirmed no firewall/VPN interference

Issue persists after all steps.

Additional Notes:

This appears to be a failure of the VS Code authentication provider, not the Copilot extension itself. The UI recognizes my GitHub identity, but no authentication sessions are created, which prevents Copilot Chat from accessing the model.

VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z)
OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200

Title: Language Model

Description:

Type: <b>Bug</b>

Bug Report: GitHub Copilot Chat – “Language model unavailable”

Title:
GitHub Copilot Chat: “Language model unavailable” despite successful GitHub sign‑in

Issue Summary:

Copilot Chat consistently displays “Language model unavailable” even though I am signed into GitHub, the Copilot extensions are installed, and the chat UI loads normally. The authentication subsystem in VS Code appears to be failing to initialize, preventing Copilot from accessing the language model.

Environment:

VS Code Version: (insert your version)

OS: Windows 11

Extensions Installed:

GitHub Copilot

GitHub Copilot Chat

GitHub Account: Successfully signed in (visible under “Manage Accounts”)

Network: Stable, no VPN, no proxy

Copilot Subscription: Active

Expected Behavior:

“Accounts: Show All Sessions” should display:

github.com – Git operations

github.com – Copilot

Copilot Chat should load the model and respond to prompts.

Actual Behavior:

Copilot Chat opens but displays “Language model unavailable.”

“Accounts: Show All Sessions” shows no sessions, only the GitHub account picker.

Authentication provider does not initialize.

Copilot Cloud Agent panel appears, but the model never connects.

Reinstalling extensions, clearing storage, and signing in/out does not resolve the issue.

Steps to Reproduce:

Install VS Code

Install GitHub Copilot + GitHub Copilot Chat

Sign in to GitHub

Open Copilot Chat

Attempt to run any prompt

Observe “Language model unavailable”

Run Accounts: Show All Sessions → no sessions appear

Troubleshooting Already Attempted

Reloaded VS Code window

Disabled/re‑enabled Copilot extensions

Fully uninstalled and reinstalled Copilot extensions

Cleared:

%APPDATA%\Code\User\globalStorage

%APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage

Signed out/in of GitHub multiple times

Verified GitHub CLI authentication (gh auth status)

Restarted VS Code and Windows

Verified network access to api.githubcopilot.com

Confirmed no firewall/VPN interference

Issue persists after all steps.


Additional Notes:

This appears to be a failure of the VS Code authentication provider, not the Copilot extension itself. The UI recognizes my GitHub identity, but no authentication sessions are created, which prevents Copilot Chat from accessing the model.

Extension version: 0.43.0
VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z)
OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200

Title: ChatSessionStore: "Invalid string length" when persisting large Copilot Chat sessions

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No

- VS Code Version: 1.116.0-insider
- Commit: 5272631ea6da49bc305e7264cbb39190604d9f40
- OS Version: Linux x64 6.17.0-19-generic (Ubuntu 24.04)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open VS Code Insiders and use GitHub Copilot Chat in a normal workspace.
2. Continue a long multi-turn Copilot Chat session with a lot of accumulated context, tool output, and large pasted or generated content.
3. After the session becomes large enough, VS Code shows this notification:

   `Error serializing chat session for storage. The session will be lost if the window is closed. Please report this issue to the VS Code team.`

4. The error includes this stack trace:

```text
RangeError: Invalid string length
    at JSON.stringify (<anonymous>)
    at VQ.write (vscode-file://vscode-app/usr/share/code-insiders/resources/app/out/vs/workbench/workbench.desktop.main.js:2996:644)
    at foe.writeSession (vscode-file://vscode-app/usr/share/code-insiders/resources/app/out/vs/workbench/workbench.desktop.main.js:2998:11766)
    at vscode-file://vscode-app/usr/share/code-insiders/resources/app/out/vs/workbench/workbench.desktop.main.js:2998:8727
    at Array.map (<anonymous>)
    at vscode-file://vscode-app/usr/share/code-insiders/resources/app/out/vs/workbench/workbench.desktop.main.js:2998:8715
    at vscode-file://vscode-app/usr/share/code-insiders/resources/app/out/vs/workbench/workbench.desktop.main.js:406:73795
```

Expected Result:

- The Copilot Chat session should be persisted successfully.
- If the session is too large, VS Code should handle it gracefully, for example by truncating, pruning, chunking, or compressing persisted data instead of failing with `RangeError: Invalid string length`.
- Closing or reloading the window should not risk losing the entire session.

Actual Result:

- Chat session persistence fails.
- VS Code warns that the session will be lost if the window is closed.
- The session appears to exceed what the current chat-session storage path can serialize safely.

Additional Notes:

- I checked the local VS Code runtime logs and found repeated renderer-side errors from `ChatSessionStore`:

```text
2026-04-14 17:01:45.632 [error] ChatSessionStore: Error writing chat session Invalid string length
2026-04-14 17:06:45.965 [error] ChatSessionStore: Error writing chat session Invalid string length
```

- I also checked the local persisted session data after the error:
  - The chat session storage file was 629495671 bytes.
  - The Copilot transcript file was 35298820 bytes.
  - The chat session storage file had 1042 lines.
  - The transcript file had 46386 lines.

- This seems consistent with a large-session serialization failure in chat session persistence rather than a normal model-response failure.

- Since GitHub Copilot Chat is required to reproduce this, testing with all extensions disabled is not practical for this specific scenario.

- Attached files:
  - `environment.txt`
  - `error-stack.txt`
  - `renderer-chat-session-errors.log`
  - `renderer-chat-session-context.log`
  - `main-extension-host-events.log`
  - `session-size-summary.txt`

- If this issue belongs in another repository, please transfer it as appropriate.

[vscode-copilot-chat-issue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26704495/vscode-copilot-chat-issue.zip)

Comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! We figured it's covering the same as another one we already have. Thus, we closed this one as a duplicate. You can search for [similar existing issues](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+). See also our [issue reporting guidelines](https://aka.ms/vscodeissuereporting).

Happy Coding!

Comments:
idem
Open the Output panel (usually at the bottom, or use Ctrl + Shift + U).

On the far right of the Output window, click the dropdown menu that currently says "Tasks" or "Terminal".

Select GitHub Copilot Chat to see general extension logs. If you see something like:
 "[error] Error: Failed to fetch models... Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused. Please upgrade your account or revert to Copilot Free..". 

As of April 13, 2026 (yesterday), GitHub officially paused all Copilot Pro trials, including existing ones(if yours is part). This isn't a bug on your machine or a local VS Code error; it’s a deliberate service-wide block.

GitHub issued a changelog update stating that due to widespread abuse of the free trial system, they have suspended access for all trial users while they "strengthen protection mechanisms."
`2026-04-14 11:32:49.304 [info] Fetched model metadata in 142ms a2156ae8-d9a8-4c9b-bd21-e316546733b8

2026-04-14 11:32:49.305 [error] Error: Failed to fetch models (a2156ae8-d9a8-4c9b-bd21-e316546733b8): Copilot Pro trials have been temporarily paused. Please upgrade your account or revert to Copilot Free.`

* https://statusgator.com/services/github/copilot
* https://www.reddit.com/r/GithubCopilot/search/?q=Language+Model+Unavailable&type=posts&sort=new

Title: MacOS Quick Look extension for code files

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
## Problem

macOS Quick Look (Space bar preview in Finder) has no syntax highlighting for code files. Third-party plugins exist but require manual theme configuration that never accurately matches VS Code's colors — because they use separate tokenization engines with different language grammars.

## Proposal

Ship an optional macOS Quick Look Preview Extension as part of VS Code's installation. Since VS Code already bundles:

- TextMate grammars for 100+ languages (vscode-textmate)
- The full token color theming engine
- The user's active theme and its exact token colors

...a Quick Look extension could reuse all of this directly, producing previews that are pixel-accurate to what the user sees in the editor. No separate configuration, no theme mismatch — it would just work, automatically reflecting whatever theme the user has active in VS Code.

## Expected behavior

- Install VS Code → Quick Look for code files works automatically
- Previews match the user's active VS Code theme exactly
- Switching themes in VS Code updates Quick Look previews

## What it is not

This is not a change to the editor itself. It is a native macOS system extension, packaged and installed alongside VS Code, that reads VS Code's existing configuration.

## Prior art

- The macOS Quick Look Preview Extension API (QLPreviewProvider) natively supports returning HTML — VS Code's renderer could generate this
- vscode-textmate already produces tokenized output that maps directly to theme colors
- Several community tools attempt this but fail to match VS Code's colors due to using different grammar engines

Comments:
<!-- 6d457af9-96bd-47a8-a0e8-ecf120dfffc1 -->
This feature request is now a candidate for our backlog. The community has 60 days to [upvote](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Issues-Triaging#up-voting-a-feature-request) the issue. If it receives 20 upvotes we will move it to our backlog. If not, we will close it. To learn more about how we handle feature requests, please see our [documentation](https://aka.ms/vscode-issue-lifecycle).

Happy Coding!

Title: read_file tool regression: removing line count metadata causes Anthropic models to silently stop reading files mid-way

Description:
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.43.0 (**EDITED** - originally reported as 0.38.2 - regression introduced between 0.32.x and 0.38.x)
- VS Code Version: 1.115.0 (**EDITED** - originally reported as 1.110.x  - worked correctly in 1.108.x)
- OS Version: Linux (RHEL 9, x86_64) - but issue is OS-independent
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): agent mode
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude 4.6 Sonnet, Claude 4.5 Sonnet, Claude 4.6 Opus 
- Logs: transcript evidence below

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open a Markdown or text file longer than 200 lines where the content around line 200 ends on a natural-looking boundary (e.g., a complete bullet point, the end of a section). A skill/documentation file works well.
2. In Agent mode, ask the model to read the file and summarize it. The model reads lines 1-200, receives raw text with no metadata indicating total file length, and reports a "complete" summary - silently losing everything after line 200. The model usually chooses 200 as the batch size; in a very rare case it chooses 100, it seems to notice the output ending abruptly and runs more `read_file` batches to read it fully.
3. Ask follow-up questions about content that exists only in lines 200+. The model will confidently say that content doesn't exist.
4. Only when explicitly challenged ("Are you sure you have the whole file?") does the model read further.

### What changed
Commit [microsoft/vscode-copilot-chat@7dc4b8e](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot-chat/commit/7dc4b8e) ("tools: avoid putting fences into file reads by default", 2026-02-06) changed the read_file tool output in two ways:

- Removed code fences (triple backticks wrapping file content)
- Removed the metadata header line that previously read: 
```
File: `path/to/file`. Lines 1 to 200 (430 lines total):
```

Old output (working, pre-1.108):

```
File: `skills/my-great-skill/SKILL.md`. Lines 1 to 200 (430 lines total):
[file content here]
```

New output (1.110+):

```
[file content here]
```

The `(430 lines total)` metadata was the **only signal** the model had that a partial read occurred. Without it, and with the content ending on a syntactically complete line, the model has no way to distinguish "I read part of the file" from "I read the whole file."

The model confirmed this in a conversation:
> *"The current `read_file` tool does not return total line count metadata. The response is just the raw file content for the requested range. If previous versions returned something like `{"content": "...", "totalLines": 430}`, that made the batching loop reliable and automatic. Without that metadata, the agent has to over-estimate or risk stopping early — which is exactly what happened here. That's a real regression in the tooling, not a model reasoning failure."*

The model was asked why it read 200 lines and then believed the file ended at line 200. Its verbatim response:
> Honestly, 200 was an arbitrary round number — a rough heuristic guess. There was no specific reason to choose 200 over 100 or 300. In hindsight, the better approach would have been to check the file length first (e.g., `wc -l`) and then read the full file in one shot, rather than guessing. Or at minimum, check whether the response was cut off at the end and read more if needed. The file happened to end right at line 200, so it worked out — but it was luck, not reason.

### Requested fix

Please, either always include the header (`N lines total`) in the tool result, or make `readFileCodeFences` a public user-facing setting.


Comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.115.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

Happy Coding!
I upgraded to VSCode 1.115.0 (and Copilot extension 0.43.0). Behaves the same. Here's the conversation (the skill file has 400+ lines), only changed the path to the file:

```
User: Hey, please read the my-great-skill/SKILL.md -> we will write a skill together.

GitHub Copilot: Read skill [my-great-skill](file:///[path to the file]/my-great-skill/SKILL.md?vscodeLinkType%3Dskill#1-1), lines 1 to 200

I've read the skill guide. Ready to write a skill together.

What topic should the new skill cover? And do you have a rough idea of what scenarios/use cases it should address?
```

receive the following error:

temporarily paused."

      - Android Studio
      - Visual Studio Code
      - Visual Studio
   - Network tested:
      - WiFi
      - Mobile hotspot (4G)
   - Already tried:
      - Logging out and logging back in
      - Reinstalling Copilot extensions
      - Restarting IDEs
      - Switching networks

It appears that my Copilot trial may have been temporarily paused or
restricted on the server side.

Could you please check my account status and help resolve this issue?

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
NChienMS

## Analysis

The error occurs in the Agents code review service when `getCodeReviewVersion` tries to call `toString` on an undefined value. The deminified stack shows:

```
at (anonymous) (codeReviewService.ts:66:34)
at (anonymous) (codeReviewService.ts:66:3)
at getCodeReviewVersion (codeReview.contributions.ts:150:0)
at (anonymous) (autorunImpl.ts:110:10)
at (anonymous) (lazyObservableValue.ts:21:9)
at (anonymous) (autorunImpl.ts:103:11)
```

This is an Agents-specific regression in the code review feature, likely caused by a nullable value in the code review service not being guarded before calling `toString()`.

## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'toString')` occurs when opening a session in the Agents app that contains file changes where `modifiedUri` is `undefined` (e.g., deleted files represented as `IChatSessionFileChange2`). The `getCodeReviewVersion()` function calls `file.currentUri.toString()` but `currentUri` can be `undefined` when the v2 type guard (`isIChatSessionFileChange2`) fails — most notably after session cache deserialization strips the `uri` field.

### Culprit Commit

No single commit in the regression range (`0271b4d2...1ec46de1`) directly modifies the crash-site files. This is a **latent bug** in the v1/v2 file-change handling code that was exposed by a data-flow change in the range — likely a commit in `extensions/copilot/` that changed how session file changes are produced, causing `IChatSessionFileChange2` objects (with optional `modifiedUri`) to appear where only v1 objects were seen before.

Most likely triggers from the range:
- `c7fd9716` (`@DonJayamanne`) — refactored `clearWorkspaceChanges` handling
- `f6d4cad1` (`@lszomoru`) — changed `git add -A` → `git add` for GVFS compatibility

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User clicks session
    participant SessMgmt as sessionsManagementService
    participant Autorun as codeReview.contributions autorun
    participant Extract as getCodeReviewFilesFromSessionChanges
    participant Version as getCodeReviewVersion

    User->>SessMgmt: openSession() → setActiveSession()
    SessMgmt->>Autorun: activeSession observable triggers
    Autorun->>Autorun: reads activeSession.changes
    Autorun->>Extract: getCodeReviewFilesFromSessionChanges(changes)
    Note over Extract: isIChatSessionFileChange2 returns false<br/>(uri field missing after deserialization)<br/>⚠️ Falls to v1 path: currentUri = change.modifiedUri
    Note over Extract: modifiedUri is undefined (deleted file)<br/>→ currentUri = undefined
    Extract->>Version: getCodeReviewVersion(files)
    Note over Version: 💥 file.currentUri.toString()<br/>Cannot read properties of undefined
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/codeReview/browser/codeReviewService.ts:66` | **Crash site** — `getCodeReviewVersion()` calls `file.currentUri.toString()` without null guard |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/codeReview/browser/codeReviewService.ts:48-61` | **Root cause** — `getCodeReviewFilesFromSessionChanges()` v1 path uses `change.modifiedUri` directly without fallback |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/codeReview/browser/codeReview.contributions.ts:149-150` | **Trigger** — autorun reads changes and calls both functions |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentSessionsModel.ts:882-887` | **Contributing** — cache deserialization only handles v1, strips `uri` field from v2 changes |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts:958-959` | **Contributing** — `_extractChanges()` unsafely casts any changes array to `IChatSessionFileChange[]` |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatSessionsService.ts:471-474` | **Type guard** — `isIChatSessionFileChange2` requires `uri instanceof URI`, fails on deserialized data |

### Repro Steps

1. Open the VS Code Agents app (Insiders 1.116.0+)
2. Create a session that produces file changes including a **deleted file** (so `modifiedUri` is `undefined` in the v2 change object)
3. Close and reopen the app (session gets cached and deserialized)
4. Click on the session in the sessions list to open it
5. The code review autorun triggers → `getCodeReviewVersion()` crashes

The error may also occur without cache if the session provider produces v2 changes where `uri` is not a proper `URI` instance.

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/codeReview/browser/codeReviewService.ts` → `getCodeReviewFilesFromSessionChanges()`

**Approach**: Add a guard in the v1 fallback path to handle `modifiedUri` being `undefined`. The simplest fix mirrors the v2 path's fallback pattern:

```typescript
// In getCodeReviewFilesFromSessionChanges, the else branch (v1 path) should guard:
// Before: currentUri: change.modifiedUri,
// After:  currentUri: change.modifiedUri ?? (change as any).uri,
// Or filter out entries with undefined currentUri
```

**Secondary fix** (recommended): Update `AgentSessionsCache.loadCachedSessions()` in `agentSessionsModel.ts:882-887` to properly handle `IChatSessionFileChange2` during deserialization — preserve the `uri` field and use `isIChatSessionFileChange2` to apply the correct revive logic.

**Tertiary fix**: Update `_extractChanges()` in `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts:958-959` to avoid the unsafe cast — use `isIChatSessionFileChange2` to properly distinguish v1 and v2 changes.

### Recommended Owner

`@bryanchen-d` — already assigned to this issue. `@benvillalobos` is the `chat-review` area owner and may want to review the fix given it touches the code review service.

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24373578551, workflow_id: errors-agents-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24373578551 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'isLoading')` occurs in `ChangesButtonBarWidget._getButtonConfiguration()` when the `reviewState` parameter is `undefined`. The `reviewState` value is captured from a `derivedOpts` observable (`reviewStateObs`) inside an autorun closure. Although the observable's computation function always returns an object, the VS Code observable system's `Derived._recompute()` silently catches computation errors via `onBugIndicatingError` — if the computation throws (e.g., a nested service call fails), the internal `_value` remains `undefined` (its initial state), and this `undefined` propagates through the closure to the button config callback. A subsequent menu change event (delivered via `DebounceEmitter.resume()`) triggers the callback, causing the crash.

### Culprit Commit

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Commit | [`a9d50bd7`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/a9d50bd72636ba403a7ee65204d27b03224ef411) |
| Author | `@lszomoru` |
| PR | #308781 |
| Message | Sessions - fix title menu context when switching between views |
| Why | This is the only commit in the regression range (`0271b4d2...1ec46de1`) that modifies `changesView.ts`. It moves the "Title actions" autorun from the `ChangesViewPane` constructor to `onVisible()`, changing the lifecycle of `renderDisposables`. This alters when `ChangesButtonBarWidget` is created/destroyed and when `updateActions()` fires, exposing a latent timing issue where `reviewStateObs` computation can fail during view initialization. |

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant V as ChangesViewPane.onVisible()
    participant W as ChangesButtonBarWidget
    participant D as reviewStateObs (derivedOpts)
    participant M as MenuWorkbenchButtonBar
    participant E as DebounceEmitter

    V->>V: renderDisposables.clear()
    V->>V: Title actions autorun → updateActions()
    V->>W: createInstance(ChangesButtonBarWidget)
    W->>D: reviewStateObs.read(reader)
    Note over D: ⚠️ If computation throws<br/>(e.g. service not ready),<br/>error caught by onBugIndicatingError,<br/>_value stays undefined
    D-->>W: returns undefined (or valid object)
    W->>M: new MenuWorkbenchButtonBar(..., buttonConfigProvider closure)
    M->>M: menu.onDidChange(update) + update()
    Note over E: Menu change queued<br/>(debounced)
    E->>M: resume() → fire() → update()
    M->>W: buttonConfigProvider(action)
    W->>W: _getButtonConfiguration(action, outgoingChanges, undefined)
    Note over W: 💥 reviewState.isLoading<br/>TypeError: Cannot read properties<br/>of undefined
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/changesView.ts` | Crash site (`_getButtonConfiguration` L173) and root cause (`reviewStateObs` derivation L104-139, autorun closure L141-159) |
| `src/vs/platform/actions/browser/buttonbar.ts` | `MenuWorkbenchButtonBar` — calls `buttonConfigProvider` during `update()` (L120, L238) |
| `src/vs/base/common/observableInternal/observables/derivedImpl.ts` | `Derived._recompute()` silently catches computation errors, leaving `_value` as `undefined` (L195-228) |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product) with a session active
2. Switch between views or trigger the Changes view to re-render (causing `onVisible()` → `renderDisposables.clear()` cycle)
3. The error is non-deterministic — it occurs when the `reviewStateObs` derived observable's computation fails during initialization (depends on service readiness)
4. The `RUN_SESSION_CODE_REVIEW_ACTION_ID` action must be present in the `ChatEditingSessionChangesToolbar` menu for the code path to reach line 173

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/changesView.ts` → `_getButtonConfiguration()` (line 172)

**Approach**: Add a defensive null guard on `reviewState` before accessing `.isLoading`. This handles the case where `reviewStateObs` returns `undefined` due to a silently failed computation:

```typescript
if (action.id === RUN_SESSION_CODE_REVIEW_ACTION_ID) {
    if (!reviewState) {
        return { showIcon: true, showLabel: false, isSecondary: true };
    }
    // existing logic...
}
```

Additionally, the autorun at line 141 should provide a fallback value when reading `reviewStateObs`:

```typescript
const reviewState = reviewStateObs.read(reader) ?? { isLoading: false, commentCount: undefined };
```

### Recommended Owner

`@lszomoru` — culprit commit author and primary contributor to the Sessions changes view code in this regression range

## Analysis

This is a `popular`-type listener leak originating from `chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts` (toolbar/menu service path). The deminified stack shows:

```
at (anonymous) (event.ts:1062:14) — LeakageMonitor.create
at (anonymous) (event.ts:1262:33) — Emitter._event
at Object.u (menuService.ts:437:0) — onWillAddFirstListener
at (anonymous) (event.ts:1271:19) — Emitter._event
at new mn (toolbar.ts:377:0) — WorkbenchToolBar constructor
at (anonymous) (instantiationService.ts) — _createInstance/createInstance
at new YFe (chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts:448:0)
at hU.createToolInvocationSubPart (chatToolInvocationPart.ts:199:36)
at (anonymous) (chatToolInvocationPart.ts:123:37)
at hU render (chatToolInvocationPart.ts:167:2)
```

The leak occurs through toolbar menu service subscriptions created when rendering terminal tool progress parts in chat. A similar leak in this component was previously fixed in #304828 (v1.112.0), but this is a different leak path going through the toolbar/menuService subscription chain.

**Note**: Both bucket IDs (`9ab4bed4` and `7ebc94dc`) represent different emitters leaking via the same component — they share the same `chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts → chatToolInvocationPart.ts` creation path but differ in which specific emitter triggers the leak warning.
