<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

Each `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` creates a `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` instance that subscribes to the shared `PersistedMenuHideState.onDidChange` singleton emitter. In long Agents sessions with many terminal tool calls, the number of concurrent toolbar instances exceeds the global leak warning threshold of **175** (configured in `sessions/browser/workbench.ts`), triggering the "potential listener LEAK detected, popular" error. The "popular" classification means no single call site dominates — many independent toolbar instances each contribute one listener.

### Culprit Commit

This is an **accumulation/architecture issue** rather than a single-commit regression. The error first appeared at low volume in a 1.115.0-insider build (commit `fc23f2d2`, March 31 — 1 user, 4 hits) and grew as the Agents user base expanded through 1.116.0-insider builds (41 users, 165 hits by commit `47add6df`). The per-instance `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` pattern has existed since `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` was introduced. Growth in Agents usage (more users, longer sessions with more terminal tool calls) pushed the concurrent listener count past the threshold.

No single commit in the regression window (`41dd792b...fc6d18c3`) introduced the leak pattern.

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Renderer as ChatListItemRenderer
    participant ThinkingPart as ChatThinkingContentPart
    participant ToolPart as ChatToolInvocationPart
    participant TerminalPart as ChatTerminalToolProgressPart
    participant Toolbar as MenuWorkbenchToolBar
    participant Menu as MenuImpl
    participant HiddenStates as PersistedMenuHideState

    Renderer->>ThinkingPart: finalizeTitleIfDefault()
    Note over ThinkingPart: Materializes lazy tool item<br/>if single tool, no thinking text
    ThinkingPart->>ToolPart: Lazy.value → new ChatToolInvocationPart
    ToolPart->>TerminalPart: createToolInvocationSubPart() → new ChatTerminalToolProgressPart
    TerminalPart->>Toolbar: new MenuWorkbenchToolBar(MENU_CHAT_TERMINAL_TOOL_PROGRESS)
    Toolbar->>Menu: menuService.createMenu() → new MenuImpl
    Menu->>HiddenStates: onDidChange subscription (via onWillAddFirstListener)
    Note over HiddenStates: ⚠️ Root cause:<br/>175+ subscriptions from<br/>concurrent toolbar instances
    HiddenStates-->>Menu: LeakageMonitor.check()
    Note over HiddenStates: 💥 ListenerLeakError thrown:<br/>"potential listener LEAK detected, popular"
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatContentParts/toolInvocationParts/chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts` | **Root cause** — creates a `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` per instance (line 448) |
| `src/vs/platform/actions/browser/toolbar.ts` | Toolbar subscribes to `menu.onDidChange` (line 377) |
| `src/vs/platform/actions/common/menuService.ts` | Menu subscribes to shared `hiddenStates.onDidChange` via `onWillAddFirstListener` (line 437) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatContentParts/toolInvocationParts/chatToolInvocationPart.ts` | Creates `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` as sub-part (line 199) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatContentParts/chatThinkingContentPart.ts` | Lazy materialization of tool parts triggers creation (line 985) |
| `src/vs/sessions/browser/workbench.ts` | Sets global leak threshold to 175 (line 325) |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Agents (sessions workbench)
2. Start a long chat session that makes many terminal tool calls (e.g., ask the agent to perform a complex multi-step task that involves running many shell commands)
3. Continue the session until 175+ terminal tool invocations have been rendered
4. The error will fire when the next `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` is created, as the shared `PersistedMenuHideState.onDidChange` emitter exceeds its listener threshold

The error is more likely in sessions with many sequential tool calls (agentic coding tasks) rather than simple Q&A sessions.

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatContentParts/toolInvocationParts/chatTerminalToolProgressPart.ts` → constructor (line 448)

**Approach**: The toolbar subscription chain (`MenuWorkbenchToolBar` → `MenuImpl` → `PersistedMenuHideState.onDidChange`) creates a per-instance listener on a global singleton emitter. Options to fix:

1. **Lazy toolbar creation** (recommended): Defer creating the `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` until the tool part is visible/interactive. Completed terminal tool invocations don't need an active toolbar with menu subscriptions. Only create the toolbar when the part is in view and the user might interact with it.

2. **Dispose completed toolbars**: After a terminal tool call completes and has been rendered in its final state, dispose the `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` to release the menu subscription. Re-create it on demand if the user interacts.

3. **Share menu instance**: Use a shared `MenuImpl` across multiple toolbar instances for the same `MENU_CHAT_TERMINAL_TOOL_PROGRESS` menu ID, rather than creating a new menu per toolbar. This would require changes in the `MenuWorkbenchToolBar` API.

Note: A previous similar leak in this component was fixed in #304828 (v1.112.0) via a different path.

### Recommended Owner

`@meganrogge` — area owner for `chat-terminal` and terminal tool progress parts (co-owned with `@tyriar`). The fix involves the `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` constructor's toolbar creation pattern.

> Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24374122692/agentic_workflow) · ● 20.2M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-investigate -->

---

Title: ADO semantic search status checks retry indefinitely on auth failure

Description:
When Copilot Chat opens a workspace connected to Azure DevOps repos, it checks the semantic search indexing status for each repo via the `semanticsearchstatus` API. If the user's account doesn't have access to the org (for various reasons), every check returns 401.

The 401 is currently treated as a `generic-error` instead of `not-authorized`, so the status remains `CouldNotCheckIndexStatus`. This causes the status to be re-checked for all repos on subsequent interactions (chat messages, auth changes, availability checks), generating repeated failing API calls that never resolve.

For workspaces with many repos (e.g., 90+), this produces significant unnecessary API traffic.

**Expected behavior:** 401/403 responses should be recognized as auth failures and stop retrying until the auth state changes.

**Suggested fix:**

1. In `adoCodeSearchService.ts`, return `not-authorized` for 401/403 (resolves the existing TODO at line 199):

```typescript
if (result.status === 401 || result.status === 403) {
    return Result.error<RemoteCodeSearchError>({ type: 'not-authorized' });
}
```

2. In `codeSearchRepo.ts`, skip `NotAuthorized` repos in `refreshStatusFromEndpoint` to prevent re-checks:

```typescript
if (!force && (this.status === CodeSearchRepoStatus.Ready || this.status === CodeSearchRepoStatus.NotAuthorized)) {
    return;
}
```

The `fetchRemoteIndexState` method already handles `not-authorized` correctly (line 266-268), so only these two changes are needed.


Comments:
The agent encountered an error and was unable to start working on this issue: Before you can use Copilot cloud agent, you need to pick a "Usage billed to" option in your [Copilot settings](https://github.com/settings/copilot/features). Update your settings, then try again. (Request id: C135:235AD0:31D05E:32407E:69DE4FCC)

Title: Agent mode create_file produces corrupted Go files due to race between empty file creation and gopls workspace/didCreateFiles

Description:
## Description

When Copilot agent mode creates a new `.go` file using the `create_file` tool, the resulting file is frequently corrupted with duplicate `package` declarations, misplaced comments, and large blocks of empty lines. This affects **only Go files** — all other file types (`.ts`, `.js`, `.json`, `.yaml`, `.md`, etc.) work correctly.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/37fb7358-602e-4a66-8958-bc9bcdcf86ee

## Previously reported (closed without fix)

- #296856 — "Go code generation with gopilot very difficult"
- #287987 — "create_file Tool Produces Corrupted Output (Intermittent)"

Both were closed as `info-needed` / `not-planned`, but the root cause has now been identified.

## Root Cause Analysis

This is a **race condition** between VS Code's chat editing session and **gopls** (the Go language server).

### The sequence

1. `_acceptEdits()` in `chatEditingSession.ts` calls `_getOrCreateModifiedFileEntry()` which calls `_createModifiedFileEntry()`.

2. Since the file doesn't exist, `createModelReference()` fails. The catch block runs:
   ```typescript
   // chatEditingSession.ts, _createModifiedFileEntry
   await this._bulkEditService.apply({ edits: [{ newResource: resource }] });
   ```
   This creates the file **empty on disk** (no `contents` property is set on the edit).

3. `WorkingCopyFileService.doCreateFileOrFolder()` creates the empty file via `fileService.createFile()`, then fires `onDidRunWorkingCopyFileOperation`.

4. `MainThreadFileSystemEventService` forwards this to the extension host via `$onDidRunFileOperation()` → `_onDidCreateFile.fire()` → the VS Code API `workspace.onDidCreateFiles` event is emitted.

5. The **vscode-languageclient** library forwards this to **gopls** as the LSP `workspace/didCreateFiles` notification.

6. **gopls** handles `DidCreateFiles` in `server/workspace.go`. It calls `completion.NewFile()` in `golang/completion/newfile.go`, which:
   - Checks if `ui.newGoFileHeader` is enabled (default: `true`)
   - Checks if the file is empty (`len(content) != 0`) — **it IS empty at this point**
   - Generates a `package <name>\n` declaration (and optionally a copyright header)
   - Sends `workspace/applyEdit` back to VS Code to insert this content

7. VS Code applies gopls's edit to the text model — the model now contains `package foo\n`.

8. **Meanwhile**, the chat editing session continues: `_createModifiedFileEntry` finishes, and `_acceptEdits` calls `entry.acceptAgentEdits()` which applies Copilot's content (which also contains `package foo`) via `modifiedModel.pushEditOperations()`.

9. **Both edits land in the same text model** → duplicate `package` line, misplaced comments, and corruption.

### Why only Go?

gopls is one of very few language servers that auto-populate new files via the `workspace/didCreateFiles` notification. This behavior is controlled by the gopls setting `ui.newGoFileHeader` (default: `true`), registered with the file operation filter `**/*.go`.

## Corruption Pattern

The resulting file typically looks like:
```go
package foo
// Package foo does something...
package foo

<many empty lines>

<truncated or missing code>
```

Instead of the expected:
```go
// Package foo does something...
package foo

import (...)

// actual code...
```

## Reproduction Steps

1. Open a Go project in VS Code with the Go extension and Copilot
2. Use agent mode to ask Copilot to create a new `.go` file
3. Observe the file has duplicate `package` declarations and corrupted content
4. Retry — sometimes it works if the file already exists from the previous attempt

## Workaround

Add to VS Code settings:
```json
"gopls": {
    "ui.newGoFileHeader": false
}
```

## Suggested Fix

In `_createModifiedFileEntry` (`chatEditingSession.ts`), when creating a new file for agent edits via `bulkEditService.apply({ edits: [{ newResource: resource }] })`, the `workspace/didCreateFiles` notification should either be suppressed or the file should not be created empty. Options:

1. **Suppress `didCreateFiles`** for files created as part of an agent editing session (e.g., by adding metadata to the file operation event)
2. **Create the file with initial placeholder content** so that gopls's `len(content) != 0` guard rejects its auto-insertion
3. **Defer the `didCreateFiles` notification** until after the agent's initial content has been applied

## Environment

Affects all OS platforms, all VS Code versions with agent mode, when the Go extension with gopls is active.

## Key Code Locations

- `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingSession.ts` — `_createModifiedFileEntry`, line where empty file is created
- `src/vs/workbench/services/workingCopy/common/workingCopyFileService.ts` — `doCreateFileOrFolder`, fires `onDidRunWorkingCopyFileOperation`
- `src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadFileSystemEventService.ts` — forwards event to extension host
- gopls `gopls/internal/server/workspace.go` — `DidCreateFiles` handler
- gopls `gopls/internal/golang/completion/newfile.go` — `NewFile()` auto-inserts package declaration into empty files


Comments:
Hi @zhichli. As a member of the team, you can help us triage this issue by referring to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot/wiki/Copilot-Inbox-Tracker and assigning an owner directly.

Title: YAML support for VSCode tasks

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
The ability to define VSCode tasks in YAML instead of JSON would be super useful, especially since task configurations tend to be repetitive by nature, and we could use YAML anchors in order to DRY away the repetitions. Often, it's only the `command` that needs to change.

In projects with dozens of tasks, this could cut down the size of the tasks file by well over 50%.

It's 2026. YAML is a pretty universal standard by now.

Comments:
Just found this very old issue:

- https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/4851

Hopefully that decision can be revised 10+ years later.

VS Code should already have the ability to parse YAML by now (because of the AI stuff that uses YAML configs).

YAML can simply be a fallback for JSON, so that it has no impact whatsoever on current users. Only if `tasks.json` is not found would `tasks.yml` be parsed (if it exists). People who do not use YAML configs would not be affected at all.

Just parse the YAML and send the resulting object into your current JSON code path (the final result/structure will be the same for both formats).

This could be considered an advanced feature, so no need for Intellisense in `tasks.yml` either.
Side note: Any JSON is a valid YAML file
not JSONC tho
`// comments` are not valid YAML

Title: AI Customization: Unify item discovery through provider abstraction & cut over sessions

Description:
> NOTE: AI Generated work log


## Summary

The AI Customization list widget (`aiCustomizationListWidget.ts`, ~2000 lines) currently maintains two completely separate code paths for item discovery — one for the core `promptsService` and one for external `IExternalCustomizationItemProvider` harnesses. This duplication makes it difficult to evolve the UI, blocks the sessions (Agents) window from using the same provider-based approach as core VS Code, and creates maintenance burden across ~650 lines of sessions-specific `PromptsService` overrides that exist solely because the widget can't consume items through a unified pipeline.

This issue tracks a two-phase refactor:
1. **Phase 1** — Route both code paths through a single provider-shaped abstraction (PR #308626)
2. **Phase 2** — Cut over the sessions window to use an extension-contributed `itemProvider`, deleting the hardcoded `AgenticPromptsService` overrides

---

## Phase 1: Route AI Customization List Through Item Sources

**PR:** #308626 (`josh/refactor-2`)

### Problem

`aiCustomizationListWidget.ts` is a monolith that handles:
- Item model definition (`IAICustomizationListItem`)
- Per-type data fetching from `promptsService` (agents, skills, prompts, hooks, instructions) via `fetchCoreItemsForSection()`
- Per-type data fetching from external providers via `fetchProviderItemsForSection()` / `fetchItemsFromProvider()`
- Storage inference (local/user/extension/plugin/builtin) via `_inferStorageAndGroup()`
- Grouping & filtering via separate `filterItemsForCore()` and `filterItemsForProvider()` methods
- Sync overlays via `fetchLocalSyncableItems()`
- Utility functions (`getFriendlyName()`, `storageToIcon()`, `findPluginUri()`, `isChatExtensionItem()`, `applyBuiltinGroupKeys()`)
- The actual list widget UI rendering

The branching happens in `fetchItemsForSection()` and `filterItems()`:
```typescript
// fetchItemsForSection — two completely different fetch pipelines
if (activeDescriptor.itemProvider && promptType) {
    return this.fetchProviderItemsForSection(activeDescriptor, promptType);
}
return this.fetchCoreItemsForSection(promptType);

// filterItems — two completely different grouping pipelines  
if (activeDescriptor.itemProvider) {
    this.filterItemsForProvider(matchedItems);
} else {
    this.filterItemsForCore(matchedItems);
}
```

### Solution

Introduce a pipeline architecture where both data sources converge on a single shape early:

```
promptsService ──→ PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider ──→ IExternalCustomizationItem[]
                                                                        │
External Provider ────────────────────────────────────────→ IExternalCustomizationItem[]
                                                                        │
                                                                        ▼
                                                  ProviderCustomizationItemSource
                                                  (merges remote + sync, expands hooks)
                                                                        │
                                                                        ▼
                                                    AICustomizationItemNormalizer
                                                  (IExternalCustomizationItem → IAICustomizationListItem)
                                                                        │
                                                                        ▼
                                                    IAICustomizationListItem[] → Widget
                                                    (single groupMatchedItems path)
```

### New Files

| File | Purpose | Lines |
|------|---------|-------|
| `aiCustomizationListItem.ts` | Extracted `IAICustomizationListItem` interface + `IAICustomizationItemSource` contract | 63 |
| `aiCustomizationItemSourceUtils.ts` | `getFriendlyName()` utility (was private widget method) | 23 |
| `aiCustomizationItemNormalizer.ts` | `IExternalCustomizationItem` → `IAICustomizationListItem` mapper. Contains `storageToIcon()`, `resolveSource()`, `inferStorageAndGroup()`, `isChatExtensionItem()`, `findPluginUri()` | 155 |
| `promptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider.ts` | Adapts `IPromptsService` to produce `IExternalCustomizationItem[]`. Contains all per-type fetch logic (agents, skills, instructions, hooks, prompts), builtin grouping, storage source filtering, workspace subpath filtering, instruction file filtering. Implements both `provideChatSessionCustomizations()` (all types) and `provideCustomizations(promptType)` (type-scoped) | 385 |
| `providerCustomizationItemSource.ts` | Unified `IAICustomizationItemSource` — merges remote provider items + local sync items, runs through normalizer. Composes `onDidChange` from provider + sync + promptsService events | 179 |

### Widget Changes (-782 lines)

The widget loses:
- `IAICustomizationListItem` interface (→ `aiCustomizationListItem.ts`)
- `storageToIcon()` (→ `aiCustomizationItemNormalizer.ts`)
- `isChatExtensionItem()` (→ `aiCustomizationItemNormalizer.ts`)
- `applyBuiltinGroupKeys()` (→ `promptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider.ts`)
- `fetchCoreItemsForSection()` (→ `promptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider.ts`)
- `fetchProviderItemsForSection()` (→ `providerCustomizationItemSource.ts`)
- `fetchItemsFromProvider()` (→ `providerCustomizationItemSource.ts`)
- `_expandProviderHookItems()` (→ `providerCustomizationItemSource.ts`)
- `_inferStorageAndGroup()` (→ `aiCustomizationItemNormalizer.ts`)
- `fetchLocalSyncableItems()` (→ `providerCustomizationItemSource.ts`)
- `getFriendlyName()` (→ `aiCustomizationItemSourceUtils.ts`)
- `findPluginUri()` (→ `aiCustomizationItemNormalizer.ts`)
- `filterItemsForCore()` — **deleted entirely**; the provider path's `filterItemsForProvider()` is renamed to `groupMatchedItems()` and used for all harnesses

The widget gains:
- `itemNormalizer: AICustomizationItemNormalizer` field
- `promptsServiceItemProvider: PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider` field
- `getItemSource(descriptor)` factory method — returns a `ProviderCustomizationItemSource` wired to either the harness's `itemProvider` or the built-in `promptsServiceItemProvider`

Direct `promptsService` event subscriptions (`onDidChangeCustomAgents`, `onDidChangeSlashCommands`, `onDidChangeSkills`, `onDidChangeInstructions`) are removed from the widget — they're now owned by `PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider.onDidChange` (which also adds `onDidChangeHooks`).

### Behavioral Analysis

The PR converges the core path onto the provider path's existing behavior. A multi-model council review initially flagged two "regressions" — but detailed analysis confirmed these are **intentional convergence**, not regressions:

**`extensionLabel` (hover tooltips):** The core path's `applyBuiltinGroupKeys` set `extensionLabel: extInfo.displayName || extInfo.id.value` for non-builtin extension items. The provider path **never set `extensionLabel`** — it showed URI-based tooltips instead. The PR converges to the provider path's behavior. This is correct because the provider API (`IExternalCustomizationItem`) has no `extensionLabel` field — the data simply isn't available through that contract.

**`'agents'` group header (hooks tab):** The core path's `filterItemsForCore` defined an explicit `{ groupKey: 'agents', label: "Agents", icon: agentIcon }` group. The provider path's `filterItemsForProvider` **never had this group** — it used the dynamic fallback (`formatDisplayName(key)`, `Codicon.folder`). The PR converges to the provider path's behavior. Agent-embedded hooks with `groupKey: 'agents'` are only emitted by the `PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider` (and only when `!isSessionsWindow`), so this only affects the core VS Code workbench's hooks tab for agent-defined hooks — a narrow edge case.

Both of these are artifacts of the core-only path that's being intentionally retired.

### Known Follow-ups in Phase 1

These are code quality items worth addressing in the PR:

1. **`getItemSource()` creates a new `ProviderCustomizationItemSource` on every call** — the autorun subscription path and `fetchItems` path use different transient instances. Should cache per active harness.
2. **Hook expansion logic duplicated** — near-identical JSONC → `parseHooksFromFile` → expand parsing in `PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider.fetchPromptServiceHooks()` and `ProviderCustomizationItemSource.expandProviderHookItems()`. Should share a utility.
3. **`isChatExtensionItem()` duplicated** — character-identical private method in both `AICustomizationItemNormalizer` and `PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider`. Should extract to `aiCustomizationItemSourceUtils.ts`.

---

## Phase 2: Cut Over Sessions to Provider-Based Approach

### Current Sessions Architecture

The sessions (Agents) window maintains ~650 lines of hardcoded overrides to make the customization list work:

**`AgenticPromptsService` (extends `PromptsService`)** — `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/promptsService.ts` (~330 lines):
| Override | What It Does |
|----------|-------------|
| `createPromptFilesLocator()` | Returns `AgenticPromptFilesLocator` (session-scoped) |
| `findAgentSkills(token)` | Discovers built-in skills from `vs/sessions/skills/{name}/SKILL.md`, appends with lowest priority |
| `listPromptFiles(type, token)` | Includes built-in skill paths, deduplicates by name |
| `listPromptFilesForStorage(type, storage, token)` | Special-cases `BUILTIN_STORAGE` to return built-in skills |
| `getSourceFolders(type)` | Uses `~/.copilot` subdirectories as user-level source folders |

**`AgenticPromptFilesLocator` (extends `PromptFilesLocator`)** — same file (~80 lines):
| Override | What It Does |
|----------|-------------|
| `getWorkspaceFolders()` | Returns only the active session's project folder |
| `getWorkspaceFolder(resource)` | Only matches resources under the active project root |
| `onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders()` | Emits from `activeProjectRoot` observable |
| `getPromptSourceFolders(type)` | Filters out Claude-specific `.claude/` folders |
| `getDefaultSourceFolders(type)` | Filters out Claude defaults |
| `getHookSourceFolders()` | Falls back to `{activeRoot}/.github/hooks/` |

**`SessionsCustomizationHarnessService`** — `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/customizationHarnessService.ts` (33 lines):
- Registers a single CLI harness via `createCliHarnessDescriptor(getCliUserRoots(userHome), [BUILTIN_STORAGE])`
- No `itemProvider`, no `syncProvider` — falls through to the core `promptsService` path

**Built-in skills** — `src/vs/sessions/skills/` (10 bundled SKILL.md files):
- `act-on-feedback`, `commit`, `create-draft-pr`, `create-pr`, `generate-run-commands`, `merge`, `sync`, `sync-upstream`, `update-pr`, `update-skills`
- Each discovered at runtime by scanning the directory, parsing YAML frontmatter, validating name/description

### How After Phase 1 the Cutover Becomes Simple

After Phase 1, **the widget doesn't care where items come from** — it always goes through `getItemSource(descriptor) → fetchItems(promptType) → IAICustomizationListItem[]`. The current sessions flow is:

```
CLI Harness (no itemProvider)
    ↓ getItemSource() sees no itemProvider
    ↓ falls back to PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider
    ↓ reads AgenticPromptsService (which overrides findAgentSkills, listPromptFiles, etc.)
    ↓ returns IExternalCustomizationItem[]
    ↓ normalizer → widget
```

The cutover changes this to:

```
CLI Harness (WITH itemProvider from copilot-cli extension)
    ↓ getItemSource() sees itemProvider
    ↓ calls itemProvider.provideChatSessionCustomizations()
    ↓ extension returns IExternalCustomizationItem[] directly
    ↓ normalizer → widget
```

### What Changes

**Copilot CLI extension (or agent-host) registers its harness with an `itemProvider`:**

The extension already knows what customizations exist (it discovers them for its own runtime). Instead of letting VS Code rediscover them through `AgenticPromptsService`, it supplies them directly via the provider API:

```typescript
const cliProvider: IExternalCustomizationItemProvider = {
    onDidChange: cliCustomizationsChangedEvent,
    async provideChatSessionCustomizations(token) {
        return [
            // Built-in skills — no more runtime SKILL.md discovery
            { uri: builtinSkillUri('commit'), type: 'skill', name: 'commit', 
              description: 'Commit staged or unstaged changes...', groupKey: 'builtin', enabled: true },
            // ... all built-in skills statically listed
            
            // Workspace agents, skills, instructions — already discovered by the CLI runtime
            ...workspaceItems,
            
            // User-level items from ~/.copilot/
            ...userItems,
        ];
    }
};

harnessService.registerExternalHarness({
    ...createCliHarnessDescriptor(cliUserRoots, extras),
    itemProvider: cliProvider,
});
```

**The provider API already supports everything sessions needs:**

| Sessions Capability | Provider API Field | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Built-in skills with "Built-in" grouping | `groupKey: 'builtin'` | ✅ Supported |
| Skill UI integration badges ("Used by Run button") | `badge` + `badgeTooltip` | ✅ Supported |
| Instruction categories (agent/context/on-demand) | `groupKey: 'agent-instructions'` etc. | ✅ Supported |
| Instruction pattern badges ("always added", glob) | `badge` + `badgeTooltip` | ✅ Supported |
| Enabled/disabled state | `enabled: false` | ✅ Supported |
| Per-type fetching | `provideCustomizations(promptType)` | ✅ Supported (type-scoped) |
| Loading/error status | `status` + `statusMessage` | ✅ Supported |
| Hook expansion | Items returned pre-expanded (one per hook) | ✅ Supported |
| Session-scoped workspace filtering | Provider only returns items for active session | ✅ Provider controls scope |

### What Gets Deleted

Once the extension supplies an `itemProvider`, these become dead code **for the UI listing**:

| File / Override | Lines | Why It's Dead |
|---|---|---|
| `AgenticPromptsService.findAgentSkills()` override | ~40 | Extension lists skills directly |
| `AgenticPromptsService.listPromptFiles()` override | ~30 | Extension lists all files directly |
| `AgenticPromptsService.listPromptFilesForStorage()` override | ~15 | Extension uses `groupKey` for storage grouping |
| `AgenticPromptsService.getSourceFolders()` override | ~10 | Extension knows its own source folders |
| `discoverBuiltinSkills()` + `getBuiltinSkills()` | ~60 | Extension ships the skill list statically |
| `AgenticPromptFilesLocator` (most overrides) | ~80 | Extension scopes to session's project |
| Built-in skills YAML parsing loop | ~45 | No runtime discovery needed |

**Total: ~280 lines of discovery/listing code deleted from sessions.**

### What Survives

- **`AgenticPromptsService` base class** — still needed for **runtime prompt resolution** (actually executing prompts, resolving instructions at chat time). The UI listing is decoupled from runtime resolution.
- **`SessionsAICustomizationWorkspaceService`** — `activeProjectRoot`, `commitFiles()`, `deleteFiles()`, `skillUIIntegrations`. These are write operations and session management, not item discovery.
- **`SessionsCustomizationHarnessService`** — still registers the CLI harness, but now with an `itemProvider` attached.

### Migration Path

1. **Phase 1 lands** (PR #308626) — widget uses single pipeline, sessions still works via `PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider` fallback
2. **Copilot CLI extension adds `itemProvider`** — extension registers harness with provider that supplies items directly
3. **Delete sessions discovery overrides** — `findAgentSkills`, `listPromptFiles`, `listPromptFilesForStorage`, `getSourceFolders` overrides, `discoverBuiltinSkills`, most of `AgenticPromptFilesLocator`
4. **Optional: delete `PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider`** — once all harnesses use providers, the adapter becomes dead code (core VS Code workbench would also need a provider)

---

## Validation

- [ ] Phase 1: `npm run compile-check-ts-native` clean
- [ ] Phase 1: `npm run precommit` passes
- [ ] Phase 1: Static harness (core VS Code) — agents, skills, instructions, hooks, prompts all render correctly
- [ ] Phase 1: Provider harness — items from external provider render with correct grouping
- [ ] Phase 1: Sync harness — remote items + local sync checkboxes work
- [ ] Phase 2: Sessions window — built-in skills appear under "Built-in" group
- [ ] Phase 2: Sessions window — workspace items scoped to active session
- [ ] Phase 2: Sessions window — skill UI integration badges ("Used by Run button") display correctly


Comments:
## Phase 1 update — merged as #309666

PR #309666 landed into `main` as `32f144f24ab`. Here's what we shipped and what's left.

### What we did

**Unified interfaces** — All three customization item producers now implement a single `ICustomizationItemProvider` / `ICustomizationItem` interface (common layer in `customizationHarnessService.ts`):

1. **`PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider`** — adapts `IPromptsService` for the default/static harness (core VS Code local path). This stays permanently.
2. **Extension API** (`mainThreadChatAgents2.ts`) — builds an `ICustomizationItemProvider` that proxies `$provideChatSessionCustomizations` back to the extension host (this is how Copilot CLI registers its harness).
3. **AHP remote** (`remoteAgentHostCustomizationHarness.ts`) — implements `ICustomizationItemProvider` for remote agent host sessions.

**Consolidated pipeline** — Four intermediate files merged into one `aiCustomizationItemSource.ts` (~400 lines):
- `IAICustomizationListItem` (view model)
- `IAICustomizationItemSource` (widget contract)
- `AICustomizationItemNormalizer` (`ICustomizationItem` → `IAICustomizationListItem`)
- `ProviderCustomizationItemSource` (merges provider + sync overlays)
- Shared utilities (`expandHookFileItems`, `getFriendlyName`, `isChatExtensionItem`)

**Eliminated lossy round-trip** — `ICustomizationItem` now carries `storage?: PromptsStorage` end-to-end. Previously the adapter stripped storage via `toProviderItems()`, then the normalizer re-inferred it from URI heuristics.

**Removed duck-typed interfaces** — All providers implement `provideChatSessionCustomizations()` (the one method). No more optional `provideCustomizations?` pattern.

**Widget simplified** — `aiCustomizationListWidget.ts` lost ~800 lines of dual-path fetch/normalize/group logic. It now consumes `IAICustomizationItemSource.fetchItems(promptType)` regardless of harness type.

### Known issues from council review (to fix in follow-up)

A 3-model council review (GPT, Opus, Gemini) identified these issues in the landed code:

| # | Severity | Issue | Detail |
|---|----------|-------|--------|
| 1 | 🔴 Blocking | **Double hook expansion → N² entries** | `fetchPromptServiceHooks()` already expands hooks, then `fetchItemsFromProvider()` calls `expandHookFileItems()` again. A file with N hooks → N² entries. Also strips the `storage` field during re-expansion. Only affects the default harness (most common path). (`promptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider.ts:159-179`, `aiCustomizationItemSource.ts:348-352`) |
| 2 | 🟡 | **Missing "Agents" group** | Old `filterItemsForCore` had a dedicated `agents` group with `agentIcon` + localized label for hooks defined in agent files. Now falls through to dynamic group → generic folder icon + lowercase unlocalized "agents". |
| 3 | 🟡 | **Extension display name regression** | Normalizer uses raw extension ID from URI path instead of `extInfo.displayName`. Tooltips show `"github.copilot-chat"` instead of `"GitHub Copilot Chat"`. |
| 4 | 🟡 | **No unit tests** | 745 lines of new pipeline logic without test coverage. The N² bug would have been caught. |
| 5 | 🟡 | **Over-fetching** | `provideChatSessionCustomizations()` fires 5 parallel async calls (all types), then the caller filters to 1 type and discards 4/5. |

### Phase 2 status

Phase 2 (sessions cutover) is now unblocked. The widget no longer cares where items come from — it always routes through `getItemSource(descriptor) → fetchItems(promptType)`. Currently sessions uses the `PromptsServiceCustomizationItemProvider` fallback (no `itemProvider` on the CLI harness descriptor). Once the Copilot CLI extension registers a harness with its own `itemProvider`, ~280 lines of `AgenticPromptsService` discovery overrides become dead code.

### Next steps

1. Fix the council review findings above (especially the N² hook expansion)
2. Sessions cutover — CLI extension supplies `itemProvider`, delete `AgenticPromptsService` listing overrides

Title: `send_to_terminal` progress message repeats first question when accepting all defaults in interactive commands

Description:
## Bug

When an interactive command like `npm init` is run and the user accepts all defaults via the `askQuestions` carousel, every `send_to_terminal` progress message displays the first question's text (e.g. "Package name?") instead of the correct question for each prompt.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a chat session in default approvals mode
2. Ask the agent to run `npm init` in a terminal
3. The agent detects the interactive prompts and shows an `askQuestions` carousel with multiple questions (package name, version, description, entry point, etc.)
4. Accept all defaults by leaving every field empty and submitting the carousel
5. The agent sends each default answer via `send_to_terminal` (pressing Enter for each prompt)

## Expected Behavior

Each `send_to_terminal` progress message should show the corresponding question:

- Pressed `Enter` in terminal (replying to: Package name?)
- Pressed `Enter` in terminal (replying to: version?)
- Pressed `Enter` in terminal (replying to: description?)
- …

## Actual Behavior

Every progress message repeats the first question:

- Pressed `Enter` in terminal (replying to: Package name? (default: @xterm/xterm))
- Pressed `Enter` in terminal (replying to: Package name? (default: @xterm/xterm))
- Pressed `Enter` in terminal (replying to: Package name? (default: @xterm/xterm))
- …

## Cause

`_getQuestionContextForTerminal` matches the sent command text against carousel answer values to find the corresponding question. When all answers are identical (empty string for defaults), the loop iterates questions in order and always matches the first one.

## Fix

Use positional matching: count prior `send_to_terminal` tool invocations after the carousel in the response parts to determine the question index, rather than relying solely on answer-value matching.


Comments:
This doesn't seem like a candidate (can wait 1 week for the next release).
/closedWith 73bf4de738c568b203a7003b4848c07fc45a8be8
Sharing media from merged PR #309598 for verification:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9edfa037-06d5-4f0f-8773-f19ce232c784

Source PR: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/309598

Title: OS toast notification shows wrong terminal command when multiple tool invocations in response

Description:
## Bug

When a chat response contains multiple terminal tool invocations, the OS notification toast shows the command from the **first** terminal invocation (which may have already completed) rather than the one actually **waiting for confirmation**.

### Repro

1. Start a session where the agent runs multiple terminal commands sequentially
2. The first command (e.g. `git status --short`) completes and the agent moves on
3. A later command (e.g. a complex `npx playwright test ...` invocation) requires user confirmation
4. The OS toast notification fires but displays the first command (`git status --short`) instead of the pending one

### Cause

`_getPendingTerminalCommand` in `chatWindowNotifier.ts` iterates over all response parts and returns the first `toolInvocation` with `toolSpecificData.kind === 'terminal'` — it does not check whether that invocation is actually in `WaitingForConfirmation` or `WaitingForPostApproval` state.

### Fix

Filter to only match tool invocations whose state is `WaitingForConfirmation` or `WaitingForPostApproval`, consistent with what `_confirmAllow` already does.

Comments:
From @karthiknadig

Title: Agents app on Linux prevents returning to classic VS Code experience

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->

**Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?:** Yes

**VS Code Version:** latest insiders
**OS Version:** Ubuntu (Linux)

---

### Steps to Reproduce:

1. On Ubuntu Linux, open VS Code Insiders.
2. Use `Chat: Open Agents Application` command.
3. Another "VS Code Insiders" instance opens with the Agents UI.
4. Close out of all "VS Code Insiders" windows.
5. Reopen "VS Code Insiders".

**Expected:** Should return to classic VS Code experience.
**Actual:** Get Agents UI again, and VS Code logo in the title bar does not fix the problem.

Additional observations:
- Agents does not show in Ubuntu's "spotlight search" (as it does on macOS), suggesting it's not treated as a separate app on Linux.
- This prevents easily returning to the standard VS Code environment and is highly disruptive for Linux users.


[View original Slack conversation](https://vscodeteam.slack.com/archives/C09MVCZKLP4/p1776098451267679?thread_ts=1776098451.267679&cid=C09MVCZKLP4)

Comments:
Note that we don't currently expect Linux to support a "spotlight search" experience, since we haven't done the Electron support for "sub apps" on Linux yet. But in the meantime, we should prevent and disruption to VS Code + Agents app usage on Linux.
I can go back to classic VS Code by clicking the VS Code button in the title toolbar.
An idea is to never disable the button even if no workspace is selected to allow to open an empty window.

Title: [Unhandled Error] Cannot resolve canonical URI

Description:
## Error Message
````
Cannot resolve canonical URI
````

## Stack Trace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;at o1.getCanonicalURI ([./src/vs/common/extensionHostManager.ts:420:0](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b/src/vs/common/extensionHostManager.ts#L420))  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;at async pmt._getCanonicalURI ([./src/vs/electron-browser/nativeExtensionService.ts:308:0](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b/src/vs/electron-browser/nativeExtensionService.ts#L308))

<details>
<summary>Raw Stack Trace (minified)</summary>

````
Error: Cannot resolve canonical URI     at o1.getCanonicalURI (out/vs/workbench/workbench.desktop.main.js:4962:64441)     at async Promise.all (index 0)     at async pmt._getCanonicalURI (out/vs/workbench/workbench.desktop.main.js:4978:3491)
````

</details>

## Details
| Property | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Bucket ID | [`44704a8c-b683-12a7-7589-730efee7c7fe`](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/44704a8c-b683-12a7-7589-730efee7c7fe?product=VSCode&commit=41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b) |
| Version | 1.115.0 |
| Commit | [`41dd792b`](https://insiders.vscode.dev/github/microsoft/vscode/tree/41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b) |
| Last Seen | Sun Apr 12 2026 23:57:23 GMT+0000 (Coordinated Universal Time) |
| Platforms | Windows, Mac, Linux |
| Product | VSCode |

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*

Comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error "Cannot resolve canonical URI" is thrown in `extensionHostManager.getCanonicalURI()` when the extension host proxy is `null` — meaning the extension host either failed to start or was disposed. This affects users with remote connections (SSH, WSL, containers, etc.) when any LocalProcess extension host has a null proxy. The error has existed at low levels since v1.110.0 but spiked in v1.115.0 stable due to the wider user base (2,681 hits / 1,395 users), with the per-user hit rate remaining roughly constant (~1.9 hits/user) across versions.

### Culprit Commit

No specific culprit commit was identified. The issue body contained no commit range, and no regression scan comments were posted. The bucket history shows the error existed in v1.110.0-insider (1 hit), v1.114.0 (2 hits), and v1.115.0-insider (7 hits) before spiking in v1.115.0 stable (2,657 hits). The per-user rate is consistent across versions, indicating the spike is proportional to the larger stable user base rather than a new code change. The affected files (`extensionHostManager.ts`, `nativeExtensionService.ts`) had no relevant changes in the v1.115.0 cycle.

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant WS as WorkspaceTrust / Terminal
    participant RAS as RemoteAuthorityResolverService
    participant NES as NativeExtensionService
    participant EHM as ExtensionHostManager

    WS->>RAS: getCanonicalURI(uri)
    RAS->>NES: _getCanonicalURI(authority, uri)
    NES->>NES: _getExtensionHostManagers(LocalProcess)
    NES->>EHM: Promise.all([extHost.getCanonicalURI()])
    EHM->>EHM: await this._proxy
    Note over EHM: ⚠️ Root cause:<br/>proxy is null (extension host<br/>failed to start or was disposed)
    EHM-->>NES: 💥 throw Error("Cannot resolve canonical URI")
    Note over NES: Promise.all propagates<br/>the rejection — even if<br/>other hosts could resolve
    NES-->>RAS: Error propagated
    RAS-->>WS: Unhandled error
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/common/extensionHostManager.ts` (L417-422) | **Crash site** — throws when `this._proxy` resolves to null |
| `src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/electron-browser/nativeExtensionService.ts` (L294-318) | **Error propagation** — `Promise.all` at L308 propagates the throw from any failed extension host |
| `src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/common/lazyCreateExtensionHostManager.ts` (L180-185) | **Alternate crash site** — same error thrown when lazy host was never created |
| `src/vs/platform/remote/electron-browser/remoteAuthorityResolverService.ts` (L45-55) | **Entry point** — `getCanonicalURI` invoked by callers like workspace trust |
| `src/vs/workbench/services/workspaces/common/workspaceTrust.ts` (L202-205) | **Trigger** — calls `getCanonicalURI` during trust evaluation for remote workspace folders |

### Repro Steps

1. Connect VS Code to a remote host (SSH, WSL, or Dev Container)
2. Have an extension host that fails to start (e.g., due to a crashing extension, resource exhaustion, or a timing issue)
3. Trigger a canonical URI resolution — this happens automatically during workspace trust evaluation on startup, or when opening an external terminal on a remote workspace
4. The `_getCanonicalURI` path queries all LocalProcess extension hosts via `Promise.all`; if any one has a null proxy, the error is thrown

This is a race condition / resilience issue: the error occurs when at least one extension host is in a failed state while a canonical URI resolution is attempted.

### Fix Proposal

The core issue is that `extensionHostManager.getCanonicalURI()` **throws** when the proxy is null, even though its return type is `Promise<URI | null>` (null is already a valid return value). The caller in `_getCanonicalURI` already handles null results gracefully (iterates results looking for non-null values). Two fixes should be applied:

**Fix location 1**: `src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/common/extensionHostManager.ts` → `getCanonicalURI()`
**Approach**: Return `null` instead of throwing when `proxy` is null. This aligns with the method's return type `Promise<URI | null>` and with how other methods (e.g., `measure()` at L186-188) handle a null proxy.

**Fix location 2**: `src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/common/lazyCreateExtensionHostManager.ts` → `getCanonicalURI()`
**Approach**: Same change — return `null` instead of throwing when `this._actual` is null.

**Fix location 3 (defensive)**: `src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/electron-browser/nativeExtensionService.ts` → `_getCanonicalURI()`
**Approach**: Wrap each `extHost.getCanonicalURI()` call in a catch that returns `null`, so `Promise.all` doesn't short-circuit on a single failure. This makes the code resilient even if individual extension host managers throw for other reasons.

### Recommended Owner

`@alexdima` — area owner for extension host process, communication protocol, resolvers, and extension activation (`triage/working-areas.md`)

### Deminified Stack (top frames)

The error occurs during observable recomputation in `chatWidget.ts` when accessing `currentModeObs` on an already-disposed object. The disposal is triggered by `languageModelToolsService.ts` cleaning up tool registrations, which cascades through observable updates. This is a disposal race condition — a derived observable reads a property that was nullified during cleanup. 24 users affected across Mac, Windows, Linux.

### Related Bucket

Bucket [`b0ec104e`](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/b0ec104e-da9c-4a58-858c-ebcc65ff3967?product=Agents&commit=47add6dfa59e9d89115b53c74511f03db9575dd0) has the same error message and top frame (`chatWidget.ts:2575`) but a different trigger path (tool registration flush vs disposal). 14+ users on the same build.

## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'currentModeObs')` occurs at `chatWidget.ts:2575` inside a `derived` observable (`scopedTools`) that is created by `getModeRequestOptions()` and passed to the chat service. When MCP tools are unregistered (e.g., due to a workspace folder change removing an MCP server), the observable re-evaluates on an already-disposed `ChatWidget`, where `this.input` returns `undefined` because `inputPartDisposable` has been cleared. This is a use-after-dispose race condition affecting 24+ users across all platforms in the Agents product.

### Culprit Commit

No single culprit commit could be definitively identified. The `scopedTools` derived observable was introduced in PR #301381 (1.112.0, by `@roblourens`) and the autorun that keeps it subscribed in `chatServiceImpl.ts:1227-1239` was part of the same PR. The bug is latent — it surfaces when a `ChatWidget` is disposed while the chat service still holds an active subscription to `scopedTools` via the autorun, and an MCP tool change triggers re-evaluation. Possible triggers within the regression range (`41dd792b..fc6d18c3`) include:

- [`bda49a1b`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/bda49a1b) — Enabled `chat.customizations.providerApi.enabled` by default, potentially activating new MCP tool management code paths
- Multiple sessions/workspace management changes that could alter widget disposal timing relative to MCP server lifecycle

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant WF as WorkspaceFolders
    participant MCP as McpWorkbenchService
    participant Contrib as McpLMToolContribution
    participant ToolSvc as LanguageModelToolsService
    participant ChatSvc as ChatServiceImpl (autorun)
    participant Widget as ChatWidget (disposed)

    WF->>MCP: onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders
    MCP->>MCP: removeWorkspaceService
    MCP->>Contrib: onDidUninstallMcpServer → sync()
    Note over Contrib: collection is null →<br/>tools.forEach(t ⇒ t.store.dispose())
    Contrib->>ToolSvc: _tools.delete(data) [line 434]
    Note over ToolSvc: ⚠️ Observable set change<br/>propagates through dependency graph
    ToolSvc->>ChatSvc: autorun triggered [line 1227]
    ChatSvc->>Widget: scopedTools derived recomputes
    Note over Widget: 💥 this.input is undefined<br/>(inputPartDisposable cleared)<br/>Cannot read 'currentModeObs'
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatWidget.ts` | **Crash site** — `scopedTools` derived at line 2575 accesses `this.input.currentModeObs` without null check |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatService/chatServiceImpl.ts` | **Subscription holder** — autorun at line 1227 keeps `scopedTools` subscribed after widget disposal |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/input/chatSelectedTools.ts` | **Data flow** — `userSelectedTools` derived depends on tool registry |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/tools/languageModelToolsService.ts` | **Data flow** — `_tools.delete()` at line 434 triggers observable propagation |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/mcp/common/mcpLanguageModelToolContribution.ts` | **Trigger** — autorun at line 112 disposes tool stores when MCP server is removed |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Agents with a multi-folder workspace that has MCP servers configured
2. Start a chat session and send a request (this creates `scopedTools` via `getModeRequestOptions()`)
3. While the request is active (or the service still holds the autorun), change workspace folders (e.g., remove a folder that has an MCP server configured)
4. The MCP server uninstall triggers tool disposal, cascading through observables to the `scopedTools` derived on the disposed widget
5. Error is non-deterministic — requires the widget to be disposed (e.g., navigated away from) while the service still holds the autorun subscription

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatWidget.ts` → `getModeRequestOptions()`, the `scopedTools` derived computation

**Approach**: Add a guard clause at the top of the `scopedTools` derived computation (line 2573-2582) to check whether `this.input` is still available before accessing `currentModeObs`. If `this.input` is `undefined` (widget disposed), return `lastToolsSnapshot` immediately. This prevents the crash while preserving the last known tools state:

```typescript
const scopedTools = derived(reader => {
    const input = this.input;          // ← add null check
    if (!input) { return lastToolsSnapshot; }
    const activeSession = this._viewModelObs.read(reader)?.sessionResource;
    const currentModeId = input.currentModeObs.read(reader).id;
    // ... rest unchanged
});
```

Additionally, consider disposing the autorun in `chatServiceImpl.ts:1227` when the widget is disposed, so the `scopedTools` derived is no longer subscribed.

### Recommended Owner

`@roblourens` — author of the `scopedTools` derived observable (PR #301381) and primary maintainer of the chat widget + chat service request flow. The fix is in `chatWidget.ts` which is core chat widget infrastructure.

Title: Custom agents should be allowed to exceed the current cost tier

Description:
Testing #309322

When invoking a custom subagent that has a model specified in its definition, and that model exceeds the current cost tier, it should still be allowed to run.

Currently it will fail with `Requested model '___' exceeds the current model's cost tier`. But since the agent is user defined, it should be able to succeed (at least with user approval).

Comments:
Real-world use case: orchestrator agent deliberately choosing a lower-cost model while routing to higher-cost specialists

We maintain a multi-agent "Dream Team" workflow where a central orchestrator agent (ProductVisionary) manages the lifecycle of a GitHub issue from intake through specification, implementation, review, and CI. The orchestrator reads the issue, classifies the work, and hands off to specialist agents — each configured with the model best suited for their domain:

ProductVisionary (orchestrator): GPT-5.4 — chosen for its reasoning style during product classification and workflow routing, not for cost savings  
SpecificationLead: Claude Opus 4.6 — chosen for deep specification writing  
SecurityEngineer: Claude Opus 4.6 — chosen for thorough security analysis  
TechLead: Claude Sonnet 4.6 — chosen for implementation work  
Other review agents on Sonnet or Haiku tiers as appropriate

The current cost-tier restriction forces us to either:

Move the orchestrator to Opus — losing the model we actually prefer for orchestration work, or  
Downgrade specialist agents to Sonnet — losing the capability we specifically chose for domain-critical tasks like specification writing and security review

Neither option reflects our intent. We're not trying to avoid cost. We're trying to pick the right model for each job. The orchestrator doesn't need Opus-level capability — it reads an issue, makes a routing decision, and verifies that the previous agent committed its work. The specialists do need their configured models for the quality of output they produce.

What we'd like: A way to explicitly opt in to allowing subagents to use higher-cost models than the parent session, preferably through a setting rather than a one-time permission prompt. The key is that the decision should be ours, not a hard block. Something like `chat.subagents.allowHigherCostModels` would fit this use case well.

Environment: VS Code with custom `.agent.md` files using `model:` frontmatter and `handoffs:` for agent-to-agent transitions, plus `runSubagent` for programmatic delegation.

---

Title: Allow more expensive models in subagents (with user approval)

Description:
Testing #309322

Not exceeding the current cost tier is a good precaution -- but in some cases it's useful; e.g. running a subagent with a large context window to scan a large number of files and report a summary.

It might be nice if, when requesting a subagent with a more expensive model, the user can choose to approve or deny (or default to deny in e.g. autopilot mode)

Comments:
### Better suggestion
Make the main request cost the highest premium request factor in the tree of subagents.

### Examples
1. GPT-5.4 Agent (1x) -> Opus 4.6 Subagent (3x) = 3x premium requests
2. GPT-5.4-mini Agent (0.33x) -> Opus 4.6 Subagent (3x) -> GPT-5.4 Subagent (1x) = 3x premium requests

This can be automatic imo then, too, since the user has to explicitly configure this, so the higher cost can be expected.

Title: Pasting image clipboard data into terminal-based LLM input does nothing in Parallels/Windows

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.115.0
- OS Version: Windows 11

Steps to Reproduce:

1. On macOS host, take a screenshot, not to file, just to clipboard
2. In a Windows VM running under Parallels, open VS Code.
3. Open the integrated terminal and start/use Claude Code in the terminal (or codex)
4. Try to paste the copied screenshot into the LLM terminal input.
5. Notice that nothing is pasted and there is no visible feedback.
6. In the same VM/session, paste the same clipboard contents into apps such as MSPaint or VS Code chat/copilot-style extension inputs.
7. Notice that the image pastes successfully in those places.

Expected Result:
Pasting image clipboard contents into the terminal-based Claude Code workflow should either:
- work,
- convert the image into an attachment or file reference,
- or show a clear message that image clipboard paste is not supported.

Actual Result:
Nothing appears to paste into the terminal / Claude Code input, with no clear error or feedback.

Additional Notes:
- This appears specific to terminal-based input.
- The same clipboard image can be pasted successfully into GUI applications like MSPaint and chat-style VS Code extension inputs.
- The environment uses macOS as host and Windows in Parallels, so clipboard format conversion between host and guest may be involved.
- It is unclear whether this is a VS Code terminal issue, a limitation of terminal paste behavior, a Parallels clipboard interoperability issue, or an issue in Claude Code specifically.

Comments:
Thanks for the issue. Bc there are so many variables you mentioned, it would be best if you narrowed this down - can you try pasting in parallels in a terminal outside of vscode in claude and let us know if it reproduces there? closing for now.

- **Trend**: Increasing — 23 users on first bad build, 262 users on latest

### Related

A similar error (same message, different stack path through `chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts`) was filed as #309130 for the Agents product and fixed. This VSCode bucket enters through `chatEditingModifiedFileEntry.ts` instead, suggesting the fix didn't cover this code path.

> Generated by [errors-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24354443170/agentic_workflow) · ● 12.1M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24354443170, workflow_id: errors-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24354443170 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-regression-scan -->
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'getRequests')` occurs when `ChatResponseModel._session` is `undefined` after disposal. A throttled scheduler in `chatEditingModifiedFileEntry.ts` fires up to 1 second after user edits, calling `_notifySessionAction` → `_notifyAction`, which accesses `telemetryInfo.modelId`. This lazy getter calls `responseModel.request`, which does `this.session.getRequests()` — but `session` is `undefined` because `ChatResponseModel.dispose()` cleared `_session`. This is the **same root cause** as #309130 (which entered through `chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts`), and was **already fixed** by PR #309093.

### Culprit Commit

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Commit | [`7c7259ff`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/7c7259ff50bc50689c167874b24fce9b5e698501) |
| Author | `@roblourens` |
| PR | #308638 |
| Message | pref: Break references at disposal to limit the impact of leaks |
| Why | This commit added `this._session = undefined!` in `ChatResponseModel.dispose()` to break GC retention cycles. However, the `_telemetryInfo` object created by `_getTelemetryInfoForModel` holds lazy getters that close over the `responseModel` and access `responseModel.session` after disposal — specifically when the 1-second `_userEditScheduler` fires. |

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User Edit
    participant Scheduler as RunOnceScheduler (1s)
    participant Entry as ModifiedFileEntry
    participant TelInfo as TelemetryInfo (lazy getters)
    participant Response as ChatResponseModel
    participant ChatModel as ChatModel

    User->>Scheduler: User modifies file
    Note over ChatModel: ⚠️ Session disposed
    ChatModel->>Response: dispose()
    Note over Response: _session = undefined!
    Scheduler->>Entry: _notifySessionAction('userModified')
    Entry->>Entry: _notifyAction()
    Entry->>TelInfo: this._telemetryInfo.modelId
    TelInfo->>Response: responseModel.request
    Response->>Response: this.session.getRequests()
    Note over Response: 💥 session is undefined<br/>Cannot read 'getRequests'
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatModel.ts` | Root cause — `ChatResponseModel.dispose()` nullified `_session` (lines 1431-1437 in original, reverted by #309093) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingModifiedFileEntry.ts:101` | Trigger — 1s throttled `_userEditScheduler` fires after disposal |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingModifiedFileEntry.ts:299` | Crash site — `_notifyAction` accesses `this._telemetryInfo.modelId` |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingSession.ts:1035-1055` | Data flow — `_getTelemetryInfoForModel` creates lazy getters that close over `responseModel` |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Insiders (builds `e4f6e114` through `47add6df`, before fix #309093)
2. Start a chat editing session that produces file modifications
3. After the agent finishes editing, manually edit the modified file (to trigger `_userEditScheduler`)
4. Close/dispose the chat session while the 1-second scheduler is pending
5. The scheduler fires after disposal, accessing `responseModel.session` which is now `undefined`

The error is timing-dependent — the session must be disposed during the 1-second window between the user edit and the scheduler firing.

### Fix Proposal

**Already fixed** by PR #309093 ("Don't remove model ref on disposal"), merged Apr 10. The fix reverted `this._session = undefined!` from `ChatResponseModel.dispose()`, keeping the `_session` reference intact after disposal. The PR description notes: _"Too risky, chat model lifecycle is too confusing and there are still some references to do things like check the URI after it's disposed."_

All 5 affected builds in the error bucket predate the fix:
- `e4f6e114` (Apr 9), `0271b4d2` (Apr 9), `1ec46de1` (Apr 9), `a4f51197` (Apr 10), `47add6df` (Apr 10 11:25 UTC)
- Fix merged: Apr 10 20:02 UTC

No error hits appear on builds after the fix. **This issue should self-resolve as users update to newer Insider builds.**

### Recommended Owner

`@roblourens` — culprit commit author who already fixed the issue via #309093. Consider closing this issue as a duplicate of #309130.

*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*

Comments:
## Regression Details

- **Product**: VSCode
- **Regression type**: Spike (11.5x increase)
- **Spike factor**: 11.5x (2 → 23 users)
- **Last release without spike**: [1.116.0-insider](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/871f307b-9f6c-553d-47d9-654f91256c5e?product=VSCode&commit=a4f5119796984330f866b3dd6b2ce0694ff5c814) (2 users)
- **First release with spike**: [1.116.0-insider](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/871f307b-9f6c-553d-47d9-654f91256c5e?product=VSCode&commit=47add6dfa59e9d89115b53c74511f03db9575dd0) (23 users)
- **Commit range**: [a4f51197...47add6df](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/a4f5119796984330f866b3dd6b2ce0694ff5c814...47add6dfa59e9d89115b53c74511f03db9575dd0)

### Deminified Stack

```
at (anonymous) (outputMonitor.ts:492)
at (anonymous) (outputMonitor.ts:492)
at detectsInputRequiredPattern (outputMonitor.ts:300)
at (anonymous) (outputMonitor.ts:156)
```

The `UnresponsiveSampleError` indicates the renderer became unresponsive while executing `detectsInputRequiredPattern` in `outputMonitor.ts`. This function uses `RegExp.test()` which may be experiencing catastrophic backtracking on certain terminal output patterns. The spike from 2→23 users between consecutive insider builds suggests a change in this commit range triggered the issue more frequently.

> Generated by [errors-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24354443170/agentic_workflow) · ● 12.1M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24354443170, workflow_id: errors-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24354443170 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-regression-scan -->
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The `UnresponsiveSampleError` is caused by **catastrophic regex backtracking** (ReDoS) in the `detectsInputRequiredPattern()` function in `outputMonitor.ts`. When the terminal output monitor polls for input-required patterns, the PowerShell prompt detection regex (line 472) exhibits O(n2) backtracking on terminal output containing a `[X]`-style prefix followed by a long sequence of whitespace mixed with non-whitespace characters. This freezes the renderer process, triggering VS Code's unresponsive window detector. The regression was introduced in PR #308587, which removed the `_lastPromptMarker` that previously bounded the input to the regex — the function now evaluates the **entire terminal output buffer** instead of just the content since the last prompt.

### Culprit Commit

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Commit | PR merge (squash) from [`e6fe46e`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/e6fe46e23b64d93da874b5c405b20057ad8b700d) |
| Author | `@meganrogge` |
| PR | #308587 |
| Message | remove LLM calls to detect prompts for input, add fg terminal support |
| Why | Removed `_lastPromptMarker` from `getOutput()` call, so regex patterns now run against the **entire** terminal output buffer instead of just new content since the last prompt. Additionally, `detectsInputRequiredPattern()` is now called in **both** foreground and async modes (previously only async). |

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant SM as _startMonitoring
    participant IH as _handleIdleState
    participant DI as detectsInputRequiredPattern
    participant RE as RegExp.test()

    SM->>IH: state = Idle (line 156)
    IH->>IH: output = execution.getOutput()
    Note over IH: ⚠️ Root cause: getOutput()<br/>returns ENTIRE terminal buffer<br/>(no marker passed since PR #308587)
    IH->>DI: detectsInputRequiredPattern(output)
    DI->>RE: PowerShell regex.test(output)
    Note over RE: 💥 O(n2) backtracking on<br/>regex with overlapping<br/>[^\[]*\s* + \s+$ quantifiers<br/>→ UnresponsiveSampleError
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts` | Crash site (line 492) and root cause (line 251: `getOutput()` without marker; line 472: vulnerable regex) |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Insiders (1.116.0)
2. Start a terminal session via chat agent tools (run a command using `run_in_terminal`)
3. Run a command that produces output containing a bracketed pattern like `[A]` followed by a long string (1000+ characters) with mixed whitespace and non-whitespace — e.g., structured .NET logs, build output with progress indicators, or pager output
4. Wait for the output monitor to enter idle state and call `detectsInputRequiredPattern()`
5. The renderer process freezes for several seconds, triggering `UnresponsiveSampleError`

The issue is more likely with longer terminal sessions that accumulate large output buffers, since the regex now runs against the entire buffer on every idle check.

### Fix Proposal

There are two complementary fixes needed:

**Fix 1 — Bound the regex input** (primary fix):
`outputMonitor.ts` → `_handleIdleState()` and `_waitForIdle()`

Extract only the **last line** (cursor line) from the terminal output before passing it to `detectsInputRequiredPattern()`, since the function's parameter is named `cursorLine` and all regexes use `$` anchoring. For example: `const cursorLine = output.trimEnd().split(/\r?\n/).pop() ?? '';`. This restores the intent of matching only the current prompt line, not the entire buffer.

**Fix 2 — Fix the regex itself** (defense in depth):
`outputMonitor.ts` → `detectsInputRequiredPattern()`, line 472

The PowerShell regex `/\s*(?:\[[^\]]\]\s+[^\[\s][^\[]*\s*)+(?:\(default is\s+"[^"]+"\):)?\s+$/` has overlapping quantifiers between `[^\[]*`, `\s*` (inside the group), and `\s+$` (outside). Fix by making `[^\[]*` exclude whitespace at its end boundary, e.g., use an atomic-style approach or restructure as `/\s*(?:\[[^\]]\]\s+[^\[\s]\S*\s*)+(?:\(default is\s+"[^"]+"\):)?\s+$/` (restrict content to `\S*` non-whitespace after the first char).

Note: A previous ReDoS in this same regex was fixed in PR #279853 (Dec 2025), but the fix was incomplete — it eliminated one source of ambiguity (`[^\[]+` → `[^\[\s][^\[]*`) but left the `[^\[]*\s*` + `\s+$` overlap.

### Recommended Owner

`@meganrogge` — PR #308587 author and terminal agent tools area owner

> Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24355457245/agentic_workflow) · ● 19.8M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

Title: `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications: false` still fires completion notifications for foreground terminals

Description:
Testing #309095 (section 1, Windows).

### What I expected
With `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications: false`, the agent should *not* receive automatic terminal-completion notifications. This is reflected in the fact that the per-call runtime instruction block drops the sentence *"You will be automatically notified on your next turn when it completes"* in this mode.

### What actually happened
Even with `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications: false`, completion notifications **still fired automatically** for foreground sync-mode terminals that had exceeded their initial timeout. Observed with two separate commands during the same session, both of which produced the full `[Terminal <id> notification: command completed with exit code 0 ...]` tail on the next agent turn with no polling:

1. `cargo check` foreground, sync mode, short initial timeout. Notification fired on its own when the build finished.
2. `pwsh -NoProfile -File ./scripts/test-prompt.ps1` foreground, sync mode. Notification fired on its own after the script exited.

So either:
- the setting only gates *input-waiting* notifications (not completion notifications) and the docs / runtime note are misleading, **or**
- completion notifications are not honoring the setting.

### Steps to reproduce (Windows, Nectarine / default agent, agent mode)
1. Set `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications` to `false` in workspace settings.
2. Ask the agent to run a long-running foreground command (e.g. `cargo check` on a non-trivial Rust workspace, or any compile that takes ≥30s) with a short initial timeout so the tool returns while the command is still running.
3. Do **not** poll `get_terminal_output`. Continue the conversation with an unrelated step.
4. Observe: when the foreground command eventually finishes, an automatic `[Terminal ... notification: command completed ...]` chunk is injected on the next agent turn anyway.

### Impact
Low-to-medium: it means the setting's off-state is not fully off — users who turn background notifications off because they find them noisy will still get completion-notification chatter. It also makes it harder to deterministically test the difference between the two setting values.

### Environment
- OS: Windows
- VS Code Insiders, agent mode, `Nectarine` model
- Repo under test: microsoft/python-environment-tools (Rust workspace, long build)

cc @meganrogge


Comments:
/closedWith ece8fd034b6be7b7868ae1992fc6cac4129fe2d0

Title: Should giving input to the terminal automatically include output after some delay

Description:
Testing #309095

There's a long sequence of interaction in https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/309506#issue-4256232160

I wonder if we should automatically include the model terminal output after some brief period of time as part of the "give input" tool, instead of having to go back and do another full agent turn

Comments:
Yes, I also wondered this. However, since "some period of time" is very hard to get right, I think letting it call the tool is better.
After investigating, I realized we should not be instructing the agent to call `get_terminal_output` unless it's a subagent (which lacks notifications). That's needless and wastes tokens as it returns the whole buffer.
I think we could also do something reasonable like include the last 5 lines after a 100ms delay of giving input. That should cover many of the interactive cases like this I think.

Title: Should the model be encouraged to use a small subagent for multi-stage inputs?

Description:
Testing #309095

<img src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f8d60fed-eb2a-4464-892d-dd539b187a2a" alt="Image" width="486" height="574">

It seems like this could be done in a small/fast subagent model rather than the main agent loop

Comments:
formerly, subagents did not have access to `send_to_terminal` or `get_terminal_output`. But we wanted to add those anyway.
The issue with this approach is the subagent does not have access to our input needed/command finished notifications. We'd need async subagents for this.
We can do this though, to reduce roundtrips https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/309509

Title: Request Failed: 401

Description:

Type: <b>Bug</b>

Sorry, your request failed. Please try again.

Copilot Request id: f7f564e4-e35d-4b4f-b432-dd4df4981c1e

GH Request Id: 4F22:531A4:69BF51E:7CE841B:69DD0973
