Title: Test: Show historical sessions in Agent Debug Panel

Description:
Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/309261

- [x] anyOS @anthonykim1

Complexity: 3




[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309262%0A%0A&assignees=vijayupadya)

---
## Agent Debug Log
To open the Agent Debug Logs panel:

- Enable the following setting:

       `github.copilot.chat.agentDebugLog.FileLogging.enabled`


- Create a chat session and send few messages in chat
- Select the ellipsis (...) menu in the Chat view and select **Show Agent Debug Logs**.
- Navigate to home page by clicking on the 'Agent Debug Logs' root item in the navigation link

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8fa56a8d-1b7e-49a4-a87c-6c844f5d6640)

- You should see old sessions listed upto 5 items with 'Show more' button
- Click on 'Show more', ensure it loads 5 more (display capped at 15 items currently)
- Select any of the old sessions
- Click "View Logs" → log list shows events with timestamps, names, details.
- Click "Agent Flow Chart" → flowchart renders with nodes for model turns, tool calls, subagents
- Send a message and verify events appear — verify new events, model turn, tool calls etc appear
- Navigate back to Debug panel home page
- Start a new chat session and send few messages in chat
- Check the new session title appears in the Debug panel, navigate to it and check events show up in logs view

First create issue link was wrong so I removed it

    '}\n' +

    '    if (subtree == nullptr) {\n' +
    '        return;\n' +
    '    } else {\n' +
    '        clearSubtree(subtree->left_);\n' +
    '        clearSubtree(subtree->right_);\n' +
    '        delete subtree;\n' +
    '    }\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'void TreeSet<T>::swap(TreeSet<T>& rhs) {\n' +

    '\n' +

    '    swap(size_, rhs.size_);\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'size_t TreeSet<T>::size() const {\n' +
    '    return size_;\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'void TreeSet<T>::insert(const T& str) {\n' +
    '    if (exists(str)) {\n' +
    '        return;\n' +
    '    } else if (style_ == LEAF){\n' +
    '        insertHelper(str, root_);\n' +
    '        ++size_;\n' +
    '    } else if (style_ == ROOT){\n' +
    '        insertRootHelper(str, root_);\n' +
    '    } else {\n' +
    '        // FIXME: add random implementation here\n' +
    '    }\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    '// This code is based on the code from the CS 70 lesson materials\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'void TreeSet<T>::insertHelper(T str, Node*& subtree) {\n' +
    '    if (subtree == nullptr) {\n' +
    '        subtree = new Node{str};\n' +
    '    } else if (str < subtree->value_) {\n' +
    '        insertHelper(str, subtree->left_);\n' +
    '    } else {\n' +
    '        insertHelper(str, subtree->right_);\n' +
    '    }\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'void TreeSet<T>::insertRootHelper(T str, Node*& subtree) {\n' +
    '    if (subtree == nullptr) {\n' +
    '        subtree = new Node{str};\n' +
    '    } else if (str < subtree->value_) {\n' +
    '        insertRootHelper(str, subtree->left_);\n' +
    '        rotateRight(subtree);\n' +
    '    } else {\n' +
    '        insertRootHelper(str, subtree->right_);\n' +
    '        rotateLeft(subtree);\n' +
    '    }\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'void TreeSet<T>::rotateRight(Node*& top) {\n' +
    '    Node* d = top;\n' +
    '    Node* b = d->left_;\n' +
    '    d->left_ = b->right_;\n' +
    '    b->right_ = d;\n' +
    '    top = b;\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'void TreeSet<T>::rotateLeft(Node*& top) {\n' +
    '    Node* b = top;\n' +
    '    Node* d = b->right_;\n' +
    '    b->right_ = d->left_;\n' +
    '    d->left_ = b;\n' +
    '    top = d;\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'bool TreeSet<T>::exists(const T& str) const {\n' +
    '    return existsHelper(str, root_);\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    '// This code is based on the code from the CS 70 lesson materials\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'bool TreeSet<T>::existsHelper(T str, const Node* subtree) {\n' +
    '    if (subtree == nullptr) {\n' +
    '        return false;\n' +
    '    } else if (str == subtree->value_) {\n' +
    '        return true;\n' +
    '    } else {\n' +
    '        return (existsHelper(str, subtree->left_)\n' +
    '                || existsHelper(str, subtree->right_));\n' +
    '    }\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'int TreeSet<T>::height() const {\n' +
    '    return heightHelper(root_);\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'int TreeSet<T>::heightHelper(const TreeSet<T>::Node* subtree) {\n' +
    '    if (subtree == nullptr) {\n' +
    '        return -1;\n' +
    '    } else {\n' +
    '        return 1\n' +
    '               + std::max(heightHelper(subtree->left_),\n' +
    '                          heightHelper(subtree->right_));\n' +
    '    }\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'double TreeSet<T>::averageDepth() const {\n' +
    '    if (root_ == nullptr) {\n' +
    '        return -1;\n' +
    '    } else {\n' +
    '        return double(totalDepth(root_)) / double(size_);\n' +
    '    }\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'int TreeSet<T>::totalDepth(const Node* subtree) {\n' +
    '    if (subtree == nullptr) {\n' +
    '        return 0;\n' +
    '    } else {\n' +
    '        return totalDepth(subtree->left_) + totalDepth(subtree->right_)\n' +
    '               + size(subtree->left_) + size(subtree->right_);\n' +
    '    }\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'int TreeSet<T>::size(const Node* subtree) {\n' +
    '    if (subtree == nullptr) {\n' +
    '        return 0;\n' +
    '    } else {\n' +
    '        return 1 + size(subtree->left_) + size(subtree->right_);\n' +
    '    }\n' +
    '}\n' +

    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'TreeSet<T>::const_iterator TreeSet<T>::begin() const {\n' +
    '    ConstIter iter = ConstIter{};\n' +
    '    iter.pushToMinimum(root_);\n' +
    '    return iter;\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'TreeSet<T>::const_iterator TreeSet<T>::end() const {\n' +
    '    return ConstIter{};\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'TreeSet<T>::const_iterator TreeSet<T>::lower_bound(\n' +
    '    const T& key) const {\n' +
    '    // We use end() to make an iterator with an empty stack, and then\n' +
    "    // the helper function builds the iterator's final value by pushing\n" +
    '    // nodes onto the stack as it searches for the lower bound.\n' +
    '    const_iterator iter = end();\n' +
    '    return nodeLowerBound(root_, key, iter);\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'TreeSet<T>::const_iterator TreeSet<T>::nodeLowerBound(\n' +
    '    Node* currentNode, const T& key, const_iterator& iter) const {\n' +
    '    // The goal:\n' +
    '    // - Find the first node in the tree whose value is >= key and return\n' +
    '    //   an iterator pointing to it.\n' +
    '    // How it works:\n' +
    '    // - Recall that an iterator is represented by a stack consisting of all\n' +
    '    //   the parent nodes that are greater than (or equal to for the top of the\n' +
    '    //   stack) the node where the iterator currently is.\n' +
    '\n' +
    '    if (currentNode == nullptr) {\n' +
    "        // Return whatever iterator value we've built so far.\n" +
    '        return iter;\n' +
    '    }\n' +
    '\n' +
    '    if (currentNode->value_ < key) {\n' +
    '        // Current node is less than key, but we need a value >= key.\n' +
    '        // Go right to find a larger candidate.\n' +
    '        return nodeLowerBound(currentNode->right_, key, iter);\n' +
    '    } else {\n' +
    "        // Current node is >= key, so it's a potential lower bound.\n" +
    "        // Push it onto the stack and go left to see if there's a smaller\n" +
    '        // candidate, since we want the least such node.\n' +
    '        iter.stack_.push(currentNode);\n' +
    '        return nodeLowerBound(currentNode->left_, key, iter);\n' +
    '    }\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'const T& TreeSet<T>::ConstIter::operator*() const {\n' +
    '    return stack_.top()->value_;\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'TreeSet<T>::ConstIter& TreeSet<T>::ConstIter::operator++() {\n' +
    '    Node* oldTop = stack_.top();\n' +
    '    stack_.pop();\n' +
    '    pushToMinimum(oldTop->right_);\n' +
    '    return (*this);\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'void TreeSet<T>::ConstIter::pushToMinimum(Node* subtree) {\n' +
    '    while (subtree != nullptr) {\n' +
    '        stack_.push(subtree);\n' +
    '        subtree = subtree->left_;\n' +
    '    }\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'bool TreeSet<T>::ConstIter::operator==(\n' +
    '    const TreeSet<T>::ConstIter& rhs) const {\n' +
    '    return (stack_ == rhs.stack_);\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'bool TreeSet<T>::ConstIter::operator!=(\n' +
    '    const TreeSet<T>::ConstIter& rhs) const {\n' +
    '    return !(stack_ == rhs.stack_);\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +

    'TreeSet<T>::const_iterator TreeSet<T>::lower_bound(\n' +
    '    const T& key) const {\n' +
    '    // We use end() to make an iterator with an empty stack, and then\n' +
    "    // the helper function builds the iterator's final value by pushing\n" +
    '    // nodes onto the stack as it searches for the lower bound.\n' +
    '    const_iterator iter = end();\n' +
    '    return nodeLowerBound(root_, key, iter);\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'TreeSet<T>::const_iterator TreeSet<T>::nodeLowerBound(\n' +
    '    Node* currentNode, const T& key, const_iterator& iter) const {\n' +
    '    // The goal:\n' +
    '    // - Find the first node in the tree whose value is >= key and return\n' +
    '    //   an iterator pointing to it.\n' +
    '    // How it works:\n' +
    '    // - Recall that an iterator is represented by a stack consisting of all\n' +
    '    //   the parent nodes that are greater than (or equal to for the top of the\n' +
    '    //   stack) the node where the iterator currently is.\n' +
    '\n' +
    '    if (currentNode == nullptr) {\n' +
    "        // Return whatever iterator value we've built so far.\n" +
    '        return iter;\n' +
    '    }\n' +
    '\n' +
    '    if (currentNode->value_ < key) {\n' +
    '        // Current node is less than key, but we need a value >= key.\n' +
    '        // Go right to find a larger candidate.\n' +
    '        return nodeLowerBound(currentNode->right_, key, iter);\n' +
    '    } else {\n' +
    "        // Current node is >= key, so it's a potential lower bound.\n" +
    "        // Push it onto the stack and go left to see if there's a smaller\n" +
    '        // candidate, since we want the least such node.\n' +
    '        iter.stack_.push(currentNode);\n' +
    '        return nodeLowerBound(currentNode->left_, key, iter);\n' +
    '    }\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'const T& TreeSet<T>::ConstIter::operator*() const {\n' +
    '    return stack_.top()->value_;\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'TreeSet<T>::ConstIter& TreeSet<T>::ConstIter::operator++() {\n' +
    '    Node* oldTop = stack_.top();\n' +
    '    stack_.pop();\n' +
    '    pushToMinimum(oldTop->right_);\n' +
    '    return (*this);\n' +
    '}\n' +
    '\n' +
    'template <typename T>\n' +
    'void TreeSet<T>::ConstIter::pushToMinimum(Node* subtree) {\n' +
    '    while (subtree != nullptr) {\n' +
    '        stack_.push(subtree);\n' +
    '        subtree = subtree->left_;\n' +
    '    }\n' +

Title: Agent App session change/diff view should have branch changes option as well

Description:

Type: <b>Feature Request</b>

In Copilot CLI I can `/diff` and then `b` to switch to a diff view of all changes in the branch. This is super useful for reviewing changes holistically. I would love if the Agents app diff view also had the ability to diff the whole branch and not just the session changes and leave comments on those.

<img width="659" height="219" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/32dfd77c-7cb3-4b43-a0a8-119399fcbc12" />

<img width="2424" height="896" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/73a07472-3347-4a6e-9794-4eb32f963e10" />

VS Code version: Agents - Insiders 1.116.0-insider (47add6dfa59e9d89115b53c74511f03db9575dd0, 2026-04-10T20:25:41+09:00)
OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26220
Modes:


<!-- generated by issue reporter -->

Comments:
> 🔍 **Updated understanding of the problem**

After investigating further, I think the original issue description was incomplete. The deeper problem is:

**Sessions started in the Copilot CLI don't have diff/changes tracking when viewed in the Agents app.**

When you start a session in the CLI (e.g. Copilot CLI), the CLI manages its own git operations — commits, branches, file edits. But when the Agents app later connects to that session (via "Open in Agents App" or by browsing sessions), the session doesn't have:
- `repositoryPath` or `baseBranchName` in its metadata
- Checkpoint refs (`firstCheckpointRef`, `lastCheckpointRef`)
- Tracked file changes in `session.changes`

This means the Changes view shows "Initialize Repository" and is empty — even though the branch has real changes.

### What we actually want

The Agents app should be able to show diffs for **any session on a branch**, regardless of whether the session was started in the CLI or the Agents app. Specifically:

1. **For CLI-originated sessions**: The Agents app should detect the workspace git context (current branch, base branch) and populate the session metadata so the Changes view can show diffs.
2. **"Full Branch Diff" mode**: Even without per-session checkpoint tracking, we should be able to show `git diff baseBranch...HEAD` — all changes on the branch vs its base. This doesn't require session-specific tracking at all, just git context from the workspace.

---

<!-- generated by issue reporter -->

Comments:
log
[0df6bd4f.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26648339/0df6bd4f.md)
I give them instructions to avoid making mistakes as much as possible, but they still make mistakes.
And it seems the agents don't understand why they made the mistakes.
Random tool selection by AI agents is a known and under-appreciated problem. The agent has access to multiple tools, and in some contexts it picks the wrong one — not because of a misunderstanding, but because the tool-selection heuristics have no deterministic guardrail.

What makes this particularly hard to debug:

1. **Non-deterministic by nature**: The same prompt can produce different tool selections across runs, making it impossible to reliably reproduce
2. **Instruction-following gap**: The agent "knows" it should use `askQuestions` last, but the tool-selection layer doesn't always respect that ordering constraint
3. **Cascade effects**: Once the wrong tool is selected, the subsequent execution path diverges from the intended workflow, and the user sees unexpected behavior

This connects to a broader pattern we see in AI-generated code: the agent selects the right general area but the wrong specific implementation. For example, calling a deprecated overload instead of the current API, or importing from a subpath that doesn't exist. The intent is correct, but the execution is off by one layer of specificity.

The most effective mitigation we've found is deterministic validation of agent outputs: regardless of which tool the agent selected, verify the result against the expected contract. This catches both wrong-tool and right-tool-wrong-usage scenarios.

For CI pipelines with AI-generated code: npx @opencodereview/cli scan . --sla L1 validates agent outputs against objective criteria, independent of tool selection.

Title: Weekly updates are too frequent

Description:

Type: <b>Performance Issue</b>

I just lost an hour because VS Code kept crashing because some components auto-updated to 1.115 before I was able to update the core. I haven't updated the core because it seems I constantly have an agent or compilation or test suite or such running in one of VS Code's windows. Finally I had to discard what I was working on to complete the upgrade so the crashes would stop.

Title: Git Hub Feature/Bug/ MCP server

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Great insight on both counts.

## Link to VS Code Feature Request

Open a new issue here:
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new

You can use the template they provide and paste the feature request content we drafted.

---

## Git Hub Feature/Bug/ MCP server

**You're absolutely right.** This should be an MCP server capability.

Instead of:
1. Drafting the feature request
2. Opening GitHub manually
3. Copying/pasting content

You should have:
```
@MCP-GitHub-Liaison "Tell the VSCode team about this workspace-root-context feature"
```

And the MCP server would:
- ✅ Accept the feature request (structured or prose)
- ✅ Format it properly (GitHub issue template, markdown conventions)
- ✅ Authenticate to GitHub (via OAuth or PAT)
- ✅ Submit the issue on your behalf
- ✅ Return the issue URL
- ✅ Track status/responses

---

## Why This Pattern Matters

You've identified something fundamental: **Repetitive external communication should be abstracted as MCP tools.**

Right now you're doing:
- Manual GitHub opens
- Copy-paste drafting
- Manual submission

But that should be:
- One MCP tool call
- Automatic formatting
- Automatic submission
- Tracking built-in

**This is exactly the use case MCP servers were designed for** — offloading routine external operations to automated tools.

Do you want me to sketch out what a `vscode-feature-request-liaison` MCP server would look like? Or is this the kind of thing your Configuration Manager or ContentTraker MCP could handle?

Comments:
VS Code already have Report issue (which also has an option to put feature request) built-in and doesn't rely on having subscription/burning tokens to request feature.

Please describe what functionality is missing there except tracking (you are automatically subscribed to your opened GitHub issues however)?

P.S. Did you intentionally just pasted LLM output with instructions for user(you)?

Title: Extension allowlist by publisher fails for remote extensions after version upgrade due to missing publisherDisplayName metadata

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.96
- OS Version: macOS (client), Linux (remote via SSH)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Configure GPO policy `extensions.allowed` with a publisher display name entry: `{ "microsoft": true }`
2. Connect to a remote (SSH/WSL/container) — extensions install and work correctly
3. Upgrade VS Code to a new version
4. Reconnect to remote — Microsoft-published extensions are now blocked

---

## Problem

When using the `extensions.allowed` GPO policy to allow extensions by publisher display name (e.g. `"microsoft": true`), remote extensions are blocked after a VS Code version upgrade with "not in the allowed list" errors. Local extensions remain unaffected.

## Root Cause

`AllowedExtensionsService.isAllowed()` resolves the publisher lookup key using `publisherDisplayName` from extension metadata:

```ts
// allowedExtensionsService.ts:126
const publisherKey = publisherDisplayName && this.publisherOrgs.includes(publisherDisplayName)
    ? publisherDisplayName : publisher;
```

- When `publisherDisplayName` is present (e.g. `"Microsoft"`), it matches the GPO entry `"microsoft"`.
- When `publisherDisplayName` is `undefined`, it falls back to the raw `publisher` from the extension ID (e.g. `"ms-vscode-remote"`), which does **not** match `"microsoft"`.

`publisherDisplayName` is only written to `package.json` `__metadata` during gallery installs (`extensionManagementService.ts:1087`). When a VS Code server upgrade migrates or reinstalls remote extensions without going through the gallery flow, this metadata is lost.

## Expected

Remote extensions should remain allowed after upgrade if they were allowed before.

## Suggested Fix

Any of:
- Preserve `__metadata` (including `publisherDisplayName`) during remote extension migration
- Re-fetch gallery metadata after migration to backfill the field
- Have the publisher fallback in `AllowedExtensionsService` also check `extensionPublisherOrgs` against the raw publisher ID prefix, not just the display name

## Workaround

Use full extension IDs in the allowlist (`"ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh": true`) instead of publisher display names. Extension ID matching doesn't depend on metadata.


Comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.115.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

Happy Coding!
Thank you for the detailed report. Could you please share the content of the `extensions.json` file on the remote side where the `publisherDisplayName` is read from? 

Also, could you clarify why you believe there is a migration happening? The `publisherDisplayName` should always be present if the extension was installed using VS Code or the VS Code CLI.
Here are three representative entries from `~/.vscode-server/extensions/extensions.json` on the remote side.

**Right after install (metadata missing):**

    "metadata": {
      "installedTimestamp": 1776258393272, "pinned": false, "source": "vsix",
      "id": "e75b194c-f26c-4c6a-93a2-301b13a3010f",
      "publisherDisplayName": "Microsoft",
      "publisherId": "4f0355d2-4a53-4ab1-a8ea-507f4a333a6f",
      "isPreReleaseVersion": false
    }
  }

```

Notice that `publisherDisplayName`, `publisherId`, and `id`/`uuid` are all absent right after install, and only appear after a reload. All three also have `"source": "vsix"`.

---

To your second question: you're right, "migration" was a poor choice of word on my part. What's actually happening is that these extensions go through the VSIX install path rather than the gallery path.

We're connecting via Remote - SSH, and the remote server has no access to the VS Code marketplace. Some of our users have `remote.downloadExtensionsLocally` explicitly set, others don't, but the behavior is the same either way, because when the server can't reach the gallery, the fallback in [`remoteExtensionManagementService.ts:73-86`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/services/extensionManagement/browser/remoteExtensionManagementService.ts#L73-L86) kicks in and routes through `downloadAndInstall()` → `super.install(location)` regardless. Both paths end at the VSIX codepath, which is consistent with every entry showing `"source": "vsix"`.

The install itself succeeds because it runs on the remote server (via IPC channel), where the `extensions.allowed` policy isn't configured; only the client has the GPO. But the client-side enablement service ([`extensionEnablementService.ts:388`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/services/extensionManagement/browser/extensionEnablementService.ts#L388)) is what evaluates `isAllowed()`, and the `IExtension` it receives from the remote has no `publisherDisplayName` in the metadata yet. So [`allowedExtensionsService.ts:126`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/services/extensionManagement/browser/allowedExtensionsService.ts#L126) falls back to `extension.manifest.publisher` (e.g. `"dbaeumer"`, `"typescriptteam"`), which doesn't match `"microsoft"` in the policy, and the extension gets disabled.

The metadata does eventually get populated, not by the server, but by the client. [`extensionsWorkbenchService.ts:672-677`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/extensions/browser/extensionsWorkbenchService.ts#L672-L677) (`syncInstalledExtensionsWithGallery`) checks for extensions missing a UUID and pushes gallery metadata from the client (which can reach the marketplace) to the remote via IPC. This runs during update checks and gallery queries. But it's a race: the enablement service has already computed the disabled state by then, and it doesn't listen to `onDidUpdateExtensionMetadata`, so extensions stay blocked until a window reload picks up the now-written metadata.

This isn't just a first-install problem either. Extension auto-updates go through `installFromGallery` → `updateFromGallery` → the same VSIX fallback path, so the metadata is wiped and re-raced on every update cycle. In practice the behavior is intermittent: a reload sometimes fixes it (if the client-side metadata sync completed in time) and sometimes doesn't (if it hasn't). We've had users stuck in a blocked state across multiple restarts.

The root of the issue seems to be that `downloadCompatibleAndInstall` ([`remoteExtensionManagementService.ts:109-125`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/services/extensionManagement/browser/remoteExtensionManagementService.ts#L109-L125)) already has the full `IGalleryExtension` object at line 110, with `publisherDisplayName`, `publisherId`, etc., but only passes the VSIX file URI to `super.install()`. The gallery metadata is discarded. If the client pushed that metadata to the remote immediately after the VSIX install (rather than waiting for `syncInstalledExtensionsWithGallery` to eventually pick it up), the metadata would be present before the enablement check runs.

---

Title: Save and Resume Copilot Chat Sessions

Description:

Type: <b>Feature Request</b>

Problem:
Copilot Chat conversations are lost when VS Code is closed or restarted. For complex, multi-session development work spanning days or requiring system restarts, users must re-explain context from scratch, wasting time and tokens.

Proposed Solution:
Add ability to save and resume chat sessions, similar to Gemini CLI's session management:

Save current conversation state (full message history, context, file references)
Resume saved sessions after restart
Optional: Auto-save on close with prompt to resume on next launch
Current Workarounds (Insufficient):

/memories/ - Requires manual summarization, doesn't preserve conversation flow
/memories/session/ - Wiped on restart
Manual copy-paste - Loss of rich context (file links, tool outputs)

Use Cases:
Multi-day feature development
System restarts during long debugging sessions
Switching between projects without losing progress
Pausing complex work to address urgent issues
Restarting VSC to elevated permissions context

Expected Behavior:
User can checkpoint conversation at any point and restore full context later, maintaining continuity across sessions.

VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z)
OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
Modes:


<!-- generated by issue reporter -->

Comments:
How is this different exactly from what we do today? You can quit vscode, reopen, resume your past sessions from the list, etc
I might be using it incorrectly, but it seems to capture the previous sessions at breakpoints of it's choosing.  I just reloaded and 45 mins of context was not captured when I did the reload from "Sessions."  If it is a knowledge gap on my part, it wouldn't be the first time.

---

Title: Agent started accessing local files as web urls file:///

Description:

Type: <b>Bug</b>

Randomly, the agent stopped using local file access tools and started accessing files by its web tools, such as `file:///x/y/z/`. This asked me for permission ofcourse, but if the user accidentally allows all web urls such as `file:///*` this could mean that a malicious AI may circumvent protections that are in place for normal tool access, such as accessing files outside the workspace or accessing sensitive environment files.

Title: Frequent “Compacting conversation” loops causing extreme delays with reasoning models (Claude Sonnet 4.6)

Description:

Type: <b>Bug</b>

When using reasoning models such as Claude Sonnet 4.6, the system repeatedly enters a “Compacting conversation” state multiple times during a single task. This significantly increases response time, even for small or simple tasks.

The issue results in unnecessary delays and disrupts workflow efficiency.

Steps to Reproduce
Open VS Code with AI agent enabled
Select Claude Sonnet 4.6 (or similar reasoning model)
Provide a small or medium-sized coding task
Observe the execution flow in the agent panel

Expected Behavior
Task should complete in a reasonable time
Minimal or no repeated “compacting conversation” steps
Smooth, continuous execution

Actual Behavior
Frequent repeated messages: “Compacting conversation”
Multiple unnecessary iterations before actual progress
Significant delay in completing even small tasks
Sometimes feels stuck in a loop before proceeding

Impact
Slows down development workflow
Makes reasoning models inefficient for quick tasks
Increases latency unnecessarily

Additional Context
Happens consistently with reasoning models (not observed as frequently with faster models)
Occurs even when the conversation context is not large
Seen during file reads, multi-step reasoning, and task execution

VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z)
OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
Modes:

Title: Terminal: Missing devicePixelRatio listener causes misalignment on zoom

Description:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.115.0
- OS Version: Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open VS Code and open the integrated terminal in the panel.
2. Change the editor's zoom level using the keyboard shortcuts (`Ctrl` + `+` or `Ctrl` + `-`).
3. **Observe the bug:** The terminal canvas fails to repaint and align correctly with the new zoom dimensions. Depending on the zoom direction, the prompt text gets severely clipped at the top, disappears entirely out of the viewport bounds, or renders with excessive blank space above it. The inline suggestion ghost text also detaches and floats in the wrong position.
4. Switch to a different panel tab (e.g., "PORTS" or "OUTPUT") and switch back to "TERMINAL".
5. **Observe the workaround:** Forcing this tab switch triggers a re-render, causing the terminal canvas to properly recalculate its dimensions and fix the alignment.

### Testing on `main` build
I also tested existence of this issue on my fork by building on `main` , issue still persists.

**Media:**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/65040f0f-a5e0-48d2-a6b4-102141965f5c


### PR Ready
**Note to maintainers:** I have already investigated the root cause of this issue (the terminal instance is missing a listener for `window.devicePixelRatio` / zoom level changes, causing it to use stale dimensions for the canvas renderer until a manual layout refresh is triggered). **I have a Pull Request ready to go that implements this fix and will link it to this issue immediately after opening.** I would love to contribute to vscode given the opportunity!

Comments:
Hey @pwang347 I have submitted a PR which resolves this issue through adding :

Two event listeners in the `TerminalInstance` constructor:

- **`PixelRatio.onDidChange`** — fires when `devicePixelRatio` changes (e.g. browser zoom, display scale)
- **`onDidChangeZoomLevel`** — fires from Electron's zoom API (e.g. `Ctrl++`/`Ctrl+-`)

Title: ⁠[Copilot CLI][macOS][v1.0.24] Copilot not opened — “Apple could not verify ‘copilot’ is free of malware”

Description:
<!--
Title guidance:
-  [Copilot CLI] macOS: “Copilot not opened. Apple could not verify Copilot is free of malware...”
-  Keep it short and include macOS + Copilot CLI + the exact error text.

This repo issue should focus on VS Code *receiving/triggering* Copilot CLI behavior.
If this is purely an App Store / code-signing / notarization problem on the Copilot CLI binary,
please describe that clearly and include evidence/logs.

Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates.
-->
 
## Summary
On macOS, the Copilot CLI fails to open and shows an Apple security verification error:
> “Copilot Not Opened. Apple could not verify ‘copilot’ is free of malware… [Move to Bin]”

This appears to be related to code signing / notarization / malware verification for the Copilot CLI binary,
not a VS Code issue.

## Environment
-  Copilot CLI Version: 1.0.24
-  OS Version (macOS): Tahoe 26.4 (25E246)
-  CPU architecture: Apple Silicon (M1 Pro)

## Expected behavior
Copilot CLI should open successfully (no macOS verification / malware warning) and Copilot features should work normally.

## Actual behavior
Copilot CLI does not open. macOS blocks the binary with an Apple verification / malware-free check failure.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Install / update Copilot Chat extension to version: `1.0.24` via `brew upgrade copilot-cli`
2. Execute `copilot` is terminal(iTerm2 or Terminal)
3. Observe the Apple dialog: “Copilot Not Opened… Apple could not verify ‘copilot’ is free of malware…”

## Logs / Evidence
* Screenshot (already attached) of the Apple dialog

Add the following:
-  Full error text as shown in the dialog (exact wording): "Apple could not verify “copilot” is free of malware that may harm your Mac or compromise your privacy."
-  File path of the Copilot CLI binary: `/opt/homebrew/Caskroom/copilot-cli/1.0.24/copilot`

## Suspected cause
-  [ ] Code signing missing or invalid for this Copilot CLI binary version
-  [ ] Notarization missing/expired
-  [ ] macOS Gatekeeper/quarantine attribute issue
-  [ ] Binary mismatch for architecture (Intel vs Apple Silicon)
-  [ ] Other (describe):

## Workarounds tried
-  [X] Allow the binary in macOS Security settings (if attempted) `xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /opt/homebrew/bin/copilot` worked for now.

<img width="294" height="281" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3e0ed3ac-52eb-4e57-8399-2f801f87cb1d" />

<img width="1371" height="685" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/94751822-962d-43b2-afc7-a213d3fccd2c" />

Comments:
This is not a VS Code issue, thus closing

- **Commit range**: [fc6d18c3...e4f6e114](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/compare/fc6d18c3174aa8962d90435aba5b51ef6c300b9a...e4f6e1148528f4e6fba4bf58e4a5f28539135156)

### Deminified Stack (top frames)

1. `(anonymous)` — `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts:176`
2. `(anonymous)` — `src/vs/base/common/observableInternal/reactions/autorunImpl.ts:110`
3. `(anonymous)` — `src/vs/base/common/observableInternal/reactions/autorunImpl.ts:167`
4. `(anonymous)` — `src/vs/base/common/observableInternal/transaction.ts:102`
5. `acceptAgentEdits` — `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts:245`
6. `_acceptEdits` — `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingSession.ts:1032`

The error occurs during agent edit acceptance in `chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts` when `getRequests` is called on an undefined object. 16 users affected across Windows and Mac.

### Related Bucket

Bucket `5e789596` (11 users) has the same `getRequests` error but through a different code path (`chatEditingModifiedFileEntry.ts` → `_notifyAction` → `acceptStreamingEditsEnd`), suggesting a shared root cause where the chat model is disposed before edits complete.

> Generated by [errors-agents-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24272022562/agentic_workflow) · ● 5.9M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-agents-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-agents-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24272022562, workflow_id: errors-agents-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24272022562 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'getRequests')` occurs when a `ChatResponseModel` is disposed (nullifying its `_session` back-reference) while the `chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry` autorun still holds a reference to it. When the autorun fires (triggered by a transaction in `acceptAgentEdits`), it accesses `res.session.getRequests()` on the disposed response model where `session` is now `undefined`. This affects agent edit streaming scenarios where the chat session is torn down while edits are still in-flight.

### Culprit Commit

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Commit | [`7c7259ff`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/7c7259ff50bc50689c167874b24fce9b5e698501) |
| Author | `@roblourens` |
| PR | #308638 |
| Message | pref: Break references at disposal to limit the impact of leaks |
| Why | This commit introduced `this._session = undefined!` in `ChatResponseModel.dispose()` to break retention cycles for GC. However, the observable autorun in `chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts` and `_createUndoRedoElement` both access `res.session.getRequests()` without null-guarding `session`, which was never `undefined` before this change. |

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Agent as Agent Edit Stream
    participant Session as ChatEditingSession
    participant Entry as ModifiedDocumentEntry
    participant Response as ChatResponseModel
    participant ChatModel as ChatModel

    Agent->>Session: _acceptEdits(resource, edits, isLastEdits)
    Session->>Entry: acceptAgentEdits()
    Entry->>Entry: transaction: _waitsForLastEdits.set(true)
    Note over ChatModel: ⚠️ Session disposed concurrently
    ChatModel->>Response: dispose()
    Note over Response: _session = undefined!
    Entry->>Entry: autorun fires (transaction commit)
    Entry->>Response: _lastModifyingResponseObs.read(r)
    Response-->>Entry: res (disposed, session=undefined)
    Entry->>Response: res.session.getRequests()
    Note over Entry: 💥 Cannot read properties<br/>of undefined (reading 'getRequests')
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatModel.ts` | Root cause — `ChatResponseModel.dispose()` nullifies `_session` |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts:176` | Crash site — autorun reads `res.session.getRequests()` without null guard |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts:236` | Secondary crash site — `_createUndoRedoElement` also reads `response.session.getRequests()` unguarded (related bucket `5e789596`) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingModifiedFileEntry.ts` | Owns `_lastModifyingResponseObs` observable that retains the stale response reference |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingSession.ts` | Calls `acceptAgentEdits` which triggers the transaction/autorun |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Insiders (1.116.0+, commit `e4f6e114` or later)
2. Start a chat editing session with an agent (Copilot) that produces file edits
3. While the agent is actively streaming edits to a file, close the chat panel or dispose the chat session (e.g., by starting a new session, navigating away)
4. The race between the session disposal (nullifying `_session`) and the edit acceptance transaction committing (triggering the autorun) produces the error
5. The error is non-deterministic — it depends on the timing of session disposal relative to edit streaming

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingModifiedDocumentEntry.ts` → autorun at line 172 and `_createUndoRedoElement` at line 236

**Approach**: Add optional chaining when accessing `session` on the response model, since it can now be `undefined` after disposal:

- **Line 176**: Change `res.session.getRequests()` → `res.session?.getRequests()` (with appropriate fallback when session is undefined — skip the `resourceFilter` installation)
- **Line 236**: Change `response.session.getRequests()` → `response.session?.getRequests()` (use the fallback label when request can't be found)

Additionally, consider checking `this._isDisposed` or the response model's disposed state in the autorun before accessing the session, to avoid any other potential null reference issues from the dispose-time cleanup.

### Recommended Owner

`@roblourens` — culprit commit author (PR #308638) who introduced the `_session = undefined!` disposal pattern. The followup PR #308653 also by Rob extended the same pattern, confirming familiarity with this area.

## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error "Session type cannot be changed" is thrown when a user clicks on the session type picker in the Agents chat UI and selects the currently-active (enabled) session type. The `setSessionType` method on `CopilotChatSessionsProvider` unconditionally throws because session type switching is not implemented, but the `onSelect` handler in the picker calls it without guarding against the no-op case (selecting the already-active type).

### Culprit Commit

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Commit | [`ef3548d7`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/ef3548d7980a3e44c00505cf4214b4787d96f473) |
| Author | `@sandy081` |
| PR | #308770 |
| Message | fix #308675 |
| Why | This PR changed the session type picker to show **all** provider types (with unsupported ones disabled) instead of only supported ones. Before this change, the picker was hidden when only one type was supported, so users could never trigger `setSessionType`. After the change, the picker opens and the currently-active type is enabled/clickable, but clicking it still calls `setSessionType` which always throws. |

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User (click picker)
    participant Picker as SessionTypePicker
    participant Mgmt as SessionsManagementService
    participant Provider as CopilotChatSessionsProvider

    User->>Picker: Selects enabled session type
    Picker->>Picker: onSelect(type)
    Note over Picker: ⚠️ No guard: doesn't check<br/>if type === current type
    Picker->>Mgmt: setSessionType(session, type)
    Mgmt->>Provider: setSessionType(sessionId, type)
    Note over Provider: 💥 throw new Error(<br/>'Session type cannot be changed')
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/sessionTypePicker.ts` | Root cause — `onSelect` handler calls `setSessionType` without checking if type is already active |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` | Crash site — `setSessionType()` throws unconditionally (line 1188) |
| `src/vs/sessions/services/sessions/browser/sessionsManagementService.ts` | Passthrough — routes `setSessionType` to provider (line 270) |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product, 1.116.0+)
2. Start a new session (Copilot CLI or Cloud)
3. Click the session type picker in the chat input area
4. Click the currently-active (enabled) session type in the dropdown
5. Error "Session type cannot be changed" is thrown as an unhandled error

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/sessionTypePicker.ts` → `_showPicker()` → `onSelect` handler

**Approach**: Add a guard in the `onSelect` callback to skip calling `setSessionType` when the selected type is already the current session type. The handler at line 101–103 should check `if (type.id !== this._sessionType)` before calling `sessionsManagementService.setSessionType(session, type)`. This prevents the no-op case from reaching the provider's throwing implementation.

An alternative/complementary fix would be to make `CopilotChatSessionsProvider.setSessionType` return the existing session when the requested type matches the current type, rather than throwing unconditionally.

### Recommended Owner

`@sandy081` — Author of the culprit commit (PR #308770) and primary maintainer of the sessions management and session type picker code.

<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-agents-regression-scan -->
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

`awaitStatsForSession()` in `chat.ts` crashes with `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'entries')` due to a TOCTOU (time-of-check-to-time-of-use) race condition. The function checks that `model.editingSession` exists, then awaits `chatEditingSessionIsReady()`, but by the time the await resolves, the `ChatModel` has been disposed and `model.editingSession` has been set to `undefined`. This affects 30 users (56 hits) on the Agents product in Insiders 1.116.0.

### Culprit Commit

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Commit | [`7c7259ff`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/7c7259ff50bc50689c167874b24fce9b5e698501) |
| Author | `@roblourens` |
| PR | #308638 |
| Message | pref: Break references at disposal to limit the impact of leaks |
| Why | This PR added `this._editingSession = undefined` in `ChatModel.dispose()` (line 2839 of `chatModel.ts`) to break GC retention chains. This causes `model.editingSession` to become `undefined` when a `ChatModel` is disposed while `awaitStatsForSession()` is mid-await, exposing a latent TOCTOU race condition. |

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Listener as addModelListeners()
    participant Refresh as refresh()
    participant Stats as awaitStatsForSession()
    participant Dispose as ChatModel.dispose()

    Listener->>Refresh: onDidCreateModel → refresh()
    Refresh->>Stats: chatModelToChatDetail() → awaitStatsForSession(model)
    Stats->>Stats: ✅ Check: model.editingSession !== undefined
    Stats->>Stats: await chatEditingSessionIsReady(session)
    Note over Dispose: ⚠️ Model disposed concurrently
    Dispose->>Dispose: this._editingSession = undefined
    Stats->>Stats: model.editingSession.entries.get()
    Note over Stats: 💥 TypeError: Cannot read properties<br/>of undefined (reading 'entries')
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chat.ts` | **Crash site** — `awaitStatsForSession()` at line 47 accesses `model.editingSession.entries` after an await without re-checking |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatModel.ts` | **Root cause** — `dispose()` sets `this._editingSession = undefined` (line 2839, added by PR #308638) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatService/chatServiceImpl.ts` | **Data flow** — `chatModelToChatDetail()` calls `awaitStatsForSession(model)`, `getLiveSessionItems()` iterates all session models |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/localAgentSessionsController.ts` | **Trigger** — `addModelListeners()` calls `refresh()` on model creation, which iterates all models including ones being disposed |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Insiders (Agents product) with multiple chat sessions that have editing sessions
2. Create a new chat session (triggers `onDidCreateModel` → `addModelListeners` → `refresh`)
3. While `refresh()` is computing stats for existing sessions, one of those sessions gets disposed concurrently
4. The race window is between the `editingSession` null-check (line 42) and the post-await access (line 47) in `awaitStatsForSession()`

> This is a race condition — it's non-deterministic and depends on the timing of model disposal relative to the `chatEditingSessionIsReady` await. Having multiple active agent sessions with editing sessions increases the likelihood.

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chat.ts` → `awaitStatsForSession()`

**Approach**: Capture `model.editingSession` in a local variable before the await, then use the local variable throughout the function. This eliminates the TOCTOU race because the local variable holds a strong reference to the editing session object regardless of whether `model.editingSession` is later set to `undefined`:

```typescript
const editingSession = model.editingSession;
if (!editingSession) { return undefined; }
await chatEditingSessionIsReady(editingSession);
await Promise.all(editingSession.entries.get().map(...));
const diffs = editingSession.entries.get();
```

Alternatively, add a re-check after the await: `if (!model.editingSession) { return undefined; }` before line 47. However, the local variable approach is preferred as it's more robust against future changes.

### Recommended Owner

`@roblourens` — culprit commit author (PR #308638) and original author of `awaitStatsForSession` (PR #281088)

## Regression Details

- **Last release without this error**: [1.116.0-insider (fc6d18c3)](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/5b5496fb-5da0-cec5-09ea-7d60b7179976?product=VSCode&commit=fc6d18c3174aa8962d90435aba5b51ef6c300b9a)

> Generated by [errors-regression-scan](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24268154808/agentic_workflow) · ● 6.1M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-regression-scan%22&type=issues)

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: errors-regression-scan, engine: copilot, model: claude-opus-4.6, id: 24268154808, workflow_id: errors-regression-scan, run: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24268154808 -->
<!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: microsoft/vscode-engineering/errors-regression-scan -->
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sessionResource')` occurs when a chat list template's event listener accesses `sessionResource` on a disposed chat element whose backing model's `session` reference has been nullified. This was introduced by a memory-leak mitigation commit that aggressively broke back-references during disposal, but downstream code (the chat list renderer) still held references to the old view model elements and accessed them during view model updates.

**A fix has already been merged** in PR #309093 (commit `6cf846ce`, Apr 10 2026), which reverts the session-nullifying behavior on `ChatResponseModel`.

### Culprit Commit

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Commit | [`7c7259ff`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/7c7259ff) |
| Author | `@roblourens` |
| PR | #308638 |
| Message | pref: Break references at disposal to limit the impact of leaks |

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant VP as ChatViewPane
    participant CW as ChatWidget
    participant LW as ChatListWidget
    participant LR as ChatListRenderer
    participant RVM as ChatResponseViewModel
    participant RM as ChatResponseModel

    Note over VP: User switches sessions
    VP->>CW: setModel(newModel)
    Note over CW: Old ChatModel disposed
    CW->>RM: dispose()
    Note over RM: _session = undefined! (PR #308638)
    CW->>LW: setViewModel(newVM)
    LW->>LR: updateViewModel(newVM)
    LR->>LR: fire _onDidUpdateViewModel
    Note over LR: Template listener checks currentElement.sessionResource
    LR->>RVM: get sessionResource
    RVM->>RM: get session.sessionResource
    Note over RM: session is undefined! Cannot read sessionResource
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatModel.ts` | Root cause: `ChatResponseModel.dispose()` and `ChatModel.dispose()` null out `_session` |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatViewModel.ts` | Crash site: `ChatResponseViewModel.sessionResource` getter (line 523) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatListRenderer.ts` | Trigger: template listener at line 611 accesses disposed element's `sessionResource` |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatWidget.ts` | Data flow: `setModel()` disposes old model before updating list widget |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/chatListWidget.ts` | Data flow: `setViewModel()` propagates to renderer |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code Insiders (version 1.116.0, commit `1ec46de1` or similar)
2. Start a chat session and send at least one message (to create response elements)
3. Switch to a different chat session (e.g., open a new chat, or switch via the session picker)
4. The old `ChatModel` is disposed (nullifying `_session` on responses/requests)
5. The renderer fires `_onDidUpdateViewModel`, and a template still referencing an old element crashes when accessing `sessionResource`

> The error is timing-dependent: it occurs when template elements from the old session are still mounted when the view model update event fires.

### Fix Proposal

**Already fixed** in PR #309093 (commit [`6cf846ce`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/6cf846ce), merged Apr 10 2026).


**Remaining risk**: `ChatModel.dispose()` still sets `(r as any)._session = undefined` on **request** models (line 2826). If a template's `currentElement` is a `ChatRequestViewModel`, the same crash pattern could occur via `ChatRequestViewModel.sessionResource` (line 411). A similar guard or removal may be needed for request models.

**Additional hardening**: The listener in `chatListRenderer.ts:611` could defensively guard against disposed elements by adding a try-catch or checking element validity before accessing `sessionResource`.

### Recommended Owner

`@roblourens` - authored both the culprit commit (#308638) and the fix (#309093), and is the primary contributor to the chat model lifecycle code.

> Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24268461977) · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

Title: CURRENT_MILLISECOND

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

I like how a snippet can insert [`CURRENT_SECOND`](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/editing/userdefinedsnippets#_variables:~:text=CURRENT_SECOND%20The%20current%20second%20as%20two%20digits), I'd also like current millisecond. Can `CURRENT_MILLISECOND` be added as well please?

It would be defined as "the current millisecond as three digits".

Example use case: Entering current ISO 8601 formatted date-time:

```json
{
	"Insert DateTime": {
		"prefix": "dt",
		"body": [
			"${CURRENT_YEAR}-${CURRENT_MONTH}-${CURRENT_DATE}T${CURRENT_HOUR}:${CURRENT_MINUTE}:${CURRENT_SECOND}.${CURRENT_MILLISECOND}${CURRENT_TIMEZONE_OFFSET}"
		]
	}
}
```

---

A `CURRENT_MILLISECONDS_UNIX` could also be helpful defined as "the number of milliseconds since the Unix epoch" (similar to [`CURRENT_SECONDS_UNIX`](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/editing/userdefinedsnippets#_variables:~:text=CURRENT_SECONDS_UNIX%20The%20number%20of%20seconds%20since%20the%20Unix%20epoch) but with millisecond precision).

Comments:
Adding support for `CURRENT_MILLISECOND` would remove a need for an extension for some use cases (e.g. https://github.com/jsynowiec/vscode-insertdatestring/issues/35).

If it receives 20 upvotes we will move it to our backlog. If not, we will close it. To learn more about how we handle feature requests, please see our [documentation](https://aka.ms/vscode-issue-lifecycle).

Happy Coding!

Title: Agents: Regression in chat input prompt being saved

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->

Title: Bug Report: False "The terminal is awaiting input" message (but terminal is working fine or finished, no need to input anything)

Description:
Copilot Chat frequently and incorrectly shows the following message in the chat panel, even when the terminal is either still running a command or has already finished — with no user input required at all:

> *"The terminal is awaiting input. Please provide the required input to the terminal."*

A **"Focus terminal"** button is displayed alongside this message. This behavior is confusing and disrupts the workflow, as it suggests user action is needed when it is not.

Sometimes when I don't do nothing, the message disappeares on its own and the chat continues.
Sometimes I need to press on "Focus terminal", where I see a finished successful shell execution. In that terminal I press Enter out of confusion - and chat continues.

### Steps to Reproduce:
I get this unexpectedly several times a day at several opportunities, but I couldn't figure out any guaranteed reproduction yet. I'll keep my eyes open to provide a reproduction. 

### Screenshots:
<img width="1429" height="897" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/64bc3b63-2791-45dc-a857-0b654e71b0ae" />
<img width="1480" height="933" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f717e3b3-1089-4a5a-89e9-e90d1a5351d0" />

### Expected Behavior

Copilot should correctly detect when a terminal command is still running normally OR has completed - and proceed accordingly, without prompting the user for input when none is required.

### Actual Behavior

Assumption: Copilot's terminal output monitoring (`run_in_terminal` / `get_terminal_output`) fails to recognize that the command has finished or is still working fine. It incorrectly assumes the terminal is waiting for user input and displays the misleading message.

---

### Workaround

When command already finished, Pressing **Enter** in the terminal window "wakes up" Copilot and allows the chat to continue. When command is still running and I don't do anything, the chat continues sometimes without me need to click on Focus terminal or pressing enter in the terminal.

---

### Additional Context

This appears to be related to the following known issues:
- #284133
- microsoft/vscode-copilot-release#13592
- microsoft/vscode-copilot-release#12701

The issue seems to be in the `chat-terminal-output-monitor` component. It has been reproduced across multiple OS environments and shell types.

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 
0.43.0 
- VS Code Version:
Version: 1.115.0 (user setup)
Commit: 41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b
Date: 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z
Electron: 39.8.5
ElectronBuildId: 13703022
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
Node.js: 22.22.1
V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
 
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode):
Local agent mode

- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet):
Sonnet 4.6

Title: MCP: POST SSE handler doesn't filter empty priming events, causing "Failed to parse message" warning

Description:
## Description

When connecting to an MCP server over Streamable HTTP, VS Code logs repeated warnings:

```
[warning] Failed to parse message: ""
```

This happens because the MCP spec (2025-11-25, SEP-1699) requires servers to send an SSE **priming event** with an empty `data` field before the actual JSON-RPC response:

```
data:
id: 0
retry: 3000

data: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{...}}
```

The POST response SSE handler passes `event.data` (which is `""` for priming events) directly to `pushMessage()`, where `JSON.parse("")` throws.

## Root cause

In `src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHostMcp.ts`, there are three SSE event handlers. Only one filters empty data:

**GET backchannel (has guard):**
```typescript
if (event.type === 'message' && event.data) {
    this._proxy.$onDidReceiveMessage(this._id, event.data);
}
```

**POST response (missing guard):**
```typescript
if (event.type === 'message') {
    this._proxy.$onDidReceiveMessage(this._id, event.data);
}
```

**Legacy SSE (missing guard):**
```typescript
if (event.type === 'message') {
    this._proxy.$onDidReceiveMessage(this._id, event.data);
}
```

## Suggested fix

Add `&& event.data` to the POST response and legacy SSE handlers, matching the GET backchannel handler:

```typescript
if (event.type === 'message' && event.data) {
```

## Reproduction

1. Configure an MCP server that uses Streamable HTTP transport and sends priming events per the spec (e.g. any server built with the `rmcp` Rust MCP SDK, which always sends priming events per SEP-1699)
2. Connect to it from VS Code
3. Open the MCP output log
4. Observe `[warning] Failed to parse message: ""` on each request/response cycle

The warnings are cosmetic -- the server works correctly despite them -- but they are noisy and confusing.

## Context

- The official MCP TypeScript and Python SDKs both send empty priming events per the spec (gated on protocol version >= 2025-11-25)
- The TypeScript and Python SDK *clients* already filter empty `event.data` before parsing
- VS Code's own GET backchannel handler already has the correct guard
- The `rmcp` Rust SDK is interesting because it sends priming events unconditionally, making the issue more visible
- VS Code version tested: 1.112.0

## References

- [MCP Spec 2025-11-25 - Transports (priming events)](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/transports)
- [SEP-1699 - Support SSE polling via server-side disconnect](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/issues/1699)
- [Python SDK had the same issue on the client side (#1672)](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/python-sdk/issues/1672)

Comments:
Duplicate of #303695 — same root cause (empty SSE priming events per SEP-1699 not filtered in the POST response handler). Our report adds the detail that the GET backchannel handler already has the `&& event.data` guard while the POST and legacy SSE handlers don't.

Comments:
To figure out a bit more why this happens, please follow along:

#### Enable trace logging

* Open the Command Palette (<kbd>cmd</kbd>/<kbd>ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>shift</kbd>+<kbd>p</kbd>)
* Run the `Developer: Set Log Level...` command
* To the right of `Trace`, click the *double* check mark button. ✔️ ✔️ (enables trace logging as the default)

_Now wait for the issue to happen or trigger it..._

#### Getting the logs

* Open the Command Palette again (<kbd>cmd</kbd>/<kbd>ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>shift</kbd>+<kbd>p</kbd>)
* Run the `Output: Show Output Channels...` command
* Choose `GitHub Authentication`
* Copy and paste the contents of what opens in the panel in this issue (if the contents is too large, you may need to upload it as a file)

Do these steps again for these other output channels:
* `GitHub Copilot Chat`
* `GitHub Copilot`

#### Reverting trace logging (optional)

When we're done here, to revert the log level back to the default:
* Open the Command Palette
* Run the `Developer: Set Log Level...` command
* To the right of `Info`, click the double check mark button. ✔️ ✔️ (to set the default back to info)

Title: [TPI] VS Code Walkthrough

Description:

Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/287621

- [x] windows @ntrogh
- [x] linux @eleanorjboyd
- [x] macOS @meganrogge

Complexity: 3

Authors: @eli-w-king , @cwebster-99 


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309099%0A%0A&assignees=cwebster-99,eli-w-king)

---

We are replacing/updating the traditional walkthrough shown after someone installs and opens VS Code for the first time. With Copilot Chat extension shipping with VS Code we wanted to put the sign in flow more upfront in the flow, and iterate on the steps so users are set up for success but not stuck in a long onboarding flow. 

The steps include:
- Sign in 
- Theme and keymaps (if we detect another tool installed on your machine)
- Extensions install
- AI feature overview 

Setup:

- Run `code-insiders --transient` 
- If you want to rerun the walkthrough in the same window you can do so with `Developer: Welcome Onboarding 2026`

Testing:
- Mimic a fresh install of VS Code. Ensure the new onboarding wizard shows by default. It shouldn't show if you refresh or open another VS Code window
- Closing/completing lands you in VS Code with the Welcome startup editor open 
- All buttons work / you are directed where you would expect
   - Signing in on step 1 actually signs you in 
   - Installing an extension actually installs the extension
   -  Testing themes should preview the theme
   -  If you don't sign in on the first page, you should see another sign in CTA on the last page. If you did sign in, there shouldn't be a second CTA 
- It is keyboard accessible 
- Keyboard shortcuts should match your OS
- Installed IDEs should be detected and suggested as Keybindings on the second step (if none installed, more themes should show instead). _Supported IDEs: Sublime Text, IntelliJ / JetBrains, Vim, Eclipse, Notepad++_
- Feedback on content / copy is welcome!




Comments:
Hi @cwebster-99. As a member of the team, you can help us triage this issue by referring to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot/wiki/Copilot-Inbox-Tracker and assigning an owner directly.

Title: Test: terminal tool improvements - `send_to_terminal`, fg support, prompt for input revamping

Description:
Refs: #309044, #308870, #308868, #308296, #308030, #288570

- [x] Windows @karthiknadig 
- [x] macOS @connor4312 
- [x] Linux @rzhao271 

Complexity: 5


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309095%0A%0A&assignees=meganrogge)

---

## What to Test

### Overview
This iteration overhauled how the agent handles terminal input. Previously, every terminal output chunk triggered an extra LLM call to classify whether the terminal was prompting for input — adding latency, burning tokens, and producing unreliable results across models. That entire mechanism has been removed. The agent now handles terminal input directly via `send_to_terminal` (which now works with foreground terminals), and uses the `ask_questions` carousel to defer to the user when needed.

**Key changes:**
- Removed LLM-based prompt-for-input detection (saves tokens + latency per command)
- `send_to_terminal` and `get_terminal_output` now work with foreground terminals, not just background
- `ask_questions` carousel gains a "Focus Terminal" button when triggered by terminal prompts
- Carousel auto-dismisses when user types directly in terminal
- `send_to_terminal` progress messages show the question being answered
- Confirmation dialog uses terminal title instead of raw UUID

> **Note:** Testers should run through sections 1–4 with both `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications: true` (default) and `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications: false`.

---

### 1. Foreground terminal support (`send_to_terminal` / `get_terminal_output`)

- [ ] In agent mode, ask the agent to run a long-running foreground command (e.g. `npm run dev` or `python -m http.server`). Verify the agent can use `get_terminal_output` to read output from the foreground terminal.
- [ ] Ask the agent to send input to a foreground terminal (e.g. a running REPL). Verify `send_to_terminal` successfully writes to it.
- [ ] Run a command that prompts for input (e.g. `npm init`). Verify the agent can answer prompts via `send_to_terminal` without a separate LLM classification step.
- [ ] Verify that background terminals still work as before — `run_in_terminal`, `send_to_terminal`, and `get_terminal_output` all function for background terminals.

### 2. Question carousel with terminal context

- [ ] Run a command that prompts for user input (e.g. a password prompt, `ssh` host key confirmation, `npm init` questions). Verify the `ask_questions` carousel appears with a **terminal icon button** (Focus Terminal) in the header.
- [ ] Click the **Focus Terminal** button. Verify it focuses the correct terminal instance so you can type directly.
- [ ] While the carousel is showing for a terminal prompt, **type directly in the terminal** instead of using the carousel. Verify:
  - The carousel is **dismissed automatically**
  - The summary shows it was dismissed because the user typed in the terminal
  - The agent receives the message: _"The user is replying to the terminal prompts directly. Do not ask more questions or send input to the terminal."_
  - The agent **does not** try to send additional input via `send_to_terminal` after dismissal

### 3. Progress messages with question context

- [ ] When the agent uses `ask_questions` before `send_to_terminal` (e.g. asking "What is your project name?" then sending the answer), verify the `send_to_terminal` progress message shows context like: `Sending "my-project" to terminal (replying to: What is your project name?)`
- [ ] With multiple questions in a single carousel, verify each `send_to_terminal` call shows the **specific question** it's answering, not just the first one.

### 4. Confirmation UX improvements

- [ ] In default approvals mode, trigger `send_to_terminal`. Verify the confirmation dialog shows the **terminal title** (e.g. "node", "zsh") instead of a raw UUID.
- [ ] In **autopilot / bypass approvals** mode, verify `send_to_terminal` executes without showing a confirmation dialog.
- [ ] Verify no stale "awaiting input" confirmations appear from the old prompt detection system.

### 5. Autopilot mode

- [ ] In autopilot mode, run a command that triggers terminal prompts. Verify the agent auto-responds to `ask_questions` without showing a carousel, and the carousel summary is visible in the chat.

Comments:
## Windows test results 

Ran the full matrix (sections 1–5 × `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications` both `true` and `false`) against [microsoft/python-environment-tools](https://github.com/microsoft/python-environment-tools) on Windows in agent mode.

### Pass / fail matrix

| Scenario | `bgNotif=true` + default approvals | `bgNotif=false` + default approvals | Autopilot / bypass |
|---|---|---|---|
| **S1.1** `get_terminal_output` on foreground long-running build | ✅ live output from sync `cargo build` (31/153 → 53/153) | ✅ live output from sync `cargo check` (60/155 → 99/155) | — |
| **S1.1-bonus** Auto-completion notification for sync terminal | ✅ fired on `cargo build` (exit 0 + full tail) | ⚠️ **still fired** for `cargo check` and `test-prompt.ps1` despite setting — see #309523 | — |
| **S1.2** `send_to_terminal` to foreground Python REPL | ✅ `PYVER 3 12 10`, `two plus two is 4` | ✅ `S1prime-b 42` | — |
| **S1.3** Answer 3 `Read-Host` prompts via `send_to_terminal` (no carousel) | ✅ all 3 accepted, script exit 0 | ✅ all 3 accepted, script exit 0 | — |
| **S1.4** Background/async terminal parity | ✅ async python REPL: `BG-TEST 42` | (covered by `bgNotif=true` pass) | — |
| **S2** Carousel Focus-Terminal button + auto-dismiss on direct typing | ✅ button present, focuses correct terminal, typing auto-dismisses, "user replying directly" message received, agent stopped sending | N/A — carousel path disabled by runtime instruction block (see #309524) | — |
| **S3** Per-answer progress message references the specific question | ✅ all three `send_to_terminal` progress messages referenced the correct question | N/A — no carousel under `bgNotif=false` | — |
| **S4** Confirmation dialog shows terminal title (not UUID); no stale "awaiting input" | ✅ Call B (`pwsh`) dialog showed **pwsh** title; Call A auto-approved from prior history (expected) | ✅ After clearing prior approval, dialog showed **pwsh** title, no stale prompts | ✅ Fresh-pattern calls executed silently (no dialogs), output produced |
| **S5** Autopilot auto-answers `ask_questions` without showing carousel | — | — | ⚠️ **carousel still appeared** and required manual input — see #309524. `send_to_terminal` under bypass: ✅ no dialogs, all 3 sends ran. |

### What worked as advertised
- Foreground `get_terminal_output` (live reads during running commands)
- Foreground `send_to_terminal` (REPL + interactive script prompts)
- Background terminal parity
- Carousel Focus-Terminal button + click-to-focus
- Carousel auto-dismiss when user types directly in terminal
- "User is replying directly" message reaching the agent
- Per-answer progress-message context
- Confirmation dialog showing terminal title instead of UUID
- No stale "awaiting input" dialogs anywhere in the session
- Bypass approvals suppressing all `send_to_terminal` dialogs

### Findings filed
- #309523 — `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications: false` still fires completion notifications for foreground terminals.
- #309524 — Section 5 unreachable: autopilot instruction block forbids `ask_questions`, and forced `ask_questions` still shows an interactive carousel.

Title: Show "Deferring to user's input in the terminal" instead of "Skipped" when questions are dismissed by terminal input

Description:
When the ask-questions carousel is dismissed because the user typed directly in the terminal, the UI currently shows a generic "Skipped" message. This is misleading — the questions weren't skipped by the user clicking skip; they were dismissed because the user is answering prompts directly in the terminal.

**Expected behavior:** When questions are auto-dismissed due to terminal input, the summary message should read **"Deferring to user's input in the terminal"** instead of "Skipped".

**Actual behavior:** The message says "Skipped" regardless of why the carousel was dismissed.

The fix is in `renderSkippedMessage()` in `chatQuestionCarouselPart.ts` — check `carousel.dismissedByTerminalInput` and use an appropriate message.

Comments:
Idea from @eli-w-king 
Sharing media from merged PR #309092 for verification:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a1f6d4d6-40b9-4486-8302-dce71957a7c9

Source PR: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/309092

Title: Terminal: Add 'Move Terminal Tab Up/Down' commands to reorder tabs

Description:
## Summary

Currently there is no built-in command to reorder terminal tab groups without drag-and-drop, and no way to bulk-close terminals below a given tab. This makes keyboard-driven terminal management cumbersome.

## Feature Request

Add three commands:

- **`Terminal: Move Terminal Tab Up`** (`workbench.action.terminal.moveTabUp`)
- **`Terminal: Move Terminal Tab Down`** (`workbench.action.terminal.moveTabDown`)
- **`Terminal: Kill Terminals Below`** (`workbench.action.terminal.killGroupsBelow`)

**Move Up / Move Down:**
- Accessible via the Command Palette
- Bindable to custom keybindings
- Active when at least 2 terminal groups are visible

**Kill Terminals Below:**
- Accessible via the terminal tab right-click context menu and Command Palette
- Kills all terminal groups below the selected one (analogous to "Close to the Right" in editor tabs)
- Active when at least 2 terminal groups are visible

## Use Case

Users who rely on keyboard navigation or prefer not to use drag-and-drop have no way to reorder terminal tabs or quickly close a set of terminals below a given point. These commands close that gap and enable full keyboard-driven terminal management.

Comments:
<!-- 6d457af9-96bd-47a8-a0e8-ecf120dfffc1 -->
This feature request is now a candidate for our backlog. The community has 60 days to [upvote](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Issues-Triaging#up-voting-a-feature-request) the issue. If it receives 20 upvotes we will move it to our backlog. If not, we will close it. To learn more about how we handle feature requests, please see our [documentation](https://aka.ms/vscode-issue-lifecycle).

Happy Coding!
Implemented in #309084.

- **Move Terminal Tab Up** / **Move Terminal Tab Down** — keyboard/Command Palette commands to reorder terminal groups (active when ≥2 groups exist)
- **Kill Terminals Below** — right-click context menu + Command Palette command to close all terminal groups below the selected one

Move Up/Down follow the editor tabs convention and are not exposed in the context menu.
