Title: MCP client: Extension host crashes when authenticating with /common authority on multi-account/multi-tenants machines (tag_7cisv)

Description:

Type: <b>Bug</b>

When an MCP server uses `/common` as the OAuth authority, and the machine has multiple accounts in WAM (e.g., a personal MSA + a work account), the VS Code extension host crashes with `tag_7cisv, StatusInternal::Unexpected`.

###steps to reprpduce
1. Windows 11 machine has both personal (e.g., outlook.com) and working account (e.g. microsoft.com) signed in. Each account also has accociated with multiple tenants.

2. Configure an MCP server (type: http) that returns OAuth metadata 
   pointing to `https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/v2.0`

3. VS Code prompts "MCP Server wants to authenticate to Microsoft" and prompts use to select account

4. Accept the prompt → log into working account --> extension host crashes

- MSAL logs: `No HomeAccountId provided to normalize the realm`
- MSAL logs: `Attempted to read cache with a non-normalized realm`
- WAM throws: `tag_7cisv, StatusInternal::Unexpected` (multiple times)
- Extension host terminates

### Workarounds Found
1. MCP server uses `/organizations` instead of `/common` (excludes personal MSA)
2. MCP server uses `/<tenant-id>` (pins to specific tenant)

### Environment
- VS Code: 1.1.15
- OS: Windows 11
- @azure/msal-node-extensions: 1.5.25
- Native Broker: enabled (true)
- Accounts in WAM: 2 (1 personal MSA with 1 tenants + 1 work/school with 2 tenants)

VS Code version: Code 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b, 2026-04-07T22:04:57Z)
OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
Modes:

<details>
<summary>System Info</summary>

|Item|Value|

Title: Ask-to-Workflow Handoff (not only Ask-to-Agent)

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

Absolutely, and that makes the feature much stronger.

Add this extension to the spec:

**Capability Addition**
Ask-to-Workflow Handoff (not only Ask-to-Agent)

**User Story**
As a developer, I want to hand off my Ask output to a named workflow (for example, Report a bug workflow) instead of a generic coding agent, so execution follows a structured process and template.

**Examples**
- Hand off to Report a bug workflow
- Hand off to Security review workflow
- Hand off to Refactor planning workflow
- Hand off to Release readiness workflow

**Expected Behavior**
1. Ask mode supports: Send to Workflow.
2. User can pick a workflow from a registry/list.
3. Ask context is packaged into workflow inputs automatically.
4. Workflow runs in background while Ask remains interactive.
5. Ask receives workflow stage updates and final structured outputs.

**Workflow Contract Requirements**
1. Workflow metadata:
- name
- purpose
- required inputs
- optional inputs
- output schema
2. Workflow execution states:
- queued
- running
- waiting for input
- completed
- failed
3. Workflow output contract:
- summary
- artifacts generated
- actions taken
- unresolved items

**Acceptance Criteria**
1. User can invoke named workflow from Ask with one action.
2. Workflow can run concurrently with ongoing Ask conversation.
3. Ask can query workflow status and retrieve final artifacts.
4. If workflow requires more input, Ask can supply it without mode switching.
5. Same handoff model works for both Agent and Workflow targets.

**Suggested UX**
- Send to Agent
- Send to Workflow
- Target picker: Agent | Workflow
- Workflow picker: Report a bug workflow, etc.

This gives you a generalized handoff model:
Ask -> Execution Target, where Execution Target can be Agent or Workflow.

Comments:

If it receives 20 upvotes we will move it to our backlog. If not, we will close it. To learn more about how we handle feature requests, please see our [documentation](https://aka.ms/vscode-issue-lifecycle).

Happy Coding!

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*

Comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error "potential listener LEAK detected, dominated" is a **listener leak** in the language status → statusbar entry pipeline. Every time an extension updates a language status item (via `$setLanguageStatus`), the old registration is disposed **after** the new one is created, causing a transient window where duplicate entries with the same ID exist. The `_update` loop in `languageStatus.ts` fails to handle this duplicate, leaking a statusbar entry accessor and its listener on the `onDidOverrideEntry` emitter. Over time, leaked listeners accumulate until the emitter's threshold is exceeded and it refuses new listeners.

This is **not a recent regression** — telemetry shows this error present across all versions from 1.113.0 through 1.117.0, with high hit counts on stable releases (e.g., 492K hits on 1.115.0, 580K on 1.114.0, 802K on 1.113.0).

### Culprit Commit

No specific culprit commit was identified. This is a long-standing design issue in the interaction between `mainThreadLanguages.ts` and `languageStatus.ts`. The bug exists across all tracked versions (1.113.0–1.117.0) and is not tied to a recent change.

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Ext as Extension (via RPC)
    participant MTL as mainThreadLanguages.$setLanguageStatus
    participant LSS as LanguageStatusService.addStatus
    participant LFR as LanguageFeatureRegistry.register
    participant LS as LanguageStatus._update
    participant SB as StatusbarPart.doAddEntry

    Ext->>MTL: $setLanguageStatus(handle, newStatus)
    Note over MTL: Evaluates addStatus() BEFORE<br/>DisposableMap.set() disposes old

    MTL->>LSS: addStatus(newStatus)
    LSS->>LFR: register(selector, newStatus)
    Note over LFR: ⚠️ Root cause: old registration<br/>still alive — both old + new coexist
    LFR->>LFR: onDidChange.fire()

    LFR->>LS: _update() [sync via event]
    Note over LS: model.dedicated has DUPLICATE<br/>entries with same status.id

    LS->>LS: Loop iteration 1: finds old accessor<br/>in _dedicatedEntries → update + delete
    LS->>SB: Loop iteration 2: not found →<br/>addEntry() creates NEW accessor
    Note over SB: 💥 New listener added to<br/>onDidOverrideEntry emitter

    LS->>LS: newDedicatedEntries.set(id, newAccessor)<br/>overwrites old accessor — LEAKED!

    MTL->>MTL: DisposableMap.set() now disposes old
    Note over MTL: Old registration removed,<br/>but old accessor already leaked
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadLanguages.ts` | **Root cause** — `$setLanguageStatus` evaluates `addStatus()` before `set()` disposes old value |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/languageStatus/browser/languageStatus.ts` | **Leak site** — `_update()` loop doesn't handle duplicate IDs; overwrites accessor in map without disposing |
| `src/vs/workbench/browser/parts/statusbar/statusbarPart.ts` | **Crash site** — `doAddEntry()` subscribes to `onDidOverrideEntry.event`; accumulated leaked listeners trigger `ListenerRefusalError` |
| `src/vs/workbench/services/languageStatus/common/languageStatusService.ts` | Data flow — `addStatus` delegates to `LanguageFeatureRegistry.register` |
| `src/vs/editor/common/languageFeatureRegistry.ts` | Data flow — `register()` fires `onDidChange` synchronously |
| `src/vs/base/common/event.ts` | Detection — `ListenerRefusalError` thrown when listener count exceeds threshold2 |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code with an extension that frequently updates language status (e.g., TypeScript, Copilot, or any language server that calls `vscode.languages.createLanguageStatusItem` and updates it repeatedly)
2. Have a language status item marked as "dedicated" (pinned to statusbar)
3. The extension repeatedly calls `$setLanguageStatus` for the same handle — each call leaks one listener
4. After enough updates (threshold2 calls), the `onDidOverrideEntry` emitter refuses new listeners and throws `ListenerRefusalError`
5. The error is non-deterministic in timing — depends on how frequently extensions update their language status items. More active extensions (e.g., TypeScript language server during heavy editing) will trigger it sooner.

### Fix Proposal

**Option A (preferred) — Fix in `mainThreadLanguages.ts`**: Dispose the old registration BEFORE creating the new one, eliminating the window where duplicates coexist.

**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadLanguages.ts` → `$setLanguageStatus()`
**Approach**: Call `this._status.deleteAndDispose(handle)` before `this._status.set(handle, this._languageStatusService.addStatus(status))`. This ensures the old registration is removed from `LanguageFeatureRegistry` before the new one is added, so `_update` never sees duplicate entries.

**Option B (defense-in-depth) — Fix in `languageStatus.ts`**: In the `_update` loop over `model.dedicated`, deduplicate by `status.id` before iterating, or track and dispose overwritten accessors after the loop.

**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/languageStatus/browser/languageStatus.ts` → `_update()`
**Approach**: After the loop, check `newDedicatedEntries` for any accessor that was overwritten (not present in the final map but also not in `_dedicatedEntries` for cleanup). Alternatively, deduplicate `model.dedicated` by ID before the loop.

### Recommended Owner

`@jrieken` — owns the API area (which includes `mainThreadLanguages.ts` and the language status API surface) and performance/leak investigations per working areas.

> Generated by [errors-investigate](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/runs/24530825899/agentic_workflow) · ● 6.2M · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+microsoft%2Fvscode-engineering%2Ferrors-investigate%22&type=issues)

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*

Comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The `InlineEditsWordReplacementView` crashes with `Cannot read properties of null (reading 'getLineContent')` when an editor tab is closed while an inline edit suggestion is visible. The `_renderTextEffect` derived observable uses a non-null assertion (`!`) on `this._editor.model.get()`, but during editor closure the model is set to `null` and the observable recomputation chain attempts to read the now-null model. This is a high-impact, long-standing bug affecting **all platforms** across versions 1.110.0–1.117.0, with 195,859 hits from 130,689 users on 1.115.0 alone.

### Culprit Commit

No single recent commit introduced this regression. The unsafe non-null assertion `this._editor.model.get()!` has been present since the `_renderTextEffect` derived observable was created as part of the inline edits word replacement view. The telemetry bucket shows consistent hits across all versions from 1.110.0 onward — this is a latent bug in the feature, not a sharp regression from a specific commit.

### Code Flow

````mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant CloseEditors as EditorGroupView.closeEditors
    participant SetModel as CodeEditorWidget.setModel(null)
    participant Observable as Observable update chain
    participant RenderText as _renderTextEffect (derived)
    participant GetModel as _editor.model.get()

    CloseEditors->>SetModel: clearInput → setModel(null)
    SetModel->>Observable: endUpdate → propagate changes
    Observable->>RenderText: recompute derived value
    RenderText->>GetModel: this._editor.model.get()!
    Note over GetModel: ⚠️ Root cause:<br/>model is null, but !<br/>asserts non-null
    GetModel-->>RenderText: returns null
    RenderText->>RenderText: tm.getLineContent(...)
    Note over RenderText: 💥 TypeError: Cannot read<br/>properties of null<br/>(reading 'getLineContent')
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/editor/contrib/inlineCompletions/browser/view/inlineEdits/inlineEditsViews/inlineEditsWordReplacementView.ts` | **Crash site & root cause** — line 98: `this._editor.model.get()!` with unsafe non-null assertion |
| `src/vs/editor/contrib/inlineCompletions/browser/view/inlineEdits/utils/utils.ts` | Data flow — `mapOutFalsy` observable wrapper in the derived chain (line 377) |
| `src/vs/base/common/observableInternal/observables/derivedImpl.ts` | Framework — derived observable recomputation engine (stable, not at fault) |

### Repro Steps

1. Open a file in VS Code that triggers inline edit / inline completion suggestions (e.g., type code that triggers Copilot inline suggestions)
2. While an inline edit suggestion is visible (the word replacement overlay is rendered), close the editor tab (Ctrl+W or click the X)
3. The editor model is set to null during the close sequence, but the observable update propagation triggers a recomputation of `_renderTextEffect` before the view is disposed
4. The error fires during the observable `endUpdate` chain

**Note**: This is a race condition between editor disposal and observable recomputation. It occurs more frequently when inline edit suggestions are actively displayed during tab close.

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/editor/contrib/inlineCompletions/browser/view/inlineEdits/inlineEditsViews/inlineEditsWordReplacementView.ts` → `_renderTextEffect` derived (line 97–112)

**Approach**: Replace the unsafe non-null assertion `this._editor.model.get()!` with a proper null guard. If the model is `null`, the derived should return early (the view is being disposed anyway, so no rendering is needed):

```
const tm = this._editor.model.get();
if (!tm) { return; }
````

This is the same pattern used in `_layout` (line 122: `if (!widgetStart || !widgetEnd ...) { return undefined; }`) — guard against transient null state during disposal. An existing community PR (#309155) proposes exactly this fix.

### Recommended Owner

`@hediet` — feature area owner of inline edits/completions views (author of the `InlineEditsWordReplacementView` class, PRs #293509, #282719; MEMBER with `author_association: MEMBER`)

| Platforms | Windows, Mac, Linux |
| Product | VSCode |

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*

Comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error `potential listener LEAK detected, popular` fires when the notebook outline panel renders many entries simultaneously. Each visible outline entry creates its own `IMenu` instance via `menuService.createMenu(MenuId.NotebookOutlineActionMenu, ...)`, and each menu's lazy listener subscribes to the **shared singleton** `PersistedMenuHideState.onDidChange` emitter. With enough visible entries (large notebooks with many cells/headings), the listener count on this shared emitter exceeds the leak detection threshold, triggering the error. This affects all platforms (Windows, Mac, Linux) and has been present across versions 1.113–1.117.

### Culprit Commit

No single culprit commit was identified. This is a **long-standing architectural pattern issue** — the per-element menu creation pattern in the notebook outline has existed for multiple release cycles. Telemetry shows hits starting from at least v1.113.0 (March 2026). There is no commit range narrowing available from the regression scan, and the error appears consistently across all recent versions. The issue was likely always latent and triggered whenever a user opens a notebook with enough cells to fill the outline panel.

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Tree as Tree/ListView
    participant Renderer as NotebookOutlineRenderer
    participant MenuSvc as MenuService
    participant MenuImpl as MenuImpl (per entry)
    participant HiddenState as PersistedMenuHideState (singleton)

    Tree->>Renderer: renderElement() [×N entries]
    Renderer->>MenuSvc: createMenu(NotebookOutlineActionMenu)
    MenuSvc-->>MenuImpl: new MenuImpl(hiddenStates)
    Renderer->>MenuImpl: menu.onDidChange(...)
    Note over MenuImpl: onWillAddFirstListener fires
    MenuImpl->>HiddenState: hiddenStates.onDidChange(...)
    Note over HiddenState: ⚠️ Listener #N added<br/>to shared singleton emitter
    HiddenState-->>HiddenState: 💥 Leak threshold exceeded<br/>ListenerLeakError("popular")
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/contrib/outline/notebookOutline.ts` | **Root cause** — `renderElement()` (L184) creates a per-element `IMenu`; `setupToolbarListeners()` (L223) subscribes to `menu.onDidChange`, triggering the lazy listener chain |
| `src/vs/platform/actions/common/menuService.ts` | `MenuImpl` constructor (L427-440) — `startLazyListener` subscribes to shared `hiddenStates.onDidChange` on first `menu.onDidChange` subscriber |
| `src/vs/platform/actions/common/menuService.ts` | `PersistedMenuHideState` (L54-60) — singleton emitter that receives one listener per active menu instance |
| `src/vs/base/common/event.ts` | Leak detection infrastructure (L1261-1264) — monitors listener counts and fires `ListenerLeakError` |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code with a notebook file containing **30+ cells** (mix of markdown and code cells)
2. Open the **Outline** panel in the sidebar (View → Open View → Outline)
3. Expand the outline so many entries are visible simultaneously
4. The error fires in telemetry as the tree renders all visible entries, each creating a menu that subscribes to the shared `PersistedMenuHideState.onDidChange`
5. The more cells visible in the outline, the more likely the threshold is exceeded

### Fix Proposal

**Fix location**: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/contrib/outline/notebookOutline.ts` → `renderElement()` / `setupToolbarListeners()`

**Approach**: Instead of creating a **separate `IMenu` per outline entry**, share a single `IMenu` instance across all entries (scoped to the renderer or the outline pane lifecycle). The menu actions can be computed on-demand during `renderElement` by temporarily updating the scoped context keys, calling `menu.getActions()`, and setting the toolbar. The shared menu's `onDidChange` listener would trigger a refresh of all visible entries, but only one listener would exist on `PersistedMenuHideState.onDidChange` instead of N.

Alternatively, a lighter fix: create the menu in `renderElement` as today, but **do not subscribe to `menu.onDidChange`** — instead, rely on the tree's natural re-render cycle to update toolbar actions. Since the outline tree already refreshes when the notebook model changes, the `onDidChange` subscription may be redundant for this use case. This matches the pattern noted in the code comment at L218: `// same fix as in cellToolbars setupListeners re #103926`.

### Recommended Owner

`@Yoyokrazy` — area owner for notebook toolbars (global toolbar, cell toolbar, context menu) per `triage/working-areas.md`

| Product | VSCode |

---
*This issue was automatically created from the VS Code Errors Dashboard*

Comments:
## 🔍 Error Investigation

### Summary

The error `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'toString')` occurs in the inline edits line replacement view when calling `.toString()` on a `Color` value returned by `getEditorBlendedColor()` that is `undefined`. This happens because the observable framework's `_recompute` method catches errors thrown during color resolution (via `onBugIndicatingError`) and leaves the derived value as `undefined`, which then propagates to the `.map(c => c.toString())` callback. The error affects all platforms (Linux, Windows, Mac) and has been persistent across versions 1.110.0–1.117.0 with **millions of total hits** (~1.6M on 1.115.0 stable alone).

### Culprit Commit

No specific culprit commit was identified — the vulnerable pattern (`getEditorBlendedColor(...).map(c => c.toString())` without null guard) has been present since the file was first created. The error was not introduced by a regression in a particular commit range; rather, it surfaces whenever the theme color resolution fails during observable recomputation (e.g., theme transitions, custom themes with incomplete color definitions).

### Code Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Theme as ThemeService
    participant ObsColor as observeColor()
    participant Derived as derivedImpl._recompute()
    participant Blend as getEditorBlendedColor()
    participant View as LineReplacementView L251

    View->>Blend: getEditorBlendedColor(originalBorderColor, themeService)
    Blend->>ObsColor: observeColor(colorId, themeService)
    ObsColor->>Theme: getColorTheme().getColor(colorId)
    Note over Theme: Color not found (undefined)
    ObsColor-->>Derived: throw BugIndicatingError("Missing color")
    Note over Derived: catch (e) { onBugIndicatingError(e) }<br/>_value remains undefined
    Derived-->>Blend: returns undefined (not Color)
    Blend-->>View: observable value = undefined
    View->>View: .map(c => `1px solid \$\{c.toString()}`)
    Note over View: 💥 TypeError: Cannot read properties<br/>of undefined (reading 'toString')
```

### Affected Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/editor/contrib/inlineCompletions/browser/view/inlineEdits/inlineEditsViews/inlineEditsLineReplacementView.ts` | **Crash site** — line 251 calls `.toString()` on potentially `undefined` color |
| `src/vs/editor/contrib/inlineCompletions/browser/view/inlineEdits/theme.ts` | **Root cause** — `getEditorBlendedColor()` and `observeColor()` throw `BugIndicatingError` when color missing, which is swallowed by observable framework |
| `src/vs/base/common/observableInternal/observables/derivedImpl.ts` | **Error absorption** — `_recompute()` catches all errors (line 226), leaving `_value` as `undefined` |
| `src/vs/editor/contrib/inlineCompletions/browser/view/inlineEdits/inlineEditsViews/inlineEditsSideBySideView.ts` | **Same pattern** — lines 453, 488 have identical unguarded `.toString()` calls |

### Repro Steps

1. Open VS Code with a custom/non-default color theme (one that may not fully define `inlineEdit.originalBorder` or `inlineEdit.tabWillAcceptOriginalBorder` colors)
2. Open a file and trigger an inline edit suggestion (e.g., via Copilot inline completions)
3. The inline edit overlay renders, triggering the observable chain
4. If the theme color lookup returns `undefined` for one of the border colors (or during a theme transition), the error fires
5. The error is non-deterministic — it depends on theme configuration and timing of observable evaluation

### Fix Proposal

**Option A (call-site guards — immediate fix):** Add null guards at each `.map(c => c.toString())` call site.

**Fix location**: `inlineEditsLineReplacementView.ts` line 251, `inlineEditsSideBySideView.ts` lines 453/488, and similar patterns in `jumpToView.ts` and `inlineEditsCustomView.ts`
**Approach**: Change `.map(c => \`1px solid \$\{c.toString()}\`)` to `.map(c => c ? \`1px solid \$\{c.toString()}\` : undefined)` (or use optional chaining).

**Option B (root cause fix — preferred):** Make `getEditorBlendedColor` return a fallback `Color` instead of propagating `undefined` when `observeColor` fails. This would prevent undefined from ever reaching callers.

**Fix location**: `theme.ts` → `getEditorBlendedColor()` or `observeColor()`
**Approach**: Instead of throwing `BugIndicatingError` when a color is missing, return a sensible default (e.g., `Color.transparent` or `Color.black`), or add a fallback in `getEditorBlendedColor`'s `.map()` chain.

> **Note:** Community PR #309156 implements Option A (null guards). It may need to be extended to cover `jumpToView.ts` and `inlineEditsCustomView.ts` which have the same unguarded pattern.

### Recommended Owner

`@hediet` — inline completions feature area owner, authored the original code, and is already assigned as reviewer on the community fix PR #309156.

Title: Integrated terminal on Windows can start with stale PATH and fail to resolve npm while standalone PowerShell works

Description:

Type: <b>Bug</b>

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?
I have not fully verified that yet.

VS Code Version
VS Code Insiders

OS Version
Windows

Repro Steps

Install Node.js on Windows so that the installer adds C:\Program Files\nodejs to PATH.
Open VS Code Insiders.
Open an integrated terminal using PowerShell 7 (pwsh).
Run:
npm
In some integrated terminal sessions, PowerShell reports:
npm: The term 'npm' is not recognized as a name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or executable program.
Check the spelling of the name, or if a path was included, verify that the path is correct and try again.
Open standalone PowerShell outside VS Code and run:
npm -v
It works correctly there.
Expected Behavior
A newly created VS Code integrated terminal should inherit the current system/user PATH and resolve npm the same way standalone PowerShell does.

Actual Behavior
Some VS Code integrated terminal sessions start without C:\Program Files\nodejs in $env:Path, so npm cannot be resolved, even though:

Node.js is correctly installed
npm.cmd exists on disk
system/user PATH already contains C:\Program Files\nodejs
standalone PowerShell resolves npm correctly
Verified Details
On my machine:

C:\Program Files\nodejs\node.exe exists
C:\Program Files\nodejs\npm.cmd exists
User PATH contains C:\Program Files\nodejs
Machine PATH contains C:\Program Files\nodejs
Standalone pwsh can resolve npm
In the failing VS Code integrated terminal session, $env:Path did not contain C:\Program Files\nodejs
I also checked my VS Code user settings and did not have any of these configured:

terminal.integrated.env.windows
terminal.integrated.inheritEnv
terminal.integrated.profiles.windows
terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.windows
So this does not appear to be caused by a custom PATH override in settings.

Workarounds
These workarounds fixed it:

Refresh PATH manually in the current terminal:
$env:Path = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('Path','Machine') + ';' + [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('Path','User')

Disable terminal persistent sessions:
"terminal.integrated.enablePersistentSessions": false,
"terminal.integrated.persistentSessionReviveProcess": "never"

Add a PowerShell profile workaround for VS Code terminals:
if ($env:TERM_PROGRAM -eq 'vscode') {
$machinePath = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('Path', 'Machine')
$userPath = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('Path', 'User')
$pathEntries = @($machinePath, $userPath) -split ';' | Where-Object { $_ }
$env:Path = ($pathEntries | Select-Object -Unique) -join ';'
}

After this, newly created integrated terminals could resolve npm.

Additional Notes
This looks like the integrated terminal may be reusing or reviving a stale environment instead of always starting a truly fresh shell environment on Windows, especially after PATH changes from software installation.

Title: [TPI] Enable BYOK for business and enterprise users

Description:
Refs https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/281199

- [x] anyOS @eleanorjboyd 
- [x] anyOS @cwebster-99

Complexity: 4


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23309023%0A%0A&assignees=pierceboggan)

---

Enterprise and business users can now use BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) model providers in Copilot Chat, which was previously limited to individual users.

## Testing

### Enterprise/Business with BYOK enabled
1. Sign in with an enterprise or business Copilot account
2. Open Copilot Chat and go to the model picker
3. Select "Manage Models..." and verify BYOK providers are available (Azure OpenAI, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Ollama, etc.)
4. Add a BYOK model provider with a valid API key
5. Select the BYOK model and send a chat  verify it works end-to-endmessage 
6. Verify the model appears in the model picker and persists across restarts

### Enterprise/Business without BYOK setting enabled
1. Sign in with an enterprise or business account that does NOT have BYOK enabled via admin policy
2. Verify BYOK options are appropriately hidden or disabled
3. Ensure the standard Copilot-provided models still work normally

### Individual users (regression check)
1. Sign in with an individual Copilot account
2. Verify BYOK still works as  add a provider, select a model, chatbefore 
3. Ensure no regressions in the individual BYOK flow

### General checks
- Verify model picker shows correct labels for BYOK vs Copilot-provided models
- Verify error handling for invalid API keys or unreachable endpoints
- Test switching between BYOK and Copilot-provided models mid-conversation

Title: VS Code main process hits ~4GB V8 heap and crashes all windows; repeated document/editor lifecycle errors from Pylance before crash

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->

### VS Code Version

  Version: 1.115.0
  Commit: 41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b
  Architecture: x64

  ### OS Version

  Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS (Noble) x64

  ### Relevant Extensions

  - ms-python.python 2026.4.0
  - ms-python.vscode-pylance 2026.2.1
  - ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh 0.122.0

  ### Problem

  VS Code frequently crashes without warning, and when it happens, **all open VS Code windows crash together**.

  From user-level logs, the direct crash reason is always the **VS Code main process** hitting the V8 heap limit at around 4 GB:

  `OOM error in V8: Ineffective mark-compacts near heap limit Allocation failed - JavaScript heap out of memory`

  This does **not** look like a kernel OOM kill or GPU/driver reset. I checked kernel logs and did not see `oom-killer`, `Killed process`,
  `segfault`, `gpu hang`, `amdgpu reset`, etc.

  ### What I observed in the crashing session

  Latest crash:
  - Local time: `2026-04-10 21:57:02 +08:00`
  - Main process: `code.desktop[910430]`

  User journal shows:
  - heap near `3962.9 / 4000.8 MB`
  - then V8 OOM in the main process
  - then all VS Code windows exit together

  In the corresponding log session (`~/.config/Code/logs/20260410T190336`), I did **not** see extension host restart loops. Each window's extension
  host appears to start once and remain running.

  However, I repeatedly see document/editor lifecycle related errors from Pylance in multiple windows.

  Typical stack shape:
  - `DiagnosticRequestor.forgetDocument`
  - `DiagnosticFeatureProviderImpl.cleanUpDocument`
  - `updateVisibleDocuments`
  - `Bg.acceptDocumentsAndEditorsDelta`
  - `Bg.$acceptDocumentsAndEditorsDelta`

  I also see repeated warnings like:
  - `DiagnosticCollection with name 'Pylance' does already exist.`
  - `Error: illegal state - object is disposed`
  - `TextEditor is closed/disposed`

  These continue for hours and are still present close to the final crash.

  ### Why I am filing this in vscode and not only in Pylance

  The app-level symptom is a **shared main process OOM that takes down all windows**, not just a single extension host crash.

  The extension-side logs strongly suggest that Pylance is one trigger or reproducer, but I am not sure whether this is:
  1. a bug in Pylance itself,
  2. a VS Code lifecycle regression affecting extensions handling document/editor deltas,
  3. or an interaction between the two.

  I am filing here because the final failure is a VS Code main process crash, and there may be a core memory leak / retention issue after repeated
  document/editor lifecycle churn.

  ### Approximate repro pattern

  I do not yet have a minimal repro, but this is the rough pattern that seems to trigger it:

  1. Open multiple VS Code windows.
  2. At least one window uses Remote-SSH.
  3. Open/edit/close Python files in one or more windows so that Python + Pylance activate.
  4. Keep VS Code running for a while and continue normal editing/navigation.
  5. Eventually the shared VS Code process grows until it reaches ~4 GB heap and all windows crash together.

  ### Additional observations

  - In this specific session, Remote-SSH did log `PendingMigrationError: navigator is now a global in nodejs`, but the remote authority still
  resolved successfully, so this did not look like the immediate crash trigger.
  - I had previously removed GitLens from my profile, but in this exact crashing process it had already been loaded before uninstall, so I cannot
  use this session as a clean “without GitLens” control.
  - The strongest active errors close to the crash come from Pylance/document lifecycle handling, not from extension host restarts.



Comments:
Agree. The OOM error occurs frequently especially under SSH connection. I experience this multiple times every day.

Title: Not showing code in proper block and indent

Description:

Type: <b>Bug</b>

### Problem Description

When generating code outputs using **Codex (model 5.3, medium reasoning level)**, the responses frequently fail to properly format code blocks.

Instead of wrapping code in Markdown-style triple backticks (` ``` `), the model often returns code mixed directly with plain text. This leads to several problems:

* Code is **not enclosed in a code block**, making it harder to read or copy.
* The output appears as **merged text and code**, without clear separation.
* **Indentation is often broken or missing**, especially in Python, where indentation is critical.
* Syntax highlighting is lost due to missing Markdown formatting.
* The issue occurs **consistently across many different code outputs**, not just isolated cases.


<img width="939" height="969" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ed533153-b07f-449a-b14b-d963e0f433eb" />


---

### Expected Behavior

* All generated code should be wrapped in proper Markdown code blocks:

  ````
  ```python
  # example code
  ````

  ```
  ```
* Code should preserve **correct indentation and structure**.
* Text explanations and code should be **clearly separated**.




---

### Actual Behavior

* Code is embedded inline with normal text.
* No Markdown code fences are used.
* Indentation is flattened or inconsistent.
* Output readability and usability are significantly reduced.

---

### Impact

* Makes it difficult to copy and execute code.
* Increases the likelihood of syntax errors (especially in Python).
* Reduces developer productivity and trust in generated outputs.

---

### Additional Notes

* The issue appears frequently even with a well-known, stable model configuration.
* It does not seem tied to a specific prompt or language.
* Occurs across multiple sessions and different types of coding tasks.

Title: Offline Mode property / param in settings. Limit of ellevations, connections, socketing and fs reading overall.

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->

//  Enable offline Mode in VSCIDE

<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->

<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->


<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
While this is doable / possible after running many OS restrictions, network restrictions and akins, most productive environments can't coope with all that is necessary to ensure they can safely run VsCode without ending up pinging microsoft, sharing buffers and IPC sockets, unrestricted in terms of anything that is not "execution permissions at filesystem / kernel level.".

Enable a way to run totally offline, disconnected from any feedback, ping, or socket hook.
Ideally even opt out of some kernel comunication, limit fs integration and etc.
Don't open agents, communications or any sybling proccess while running this.

Reasoning and goals :

Avoiding mapping and sharing anything when running privacy critical mode, and enable running in a more "restricted" environment that may work akin to Don't trust project, but related to NetworkINtegration and Socket.

If this is analyzed and intended to be developed, I'm open to help implement the effort.


Comments:
Running without extensions enabled and with updates turned off should get you pretty close.  If you have any specific asks that aren't solved after configuring these two options, please share.

---

Title: feature request: add function like `languages.match()` for `Uri` in extension API

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
For extension authoring, I request **a function to test whether a language identifier or a glob pattern matches a file path (`Uri` or `string`)** in the extension API.

According to [the official document about glob patterns](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/editor/glob-patterns), VS code has its own pattern matcher. I think `languages.match()` uses this but it is for `TextDocument`, i.e., a file open in the editor. I think there are cases where extensions want to know the language of file not open in an editor.

## Scenario this function is required

* A extension can handle files in two or more different format using different language identifiers, e.g., `"{ *.foo": "foo-lang"}` and `{*.bar": "bar-lang"}`. 
* The extension can show these kind of files in a webview or so, without a document editor.
* The extension wants to implement for context menu in explorer view for opening a file in a webview, for example. `when` clause in package.json limits the command (e.g., `"when": "resourceLangId =~ /^(foo|bar)-lang$/"`
* A command handler receives the file URI. Then it wants to know which languages the URI is to use appropriate parser. User may have customized the `files.associations` setting and so, just comparing the file extension with the default extensions (e.g., `".foo""` or `".bar"`) is not a solution. Here the request feature will be helpful.

The extension here may be similar to the built-in markdown extension. Its preview can also be open without an editor. A big difference is that it only handle one language and so, the "when" clause guarantees the language of the file.

Even for extensions that use a built-in text editor, in some situations it will be helpful if they can know the language of a file before it is opened.

## Workaround using the current API and Its Limitation 

Though `when` clause in package.json correctly reflects this setting, from the extension's code it seems difficult to check it. Extensions can read the glob patterns in `files.associations` setting via `WorkspaceConfiguration`'s `get()` method but anyway VS Code's built-in matcher is unavailable for URI or string. There are several popular their-party matchers but to completely imitate the behavior to VS Code's one is very hard (and maybe impossible). Also it seems there is an (as far as I know) undocumented rules about precedence between items defined in `files.associations`.
- multiple rules that both match: e.g., `*.json` and `*.config.json` (longer one is always preceded?)
- rules defined in global (user) and workspaces
  - Especially when two keys whose case is different, such as `*.foo` in global and `*.FOO` in workspace, the situation becomes difficult. The object returned by `get()` methods contain both key-value pairs but the extension can not judge which one is from which scope. Then one may have to use `inspect()` and then merge it manually.

Practically it will be very hard for extension authors to perfectly imitate VS Code's behavior about the file association within the current API. This is the reason I request the function.

Comments:
I don't understand what this request is about. Yes, VS Code has a custom glob match implementation but that's not incompatible with popular 3rd libraries and extension can use those. I am also confused how `when` condition fit into this

---

Title: Copilot Chat 0.43.0: Claude agent mode silent — tool permission stream closes before response received (hook_6)

Description:
Type: **Bug**

## Summary

When running Copilot Chat in **Claude agent mode**, the chat window is silent during any turn that requires tool calls. The VS Code output panel logs a specific error from the claude-agent-sdk: the tool permission hook stream (`hook_6`) closes before the permission decision arrives, aborting the tool call before any response can be generated. This started approximately 27 March 2026 and has been consistently reproducible since.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open VS Code (1.115) with GitHub Copilot Chat 0.43.0.
2. In Copilot Chat, switch to Claude agent mode (e.g. `claude-sonnet-4.6`).
3. Issue any prompt that requires tool use — file reads, web fetches, shell commands, or git operations.
4. Observe that the chat window shows no activity and no response while the agent is working.
5. Open the VS Code output panel and filter to the Copilot Chat output channel.

## Expected Behaviour

The agent narrates progress and shows tool call activity in the chat window throughout the turn. A final response is delivered when the turn completes.

## Actual Behaviour

The chat window is silent throughout. The output panel logs one or more instances of:

```
[error] claude-agent-sdk stderr: Error in hook callback hook_6: Error: Tool permission stream closed before response received
    at F58.read (file:///home/ciara/.vscode-server/extensions/github.copilot-chat-0.43.0/dist/cli.js:8246:342)
    at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:103:5)
    at async file:///home/ciara/.vscode-server/extensions/github.copilot-chat-0.43.0/dist/cli.js:16742:1569
```

The claude-agent-sdk receives no tool permission response and aborts the tool call. Because the turn never completes, no output is generated for the chat window.

## Session Log Evidence

The Copilot Chat debug log (exported via "Export All Prompts as JSON") shows the symptom directly.

**ChatMLCancelation — aborted turn (duration 157 ms, no work done):**
```json
{
  "id": "37bfd4cc",
  "kind": "request",
  "type": "ChatMLCancelation",
  "name": "Claude Copilot Proxy",
  "metadata": {
    "requestType": "ChatMessages",
    "model": "claude-sonnet-4.6",
    "startTime": "2026-04-10T11:35:40.129Z",
    "endTime": "2026-04-10T11:35:40.286Z",
    "duration": 157
  }
}
```

**Multiple empty-response turns in the same session** (ids: `4d5a311f`, `a799007f`, `51c1061c`) — turns where Claude responded with an empty message because the tool call it depended on was never completed.

**Contrast:** Turns where no tool call was needed completed normally and their responses were relayed to the chat window without issue. The failure is specific to the tool permission stage, not to text relay.

## Analysis

The tool permission hook system in the claude-agent-sdk uses a stream (`hook_6`) to deliver the allow/deny decision for each tool call. That stream is closing before the decision arrives. Possible causes:

1. **Timing regression** — the permission decision now arrives after a threshold that causes the read stream to time out or be garbage-collected.
2. **`use-responses-api` experiment interaction** — two previously closed related issues (#297982, #297879) both had the `use-responses-api` A/B experiment active. If switching from the streaming completions API to the Responses API changes the timing or lifecycle of hook callbacks, this could cause premature stream closure.
3. **SDK version mismatch** — a version incompatibility between the claude-agent-sdk bundled in `cli.js` and the callback contract expected by the extension host.

The obfuscated symbol `F58.read` at `cli.js:8246:342` is the stream reader for the hook callback; the async dispatch at `cli.js:16742:1569` is the main agent loop awaiting the permission result.

## Onset

The problem began approximately **27 March 2026**, coinciding with the VS Code 1.113 release cycle. VS Code 1.113 introduced bridging of MCP servers to Claude agent sessions and changed session listing to use the official Claude agent SDK API. None of the 1.113–1.115 release notes document a change to tool permission or hook callback handling, suggesting the cause may be a server-side experiment rollout rather than a VS Code binary change.

## Related Issues

- microsoft/vscode#297982 — Copilot Chat incorrectly stating MCP tools unavailable (closed not_planned; had `use-responses-api` experiment active)
- microsoft/vscode#297879 — Unable to access any available MCP (closed not_planned; had `use-responses-api` experiment active)

## Environment

Extension version: **0.43.0**
VS Code version: **1.115** (released 2026-04-07)
OS: **Linux x64** (WSL2, kernel 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2)
Running in: VS Code Server (remote/Codespace)

Comments:
## Session Log Attachment

Sanitised Copilot Chat session debug log (exported via "Export All Prompts as JSON") from a session exhibiting this bug.

**Sanitisation applied:**
- `requestMessages` omitted from all entries (contained full system prompt and conversation history)
- File paths redacted to `[HOME]/`
- Context compaction summaries replaced with `[context compaction summary — omitted]`
- Request/server UUIDs preserved (needed for correlation)

**Gist:** https://gist.github.com/minouris/83c0b3a68ef6ef31d93d0bf094e6a9f8

### What the log shows

The session contains 3 prompts, 86 total log entries. Key entries demonstrating the bug:

| Entry ID | Type | Duration | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `37bfd4cc` | `ChatMLCancelation` | 157 ms | Turn immediately aborted — stream closure during tool permission |
| `4d5a311f` | `ChatMLSuccess` | — | Empty response — dependent tool call never completed |
| `a799007f` | `ChatMLSuccess` | — | Empty response — dependent tool call never completed |
| `51c1061c` | `ChatMLSuccess` | — | Empty response — dependent tool call never completed |

For contrast: turns where no tool call was required (e.g. `a64f6731`, `5ae26250`) completed normally and their responses reached the chat window, confirming relay itself works when tool calls do not abort.

The `ChatMLCancelation` at `37bfd4cc` occurred within 157 ms — far too fast to have dispatched or received any tool result. This matches exactly the timing of a permission stream that closes immediately on open.

Thanks for raising this: @minouris 

Confirming this issue — scope is wider than tool-call silencing, and the root cause may be an API routing compatibility break specific to Claude/Anthropic models

We're seeing the same regression on VS Code 1.115.0 (commit 41dd792b) + Copilot Chat 0.43.0, running in WSL2 on Windows with a Copilot Pro+ account.

Visible impact: Claude models produce no response whatsoever in the Copilot Chat panel — the chat window shows no activity, no message, no error regardless of which Claude model is selected. The failure is completely silent to the user.

Log evidence: When Claude Sonnet 4.6 is selected, no [panel/editAgent] log entry appears at all — the substantive request never routes to Claude. Only gpt-4o-mini entries appear for [title] and [progressMessages]. By contrast, selecting GPT-5.2-Codex routes correctly, with gpt-5.2-codex correctly appearing for [panel/editAgent]. This appears to be specific to Claude/Anthropic models — all GPT models tested work correctly end-to-end.

Hypothesis on root cause: Copilot's backend uses separate API paths for Claude vs GPT models — Claude requires routing to Anthropic's native /v1/messages endpoint rather than the OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions path that GPT models use. Third-party proxy projects that wrap the Copilot API explicitly implement this routing fork with a useMessagesApi flag for Claude-family models. If the 0.43.0 agent mode changes (or the VS Code 1.113–1.115 Claude agent SDK bridging changes) broke or regressed that routing fork, Claude requests would fail silently while GPT requests continue working normally — which is exactly what we're observing.

This would also explain why the same pattern has been reported on other OpenAI-compatible frontends (such as Open Web UI) when Claude models are selected — it's not VS Code-specific behaviour, it's a mismatch between how Claude's API responses are structured versus what the agent orchestration layer expects.

Environment:

VS Code: 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b)
Copilot Chat: 0.43.0
OS: WSL2 (Ubuntu), Windows host
Copilot plan: Pro+
Clean single ~/.vscode-server install, no duplicate extensions, no local model overrides in settings.json

Title: Hide "Install" Button from MCP Server Gallery when Server provides neither packages nor remotes

Description:
When the MCP Registry is configured to be used as an allowlist, VSCode allows to only run those MCP Servers that are part of the registry. However, some MCP Servers have custom installation mechanisms and can't be modeled as either `packages` or `remotes`. The MCP Registry Specification acknowledges this:

> For MCP servers that follow a custom installation path or are embedded in applications without standalone packages, use the websiteUrl field to direct users to setup documentation:
>
> ```json
> {
>   "$schema": "https://static.modelcontextprotocol.io/schemas/2025-12-11/server.schema.json",
>   "name": "io.modelcontextprotocol.anonymous/embedded-mcp",
>   "description": "MCP server embedded in a Desktop app",
>   "websiteUrl": "https://anonymous.modelcontextprotocol.io/embedded-mcp-guide",
>   "version": "0.1.0"
> }
> ```
> 
> https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry/blob/main/docs/reference/server-json/generic-server-json.md#server-with-custom-installation-path

There is various practical examples:
- XCode wants to use the command `xcrun mcpbridge`, with the binary being distributed as part of xcode: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/xcode/giving-external-agents-access-to-xcode
- Playwright CLI wants the user to set up the server via `npx playwright init-agents --loop=vscode`: https://playwright.dev/docs/test-agents
- Moderne wants the server to be set up via `mod config agent-tools install`, which generates OS-dependent code: https://docs.moderne.io/user-documentation/agent-tools/mcp/

Still, to make these work with the allowlist, we need to put something in the registry -- and that entry won't have packages or remotes.

However, in VSCode's MCP Gallery, the Server is still shown with an Install button. Clicking it will fail every time with an error message.

<img width="262" height="48" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8f7b7a4e-5359-40ed-9f52-23dd2eee02d7" />

**Instead of failing with an error, VSCode should hide the install button(s) of MCP Server entries without any package or remote.**

Comments:
If the MCP server does not provide any packages, why is it in the gallery at all? Could you clarify the use case? Closing as this seems like the server should not be listed if it provides nothing to install.

Title: Agents app - context convenience features from VS Code

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
In "Agents - Insider" app:
- it would be good to be able to have the nice context features from VS Code such as copy a symbol reference from a code file and paste into the prompt.

e.g. here is the VSCode example:
<img width="1242" height="673" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a6a91237-718b-4334-883a-047344ea1219" />

Comments:
@mjbvz can you maybe help out? I can not copy/paste a method into the chat input in the agents app and get the behaviour of seeing the reference added as context. It works fine in VS Code (even for Copilot CLI sessions). This makes me wonder if there is a contribution missing in the agents app that you maybe recently added?
This is powered by a `DocumentPasteEditProvider`: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/ad2f77efc987779a66ff5d1c6372c9edf87182b0/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/input/editor/chatPasteProviders.ts#L571

Our custom paste providers currently are only in-memory for a given window. They don't write back to the system clipboard or even to a clipboard that can be used across VS Code instances

I looked into this when I first added the feature but the dom clipboard APIs at the time didn't seem to support it and I couldn't figure out how to get it working using the Electron APIs either. Now however I think we support it using using a web mime value (`web application/vnd.code.customData`)

Here's an example where we do something similar today:

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/ad2f77efc987779a66ff5d1c6372c9edf87182b0/src/vs/platform/clipboard/browser/clipboardService.ts#L221
@mjbvz thanks, but just to clarify: this is in the same window, between an editor opened as modal (still same window) and pasting to the chat input in the same window.

Title: VS Code: Optional auto-jump to first error line when opening a file

Description:

Type: <b>Feature Request</b>

Summary
When opening a file that already contains diagnostics, VS Code shows red markers but does not move the cursor to the first error line.
For debugging-heavy workflows, this adds repetitive manual navigation.

Request
Please add an optional setting to auto-jump to the first diagnostic line when a file is opened.

Proposed behavior
New setting (default off), for example:
editor.autoRevealFirstErrorOnOpen: false
When enabled:
On file open, if the file has diagnostics, jump to the first error line.
Optional scope:
current file only
include warnings (optional)
Keep current default behavior unchanged for users who do not want cursor movement.
Why this helps
Reduces repetitive F8 / Problems click navigation
Speeds up fix loops for static analysis and lint-heavy projects
Improves focus in large files with known issues
Current workaround
Manually press F8 after opening each file
Click diagnostics in Problems panel
Environment
VS Code + language diagnostics (e.g., Intelephense / linters / static analyzers)
Reproducible with files that already have diagnostics before opening

VS Code version: Code - Insiders 1.115.0-insider (fc23f2d26631c6a2c4bf9f69506ea74c90a32804, 2026-03-31T17:13:42+01:00)

Title: Copilot Chat should support cross-device cloud conversation memory for repository workflows

Description:

Type: <b>Feature Request</b>

Summary
When using Copilot Chat for multi-day coding tasks, conversation context is lost across devices/sessions unless users manually copy and paste summaries.
For repository-based workflows, this causes repeated setup, repeated debugging, and lower productivity.

Problem
Current behavior requires manual context handoff.
Long-running tasks (static analysis tuning, refactor iterations, toolchain setup) are frequently interrupted by session/device changes.
Users often work mostly with text/code context, so storage size is small and this should be technically feasible.
Expected behavior
Persist chat history in cloud for the signed-in user.
Allow seamless continuation of context across devices.
Optionally bind memory to repository scope.
Optionally allow users to store structured “project memory” in GitHub (private by default, user-controlled).
Actual behavior
New device/session often starts without prior conversational context.
Users must manually reconstruct prior decisions and tool settings.
Why this matters
Reduces repeated troubleshooting.
Improves continuity for complex engineering tasks.
Better user experience for professional development workflows.
Suggested solution
Add opt-in cloud memory for Copilot Chat history.
Add repository-scoped memory mode with clear privacy controls.
Provide retention settings and explicit “forget/reset” controls.
Surface a “resume previous context” entry point when opening the same repo.
Environment
Product: GitHub Copilot Chat
Client: VS Code
Use case: Multi-session static analysis/tooling setup across machines

VS Code version: Code - Insiders 1.115.0-insider (fc23f2d26631c6a2c4bf9f69506ea74c90a32804, 2026-03-31T17:13:42+01:00)
OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.28000
Modes:


<!-- generated by issue reporter -->

Comments:
I really want to +1 this.

I use Copilot Chat across long-running tasks that span multiple days and multiple machines. Right now, losing context between sessions means I have to keep re-explaining prior decisions, tool settings, and what we already tried. That repetition adds a lot of friction.

What would make a big difference:
- Opt-in cloud memory tied to my signed-in account
- Optional repository-scoped memory
- Clear retention controls plus easy forget/reset options
- A simple "resume previous context" prompt when I reopen the same repo

For enterprise users, control and transparency are critical. If this is added, the scope, retention, and reset behavior should be explicit and easy to manage.

Title: VSCode MCP Task Compliance - Terrible UX

Description:
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: Latest (pre-release)
- VS Code Version: 1.110+
- OS Version: Windows 11 Pro 10.0.22631
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent mode (MCP tool calls)
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude Sonnet 4.6, GPT-4.5 (model-independent, issue is in the MCP client layer)
- Logs: N/A (protocol behavior, not a crash)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Configure an MCP server (Streamable HTTP transport) that declares `tasks.requests.tools.call` in its server capabilities
2. The server exposes tools with `execution.taskSupport: "optional"` that wrap async processes (e.g. deploying infrastructure, triggering CI pipelines, running test suites)
3. In Agent mode, invoke one of these tools
4. The server returns a `CreateTaskResult` immediately with `taskId`, `status: "working"`, and a `pollInterval`
5. The conversation is completely frozen. No indication a task was created. No status updates. No way to continue chatting. The window is unusable until the task reaches a terminal state.

## The UX problem

When an MCP tool returns a Task (per the [2025-11-25 spec](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/utilities/tasks)), the chat window becomes a dead zone. The user experience is:

- A generic spinner with no context. No mention of a task, no task ID, no status, no indication of what's being waited on.
- The conversation is completely locked. You cannot ask follow-up questions, invoke other tools, or interact with the chat in any way.
- There is no cancel button, no "continue without waiting" option, no way to dismiss the pending result.
- If the task takes 5 minutes, the window is dead for 5 minutes. If the task takes 3 weeks, the window is dead for 3 weeks.
- Closing and reopening the chat loses all task context, even though the task is still alive on the server.

This is pragmatically unusable for any MCP tool that wraps an async process. Cloud deployments, CI/CD pipeline statuses, many SaaS MCP use cases, tracking statuses etc.: these are the exact use cases Tasks was designed for, and none of them work in practice because the client blocks.

The MCP Tasks spec exists specifically to decouple tool invocation from result retrieval. The server does its part: it returns immediately with a task ID and polls correctly. The entire point is that the conversation should continue while the task runs in the background.

## Expected behavior

When a `tools/call` returns a `CreateTaskResult`:

1. Return to the LLM immediately with the task metadata (taskId, status, statusMessage) and any content from the response, so it can inform the user: "I've submitted your order. Task ID: abc123. I'll let you know when it completes."
2. Show a visible task status indicator in the chat (task name, current status, elapsed time)
3. Allow the conversation to continue while the task polls in the background
4. When the task reaches a terminal state, surface the result as a new message or notification
5. Allow the user to cancel a running task from the chat UI
6. Persist task awareness across chat sessions (tasks have TTL and survive reconnection by spec design)

<details>
<summary>Potential cause in the codebase</summary>

VS Code has full MCP Tasks protocol support. The issue is not missing implementation but that the task lifecycle is treated as a transparent transport detail rather than a UX concept.

### `callTool()` awaits the task result Promise

In [`src/vs/workbench/contrib/mcp/common/mcpServerRequestHandler.ts`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/mcp/common/mcpServerRequestHandler.ts) (~line 517):

```typescript
async callTool(params, token?, onStatusMessage?): Promise<MCP.CallToolResult> {
    const response = await this.sendRequest<MCP.CallToolRequest, MCP.CallToolResult | MCP.CreateTaskResult>(
        { method: 'tools/call', params }, token
    );
    if (isTaskResult(response)) {
        const task = new McpTask<MCP.CallToolResult>(response.task, token, onStatusMessage);
        this._taskManager.adoptClientTask(task);
        task.setHandler(this);
        return task.result.finally(() => {       // <-- blocks here until terminal state
            this._taskManager.abandonClientTask(task.id);
        });
    }
    return response;
}
```

`task.result` is a Promise that resolves only when the task reaches `completed`, `failed`, or `cancelled`. The LLM's tool call is suspended on this Promise, so the entire conversation turn blocks regardless of how quickly the server returned the task.

### The polling loop runs silently

In the same file (~line 678), `McpTask` polls `tasks/get` using `task.pollInterval` (or a 2-second default). Status changes are tracked internally but never surfaced to the user.

### Immediate response metadata is discarded

MCP servers can include actionable content alongside the task in response metadata (e.g. order confirmations or status summaries the LLM could present immediately). Because `callTool()` blocks on the terminal result, this content never reaches the LLM.

</details>


Comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.115.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

Happy Coding!
Related: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/308549

I have a WIP on https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/tree/connor4312/tasks-backgrounding, will probably finish it in a week or so.


> Related: [#308549](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/308549)
> 
> I have a WIP on [`connor4312`/tasks-backgrounding](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/tree/connor4312/tasks-backgrounding), will probably finish it in a week or so.

Awesome, thanks for the heads up Connor :)

Title: ask_questions auto-replies with incorrect answers in autopilot mode

Description:
I saw the agent going down a weird rabbit hole, and it started with this question. I didn't type that answer, and the answer is totally wrong. I have `chat.autoReply` disabled but am in autopilot mode

<img width="625" height="394" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5dc42916-3a82-415c-a3a0-f638d7ca5f3a" />

I thought we said that this feature was only for evals, and wouldn't be used in the product? Why would we feed in random incorrect answers, which the model then thinks came from me?

Comments:
I believe we decided that the model should indicate the user is not available to answer and to use best judgement in these cases.
I see here we're just selecting the default 

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/fc5edfe78eb2cf345ae03b85cb95193d8ac8b52e/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/tools/builtinTools/askQuestionsTool.ts#L490-L506

Title: Incorrect command line argument quoting/escaping in `extHostTerminalShellIntegration.ts`

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

Version: 1.116.0-insider (system setup)
Commit: 1ec46de152cab65d0304db62e2aa0fcb6e1a5b1f
Date: 2026-04-09T09:55:17-07:00
Electron: 39.8.5
ElectronBuildId: 13703022
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
Node.js: 22.22.1
V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200

This is regarding the follow suspicious code, which does not appear to properly handle converting an executable/command path and an arguments array (which should always be assumed to not contain quoting/escaping, as that is a 'shell command line' concept), to a shell command line:

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/c5729b10dcc60970b844987fc751a831d0e7ae3b/src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHostTerminalShellIntegration.ts#L207-L223

Some related background:
[Everyone quotes command line arguments the wrong way](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/archive/blogs/twistylittlepassagesallalike/everyone-quotes-command-line-arguments-the-wrong-way)
[How Command Line Parameters Are Parsed](https://daviddeley.com/autohotkey/parameters/parameters.htm)
[CommandLineToArgvW function (shellapi.h)](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/shellapi/nf-shellapi-commandlinetoargvw)

AI generated bug details:

# Bug: `TerminalShellIntegration.executeCommand(executable, args)` serializes structured input into shell text using naive quoting

## Summary

The `TerminalShellIntegration.executeCommand(executable, args)` overload looks like it accepts structured `argv`-style input, but the current implementation in `src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHostTerminalShellIntegration.ts` simply builds a single shell command line string:

```ts
let commandLineValue = commandLineOrExecutable;
if (args) {
	for (const arg of args) {
		const wrapInQuotes = !arg.match(/["'`]/) && arg.match(/\s/);
		if (wrapInQuotes) {
			commandLineValue += ` "${arg}"`;
		} else {
			commandLineValue += ` ${arg}`;
		}
	}
}
```

This means the executable token is copied verbatim and the arguments are appended with only a minimal quoting heuristic before the whole string is reparsed by the active shell.

## Problem

This serialization is shell-agnostic and incomplete:

- the **command/executable portion is not quoted or escaped at all**,
- arguments are only quoted in a narrow whitespace-only case,
- literal quote characters inside arguments are not escaped,
- shell metacharacters like `;`, `&`, `|`, `<`, `>`, `` ` ``, and `$()` are not handled safely,
- and quoting/escaping rules differ significantly across shells and platforms (`zsh`, `bash`, `pwsh`, `cmd`, etc.).

As a result, this API overload does not preserve structured input reliably. Both the executable token and the argument boundaries can change when the shell reparses the constructed command line.

## Why this matters

An API shaped like:

```ts
executeCommand(executable: string, args: string[])
```

strongly suggests that `executable` and each item in `args` are treated as literal tokens. But the current behavior is really “best-effort shell string construction”, which is a weaker and more error-prone contract.

At minimum, arguments containing literal double quotes are mishandled. More broadly, this can lead to incorrect `argv` at the target process or unexpected shell interpretation.

## Relevant code path

1. `InternalTerminalShellIntegration.executeCommand(...)` builds `commandLineValue`
2. `MainThreadTerminalShellIntegration.$executeCommand(...)` forwards the string
3. `TerminalInstance.runCommand(...)` / `sendText(...)` writes it into the PTY
4. the active shell reparses that text and launches the subprocess

So this API does not directly spawn a process from an argument array; it sends a command line string to the shell.

## Example problematic inputs

These should be treated as literal tokens, but may be misparsed by the shell:

- executable: `'/tmp/My Tool'`
- args: `['a"b']`
- args: `['a b"c']`
- args: `['foo;bar']`
- args: `['$(uname)']`

## Expected behavior

Either:

1. the `executeCommand(executable, args)` overload should use shell/platform-specific escaping that preserves the executable token and argument boundaries as accurately as possible for the active shell,

or

2. the API/docs should explicitly state that this overload is only a best-effort conversion to shell text and cannot guarantee exact `argv` preservation.

At minimum, literal quotes inside arguments should be escaped correctly, and the executable token should not be emitted verbatim when it requires quoting.


Comments:
(AI generated as well: )

Follow-up note: while looking into this, I noticed a few other places in the VS Code codebase that appear to construct shell command lines from structured executable/arg input using ad hoc quoting/escaping logic. They may be unrelated, but they seem worth a quick review for the same class of issue:

- `src/vs/platform/externalTerminal/node/externalTerminalService.ts`
  - has hand-built command strings for external terminals, including simple `args.join(...)` and a `quote(args)` helper that appears to only wrap whitespace-containing args in double quotes.

- `src/vs/workbench/api/node/extHostMcpNode.ts`
  - `formatSubprocessArguments()` switches to `shell: true` for `.bat`/`.cmd` and appears to use a space-only quoting heuristic.

- `extensions/copilot/src/extension/chatSessions/vscode-node/copilotCLITerminalIntegration.ts`
  - `buildCommandForTerminal()` joins CLI args into shell text, and `quoteArgsForShell()` looks potentially incomplete for shell-specific escaping.

- `src/vs/workbench/contrib/debug/node/debugAdapter.ts`
  - the Windows `.bat`/`.cmd` path appears to manually quote/escape args before spawning through the shell.

To be clear, I have not fully validated each of these as a bug, but they look similar enough architecturally (structured input being flattened back into shell text) that they may be worth checking while this issue is being investigated.

Observe failure such as outside workspace and not open in editor, or resource not found for returned URI.

Comments:
Hi @meganrogge. As a member of the team, you can help us triage this issue by referring to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot/wiki/Copilot-Inbox-Tracker and assigning an owner directly.

Title: Regression: "e is not iterable" at POe.setItems causes repeated extension host crashes (copilot-chat 0.43.0)

Description:
## Summary

`TypeError: e is not iterable` at `POe.setItems` in `github.copilot-chat-0.43.0` crashes the extension host repeatedly. This appears to be a regression of [microsoft/vscode-copilot-release#7993](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot-release/issues/7993) (same call site: `setItems` → `_computeFn` → `_recompute` → `recomputeInitiallyAndOnChange`).

## Environment

- **Copilot Chat Extension Version:** 0.43.0 (stable)
- **VS Code Version:** 1.100+ (stable channel)
- **OS:** Windows 11

## Stack Trace

```
TypeError: e is not iterable
    at POe.setItems (github.copilot-chat-0.43.0/dist/extension.js:1162:19962)
    at Yd._computeFn (github.copilot-chat-0.43.0/dist/extension.js:1162:19701)
    at Yd._recompute (github.copilot-chat-0.43.0/dist/extension.js:1162:11920)
    at Yd.get (github.copilot-chat-0.43.0/dist/extension.js:1162:11254)
    at Yd.reportChanges (github.copilot-chat-0.43.0/dist/extension.js:1162:497)
    at DOe.endUpdate (github.copilot-chat-0.43.0/dist/extension.js:1162:19460)
    at abn (github.copilot-chat-0.43.0/dist/extension.js:1162:19178)
    at Yd.recomputeInitiallyAndOnChange (github.copilot-chat-0.43.0/dist/extension.js:1162:1092)
```

Compare with [vscode-copilot-release#7993](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot-release/issues/7993) (0.27.2025041701):
```
at EX.setItems (...extension.js:419:27542)
at Ba._computeFn (...extension.js:419:27274)
at Ba._recompute (...extension.js:419:21114)
```

Same code path, different minified symbols.

## Impact

- **21 extension host restarts** in a single VS Code session (one day of work)
- Each restart kills all extensions, not just Copilot Chat — Python language server, ESLint, GitLens, etc. all restart
- Extension host process consuming ~4 GB memory before crashes

## Reproduction

Occurs during normal Copilot Chat agent-mode usage (multi-turn conversations with tool calls). Not tied to a specific action — happens intermittently during chat sessions.

## Bisect

- **0.42.3:** No crashes. Stable over multiple days of heavy usage.
- **0.43.0:** Crashes repeatedly (installed 2026-04-08, crashes started immediately).

Rolling back to 0.42.3 eliminates the issue.

## Secondary Errors (likely consequence, not cause)

After the primary crash, the GitHub PR extension sometimes hits API timeouts during recovery, producing a secondary `TypeError: Converting circular structure to JSON` when trying to serialize a TLSSocket error object. This appears to be the PR extension failing to handle its own error gracefully during extension host restart, not the root cause.

## Notes

In [vscode-copilot-release#7993](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot-release/issues/7993), @hediet identified the probable cause: `setItems` receives an `undefined` argument. The original reporter updated their extension and it resolved, so no root-cause fix was confirmed. This regression suggests the underlying issue was masked rather than fixed.


Comments:
Thanks for creating this issue! It looks like you may be using an old version of VS Code; the latest stable release is 1.115.0. Please try upgrading to the latest version and checking whether this issue remains.

Happy Coding!

Title: [Unhandled Error] Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'clearTodoListWidget')

Description:
## Error Message
````
Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'clearTodoListWidget')
````

## Stack Trace

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;at Bx.showModel ([./src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widgetHosts/viewPane/chatViewPane.ts:749:0](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widgetHosts/viewPane/chatViewPane.ts#L749))  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;at async Bx.clear ([./src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widgetHosts/viewPane/chatViewPane.ts:801:0](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widgetHosts/viewPane/chatViewPane.ts#L801))

## Details
